v.t: In the Name of Allah, the Most-Compassionate, the All-Merciful.
All praise be to Allah, the Lord of all worlds,
the Most Compassionate, the All-Merciful,
Master of the Day of Judgement.
(O Allah!) You alone we worship, and from You alone we seek help.
Guide us upon the straight path,
the path of those whom You have blessed, not of those who have incurred Your wrath, nor of those who have gone astray.
Alif, Lam, Mim.
This is the (Glorious) Book. There is no doubt about it. It is a guide for those who are God-conscious,
who believe in the Unseen, establish prayer and spend from what we have provided them,
and who believe in what has been revealed to you (O Prophet) and what was revealed before you, and have sure faith in the Hereafter.
They are the ones upon guidance from their Lord, and they are the ones who shall be successful.
As for those who persist in disbelief, it is the same for them whether you warn them or do not warn them, they will never believe.
Allah (as a result of their choice) has set a seal on their hearts and their hearing, and there is a blindfold on their eyes. And there is a grievous punishment for them.
And among the people are some who (apparently) say: ‘We believe in Allah and the Last Day,’ but they are not believers.

They seek to deceive Allah and the believers, but they deceive none except themselves, though they do not realise it.* [* There is an omitted and inherent co-related noun ( al-mudaf al-mahdhuf ), which is referred to the Messenger (al-Rasul). The words ‘they seek to deceive Allah’ in fact, denote ‘they seek to deceive the Messenger of Allah’. Most of the leading authorities of tafsir have drawn the same meaning. See: Tafsir al-Qurtubi, al-Baydawi, al-Baghawi, al-Nasafi, al-Kashshaf, al-Mazhari, Zad al-Masir, al-Khazin, et al.]
There is a sickness in their hearts, which Allah has increased, and there will be a painful punishment for them because of the lies they used to tell.
When it is said to them, ‘Do not cause corruption in the land,’ they reply: ‘We are only reformers’.
However, they themselves are the corrupters, but they do not realise it.
And when they are told: ‘Believe as others believe,’ they reply, ‘Are we to believe as fools believe?’ It is they who are the fools, but they do not know (it).

When they (i.e. the hypocrites) meet the believers, they declare, ‘We too are believers,’ but when they are alone with their devils, they say (to them), ‘We are with you. We are only mocking (the believers).’
Allah shall throw their mockery back at them and leave them alone, wandering confused in their rebellion.
They are the ones who bought error for guidance, but their trade did not profit them, nor are they on the right path.
Their parable is that of one who kindled a fire, but as soon as it lit up all around him, Allah took away their light and left them in utter darkness; they do not see.
(They are) deaf, dumb and blind; they shall never return (to the right path).

Alternatively, (their parable is) that of a rainstorm from the sky, with utter darkness, thunder and lightning. They put their fingers into their ears, fearing death due to the thunderclaps. Allah thus encompasses the disbelievers.

The lightning almost takes away their sight; whenever it flashes upon them, they start walking on, but as soon as darkness falls over them, they stand still. Had Allah willed, He would have taken away their hearing and sight. Indeed, Allah is All-Capable of everything.
O people! Worship your Lord, Who has created you and those before you, perhaps you may become God-conscious.

He Who has made the earth a bed for you and the sky a dome, and sends down rain from the cloud, and with it He brings forth fruits for your sustenance. Therefore, do not set up rivals to Allah knowingly.

If you are in doubt concerning what we have revealed to Our (exalted) servant, produce only one chapter comparable to this Book (the Qur’an). And call upon your helpers (to assist you) besides Allah if you are truthful.
However, if you cannot do so, and you will not, then fear the Fire whose fuel is humans (i.e. the disbelievers) and stones (i.e. their idols), prepared for the disbelievers.

So give glad tidings to those who believe and perform righteous deeds. They shall have Gardens with rivers flowing beneath them. Whenever they are given its fruit to eat, they will say: ‘This is what we were given before,’ for they shall be given the like. In Paradise, there shall be chaste spouses for them, and they shall abide there forever.

Indeed, Allah is not reluctant to draw a parable, whether it is a gnat or anything more substantial than that. As for the believers, they know that it is the truth from their Lord. As for the disbelievers, they ask: ‘What could Allah mean by this parable?’ He leaves many to stray and guides many by the same. However, He leaves none to stray except the evildoers.
Those who break the covenant of Allah after it is confirmed, and sever (the relationship), which Allah has commanded to be joined, and cause corruption on the earth, it is they who have much to lose.

How do you disbelieve in Allah, (seeing that) you were lifeless and He gave you life, then He will cause you to die, and then He shall restore you to life, and then you shall be returned to Him (at last)?
He is the One Who created for you all that is on the earth. Then He turned to the higher regions (of the universe) and fashioned them into seven heavens. And He is All-Knowing of everything.

(Recall) when your Lord said to the angels: ‘Indeed, I am placing a vicegerent upon the earth.’ They submitted: ‘Will You place in it someone who will cause corruption in it and shed blood, while we glorify Your praise and proclaim Your sanctity?’ He said: ‘Indeed, I know what you do not know.’
He taught Adam the names of all things, then presented them to the angels and said: ‘Tell Me the names of these (things) if you are truthful (in your assumption).’
They (humbly) replied: ‘Glory be to You! We do not know except what You have taught us. Surely, You are the All-Knowing, All-Wise.’

He said: ‘O Adam! Tell them their names.’ So when he told them their names, He said: ‘Did I not tell you that I know the unseen of the heavens and the earth? I know all that you reveal and all that you were concealing?’
And (recall) when We said to the angels: ‘Prostrate before Adam.’ They all prostrated except Iblis (Satan), who refused and acted arrogantly. And he was among the disbelievers.
Then We said: ‘O Adam, live you and your wife in Paradise, and eat of its fruits freely, wherever you wish, but never approach this tree, lest you shall both become wrongdoers.’

But Satan made them both fall from it and caused them to depart from what they were in. We said: ‘Descend (to the earth); may (your offspring) be enemies of one other. And there shall be a dwelling place and sustenance for you on the earth for a while.’
Then Adam received certain words (of repentance) from his Lord. So He turned to him mercifully. Indeed, He is the Most-Clement, the All-Merciful.
We said: ‘Go down hence all! When the guidance comes to you from Me, those who follow My guidance shall have no fear, nor shall they grieve.
However, those who deny and reject Our revelations shall be the heirs of Hell, and they shall abide in it forever.’
O Children of Israel! Remember My blessing I bestowed upon you, and fulfil My covenant (so that) I may fulfil your covenant. And fear Me alone.

Believe in (the Book) that I have revealed (to My Messenger Muhammad (blessings and peace be upon him), which verifies (the Scriptures) that are with you. And do not be the first to deny it, and do not sell My revelations for a paltry gain. And fear Me alone.
Do not confound truth with falsehood, nor hide the truth while you know (it).
Establish the prayers, pay the alms due and bow down with those who bow down (in worship).
Would you enjoin righteousness on others and neglect yourselves, even though you recite the Scripture? Do you not then understand?
Take recourse to patience and prayer. It may indeed be hard, except for the devoutly humble,
those who are confident that they will meet their Lord and that they will return to Him.
O Children of Israel! Remember My blessing I bestowed upon you, and that I exalted you above the nations (of your age).

Fear that Day when no soul shall compensate for another. Neither any intercession (of a person who does not have permission to intercede) shall be accepted from it, nor any ransom shall be taken from it, nor shall they be helped (against Allah’s decree).
(Remember) when We delivered you from Pharaoh’s people, who had oppressed you terribly, slaying your sons and sparing your daughters. There was a great trial in that from your Lord.
(Recall) When We parted the sea for you, We delivered you and drowned Pharaoh’s people as you were yourselves watching.
(Remember) when We settled with Musa (Moses) an appointment for forty nights. Then you took up the calf (for worship) in his absence, and you were transgressors.
We still pardoned you after that; perhaps you would be thankful.
And (remember) when We gave Musa (Moses) the Book and the Criterion (that distinguishes between right and wrong), perhaps you might be guided.

And (recall) when Musa (Moses) said to his people: ‘Indeed you have wronged yourselves, my people, by taking up the calf (for worship). Now turn in repentance to your Creator. So, slay (the guilty among) your folks. This (act) would be better for you (as repentance) in the sight of your Creator.’ Then He relented towards you. Indeed, He is the Most-Clement, the All-Merciful.
(Remember) when you said: ‘O Musa (Moses)! We will not believe you until we see Allah manifestly.’ So, a thunderbolt struck you while you were watching.
Then We revived you after your death (accepting Musa’s supplication) so that you might become thankful.

And (remember when) We shaded you with clouds (in the Tiha valley) and sent down to you manna and quails, saying: ‘Eat of the good things We have given you.’ They did not harm Us (by disobeying), but they used to harm (only) themselves.

And (remember) when We said: ‘Enter this town,* and eat from it freely wherever you wish, but enter prostrating at the gate (of the town), and say: ‘We repent (for all of our sins),’ that We may forgive your sins. And We shall soon increase the virtuous (in their rewards).’ [* The town here is said to be Jerusalem, or Ariha (Jericho), a Palestinian city in the West Bank, or the land of Syria, or Palestine generally.]
But the wrongdoers substituted a different word from the one they were told. So, We sent down upon the wrongdoers a plague (as a punishment) from heaven because of their persistent disobeying.

(Remember) when Musa (Moses) prayed for water for his people, We said (to him): ‘strike the rock with your staff.’ Thereby, twelve springs gushed from it. Each tribe came to know its drinking place. (We said): ‘Eat and drink of Allah’s provision, but do not act sinfully in the land, causing corruption.’

And (remember) when you said, ‘O Musa (Moses), we cannot bear to eat only one kind of food (i.e. manna and quails), so pray to your Lord to bring out for us some of the earth’s produce, its green herbs, its cucumbers, its garlic, its lentils and its onions.’ ‘What!’ He answered, ‘Would you exchange what is better for what is worse? Go down to any city. There you shall find all that you have asked for.’ So they were struck with disgrace and poverty. And they incurred the wrath of Allah because they persistently rejected the signs of Allah and killed the Prophets unjustly. All this was because they used to disobey and would (always) commit transgressions.

Indeed, the (Muslim) believers, the Jews, the Christians and the Sabians—all those who (sincerely) believe in Allah and the Last Day and act piously shall have their reward with their Lord. There shall be no fear on them, nor shall they grieve.

(Remember) when We took a pledge from you and raised the Mount (of Sinai) above you, (and said:) ‘Hold fast to what We have given you, and bear its contents in mind, so that you may be God-conscious.’
Even after that, you turned away. Had it not been for Allah’s favour and His mercy on you, you would have certainly been among the losers.
Certainly, you know those of you who violated the Sabbath. So We said to them: ‘Be like apes: disgraced and rejected.’
So, We made it an exemplary punishment for the present and for succeeding generations, and a lesson to those who are God-conscious.

(Recall) when Musa (Moses) said to his people: ‘Indeed, Allah commands you to slaughter a cow.’ They said: ‘Are you making fun of us?’ Musa (Moses) said: ‘I seek Allah’s protection from being among the ignorant.’
They said: ‘Call upon your Lord for us, that He may clarify for us what she is.’ He answered: ‘God says the cow should neither be too old nor too young, but in between. So do as you are commanded.’
They said: ‘Call upon your Lord for us, that He may clarify for us what her colour should be.’ He said: ‘God says it should be yellow, a bright yellow cow, pleasing to the viewers.’
They said: ‘Call upon your Lord for us, that He may clarify for us what exact type of cow it is; all cows are more or less alike to us. If God wills, we shall be guided (to the right one).’

Musa (Moses) said: ‘Your Lord says: ‘It should be a healthy cow, not broken with cultivating the earth or watering the field, sound and free from any blemish.’’ They said: ‘Now you have brought the truth.’ So they slaughtered it after they had nearly failed to do so.
(Recall) when you killed someone and accused one another of it, although Allah was to expose what you were concealing.
Then We said: ‘Strike the corpse with a part of this (cow).’ Thus, Allah restores the dead to life and shows you His signs so you may understand.

Then after that, your hearts became as hard as rocks, or even harder, for there are some rocks from which streams spring out, and some from which water comes out when they split asunder, and some which crash down in fear of Allah. And Allah is not unaware of what you do.

(O believers!) Do you still hope that such people will believe you when some of them have already heard Allah’s word and then distorted it after they have understood it? And they knew (what they were doing).

When they meet the believers, they say: ‘We too are believers.’ But when they meet one another in privacy, they say: ‘Do you tell them what Allah has revealed to you (in the Torah about Prophet Muhammad (blessings and peace be upon him) so they may argue with it against you before your Lord. Do you not understand?’
Do they not know that Allah is aware of whatever they conceal and whatever they reveal?
There are some illiterate men among them (the Jews) who know nothing of the Book except lies and vague fancies. They rely only on assumption and speculation.

So, woe to those who write down the scripture with their hands and then claim, ‘This is from God,’ so they may sell it for a paltry gain. Woe to them for what their hands have written! Woe to them for what they have earned.

They say: ‘The Fire will never touch us except for a few days.’ Say: ‘Have you received a promise from Allah? If so, Allah will never break His promise. Or do you assert about Allah what you have no means to know?’
Truly, whoever commits misdeeds and is surrounded by his evil, it is they who shall be the inmates of the Hellfire. They shall abide in it forever.
And those who believe and perform righteous deeds, it is they who shall be the residents of Paradise. They shall abide in it forever.

(Remember) when We took a pledge from the Children of Israel: ‘Worship none but Allah and behave excellently with (your) parents and near kinsfolk, and the orphans and the poor. Speak good words to all people. Establish prayer and pay the alms-due.’ Then all except a few of you turned away (from the pledge), and you are but averse (to the truth).

(Recall) when We took a pledge from you: ‘You shall not shed your (own people’s) blood, nor shall you expel one another from your homelands.’ Then you acknowledged this (covenant) and testified to it.

Yet here you are, killing your kinsfolk, banishing some of your people from their homelands and helping others against them with sin and aggression. Had they come to you as captives, you would have ransomed them (so that they remained obliged to you), whereas their expulsion was (also) unlawful for you. Do you believe in one part of the Book and deny another? So the punishment for those of you who act like this will be nothing but disgrace in the worldly life, and on the Day of Resurrection, they shall be returned to the harshest torment. Allah is not unaware of your deeds.
These are the people who buy the life of this world at the price of the Hereafter. Their punishment shall not be lightened, nor shall they be helped.

Surely We gave Musa (Moses) the Book (the Torah) and sent (many) messengers after him in succession, and We gave ‘Isa (Jesus), the son of Maryam (Mary), clear signs and strengthened him with the Holy Spirit. How is it that whenever a messenger brought you something which did not suit your fancies, you became arrogant, charging some with imposture and slaying others?
They (the Jews) say: ‘Our hearts are wrapped up in covers (i.e. unreceptive).’ Instead, Allah has cursed them for their disbelief. They have but little faith.

When that Book (the Qur’an) came to them from Allah, confirming what they already had; whereas, before, they used to pray for victory against the disbelievers (through the means of the Last Prophet, Muhammad (blessings and peace be upon him), and his Book, the Qur’an). But when there came to them (the Prophet) whom they had (already) recognised (through their scriptures), they denied him. May the curse of Allah be on such disbelievers.

Low indeed is the price for which they have bartered away their souls to deny Allah’s revelation, grudging that Allah should reveal His bounty to whom He chooses from His servants. So they have ended up with wrath upon wrath. There is a humiliating punishment for the disbelievers.

When it is said to them: ‘Believe in what Allah has revealed,’ they say: ‘We only believe in what was revealed to us,’ and they reject what is besides that. However, it is the truth which corroborates the scriptures they already possess. Say: ‘Then why did you kill the prophets of Allah in the past if you were true believers?’
Indeed, Musa (Moses) brought you clear signs, but in his absence, you chose to worship the calf, and you committed wrong.

(Remember) when We took your pledge and raised the Mount (of Sinai) high above you, (declaring:) ‘Hold fast to what We have given you and listen to (Our commandment).’ They said: ‘We hear but disobey.’ So, for their disbelief, they were made to drink (the love of) the calf, which sunk deep into their hearts. Say: ‘How evil is what your (so-called) faith prompts you to do if you (actually) believe (in the Torah).’
Say: ‘If the abode of the Hereafter in the sight of Allah is to be for you alone and no one else, then you should long for death (without any fear) if your claim is true.’
However, they will never long for it because of what their hands have sent ahead. Allah is All-Knowing of the wrongdoers.

Indeed, you will find them ambitious for life more eagerly than any other people, even the polytheists. Each of them would wish to be given a life of a thousand years. Even if their lives were prolonged, it would not save them from the torment. Allah is All-Seeing of what they do.

(O Prophet) say: ‘Whoever is an enemy of Jibril ([Gabriel], should know that) it is he who has brought down the Qur’an on your heart with the command of Allah, confirming the previous scriptures, as a guide and glad tidings for the believers.
Whoever is an enemy of Allah, His angels, His Messengers and Jibril (Gabriel) and Mika’il (Michael), (should know that) Allah is indeed the enemy of such disbelievers.’
Certainly, We have sent down clear revelations to you; none will deny them except the evildoers.
Is it not so that whenever they make a covenant, some of them cast it away? Rather, most of them do not believe.

And (now) when a Messenger (Muhammad [blessings and peace be upon him]) has come to them from Allah, confirming that which they already had, some of those given the Book (i.e. the Torah) throw the Book of Allah behind their backs, as if they did not know (that it was Allah’s Book).

Moreover, they follow what the devils fabricated about the kingdom of Sulayman (Solomon). Not that Sulayman (Solomon) was faithless, but the devils themselves were faithless. They used to teach people black magic and what was sent down upon the two angels named Harut and Marut in Babylon. Yet, these two would not teach anyone until they had said, ‘We are only a temptation (to test you); do not renounce your faith (by trusting in black magic).’ However, they learnt (black magic) from both of them by which they could cause separation between husband and wife, although they harm no one with it except by Allah’s Leave. They would learn what would harm them and bring them no benefit. And they certainly knew that anyone who buys it has no share in the Hereafter. Most evil is that for which they sold their souls. Had they known (this)!
Had they believed and become God-conscious, the reward from Allah would have been far better if they only knew!

O believers! Do not say (when seeking the attention of the Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him): ‘ra‘ina (Pay attention to us).’ But say: ‘unzurna (Bless us with your kind regards),’ and attentively listen (to him). The disbelievers shall have a painful punishment.

Neither the disbelievers among the People of the Book nor the polytheists wish that any blessings should be sent upon you from your Lord. However, Allah especially chooses whom He pleases for His mercy. Allah is the Lord of Mighty Grace.
If We abrogate any verse or cause it to be forgotten, We will replace it with one better or similar. Do you not know that Allah is All-Capable of everything?
Do you not know that the kingdom of the heavens and the earth belong to Allah and that there is none besides Him to protect or help you (but with His command)?
(O Muslims!) Do you wish to question your Messenger as Musa (Moses) was questioned before? So, whoever exchanges belief for disbelief has undoubtedly strayed from the right path.

Many of the People of the Book wish to turn you back into disbelievers after you have believed, out of envy in their souls, even after the truth has become clear to them. Excuse (them) and forbear until Allah issues His edict. Indeed, Allah is All-Capable of everything.
Establish the prayer and pay the alms-due. Whatever good you send ahead for yourselves, you will find it with Allah. Indeed, Allah is All-Seeing of what you do.
They (i.e. the People of the Book also) say: ‘None shall enter Paradise except one who is Jew or a Christian.’ These are their false hopes. Say: ‘Produce your evidence if you are truthful.’
Nay, whosoever surrenders himself entirely to Allah and is excellent (in devotion), shall have his reward with his Lord. There shall be no fear on them, nor shall they grieve.

The Jews say: ‘The Christians stand on nothing,’ and the Christians say: ‘The Jews stand on nothing,’ though they all recite the (same) Book. Likewise, they (the polytheists) who do not have any (heavenly) knowledge make statements similar to what they say. Surely, Allah will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning what they used to dispute.

Who is a greater wrongdoer than one who prohibits His name from being celebrated in the mosques of Allah and strives to ruin them? Such people have no right to enter these (places) except with fear (in their hearts). There is a disgrace for them in this world and a great punishment for them in the Hereafter.
To Allah belong the East and the West. So, whichever way you turn, there is the Face of Allah. Surely, Allah is All-Encompassing, All-Knowing.
They assert: ‘Allah has begotten a son.’ Glory be to Him! Instead, everything the heavens and the earth contain belongs to Him. All things (being His creatures) are devoutly obedient to Him.
He is the Originator of the heavens and the earth. When He decrees (to originate) a thing, He only says to it, ‘Be!’ And it is.

The ignorant say: ‘Why does Allah not speak to us, or why does any (miraculous) sign not come to us (directly)?’ People before them said the same thing: their hearts are all alike. However, We have (already) made our Signs clear enough for those who are firm (in their faith).
(Esteemed Prophet!) Indeed, We have sent you with the truth as a Bearer of good news and as a Warner. You will not be questioned about the inmates of Hell.

Never will the Jews be pleased with you, nor the Christians, unless you follow their faith. Say: ‘Surely, the guidance of Allah is the only true guidance.’ If you were to follow their desires after the knowledge has come to you (from Allah), you would not have any guardian nor any helper (against the wrath of Allah).’

Those to whom We have given the Book, who recite (and follow) it as is the right of its recitation, are the ones who genuinely believe in it. However, those who deny its truth shall be the real losers.
O Children of Israel! Remember My blessing I bestowed upon you and favoured you over other nations (of that age).

Fear the Day when no soul shall compensate for another, neither shall any ransom be accepted from it, nor shall any intercession benefit it (without Allah’s permission), nor shall they be helped (against the command of Allah).

Remember when the Lord of Ibrahim (Abraham) tested him with certain commandments, which he fulfilled. He said: ‘I am going to make you the leader of humankind.’ He asked: ‘And from my descendants as well?’ Allah answered: ‘(Yes, but) My promise does not extend to the unjust.’

(Remember) when We made this House (i.e. the Ka‘ba) a place of visitation for humankind and a sanctuary (for peace and security), saying: ‘Take the standing place of Ibrahim (Abraham) as a place of prayer.’ We enjoined Ibrahim and Isma‘il (Abraham and Ishmael), saying: ‘Purify My House for those who circulate it, who stay there in retreat, and who kneel and prostrate (in worship).’

(Recall) when Ibrahim (Abraham) prayed: ‘O my Lord, make this a land of peace and bestow plenty of produce upon its people, those among them who believe in Allah and the Last Day.’ (Allah) said: ‘And as for those who disbelieve, I will (also) grant them enjoyment for a short while and then subject them to the torment of the Fire. That is an evil destination.’
As Ibrahim (Abraham) and Isma‘il (Ishmael) raised the foundations of the House, (they prayed:) ‘O our Lord, accept (this) from us. Indeed, You are the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.’

‘Our Lord! Make both of us submissive to You and (raise) from our descendants a nation submissive to You. Teach us our ways of worship and turn to us mercifully. Indeed, You are the Most-Relenting, the All-Merciful.
Our Lord! And raise a Messenger to them from among them, who shall recite Your revelations to them, teach them the Book and Wisdom, and purify them. Indeed, You are the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.’

And who will ever renounce the faith of Ibrahim (Abraham) except one who fools himself? Indeed, We have chosen him in the (present) world, and most surely, he shall be among the righteous in the Hereafter.
(Recall) when his Lord said to him: ‘Submit.’ He answered: ‘I have submitted to the Lord of all worlds.’

Moreover, Ibrahim (Abraham) enjoined this (faith) upon his children, and (so did) Ya‘qub (Jacob), saying: ‘My children, Allah has indeed chosen for you the (true) religion. Therefore, never die except as Muslims.’

Were you (Jews) present when death came upon Ya‘qub (Jacob), when he said to his sons: ‘What will you worship after me?’ They said: ‘We will worship your God and the God of your forefathers Ibrahim (Abraham) and Isma‘il (Ishmael) and Ishaq (Isaac): the One God, and to Him, do we submit.’
It was a community that had already gone before. Whatever they earned belonged to them, and whatever you earn belongs to you. You shall not be questioned about their actions.
They say: ‘Become Jews or Christians, that you may be (rightly) guided.’ Say: ‘Instead, (we follow) the faith of Ibrahim (Abraham), the upright one. He was not among the polytheists.’

Say: ‘We believe in Allah and in what was revealed to us, and what was revealed to Ibrahim (Abraham), Isma‘il (Ishmael), Ishaq (Isaac), Ya‘qub (Jacob) and the Tribes, and what Musa (Moses) and ‘Isa (Jesus) were given, and what was given to other Prophets by their Lord. We make no distinction between any of them, and to Him, do we submit.’

So if they believe as you believe in Him, they shall be surely guided, and if they turn away, they shall only be (steeped) in defiance and hostility. So Allah shall suffice you against them, and He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
The colouring of Allah, and who can give a better colour than Allah? Moreover, Him do we worship.

Say (to the Jews and Christians): ‘Would you dispute with us about Allah, whereas He is (not only) our Lord but your Lord (too)? Our deeds belong to us, and your deeds belong to you. And we devote ourselves sincerely to Him.

(O People of the Book!) ‘Do you say that Ibrahim (Abraham), Isma‘il (Ishmael), Ishaq (Isaac), Ya‘qub (Jacob) and their sons and the Tribes were Jews or Christians?’ Ask them: ‘Who knows better, you or Allah?’ Who is more unjust than him who conceals the testimony that is with him (in the Book) from Allah? Allah is not negligent of what you do.
It was a community that had already gone before. Whatever they earned belonged to them, and whatever you earn belongs to you. You will not be questioned about what they used to do.

The foolish among the people will say (about the Muslims): ‘What has turned them away from the direction of prayer they were following before?’ Say: ‘The East and the West belong to Allah. He guides whomever He likes to the straight path.’

Thus (O Muslims!) We have made you a just and moderate nation so that you may be witnesses for humankind and that the Messenger (Muhammad [blessings and peace be upon him]) may be a witness for you. Moreover, We appointed your former prayer direction (the Qibla) only to distinguish those who faithfully follow the Messenger from those who turn back on their heels. So, this (change of prayer direction) was a difficult test, except for those whom Allah had guided. However, Allah would not let your faith be in vain. Indeed, Allah is Most Kind (and) All-Merciful to humankind.

(O Beloved Prophet!) Indeed, We have seen you turning your face towards heaven (during your prayer). So, We will surely turn you towards a prayer direction that pleases you. So turn your face towards the direction of the Sacred Mosque; wherever you (believers) may be, turn your faces towards it. Those given the Book surely know this is the truth from their Lord. And Allah is not negligent of what they do.

So even if you bring every proof to those given the Book, they would not follow your prayer direction, nor would you follow their prayer direction; nor would any of their sects follow the prayer direction of others. And if you yield to their desires, after the knowledge that has come to you, you will indeed be one of the wrongdoers.

Those to whom We have given the Book recognise him (i.e. the Prophet Muhammad [blessings and peace be upon him]) as they recognise their sons. However, a group of them indeed conceals the truth while they know (very well).
(This is) the truth from your Lord. So, do not be among those who doubt (the truth).
Everyone has a direction towards which he turns. So take the lead in all good works. Wherever you may be, Allah will bring you all together. Indeed, Allah is All-Capable of everything.

So whichever path you set out on, turn your face towards the direction of the Sacred Mosque (at the time of prayer). And this is indeed the truth from your Lord. And Allah is not negligent of what you do.

So, whichever path you set out on, turn your face towards the direction of the Sacred Mosque (at the time of prayer). And wherever you may be (O Muslims), turn your faces towards it, so that the people may have no argument against you, except the wrongdoers among them. Do not fear them, but fear Me, so that I may complete My favour on you and so you may be (rightly) guided.

Thus, We have sent you a Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) from among yourselves, who recites Our revelations to you, purifies you, teaches you the Book and Wisdom and instructs you in (other) matters you did not know.
So remember Me; I will remember you. Be thankful to Me, and never be ungrateful to Me.
O believers! Seek help through patience and prayer. Indeed, Allah is with the patient.
And do not call those who were slain in the cause of Allah ‘dead’. Instead, they are alive, but you do not perceive (it).
Moreover, We will most surely test you with something of fear and hunger and loss of wealth, lives and fruits. And give glad tidings to the patient,
those who, when a calamity befalls them, say: ‘Indeed, to Allah, we belong, and to Him, we shall return.’
It is they who receive the blessings from their Lord and (His) mercy, and they are the ones who are rightly guided.

Safa and Marwa are indeed among the symbols of Allah. So, whoever performs Hajj (Pilgrimage) or ‘Umra (minor pilgrimage) to the (Sacred) House, there shall be no blame on him if he circulates between the two. And whoever does a pious act of his own accord (would be rewarded), for Allah is certainly Most Appreciative, All-Knowing.
Indeed, those who conceal the clear proofs and the guidance that We have revealed, after We have clarified it for the people in the Book, shall be cursed by Allah and condemned by the cursers,
except those who repent, make amends and declare the truth. I will certainly accept their repentance. And I am the Most Clement, the All-Merciful.
Indeed, those who disbelieve and die as disbelievers are the ones on whom shall be the curse of Allah, the angels and all humankind.
They will remain in it forever. Their punishment shall neither be lightened nor shall they be granted any respite.
And your God is (only) One God. There is no God except Him, the Most Compassionate, the All-Merciful.

Indeed, in the creation of the heavens and the earth; in the alternation of night and day; in the ships that sail upon the seas with cargoes beneficial to people; in the water which Allah sends down from the sky and with which He revives the earth after its death, and scatters therein all kinds of creatures; in the changing of the winds and in the clouds that are driven between the sky and the earth: Surely there are signs (of Allah’s Power) in all of these (things) for the people who understand.

Still, there are some who set up equals with Allah (in worship), loving them like the love of Allah, but the believers are more ardent in loving Allah. And if the wrongdoers could see (the horrors of the Hereafter) as they will see when they face the punishment, that power belongs to Allah. And that Allah is severe in punishment.
(Consider the Day) when those (leaders of misguidance) who were followed will disown the followers, when they face the punishment, and all their bonds (which now unite them) will break asunder.

And when those (polytheists) who follow will say: ‘Had there been another turn (of worldly life) for us, we would disown them as they have disowned us now.’ Thus, Allah will show them their deeds as sources of regret for themselves. However, they shall never come out of the Fire.
O people! Eat from what is lawful and pure on the earth. And do not follow Satan’s footsteps, for he is your sworn enemy.
He only incites you to commit evil and indecency and that you attribute to Allah what you do not know.

Moreover, when they are told: ‘Follow what Allah has revealed,’ they say: ‘We will instead follow what we have found our fathers practising.’ Even though their fathers neither applied any reason (to understand the truth) nor were they guided.
And the parable of (inviting) the disbelievers (to guidance) is that of someone who calls on that which can hear nothing but a shout and a cry. Deaf, dumb and blind, they understand nothing.
O believers! Eat of the good things We have provided you, and be grateful to Allah if it is He Whom you worship.

He has only forbidden you carrion, blood, the flesh of swine and animals over which any name other than Allah’s has been invoked while slaughtering. But whoever is compelled by extreme necessity (to eat any of these), neither intending to disobey nor transgress, there shall be no sin upon him. Allah is indeed Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.

Indeed, those who conceal any part of the Book Allah has revealed and sell it for a paltry gain shall swallow nothing but fire into their bellies. Allah shall neither speak to them on the Day of Resurrection nor purify them. And there is a painful punishment for them.
They are the ones who bought error for guidance and punishment for pardon. What has made them patient to face the Fire?
This is because Allah has revealed the Book with the truth. So those who differ about the Book are surely in extreme defiance.

Righteousness is not only that you turn your faces to the east or the west. Instead, righteousness is (personified by) those who believe in Allah and the Last Day, the angels, the Book and the prophets, and who, for the love of Allah, give away (some of) their wealth to their (needy) relatives, to the orphans, to the poor, to the travellers, to the beggars and for the liberation of captives; and who establish the prayer and give the alms due. Moreover, those who fulfil their covenants when they pledge, and those who are patient in distress and adversity and at times of danger (or war) are the ones who are true (in belief and righteousness) and the ones who are God-conscious.

O believers! (The law of) retribution is prescribed for you in case of killing: a free man for a free man, a slave for a slave and a female for a female. However, if the offender is pardoned by the victim’s brother, let the follow-up (for the blood money) be honourable. And let the payment to him be in an excellent manner. That is a concession from your Lord and a mercy. And if anyone exceeds these limits, there shall be a painful punishment for him.
In (the law of) retribution, there is (a safeguard of) life for you, O possessors of intellect! So that you may become God-conscious.

It is prescribed for you when death approaches one of you, and he leaves some property behind; he should make a bequest for his parents and relatives equitably. It is a duty incumbent on those who are God-conscious.
If anyone alters the bequest after hearing it, its sin will only be on those who change it. Allah is undoubtedly All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

However, if anyone fears injustice or wrong on the part of a testator and brings about a fair settlement between the parties, there shall be no sin upon him. Indeed, Allah is Most Forgiving, All-Merciful.
O believers! Fasting is prescribed for you, as was prescribed for those before you, so perhaps you may become God-conscious.

(Fast for) a fixed number of days. But whoever of you is ill or on a journey, then a (similar) number of other days later. Moreover, those who find it extremely difficult shall be liable to atonement by feeding a needy person. If anyone voluntarily performs a pious act, that is better for him. However, your fasting is better for you if you understand.

Ramadan is the month in which the Qur’an was revealed as guidance for humankind, with clear proofs of guidance and as the Criterion (to distinguish between right and wrong). So, let him among you who is present fast during this (month). And whoever is ill or on a journey, then (let him fast) a similar number of days later. Allah desires ease for you, and He does not desire hardship for you. And He wants you to complete the prescribed number (of fasting days) and to glorify Allah for having guided you, so perhaps you may give thanks.

(O Beloved Prophet!) When My servants ask you about Me, (tell them that) I am (most) near. I answer the supplicant’s call whenever he calls Me. So, let them respond to Me (with obedience) and believe in Me (firmly); perhaps they will be guided (in the right way).

You are permitted to go to your wives during the nights of the fast. They are like garments to you, as you are like garments to them. Allah knew you were betraying yourselves, so He accepted your repentance and pardoned you. So now (you can) have intercourse with them and seek what Allah has ordained for you. And eat and drink until the white thread (of dawn) becomes distinct to you from the black thread (of the night). Then, complete the fast until nightfall. And do not consort with them while confined to a (devotional) retreat in the mosques. These are the bounds set by Allah. So do not approach them. Thus, Allah clarifies His revelations to people so they may become God-conscious.
And do not usurp one another’s wealth by unfair means, nor bribe the judges with it to consume a part of the people’s wealth sinfully, while you know (that it is immoral and unlawful to do so).

They ask you about the phases of the moon. Say: ‘These are signs for people to determine the time and (the month of) pilgrimage.’ It is not righteousness that you enter the houses from their backs (i.e. observing such meaningless customs). Instead, the righteous man is the one who is God-conscious. Enter your homes from their doors and be God-conscious, so perhaps you may prosper.
Fight in the cause of Allah (only) those who wage war against you, but do not transgress (by attacking them first or exceeding the limits of just war). Indeed, Allah does not like transgressors.

And kill these (aggressing combatants) wherever you confront them, and expel them from where they drove you out. Of course, persecution is worse than killing. But do not fight them near the Sacred Mosque unless they attack you there. Then, if they attack you, kill them (in defensive fighting), for that is the proper requital of (such aggressing) disbelievers.
However, if they stop (their aggression), Allah is indeed Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.

Fight these (aggressors) until there is no more persecution, and the order of life becomes (secure) according to the command of Allah. However, if they stop (the persecution), let there be no hostility except against the aggressors.

(There is retribution in) a sacred month for (aggression in) a sacred month, and all sanctities are (under the law of) retribution. Therefore, if someone assails you, assail him in the manner in which he attacked you (i.e. the defensive response should be proportional to the offence). So be God-conscious, and know that Allah is with those who are God-conscious.
Give generously for the cause of Allah, and do not cast yourselves into destruction with your own hands and be excellent in virtuousness. Indeed, Allah loves the virtuously excellent.

Complete (the rituals of) pilgrimage (Hajj) and minor pilgrimage (‘Umra) for Allah’s sake. However, if you are prevented (on the way), then (make) such sacrificial offering as is feasible. And do not shave your heads until the sacrificial animal reaches its destination. If any of you is sick or suffers from an ailment of the head, he must pay a ransom either by fasting, alms-giving, or offering a sacrifice. And when you are safe, those who want to enjoy combining the minor pilgrimage (‘Umra) with the major pilgrimage (Hajj), let them make the sacrificial offering as it is affordable. As for someone who cannot afford (the offering), let him fast for three days during the pilgrimage and seven when you return; that is ten days in total. That is for someone whose family does not dwell by the Holy Mosque. Be God-conscious and know that Allah is severe in retribution.

The pilgrimage is (performed in) the appointed months. Whoever intends Hajj in these months (should know that) there cannot be any sexual contact, vicious talk, misbehaviour or quarrelling during the pilgrimage. And whatever good you do, Allah is well aware of it. Take (necessary) provisions (for the journey). Indeed, the best provision is God-consciousness. Therefore, be conscious of Me, O people of sound understanding!

There is no sin upon you in seeking your Lord’s grace (by trading during the pilgrimage season). When you move out from ‘Arafat, remember Allah at the sacred monument (Muzdalifa). So remember Him as He has guided you. Before this (guidance), you were indeed among the astray.
Then, go forth, from where the people move out and seek Allah’s forgiveness. Indeed, Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.

So when you have performed your (sacred) rites, remember Allah (at Mina) as you used to remember your forefathers (before Islam) or with a more passionate remembrance. And some people say: ‘Our Lord, give us in this world.’ For such, there will be no share in the Hereafter.
And yet others among them say: ‘Our Lord, grant us excellence in this world and excellence in the Hereafter, and save us from the punishment of the Fire.’
These shall have a (tremendous) share (of reward) for what they have earned, and Allah is Swift in reckoning.

Remember Allah in the appointed days. If anyone is in a hurry to depart after two days, there is no sin upon him, and whoever stays on, nor is there any sin upon him (either). This (dispensation) is for him who is God-conscious. Be God-conscious, and know that you shall be gathered to Him.
Among the people, there may be someone whose views about worldly life seem impressive to you, and he holds Allah witness to what is in his heart, yet he is the most violent of opponents.
And if he turns away, he tries to cause corruption and disorder in the land, and he ruins crops and livestock. And Allah does not like corruption.
When it is said to him: ‘Be God-conscious’, his arrogance leads him to (more) sins. So, Hell is sufficient for him. It is indeed an evil abode.
Moreover, among the people, there may be someone who sells his life seeking Allah’s pleasure, and Allah is Most Kind to (His) servants.
O believers! Submit all of you to Allah, and do not follow in Satan’s footsteps. He is indeed your manifest enemy.
If you stumble after clear proofs have come to you, then know that Allah is All-Mighty, All-Wise.
Are they waiting for Allah (Himself) to come to them in the shades of the clouds, together with the angels, for the matter to be settled (at once)? And to Allah, all matters will be returned.
Ask the Children of Israel how many clear signs We gave them. If anyone tampers with Allah’s blessing after it has reached him (then he should know that) Allah is indeed severe in retribution.

The worldly life has been made to seem glamorous for the disbelievers. They ridicule the believers, but those who are God-conscious shall be above them on the Day of Resurrection. Allah provides (His blessings) for whomever He wishes without measure (or without reckoning).

Humankind was once a single community (of believers before they went astray). Then Allah sent the prophets as bearers of good news and as warners. He sent down with them the Book with the truth so that it may judge between the people concerning their disagreements. None differed in it except those who had been given it despite the clear proofs that had come to them. It was just out of rivalry between them. Then, Allah guided, by His will, those who believed in the truth regarding what they differed. Allah guides whomever He wishes to the straight path.

(O believers!) Do you suppose that you shall enter Paradise, though the like of that (suffering) has not yet come to you, which was endured by those who passed away before you? Affliction and adversity befell them, and they were so (severely) shaken that even the messenger and the believers with him said: ‘When will Allah’s help arrive?’ Indeed, Allah’s help is ever near.

(O Prophet!) They ask you what they should spend. Say: ‘Whatever (wealth) you spend in charity, let it be for parents, close relatives, orphans, the needy and the traveller.’ And whatever good (act) you perform, Allah is indeed All-Knowing.

Fighting (in defence) has (now) been prescribed for you, though it is an object of dislike to you. However, you may hate a thing while it is good for you, and you may love a thing while it is bad for you. Allah knows, and you do not know.

(O Prophet!) They ask you about fighting in the sacred month. Say fighting in it is a grave offence, but hindering (others) from the path of Allah, disbelieving in Him, preventing (the worshippers from access to) the Holy Mosque and expelling its people from it are far greater (offences) in the sight of Allah. And persecution is worse than killing. And they will not cease fighting you until they turn you away from your religion if they can. But whoever from you turns away from his religion and dies as a disbeliever—they are the ones whose (previous) works (of devotion) shall come to nothing in this world and the Hereafter. They shall be the inhabitants of the Fire, remaining in it forever.

Those who have believed and those who have migrated (because of persecution) and have striven hard for Allah’s cause, it is they who shall expect Allah’s Mercy, and Allah is Most Forgiving, All-Merciful.

They ask you about drinking (wine) and gambling. Say: ‘There is a great sin in both, although they have some means of profit for the people, and their sin is far greater than their profit.’ And they (also) ask you what they should spend. Say: ‘All that is surplus’. Thus, Allah clarifies His revelations to you, so perhaps you may reflect,

upon this world and the Hereafter. They ask you about orphans. Say: ‘(Making an effort for) improving their condition is best. So, if you intermingle with them (by becoming their partners), they are your brothers. Allah knows the one who is corrupt from the one who reforms. Had Allah willed, He would have put you in hardship. Indeed, Allah is All-Mighty, All-Wise.’

Do not marry idolatrous women unless they believe. A believing slave girl is better than an idolatress, although she may please you. Do not wed (your daughters with) idolaters unless they believe. A believing slave is better than an idolater, although he may please you. They invite (others) to the Fire, but Allah invites (them) to Paradise and forgiveness by His Will. He clarifies His revelations for the people so that they may be mindful of advice.

They ask you about (intercourse during) menstruation. Say: ‘It is hurtful.’ So, keep away from (your) wives during (their) menstrual periods, and do not approach them until they are (again) clean. And when they become clean, you may approach them as Allah has commanded you. Indeed, Allah loves those who strive for repentance. And He loves those who strive to purify themselves.

Your wives are (like) your fields; go to your fields whenever you like. And send ahead (good deeds) for your souls and be God-conscious. Bear in mind that you shall meet Him. Give glad tidings to the believers.
(O believers!) Do not make (the name of) Allah a hindrance through your oaths from being pious and God-conscious and from making peace between people. Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.
Allah shall not hold you accountable for what is uttered carelessly in your oaths. But He shall call you to account for what your hearts have intended. Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Forbearing.

For those who swear to refrain from their wives (in having sexual relations), there shall be a waiting period of four months. So, if (during this period) they return (to their relations), Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.
But if they are determined to divorce, Allah is indeed All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

Divorced women shall wait, keeping themselves (away) from men, for three menstrual cycles. It is not lawful for them to conceal what Allah has created in their wombs if they believe in Allah and the Last Day. Their husbands have a greater right to take them back (into conjugal relation) during this period, provided they (both) desire reconciliation. The wives shall (with justice) have rights similar to the obligations upon them, following honourable norms. Men (i.e. their ex-husbands) have a degree (of responsibility) over them (in the matter of reconciliation). Allah is All-Mighty, Most-Wise (to decide their future).

Divorce is (revocable only) two times, then (let there be either) an honourable retention or an excellent release. Moreover, it is not lawful for you to take back anything from what you have given them except where both fear that they may not maintain the bounds set by Allah. So, if you (the arbitrators or the judges) fear that they may not maintain the bounds set by Allah, there shall be no sin upon either of them if she opts to give something for her release. These are the bounds set by Allah, so do not transgress them. Whoever transgresses the bounds of Allah, it is they who are the wrongdoers.

If he divorces his wife (for the third time), she will not be lawful for him until she marries a husband other than him. If he (the latter also) divorces her, in that case, there shall be no offence for either of them to return to one another—if they think that they can (now) maintain the bounds of Allah. These are Allah’s bounds, which He clarifies for those who understand.

When you divorce women, and they have reached the end of their waiting period, either retain them honourably or let them go honourably. But do not retain them to cause (them) harm and to commit aggression (against them). Whoever does that, certainly wrongs his soul. Do not take Allah’s revelations in mockery. Remember Allah’s blessings upon you and what He has sent down of the Book and Wisdom, advising you thereby. Be God-conscious, and know that Allah is All-Knowing of everything.

When you divorce women, and they have reached the end of their waiting period, do not prevent them from remarrying their (former) husbands if they have consented to each other. That is advised to those among you who believe in Allah and the Last Day. That will be more decent and purer for you. Allah knows, and you do not know.

Mothers shall suckle their infants for two full years. That is for the one who wishes to complete the suckling. The child’s father shall be responsible for their maintenance and clothing (during that period) in a reasonable manner. No soul will be charged with more than it can bear. Neither the mother shall be made to suffer harm on her child’s account nor the father on account of his child. And the father’s heirs are under similar obligations. If, after consultation, they choose by mutual consent to wean the child, there will be no sin upon them. Nor shall it be any sin upon you if you prefer to engage a wet nurse for your children, provided you pay her what you promised, according to fair terms. Be God-conscious, and know Allah is All-Seeing of what you do.

As for those among you who die leaving widows behind, they (i.e. the widows) shall wait by themselves for four months and ten days (after their husband’s death). When they have reached the end of their waiting period, you are not accountable for what they reasonably decide for themselves. Allah is All-Knowing of what you do.

There is no sin upon you in giving such women a hint of a marriage proposal (during the waiting term) or what you may secretly cherish in your hearts. Allah knows that you will be thinking of (proposing to) them. But do not make a secret arrangement with them unless you speak (to them) honourably. Do not confirm the marriage tie until the prescribed term reaches its end. Be aware that Allah knows what is in your hearts, so beware of Him, and know that Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Forbearing.

There is no sin upon you if you divorce your wives while you have not touched them (by consummating the marriage) or settled a dowry for them. (Even if the dowry is not fixed) yet provide for them reasonably: the wealthy according to his means and the poor man according to his capacity, with honourable sustenance. (It is) an obligation on the virtuous.

And if you divorce them before you have touched them (to consummate the marriage), but after you have fixed the dowry for them, (pay) half of what you have previously fixed, unless they waive (their right), or unless the one who holds the marriage tie waives (his right). However, waiving your right (O husbands) is nearer to God-consciousness. So, do not forget generosity towards one another (even in these moments of stress). Indeed, Allah is All-Seeing what you do.
Be (constantly) watchful of your prayers, and (especially) the middle prayer, and stand before Allah with all devotion.
When you fear (danger), pray on foot or while riding, and when you are safe again, remember Allah, as He has taught you what you did not know.

Those of you who (are about to) die leaving widows behind shall bequeath for their wives, providing (them) a year’s maintenance without causing them to leave their homes. However, if they leave (of their own accord), you are not accountable for what they decide for themselves according to reasonable norms. And Allah is All-Mighty, All-Wise.
For divorced women, there shall (also) be a provision according to honourable norms. (This is) an obligation on the God-conscious.
Thus does Allah clarify His revelations to you so that you may understand (the laws and norms).

Have you not seen those who left their homelands fearing death, and they were in thousands? Allah said to them: ‘Die’ (and they died). Then He revived them. Indeed, Allah is Gracious to humankind, but most people are not grateful.
So fight in the way of Allah, and know that Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.
Who shall lend Allah a generous loan which He may multiply for him manifold? And Allah seizes and expands (the means of life), and to Him shall you be returned.

Have you not heard of the leaders of the Israelites after Musa (Moses), when they said to their prophet: ‘Appoint a king for us, so that we may fight in the way of Allah (under his command).’ He said (to them): ‘May it not be that! Were the fighting prescribed for you, you would not fight’. They said: ‘Why should we not fight in the way of Allah when we have been expelled from our homelands and separated from our children?’ But when fighting was prescribed for them, they turned back except a few among them. Allah is All-Knowing of the wrongdoers.

Their prophet said to them: ‘Allah has appointed Talut (Saul) to be your king.’ They said: ‘How can he have kingship over us when we have a greater right to kingship than him, as he has not been given plenty of wealth?’ He said: ‘Indeed Allah has chosen him over you and greatly increased him in knowledge and physique. Allah gives His kingdom to whom He wills. Allah is Magnanimous, All-Knowing.’

Their prophet said to them: ‘Indeed, the sign of his kingship will be that the Ark (of the Covenant) will come to you. There will be in it (the gift of) tranquillity from your Lord and the relics left behind by the House of Musa (Moses) and the House of Harun (Aaron). The angels will be carrying it. Indeed, there is a sign in that for you, if you are true believers.’

So, when Talut (Saul) set out with the troops, he said: ‘Allah will indeed test you with a river. Anyone who drinks from it will not belong to me (i.e. he shall cease to be my soldier). And whoever does not taste it will belong to me, except he who scoops up just one handful (he will be excused).’ But they all drank from it, except a few of them. So, when he and the (remaining) believers with him crossed it (i.e. the river), they said: ‘We have no strength today against Jalut (Goliath) and his troops.’ Those (believers) who were confident that they would meet Allah (on the Day of Judgement) said: ‘How often a small faction has overcome a large faction by Allah’s will! And Allah is with the patient.’
So when they advanced to encounter Jalut (Goliath) and his troops, they said: ‘O our Lord, fill our hearts with patience, make our feet steady and help us against the disbelieving people.’

So they defeated them (i.e. the army of Goliath) by Allah’s will. Dawud (David) killed Jalut (Goliath), and Allah gave him the kingdom and wisdom and taught him whatever He pleased. Were it not for Allah’s repelling people, some by the means of others, the earth would surely have been corrupted. However, Allah is Gracious to (the creatures of) all worlds.
(O Prophet Muhammad!) These are the revelations of Allah, which We recite to you with the truth. And you are indeed among the messengers.

Those are the Messengers—We have honoured some above others. Among them is the one to whom Allah spoke (directly), and He raised some of them in ranks. And We gave ‘Isa (Jesus), the son of Maryam (Mary), clear signs and strengthened him with the Holy Spirit. Had Allah willed, those who succeeded them would not have fought one another after the clear proofs had come to them. But they differed (between themselves): among them were some who believed, and among them were others who disbelieved. Had Allah willed, they would not have fought (among themselves). However, Allah does whatever He wills.
O believers! Spend from what we have provided you before a day arrives when there shall be neither trading, nor friendship, nor intercession. And the disbelievers—they are the wrongdoers.

Allah! There is no god except Him, the Ever-Living, the All-Sustainer. Neither drowsiness nor sleep overtakes Him. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Who can intercede with Him except by His permission? He knows what is before them and what is behind them. They cannot comprehend anything of His knowledge except what He wills. His Throne extends over the heavens and the earth, and it does not tire Him to preserve them both. He is the All-Exalted, the All-Supreme.

There is no compulsion in religion. True guidance has become distinct from error. So, the one who renounces false gods and believes in Allah has grasped the firmest handhold. It is not breakable, and Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

Allah is the Guardian of the believers. He brings them out of darkness into light. As for the disbelievers, their guardians are the idols. They bring them out of light into darkness. They are the inhabitants of the Fire. They shall remain in it forever.

(O Prophet!) Have you not heard (the account) of the one who argued with Ibrahim (Abraham) about his Lord because Allah had given him the kingdom? When Ibrahim (Abraham) said (to Namrud): ‘My Lord is the One Who gives life and causes death.’ He (Namrud) replied: ‘I (too) give life and cause death.’ Ibrahim (Abraham) said: ‘Indeed, Allah brings the sun from the east, so you bring it from the west.’ So, the faithless one became confounded. And Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.

Or likewise, (the account of) the one who passed by a town and it had fallen into ruins? He (‘Uzayr [Ezra]) said: ‘How will Allah revive this after its death?’ So Allah caused him to die for a hundred years, and then He raised him to life. He asked: ‘How long have you remained (in this state)?’ He said: ‘I have remained a day or a part of a day.’ Allah said: ‘No! You have remained (like this for) a hundred years. Look at your food and drink; it has not changed with time. (Now) look at your donkey (how it is reduced to bones). (This is) so that We may make you a sign for the people. (Now) observe the bones—how We bring them together and then clothe them with flesh.’ When it became evident to him, he said: ‘I know (now) that Allah is All-Capable of everything.’

And (recall) when Ibrahim (Abraham) said: ‘My Lord! Show me how You revive the dead.’ Allah asked: ‘Do you not believe?’ He said: ‘Yes, but for my heart to be content.’ Allah said: ‘Take four birds, train them to come back to you and then (cut their bodies to pieces and) scatter their parts on every mountain, and then call them—they will come hastening to you. And know that Allah is All-Mighty, All-Wise.’

The example of those who spend their wealth in the way of Allah is that of a grain (of wheat or corn), which grows into seven ears; in each ear, there are a hundred grains. Allah multiplies for whomever He wills. Allah is All-Embracing, All-Knowing.

Those who spend their wealth in the way of Allah and then do not follow up what they have spent with reproaches and insults shall have their reward with their Lord. There shall be no fear on them, nor shall they grieve.
Kind words and forgiveness are better than a charitable deed followed by a hurtful act. Allah is All-Sufficient, All-Forbearing.

O believers! Do not render your charities void by reproaches and hurtful words and deeds; like someone who spends his wealth only to be seen by people, not believing in Allah and the Last Day. His example is that of a smooth rock covered with soil; heavy rain falls upon it and leaves it barren. They shall not be able to gain anything of what they have earned. Allah does not guide such disbelieving people.

And the example of those who spend their wealth seeking Allah’s pleasure and reassuring themselves (in their belief) is that of a garden on a hill-side—if heavy rain falls upon it, it yields up its fruits twofold; even if no rain falls upon it, (it is watered by) the dew. Allah is All-Seeing of what you do.

Would anyone of you like to have a garden of palm trees and grapevines with streams flowing beneath it, with all kinds of fruit for him therein, and old age overtakes him while he has weak offspring, then a fiery hurricane strikes it and burns it down? In this way, Allah clarifies His communications for you so that you may reflect.

O believers! Spend from the good things you have earned (lawfully) and from what We have produced for you from the earth, and do not intend to give the bad part of it as charity, which you would not accept for yourselves unless you overlook it. And know that Allah is All-Sufficient, All-Praiseworthy.
Satan threatens you with poverty and prompts you to indecency, whereas Allah promises you (His) pardon and bounty. And Allah is All-Embracing, All-Knowing.
He grants wisdom to whom He pleases, and whoever is granted wisdom is certainly given an abundant good. Yet none will remember except those possessing insight.
Whatever charity you may give, and whatever vow you may vow, Allah indeed knows it, and the wrongdoers shall have no helpers.

If you disclose charitable deeds (in public), that is fine, but if you hide them and give them to the poor (privately), that is better for you. It will atone for (some of) your misdeeds. Allah is All-Aware of what you do.

You are not responsible for their guidance. Rather, Allah guides whomsoever He wills. Whatever wealth you spend (in charity) is for your (own) benefit, as long as you spend only to seek Allah’s pleasure. And whatever wealth you spend (in charity) shall be repaid to you in full, and you will not be wronged.

(Charity is) for the needy wholly occupied in the way of Allah, not capable of moving about in the land (for trade). The ignorant (of their position) consider them to be wealthy because of their abstinence (and modest behaviour). But you recognise them by their characteristic; they do not ask the people importunately. And whatever wealth you spend—indeed Allah is All-Knowing of it.
Those who spend their wealth (in charity) by night and day, secretly and openly—for them is their reward with their Lord; there shall be no fear on them, nor shall they grieve.

Those who consume usury will not stand (on the Day of Resurrection) but like the one whom Satan has demented by his touch. That is because they say: ‘Trade is just like usury.’ But Allah has permitted trade and forbidden usury. On receiving a warning from his Lord, whoever stops (taking usury) shall keep (the gains of) whatever has passed. And his matter is in the hands of Allah. And those who go back (to usury) shall be the inhabitants of the Fire. They shall remain in it forever.
Allah eliminates (blessings from) usury and makes charity flourish. Allah does not like any ingrate sinner.
Indeed, those who believe, perform righteous deeds, establish the prayer and give the alms-due shall have their reward with their Lord. There shall be no fear on them, nor shall they grieve.
O believers! Be God-conscious, and abandon (all claims to) what remains of usury if you are true believers.

If you do not (comply), then be warned of war from Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him). If you repent, you will have your principal, neither doing wrong to others nor being wronged.
And if the debtor is in adversity, let there be respite until the time of ease. And if you remit (the debt) as an act of charity, it will be better for you if you know (the worth of generosity).
And be cautious of the Day when you (all) shall be brought back to Allah. Then, every soul shall be recompensed fully for what it has earned—and they will not be wronged.

O believers! Whenever you contract a loan for a specific period, put it in writing. Let a scribe write it down between you justly. No scribe should refuse to write as Allah has taught him. Therefore, let him write, and let the debtor dictate, and let him be conscious of Allah, his Lord, and not diminish anything from it. If the debtor is feeble-minded, weak or incapable of dictating himself, then let his guardian dictate justly. And call in to witness from among your men two witnesses. If two men are not there, then a man and two women out of those you judge fit to act as witnesses. So if one of the two (women) defaults, the other one of the two can remind her (—this being a privilege exclusive to women). The witnesses must not refuse when they are summoned (to give evidence). Do not consider it burdensome to write it (i.e. the loan transaction) down, (be it) small or great, along with the time it falls due; this way is more equitable according to Allah, more reliable as testimony and more likely to prevent doubts arising between you. But if the transaction is a deal on the spot, which you conclude among yourselves from hand to hand, then there is no offence for you in not writing it down. Have witnesses present whenever you barter with one another, and let no harm be done to the writer or the witness. If you did cause them harm, it would be a transgression on your part. Be God-conscious; Allah shall teach you, and Allah is All-Knowing of everything.

If you are on a journey and cannot find a scribe, then (something) may be taken into possession as security. If one of you entrusts to another, let the trustee restore the trust to its owner. He should be God-conscious, his Lord. Do not conceal testimony; whoever conceals it, his heart will indeed be sinful. And Allah is All-Knowing of what you do.

To Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Whether you reveal whatever is in your hearts or conceal it, Allah will bring you to account for it. Then He will forgive whomever He wills and punish whomever He wills. And Allah is All-Capable of everything.

The Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) believes in what has been revealed to him from his Lord, and so do the believers. They all believe in Allah, His angels, His Books and His Messengers. (They declare:) ‘We make no distinction between any of His Messengers.’ And they say: ‘We hear and obey. (Grant us) Your forgiveness, our Lord, and to You is the final return.’

Allah does not burden any soul beyond its capacity. Whatever (good) it earns will be for its (own) benefit, and whatever (evil) it incurs will be its (own) loss. ‘Our Lord! Do not take us to task if we forget or make mistakes. Our Lord! Do not burden us as you burdened those before us. Our Lord! Do not burden us with more than we have the strength to bear. Excuse us, and forgive us and have mercy upon us. You are our Master. So help us against the disbelieving people.’
Alif, Lam, Mim.
Allah! There is no god except Him, the Ever-Living, the All-Sustaining.
(O Esteemed Prophet!) He has (gradually) revealed to you the Book with the truth confirming what was (revealed) before it, as He had revealed the Torah and the Injil (the Gospel),

previously, as a guide for people. And (now) He has revealed the Criterion (i.e. the Holy Qur’an). Indeed, those disbelieving in Allah’s revelations will have a severe punishment. And Allah is All-Mighty, the Lord of Retribution.
Nothing is hidden from Allah on the earth or in the sky.
He is the One Who shapes you in the wombs (of your mothers) as He wills. There is no god except Him, the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.

(O Prophet!) He is the One Who has revealed to you the Book. Some of its verses are definite (and clear)—they are the foundation of the Book—while others are symbolic. As for those who have deviance in their hearts, they follow what is symbolic in it, seeking doubt and temptation and a misleading interpretation. But no one knows its real meaning except Allah, and those firmly rooted in knowledge say: ‘We believe in it; all of it is from our Lord.’ And none takes cognisance except those who possess a deep understanding.
(They say:) ‘Our Lord! Do not make our hearts deviate after You have guided us, and grant us mercy from Yourself. Indeed, You are the All-Bestower.
Our Lord! You will indeed gather (all) humankind for a Day about which there is no doubt. Indeed, Allah never breaks (His) promise.’
As for the disbelievers, neither their wealth nor their children shall avail them (in the least) against Allah. It is they who are the fuel of the Fire.
(It is) like the affair of Pharaoh’s folk and those before them. They denied our signs. So Allah seized them for their sins, and Allah is severe in retribution.
(O Prophet!) Say to the disbelievers: ‘You shall be overcome and driven (together) into Hell; it is an evil resting place.’

Indeed, there was a sign for you in the two armies which met (on the battlefield at Badr); one group fighting in the way of Allah and the other disbelieving, whom they (the former) saw as twice themselves, as the eye sees. Allah strengthens with His help whom He wills. There was indeed a lesson in that for those who have insight.

The love of (worldly) temptations has been adorned for humankind, such as (the love of) women and children, accumulated treasures of gold and silver, splendid horses, livestock and farms. That is the enjoyable provision of the worldly life. But Allah—with Him is the best abode.

(O Esteemed Prophet!) Say: ‘Shall I inform you of something better than all of these? For those who guard themselves against evil, there will be Gardens from their Lord with rivers flowing beneath them, to dwell in forever along with chaste spouses and Allah’s good pleasure.’ Allah is All-Seeing of (His) servants.
(They are) those who say: ‘Our Lord, we believe, forgive us our sins and save us from the punishment of the Fire.’
(They are) patient, truthful, obedient, charitable and imploring (Allah’s) forgiveness at dawn.
Allah bears witness that there is no god except Him. And (so do) the angels and those possessing knowledge. (He is) the Maintainer of Justice. There is no god but Him, the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.

Indeed, the (true) religion in Allah’s sight is Islam. Those given the Book did not differ except after knowledge had come to them. (This opposition appeared only)—out of rivalry among themselves. Whoever disbelieves in Allah’s revelations (should know that), Allah is quick in reckoning.

(O Esteemed Prophet!) If they argue with you, say: ‘I have surrendered myself to Allah, and so have those who follow me.’ And tell those who are given the Book and the unlearned people: ‘Do you submit (to Allah)?’ If they submit, they shall be rightly guided. But if they turn away, then you only have to convey (the message). Allah is All-Seeing of (His) servants.
Those who disbelieve in Allah’s revelations, slay the Prophets wrongly and slay those people who call for justice—give them the tidings of a painful punishment.
They are the ones whose deeds shall come to nothing in this world and the Hereafter, and they shall have no helpers.
Have you not seen those given a portion of the Book being called to Allah’s Book that it might judge between them, then a group of them turn away (refusing to comply)? And they are disregardful.
That is because they say: ‘The Fire shall not touch us except for a (small) number of days. They have been deceived in their religion by the lies they used to fabricate.
But how (horrible) will it be when we gather them on a Day about which there is no doubt, when every soul shall be paid in full for what it has earned, and they will not be wronged?

Say: ‘O Allah, Master of the kingdom! You give authority to whomever You will, and You take away authority from whomever You will. You honour whomever You will, and You disgrace whomever You will. All good is in Your Hand. Indeed, You are All-Capable of everything.

You merge the night into the day, and You merge the day into the night. You bring the living out of the dead, and You bring the dead out of the living. And You provide sustenance to whomever You will without measure.’

The believers should not take the disbelievers as guardians (or trustworthy protectors) instead of the believers. And whoever does that will get nothing from (the guardianship of) Allah unless you guard yourselves against them with full consciousness. Allah warns you to beware of (disobeying) Him, and to Allah is the eventual return.

Say: ‘Whether you conceal what is in your hearts or reveal it, Allah knows it, and He (thoroughly) knows whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And Allah is All-Capable of everything.’

The Day (will surely come) when every soul shall find present (before it) whatever good it has done. And whatever evil it has perpetrated, it will wish there was a great distance between it and that evil. Allah warns you to beware of (disobeying) Him, and Allah is Most-Kind towards (His) servants.
(O Esteemed Prophet!) Say: ‘If you love Allah, then follow me; Allah will love you and forgive you your sins, and Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.’
Say: ‘Obey Allah and the Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him).’ But if they turn away, then Allah does not like the disbelievers.
Indeed, Allah chose Adam and Nuh (Noah) and the descendants of Ibrahim (Abraham) and the descendants of ‘Imran above all the nations,
as offspring, one from another. And Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

And (recall) when the wife of ‘Imran (Maryam’s father) said: ‘My Lord, I dedicate to You what is in my womb, freeing him (of worldly obligations). So accept it from me. Indeed You are the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.’

And when she delivered a baby girl, she said: ‘My Lord, I have given birth to a female.’ But Allah knew very well what she had given birth to; the male (she prayed for) was not like the female (that Allah had blessed her with). (She further said:) ‘I have named her Maryam (Mary—the worshipper). And indeed, I commend her and her offspring to Your care against (the evil of) the accursed Satan.’

So her Lord accepted her with gracious favour, made her grow in a worthy upbringing and entrusted her to the care of Zakariyya (Zachariah). Whenever Zakariyya (Zachariah) visited her in the prayer chamber, he would find provisions with her (i.e. novel food items and out-of-season fruit). He said: ‘O Maryam, from where has this come to you?’ She said: ‘It comes from Allah. Indeed Allah provides for whomever He pleases without measure.’
