And help one another in righteousness and God-vigilance (guarding yourselves against evil), and do not cooperate in sin and aggression. And be God-conscious. Indeed, Allah is severe in retribution.

Forbidden to you are carrion, blood, the flesh of swine, that animal on which the name of someone other than Allah is invoked at the time of slaughtering, and the animal that dies by strangling, and the animal beaten to death by a violent blow, and the animal which dies by falling, and the animal gored to death, and the animal which wild beasts have eaten, unless you still slaughter it (after it being wounded, in the correct manner), and the animal slaughtered on the stones set up for the idols and that you should allot shares (or decide fortunes) by the divining arrows—all of that is a transgression. Today, the disbelievers have lost all hope of (overcoming) your religion. Therefore, do not fear them but fear Me. Today, I have perfected your religion for you, completed My blessing upon you and approved Islam as your religion. But whoever is extremely compelled by hunger, without inclining purposely to sin, then Allah is indeed Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.

(O Prophet!) They ask you what is permissible for them (to eat). Say: ‘All good things are permissible for you. And as for what you have taught among the hunting dogs (to catch), teaching them out of what Allah has taught you, eat from what they have seized for you, but mention Allah’s Name over it, and be God-conscious. Indeed, Allah is swift at reckoning.’

This day, all good things have been made permissible for you. The food of the People of the Book is made permissible for you, and your food is permissible for them. (Similarly,) the chaste from among the believing women and the chaste from among those given the Book before you (are permissible for you in marriage) when you have given them their bridal gifts, desiring chastity, not unlawful sexual intercourse or taking (them) as secret mistresses. And whoever renounces (his) faith, all his deeds will become void, and in the Hereafter, he will be among the losers.

O believers! When you (intend to) stand for prayer, wash your faces and your hands up to the elbows, wipe your heads, and (wash) your feet up to the ankles. If you are in a state of major ritual impurity, then purify yourselves (by washing your whole body). But if you are ill, or on a journey, or one of you has come from answering the call of nature, or you have intimate contact with a woman (having sexual intercourse) and cannot find water, then (in these cases) make recourse to clean earth, and wipe your faces and your hands with it. Allah does not want to put you into hardship, but He wants to purify you and complete His blessing upon you so that you may be grateful.
Remember Allah’s blessing upon you and His covenant by which He bound you when you said: ‘We hear and we obey.’ And be conscious of Allah. Indeed, Allah is All-Knowing of what is in the breasts.

O believers! Stand firm for Allah, bearing witness to justice, and never let the hatred of a people lead you to be unjust. Be just! That is nearer to God-consciousness. And be conscious (of your duty) to Allah. Allah is indeed All-Aware of what you do.
Allah has promised those who believe and perform righteous deeds (that) they shall have forgiveness and a great reward.
And those who disbelieve and deny Our revelations shall be the inhabitants of the Hellfire.

O believers! Remember Allah’s blessing upon you when (some) people sought to extend their hands against you, but He restrained their hands from you. Be conscious of Allah. And in Allah, let the believers put their trust.

Allah made a covenant with the Children of Israel, and We raised twelve leaders among them. And Allah said: ‘I am surely with you.’ If you establish the prayer, pay the alms due, believe in My messengers, support them and lend Allah a generous loan, I will most certainly absolve you of your misdeeds and will surely admit you into Gardens with rivers flowing beneath them. But whoever among you disbelieves after that has certainly strayed from the right path.

Then, for their breaking their covenant, We cursed them and hardened their hearts. They distort the words (of Allah’s Book) from their places (or their meanings), and they have forgotten a part of what they were reminded of. And you will not cease to discover treachery on their part, except a few of them. So pardon them and forbear. Indeed, Allah loves the benevolent.

(We also took a pledge) from those who say: ‘We are Christians.’ We took their pledge, but they (too) forgot a portion of what they were reminded. So, We let hostility and enmity arise between them till the Day of Resurrection. And soon Allah will inform them of what they had been doing.

O People of the Book! Indeed, Our Messenger has come to you, who clarifies for you much of what you have been concealing of the Book, and overlooks many things (that you have been doing). There has indeed come to you a light from Allah (i.e. Prophet Muhammad (blessings and peace be upon him) and a Manifest Book (i.e. the Holy Qur’an).
Allah guides by it whosoever follows (the course of) His pleasure to the ways of peace, and He brings them out of darkness into light by His will, and He guides them to a straight path.

They indeed have disbelieved, who say: ‘Allah is the Messiah, son of Maryam (Mary).’ Say: ‘Who would have any power over Allah if He intended to destroy the Messiah, the son of Maryam, his mother, and all those on the earth?’ And to Allah belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and whatever is between them. He creates whatever He wills, and Allah is All-Capable of everything.

The Jews and the Christians say: ‘We are the children of Allah and His beloved ones.’ Say: ‘Then why does He punish you for your sins?’ Instead, you are merely humans from among those whom He has created. He forgives whom He pleases and punishes whom He wills. And to Allah belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, and towards Him is the eventual return.

O People of the Book! Indeed, Our Messenger has come to you, clarifying (the Divine teachings) for you after an interval between the messengers, lest you say: ‘There has not come to us any bearer of glad tidings, nor any warner.’ Indeed, a bearer of glad tidings news and a warner has come to you, and Allah is All-Capable of everything.

And (recall) when Musa (Moses) said to his people: ‘O my people! Remember Allah’s blessing upon you when He raised prophets among you and made you kings, and He gave you (the miraculous favours) that He had never given anyone in the world.
O my people! Enter the Holy Land Allah has assigned for you, and do not turn on your backs, for then you will become losers.’
They said: ‘O Musa (Moses)! Surely, there are dominant (and powerful) people in it, and we shall never enter it until they leave it. If they leave it, we will certainly enter.’

Said two men from among those feared (Allah), whom Allah had blessed: ‘Enter upon them through the gate. For once you have entered it, you shall be victorious. And put your trust in Allah if you are believers.’
They said: ‘O Musa (Moses)! We will never enter it as long as they remain there. So, go ahead, you and your Lord, and you both fight. We shall stay right here.’
He (Musa [Moses]) said: ‘My Lord! I have no control (over anyone) except for myself and my brother (Harun [Aaron]). So separate us from the rebellious people.’
(The Lord) said: ‘So this (land) shall be forbidden to them for forty years, while they will be wandering through the earth aimlessly. So do not grieve over the rebellious people.’

(Esteemed Prophet!) Relate to them with truth the story of Adam’s two sons (Habil and Qabil [Abel and Cain]): Each of them offered a sacrifice. It was accepted from one of them (Habil [Abel]) and was not from the other (Qabil [Cain]). He (Qabil) said: ‘I will most certainly slay you.’ He (Habil) replied: ‘Indeed Allah accepts (offering) only from the God-conscious.
Even if you extend your hand towards me to kill me, I will not extend my hand towards you to kill you. Indeed, I fear Allah, the Lord of all worlds.

I desire you to bear (the burden of) my sin (i.e. the sin of killing me) and also your (other) sins (i.e. the preceding ones for which your offering was rejected). You will then become an inhabitant of the Fire. And that is the recompense of the wrongdoers.’
Then his soul prompted him to slay his brother, so he killed him; thus, he became one of the losers.

Then Allah sent a crow, scratching into the earth, to show him how he might bury the dead body of his brother. He said: ‘Oh, woe to me! Am I unable to be as this crow and bury my brother’s corpse?’ And he became one of the regretful.

For this reason, We prescribed for the Children of Israel that whoever kills a soul, without it being (a lawful punishment) for another soul or (a lawful punishment for spreading) disorder in the land, it is as if he has killed all humanity. And whoever saves a life, it is as if he has saved all humanity. Indeed, Our messengers came to them with clear signs; even after that, many of them continued transgressing in the land.

Indeed, the penalty for those who wage war against Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) and try to cause corruption (and disorder) in the land (by perpetrating robbery, bloodshed and terrorism) is that they should be (lawfully) awarded capital punishment, or they should be crucified, or their hands and feet should be (lawfully) struck off from opposite sides, or they should be (lawfully) imprisoned (or banished from the land). That is their disgrace in this world, and there is a terrible punishment for them in the Hereafter.
Except those who repent before you overpower them; then know that Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.
O believers! Be conscious of Allah, seek the means that bring you closer to Him, and strive hard in His way, so that you may be prosperous.

Indeed, if the disbelievers were to possess everything on the earth and twice over (and offer it all) to ransom themselves from the punishment of the Day of Resurrection, it shall not be accepted from them. And there will be a painful punishment for them.
They will wish to come out from the Fire, but they shall never be able to leave it, and there is a lasting punishment for them.
As for the male and female thieves, cut off their hands as a requital for what they have done. (That is) an exemplary punishment from Allah. And Allah is All-Mighty, All-Wise.
But whoever repents after his wrongdoing and reforms (himself), then Allah shall accept his repentance. Indeed, Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.
Do you not know that to Allah belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth? He punishes whomever He wills and forgives whomever He pleases. And Allah is All-Capable of everything.

O Messenger! Let them not grieve you, those who hasten in (promoting) disbelief, those who say: ‘We believe’ with their mouths, but their hearts do not believe. Nor, those among the Jews who listen eagerly intending (to tell) lies (about you) to others who have not yet come to you, (and they) distort the words from their meanings (and contexts), and say (to each other): ‘If you are given this (particular ruling), then take it, but if you are not given this, then beware!’ And whomever Allah intends to try (by error or temptation), you cannot avail him anything against Allah. They are the ones whose hearts Allah did not desire to purify. For them is disgrace in this world, and there is a grievous punishment for them in the Hereafter.

(They are) listeners of falsehood, (and) consumers of the unlawful. If they come to you, you can either judge between them or disregard them. If you disregard them, they shall not harm you in any way. But if you (decide to) judge, judge between them with justice. Indeed, Allah loves the just.
And how should they make you their judge when they (already) have the Torah with them, which contains Allah’s judgement? Yet they turn their backs after that. And they are not believers.

Indeed, We sent down the Torah, containing guidance and light, by which the prophets who had submitted themselves (to Allah) judged the Jews. So, too, did the rabbis and scholars (give the judgements) according to Allah’s Book, as they were entrusted to preserve and to which they were (made) witnesses. So, do not fear the people but fear Me, and do not sell My revelations for a small gain. And those who do not judge according to what Allah has revealed, it is they who are the disbelievers.

And We ordained for them in this (Book, the Torah): ‘A life for a life, an eye for an eye, a nose for a nose, an ear for an ear, a tooth for a tooth and equal retaliation for wounds. But whoever waives it charitably, it shall be an atonement for him. And those who do not judge according to what Allah has revealed, it is they who are the transgressors.

And following in their footsteps, We sent ‘Isa, the son of Maryam (Jesus, the son of Mary), confirming what was before him of the Torah. We gave him the Injil (the Gospel), containing guidance and light, confirming what was (revealed) before him in the Torah—a guidance and admonition for the God-conscious.
Let the People of the Injil (the Gospel) judge according to what Allah has revealed in it. Those who do not judge according to what Allah has revealed, it is they who are disobedient.

And We have sent down to you the Book with the truth, as a verifier of all Scriptures that had come before it and as a guardian over it. So, judge between them according to what Allah has revealed, and do not follow their desires against the truth that has come to you. For each (community) among you, We have assigned a code of law and a way of life. And if Allah had willed, He would have made you one nation, but (He wills) that He should test you in respect to what He has given you. So take the lead in all good works. To Allah shall be your return all together. Then, He will inform you about what you used to differ.

So judge between them according to what Allah has revealed, and do not follow their desires. And beware of them lest they (try to) tempt you away from any part of what Allah has sent down to you. But if they turn away (from your judgement), then know that Allah wants to punish them for some of their sins. And indeed, many people are disobedient.
Is it the law of pagan ignorance that they are seeking (to be judged by)? And who is better than Allah in judgement for people of firm faith?

O believers! Do not consider the (hostile) Jews and Christians as (your) guardians (or reliable allies). They are guardians (and supporters) of each other. Anyone of you who trusts them as an ally will (also) become one of them. Indeed, Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.

You see those with sickness in their hearts rushing towards them, saying: ‘We fear lest fortune may turn against us.’ But perhaps Allah will grant victory or some (other) command (as a sign of success) from Him. Then, they shall be remorseful for what they kept secret in their hearts.
And the believers will say: ‘Are these the ones who swore by Allah their solemn oaths that they were with you?’ All their deeds have gone void, and they have become losers.

O believers! Whoever among you turns away from his faith, Allah will soon bring a people; He shall love them, and they shall love Him. They will be humble towards the believers, firm against the disbelievers, striving hard in Allah’s way and not fearing any blamer’s blame. That is Allah’s grace. He grants it to whomever He pleases. And Allah is All-Encompassing, All-Knowing.
Your only guardians are Allah, His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him), and the believers who establish the prayer and give the alms due while bowing down (to Allah).
Whoever takes as his guardians Allah, His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him), and the believers; (these are) Allah’s allies, who will surely be victorious.

O believers! Do not take those who ridicule your religion and make fun of it from among those given the Book before you and the disbelievers as (your) trustful allies. And be conscious of Allah if you are (true) believers.
When you call to the prayer, they take it as mockery and play. That is because they are people who do not understand.

Say: ‘O People of the Book! Are you seeking revenge against us only because we believe in Allah, and (we believe in) what has been revealed to us, and what was revealed before, whereas most of you are disobedient?’

Say: ‘Shall I inform you of something worse than that (punishment) as a requital from Allah? (It is those) whom Allah has cursed, and upon whom He brought His wrath. He turned some of them into apes and swine and (also) the worshipers of idols (i.e. the slaves of Satan). These are in a far worse situation and more astray from the right way.’
When they come to you, they say: ‘We believe.’ Indeed, they enter with disbelief and certainly leave with it. And Allah knows best what they have been hiding.
You see many of them hastening actively into sin and enmity and consuming illicit gains. Indeed, evil is what they have been doing.
Why do the rabbis and scholars not forbid them from their sinful speech and their consumption of illicit gains? Indeed, evil is what they have been doing.

The Jews say: ‘Allah’s hand is tied up (with miserliness and helplessness).’ Tied up are their hands, and they are cursed for what they say! Instead, His hands are wide open. He bestows (as much) as He pleases. What has been revealed to you from your Lord will surely increase many of them in rebellion and disbelief. And We have cast between them enmity and hatred till the Day of Resurrection. Whenever they kindle a fire for war, Allah extinguishes it. They seek to cause corruption on the earth, and Allah does not like the corruptors.
But had the People of the Book only believed and remained God-conscious, We would have surely acquitted them of their misdeeds and admitted them to the Gardens of bliss.

Had they observed (the commandments of) the Torah and the Injil (the Gospel) and whatever was revealed to them from their Lord, they would surely have received nourishment from above them and from beneath their feet. There is a moderate (and upright) community among them, but what most of them are doing is evil.

Esteemed Messenger! Convey everything that has been revealed to you from your Lord. And if you do not, then you have not delivered His Message. Allah shall protect you from the people. Indeed, Allah does not guide the disbelieving people.

Say: ‘O People of the Book, you do not stand on anything unless you observe (the commandments of) the Torah and the Injil (the Gospel) and whatever has been revealed to you from your Lord.’ And what has been revealed to you from your Lord will surely increase many of them in rebellion and disbelief. So do not worry about the disbelieving people.
Indeed, the believers, the Jews, the Sabaeans, and the Christians, whoever (truly) believes in Allah and the Last Day and acts righteously, there shall be no fear on them, nor shall they grieve.

Surely, We took a pledge from the Children of Israel and sent messengers to them. Whenever a messenger brought them what their souls did not desire, they would accuse some of lying, and some they would slay.

They supposed there would be no trial, so they became blind and deaf. Then Allah accepted their repentance, but again, many of them became blind and deaf. And Allah is All-Seeing of what they are doing.

They are certainly disbelievers who say: ‘Allah is the Messiah, son of Maryam (Mary).’ Whereas the Messiah himself said: ‘O Children of Israel! Worship Allah, my Lord and your Lord.’ Whoever ascribes partners to Allah, indeed Allah has forbidden him entrance to Paradise and his refuge shall be the Fire. And the wrongdoers shall have no helpers.

Certainly, they (also) are disbelievers who say: ‘Allah is the third of three,’ while there is no god except the One God. If they do not refrain from what they say, a painful punishment will surely befall the disbelievers among them.
Will they not turn to Allah in repentance and plead with Him for forgiveness? And Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.

The Messiah, son of Maryam (Mary), was only a messenger. Indeed, many messengers have passed before him, and his mother was a truthful woman. They both would eat food. Look how we make the signs clear to them, yet see how they go astray!
Say: ‘Do you worship (something) besides Allah that has no power to bring you any harm or benefit, while Allah alone is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing?’

Say: ‘O People of the Book! Do not exaggerate in your religion beyond the truth, and do not follow the low desires of a people who went astray before and led many astray, and (now again after the raising of Prophet Muhammad (blessings and peace be upon him) have strayed from the right path.’
The disbelievers among the Children of Israel were cursed by the tongue of Dawud (David) and ‘Isa, son of Maryam (Jesus, son of Mary). That was because they disobeyed and used to transgress.
They would not forbid one another from the wrong they committed. Surely, evil is what they had been doing.

You see many of them allying themselves with the disbelievers. Indeed, evil is what they have forwarded to their account. Since Allah has become angry with them, they shall remain in everlasting punishment!
Had they believed in Allah, the Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him), and what was revealed to him, they would never have taken those (pagans) for allies. But most of them are disobedient.

You will surely find the most hostile of all people towards the believers to be the Jews and the polytheists. And you will surely find the nearest of them in affection to the believers to be those who say: ‘We are Christians.’ That is because among them are priests and monks and because they are not arrogant.

When they listen to what has been revealed to the Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him), you see their eyes overflowing with tears because of the truth they recognise. They say: ‘Our Lord! We believe, so write us down among the witnesses.’
Why should we not believe in Allah and the truth that has come to us? And we long for our Lord to include us in the company of the righteous people.
So Allah rewarded them for what they said, with Gardens with rivers flowing beneath them, to abide in them forever. That is the reward of the virtuously excellent.
But those who disbelieve and deny Our revelations—such shall be the inhabitants of the Hellfire.
O believers! Do not prohibit the good things Allah has made lawful for you, and do not transgress. Indeed, Allah does not like the transgressors.
Eat from the lawful and pure things Allah has provided you and be conscious of Allah in Whom you (all) believe.

Allah does not call you to account for what is frivolous in your oaths, but He will hold you accountable for your deliberate oaths. The atonement for it (i.e. the breaking of an oath) is feeding ten poor persons the average food you would normally serve to your own family, or their clothing, or freeing a slave. And whosoever does not find (the means) shall fast for three days. That is the atonement for your oaths when you have sworn (them). But guard your oaths. Thus, Allah makes clear to you His revelations so that you may be grateful.

O believers! Indeed (all) intoxicants and (every kind of) gambling and idolatrous stones for sacrificing animals and divining (with) arrows are the filth of Satan’s act. Therefore, avoid them so that you may be successful.
Satan desires only to cause enmity and hatred among you through intoxication and gambling and to bar you from the remembrance of Allah and prayer. Will you, then, refrain?

And obey Allah and obey the Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him), and always be on your guard. But if you turn away, then know that Our Messenger’s duty is only the clear proclamation (of the divine message).

There is no blame upon those who believe (in Allah) and perform righteous deeds regarding what they have eaten (before the prohibition), as long as they guard themselves (against evil), believe and perform righteous deeds, and then (continue to) be God-conscious and believe, and then remain (ever) conscious (of the commands of Allah) and excel—and Allah loves those who are virtuously excellent.

O believers! Allah will surely test you with something of the game which your hands and your spears will reach so that Allah may distinguish who fears Him privately. Then, whoever transgresses after that shall have a painful punishment.

O believers! Do not kill any game while you are in pilgrim sanctity. And whosoever of you kills it intentionally, the compensation (for it) is (to sacrifice) cattle equivalent to the one he has killed, as judged by two just men among you, as an offering brought to the Ka‘ba. Alternatively, (its compensation is) the atonement by feeding the needy or fasting of its equivalent so that he may taste the consequence of his violation. Allah has forgiven what has already passed. But whosoever repeats (a similar act), Allah will take vengeance on him. And Allah is All-Mighty, the All-Capable of Vengeance.

You are permitted to hunt and eat seafood as a provision for you and the travellers. But you have been forbidden the game of the land so long as you remain in pilgrim sanctity. And be God-conscious, to Whom you shall be gathered.

Allah has made the Ka‘ba, the Sacred House, a sanctuary of well-being for people, and (also) the sacred month, the offering (of the sacrificial animals) and garlands. All that (is ordained) so you may know that Allah knows whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth, and that Allah is All-Knowing of everything.
Know that Allah is Severe in retribution, and Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.
The Messenger’s responsibility is only to communicate, and Allah knows what you reveal and what you conceal.
Say: ‘The good and the evil are not equal, though the abundance of evil may impress you. Be conscious of Allah, O possessors of intellect, that you may succeed.’

O believers! Do not ask regarding things (the Qur’an is silent about) which, if they are made clear to you, may trouble you. And if you ask about them while the Qur’an is being revealed, they shall be disclosed to you (and you will be left with no discretion). Allah has (so far) excused these matters. And Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Forbearing.
Indeed, some people before you asked such questions, then became disbelievers on account of them.

Allah has not prescribed any such thing as Bahira, Sa’iba, Wasila and Ham.* But the disbelievers fabricate lies against Allah, and most of them do not understand. [* These four terms refer to particular kinds of camels that the pagan Arabs used to dedicate to different idols and consider them sacred. Such camels could not be ridden or milked or made to carry any burden, but they had to be allowed to wander and graze freely. These camels would often be marked by slit ears to make the people aware of their sacred status. The Qur’an has rejected and condemned this idolatrous practice of the pre-Islamic Arabs.]

And when it is said to them: ‘Come to what Allah has revealed and (come) to the Messenger,’ they say: ‘What we have found our forefathers practising is good enough for us.’ What! Even if their forefathers did not know anything and were not guided?

O believers! Take care of your own souls. The one who deviates from the right path cannot harm you if you are rightly guided. To Allah shall be your return all together. Then He shall inform you regarding what you used to do.

O believers! When death approaches any of you while making a will, the witness between you shall be two just persons among you; otherwise, two from among others (i.e. non-muslims, if Muslim witnesses are not available). It is (needed) if you are travelling in the land and the calamity of death befalls you. You shall detain the two of them after prayer, and if you doubt (their testimony), then they shall testify under oath (saying): ‘By Allah! We will never sell it for any price, even if he were a close relative, nor will we conceal the testimony of Allah, for then we would surely be among the sinful.’

If it is found that both of them were guilty of sin (i.e. perjury), then let two others stand in their place from those most entitled as claimants (among the deceased’s closest heirs affected by the bequest). Then they should swear by Allah (saying:) ‘Our testimony is truer than their testimony, and we have not transgressed. Otherwise, we would indeed be among the wrongdoers.’

It is more likely that they (i.e. the witnesses) will give the testimony in its accurate form or fear that their oaths might be refuted by other oaths afterwards. Be conscious of Allah and listen. Allah does not guide the transgressing people.
The Day when Allah will gather the messengers and say: ‘What response did you receive?’ They will say: ‘We have no knowledge (compared to You). Indeed, You are the Great Knower of all unseen things.’

When Allah will say: ‘O ‘Isa, son of Maryam (Jesus, son of Mary), remember My blessing upon you, and upon your mother when I strengthened you with the Holy Spirit so that you would speak to the people in the cradle and adulthood, and when I taught you the Scripture, Wisdom, the Torah and the Injil (the Gospel), and when you would create from clay the shape of a bird by My permission, and you would breathe into it and it would become a (flying) bird by My permission, and you would heal the blind and the leper by My permission and you would bring the dead back to life by My permission. And when I restrained the Children of Israel from (killing) you while you brought them clear proofs, but the disbelievers among them said: ‘This is nothing but clear magic.’
And when I inspired the disciples to believe in Me and My messenger (‘Isa [Jesus]), they said: ‘We believe and bear witness that we submit to God entirely (i.e. that we are Muslims).’

When the disciples said: ‘O ‘Isa, son of Maryam (Jesus, son of Mary)! Can your Lord send down to us a feast (i.e. a table spread with food) from heaven?’ ‘Isa (Jesus) said: ‘Be conscious of Allah if you are (true) believers.’
They said: ‘We desire to eat from it so that our hearts will be at peace, and we shall know that you have told us the truth, and we shall be among the witnesses to it.’

‘Isa, the son of Maryam (Jesus, the son of Mary) said: ‘O Allah, our Lord! Send down to us a feast (i.e. a table spread with food) from heaven to be a festival for us, for the first of us and the last of us, and as a sign from Yourself. And provide for us, for You are the best of providers.’
Allah said: ‘I am sending it down to you. But whosoever among you disbelieves after this, I shall subject him to a punishment I have never inflicted on any other in all the worlds.’

And when Allah will say: ‘O ‘Isa, son of Maryam (Jesus, son of Mary)! Did you say to the people: ‘Take me and my mother as two gods besides Allah?’’ He will say: ‘Glory be to You! It does not befit me that I should say what I have no right to (say). Had I said such a thing, You would indeed have known it. You know what is (hidden) within me, and I do not know what is within Yourself. Indeed, You are the Great Knower of all unseen things.

I did not tell them (anything) except what You had commanded me (to say): ‘Worship Allah, my Lord and your Lord.’ And I was witness over them as long as I remained among them. But since You took me (to Yourself), You were the Watcher over them. And You are Witness over everything.
If You punish them, they are indeed Your servants. But if You forgive them, You are indeed the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.’

Allah will say: ‘This is the Day when truthfulness shall benefit the truthful. For them shall be Gardens with rivers flowing beneath them, to abide therein forever. Allah is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him. That is the ultimate success.
To Allah belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and whatsoever is in them, and He is All-Capable of everything.
All praise belongs to Allah, Who created the heavens and the earth and made (manifold) darknesses and the light. Yet the disbelievers equate (others) to their Lord.
He is the One Who created you from clay and then determined a term (of your life). (The knowledge of) the specified time (for your death) is with Him, yet you continue to doubt.
And He is Allah in the heavens and on the earth; He knows your secret (thoughts) and your open (words and acts). And He knows what you earn.
Whenever a sign came to them from among the signs of their Lord, they used to turn away from it.
They indeed denied the truth when it came to them, but soon, the news will come to them regarding what they have been mocking.

Have they not realised how many generations We have destroyed before them? We had established them in the land in a way that We have never established you. And We sent (from the sky) abundant rains upon them and made rivers flow beneath them. Then, We destroyed them for their sins and brought about another generation after them.

(Esteemed Prophet!) Even if We had revealed to you a Book written on parchment, and they had touched it with their own hands, the disbelievers would (still) have said: ‘This is nothing but clear magic.’

And they would say: ‘Why has an angel not been (physically) sent down to (support) him?’ But had We sent down an angel, the matter would have certainly been decreed (at once), and they would have never been given respite.

And if We had sent an angel himself (as a messenger), We would have surely sent him as a man, and (even in that case) We would have put them in the same doubt which they already are in (i.e. being confused over his physical appearance, they would say: ‘He is just a man like us.’)
(Other) messengers had already been mocked before you (Esteemed Prophet). Then, those who mocked them were besieged by what they used to ridicule.
Say: ‘Travel through the land, then see how the end of the deniers was.’

Say: ‘To whom belongs all that is in the heavens and the earth?’ Say: ‘To Allah.’ He has prescribed mercy upon Himself (as a gracious obligation). He will surely gather you on the Day of Resurrection, in which there is no doubt. Those who have ruined themselves will never believe.
And to Him belongs whatever rests in the night and the day. And He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.

Say: ‘Shall I take (someone) as guardian other than Allah, the Originator of the heavens and the earth, Who feeds and is not fed,’ Say: ‘I have been commanded to be the first who submits himself (to Allah). And you should not be among the polytheists.’
Say: ‘Indeed, I fear—if I were to disobey my Lord—the punishment of a tremendous Day.’
Whoever is spared (from the punishment) on that Day, He has surely shown His Mercy to him, and that is the manifest success.
And if Allah afflicts you with some suffering, no one can remove it except Him, and if He bestows some good upon you, then He is All-Capable of everything.
And He is Dominant over His servants, and He is the All-Wise, the All-Aware.

Say: ‘What thing is greatest as a witness?’ Say: ‘Allah! (He is) witness between me and you. And this Qur’an has been revealed to me that I may warn you through it, and whomever it may reach. Do you truly testify that there are other gods besides Allah?’ Say: ‘I do not testify.’ Say: ‘He is only One God, and I indeed disown whatever you associate (with Him).’
Those to whom We have given the Book recognise him (i.e. the Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) just as they recognise their sons. Those who have ruined their souls will never believe.
Who is more unjust than the one who fabricates a lie against Allah or rejects His revelations? Indeed, the unjust will not prosper.
On the Day We assemble them all together, We shall say to those who ascribed partners (to Allah): ‘Where are your partners whom you have been asserting?’
Then their argument would be nothing but to say: ‘By Allah, our Lord, we were not polytheists.’
See how they lie against themselves; what they used to fabricate has deserted them!

Among them are those who (appear to) listen to you, but We have placed covers over their hearts, lest they should understand it, and (there is) deafness in their ears. Even if they were to see every sign, they would (still) not believe in it. When these disbelievers come to you to dispute with you, they say: ‘This (Qur’an) is nothing but the fictions of the ancients.’
And they prevent (others) from (following) him and (also) keep themselves away from him. They destroy none but themselves, though they fail to realise this.

Only if you could see when they will be detained before the Fire! They will say: ‘Oh! If only we were sent back (to the world), we would not reject our Lord’s communications, and we would be among the believers!’
Instead, what they used to hide before has become evident to them. Even if they were sent back, they would (again) return to what they were forbidden. And most surely, they are liars.
They say: ‘There is nothing beyond our life of this world, and we shall not be resurrected (after death).’

If (only) you could see when they will be made to stand before their Lord! He will say: ‘Is this not the truth?’ They will say: ‘Yes, by our Lord.’ He will say: ‘So taste the punishment because you used to disbelieve.’

Indeed, lost are those who deny the meeting with Allah. When the Hour takes them suddenly, they will say: ‘Alas for us, for we did not pay regard to this (Hour).’ And they will bear their burdens upon their backs. Indeed, terrible is what they bear!
The life of this world is nothing but game and amusement. And the abode of the Hereafter is better for those who are God-conscious. Do you not understand?
(Esteemed Prophet!) We surely know that what they say grieves you. Yet, they are not denying you (only)—but these are the signs of Allah that the wrongdoers deny.

Surely messengers were rejected before you, but they endured patiently under the denial and persecution till Our help came to them. No one can change the Words of Allah, and there have certainly come to you some accounts of the messengers.

And if their turning away is distressing to you, then find, if you can, a tunnel (to go down) into the earth or a ladder (to climb up) into heaven and bring them some (exceptional) sign (even then they will not believe). Had Allah willed, He would certainly have gathered them all upon guidance. So (because of your overwhelming concern and compassion for them), do not be unaware (of their ill fortune).
Only those who listen (sincerely) will respond (to you). As for the dead, Allah will resurrect them, and to Him, they shall be returned.
They say: ‘Why has a (visible) sign not been sent down to him from his Lord?’ Say: ‘Allah is indeed capable of sending down a sign, but most of them do not know (the secrets behind His actions).’

There is no walking (or crawling) creature on the earth, nor a bird that flies with its wings, but they are species like yourselves (in several traits). We have not omitted anything in the Book. Then, they will be gathered to their Lord (in the end).
And those who reject Our revelations are deaf and dumb (and are trapped) in (manifold) darkness. And Allah leaves whomever He wills to stray and guides whomever He pleases to the straight path.
Say (to them): ‘What do you think: If Allah’s punishment comes upon you, or the Hour overtakes you, will you supplicate somebody other than Allah, if you are truthful?’
Instead, it is only Him you will supplicate, and if He wills, He will remove (the affliction) that had caused you to supplicate Him, and you will forget whatever partners you ascribe to Him.
We had indeed sent (messengers) to (different) nations before you, then We afflicted them with hardships and sufferings so that they might humble themselves (to Us).
So why did they not humble themselves when our punishment overtook them? Instead, their hearts had hardened, and Satan made what they used to do alluring to them.

So when they forgot what they had been reminded of, We opened the gates of all (good) things for them (to flourish abundantly), until, when they rejoiced in what they were given, We seized them suddenly (with terrible punishment), and they were struck with utter despair.
Thus, the (last) remnants of those people who were unjust were cut off. And all praise belongs to Allah, the Lord of all worlds.

Say: ‘What do you think: If Allah takes away your hearing and your sight and seals your hearts, which god besides Allah can bring it (back) to you?’ Look how We explain Our revelations in various ways! Yet they still turn away.
Say: ‘What do you think: If Allah’s punishment comes to you suddenly or openly, would anyone be destroyed except the wrongdoing people?’
We do not send the messengers except as bearers of glad tidings and as warners. As for those who become believers and righteous, there shall be no fear on them, nor shall they grieve.
But as for those who reject Our Signs, the punishment shall afflict them because of the disobedience they had been committing.

Say (to the disbelievers): ‘I do not say to you that I possess the treasures of Allah, nor do I know the Unseen myself, nor do I say to you that I am an angel. I only follow what is revealed to me.’ Say: ‘Can the blind and the seeing be equal? Will you not then reflect?’
And warn with this (Qur’an) those fearful of being gathered towards their Lord—when they shall have no guardian except Him nor any intercessor; perhaps they may be God-conscious.

Do not drive away those (poor believers) who call upon their Lord morning and evening, seeking nothing but His countenance (i.e. His nearness and pleasure). Neither are you accountable for them whatsoever, nor are they accountable for you in any way, so that you may turn them away and become among those who deprive others of their rights (and such is not befitting for your compassionate being).
Thus, We have made some of them a test for others so they might say: ‘Are these (poor believers) the ones whom Allah has favoured from among us?’ Does not Allah know best who are grateful?

When those who believe in Our revelations come to you, say: ‘Peace be upon you!’ Your Lord (out of His Grace) has prescribed mercy upon Himself. Whoever among you commits evil out of ignorance, then repents after that, and reforms (himself)—He is indeed Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.
In this way, We expound the signs so that the way of the evildoers is exposed.
Say: ‘I have been forbidden to worship those you invoke besides Allah.’ Say: ‘I do not follow your desires, for then I would have gone astray, and I would not have been among the rightly guided.’

Say: ‘Indeed I stand upon a clear proof from my Lord, though you have denied it. (The punishment) that you seek to hasten is not up to me. The judgement belongs only to Allah. He tells the truth, and He is the Best of judges.’
Say: ‘If what you seek to hasten were within my power, the matter would already have been decided between you and me, and Allah knows best the wrongdoers.’

With Him are the keys of the Unseen. No one knows them (on his own) except Him. And He knows whatever is in the land and the sea. And no leaf falls, but He knows it, nor is there a grain in the darkness of the earth, nor anything moist or dry, but it is (written) in a manifest Book.

He is the One Who takes your souls at night (in sleep) and knows whatever you earn by day. Then He raises you up (again) in the daytime until the fixed term (of life) is completed. Then to Him shall be your return, and He will inform you of what you have been doing.

And He is All-Dominant over His servants. And He sends upon you recording angels (to watch over you), until, when death comes to one of you, Our messengers take him away, and they never neglect (their duty).
Then they are returned to Allah, their true Lord. Beware! The judgement belongs to Him. And He is the swiftest of reckoners.
Say: ‘Who saves you from the darkness of land and sea, (when) you supplicate Him humbly and secretly, (saying:) ‘If He delivers us from this (adversity), we will surely be among the grateful?’’
Say: ‘Only Allah delivers you from them, and every (other) distress, yet you ascribe partners to Him.’

Say: ‘He (alone) has the power to send upon you a punishment from above you or beneath your feet, or to confuse you by splitting you into (hostile) sects and make you taste the violence of one against the other.’ See how We explain (Our) revelations in various ways so perhaps they may understand.
Your people have denied it, though it is the truth. Say: ‘I am not a guardian over you.’
For every (destined) news, there is a fixed time to transpire, and you shall soon come to know.

When you see those engaged in false discussions about Our revelations, avoid them until they engage in a different discourse. But if Satan causes you to forget, then, after remembering, do not sit in the company of the wrongdoing people.
Those who are God-conscious are in no way accountable for them (i.e. the wrongdoers), but this is merely a reminder so that they may guard themselves (against evil).

Leave those who take their religion for play and amusement, and whom the life of this world has deceived. But keep reminding (them) by this (Qur’an), so that no soul should be ruined for what it has earned. The (disbelieving) soul shall have no guardian or intercessor besides Allah; even if it were to offer every possible ransom, none would be accepted from it. They are the ones who will be ruined because of what they have earned. There shall be a drink of boiling liquid and a painful punishment for what they used to disbelieve in.

Say: ‘Should we worship something besides Allah that can neither benefit us nor harm us and (should we) turn back on our heels (to disbelief) after Allah has guided us? (If we do so, we will be) like someone whom the devils have seduced in the wilderness on the earth, though he has companions who call him to guidance (saying): ‘Come to us?’ Say(O Prophet): ‘Indeed, the guidance of Allah is the (only) true guidance. And We have been commanded to submit to the Lord of all worlds,
and to establish the prayer and to be conscious of Him. And He is the One to Whom you shall be gathered.’

He is the One Who created the heavens and the earth with truthful purpose. On the Day when He says (to something): ‘Be!’ There it will be. His Word is the truth. And to Him belongs all sovereignty on the Day when the Trumpet will be blown (by Israfil). The Knower of the Unseen and the seen, and He is the All-Wise, the All-Aware.

When Ibrahim (Abraham) said to his (fostering) father Azar*: ‘Do you take idols for gods? Indeed, I see you and your people in obvious error.’ [* Azar was, in fact, Abraham’s paternal uncle and caretaker, not his birth father. The name of his birth father was Tarekh.]
Thus did We show Ibrahim (Abraham) the kingdoms of the heavens and the earth, that he might be among the possessors of firm certitude.
So when the night grew dark upon him, he saw a star. He said: ‘This is my Lord!’ But when it set, he said: ‘I do not love things that set.’
When he saw the moon rising, he said: ‘This is my Lord!’ But when it set, he said: ‘Had my Lord not guided me, I would surely have been among the misguided people.’
When he saw the sun rising, he said: ‘This must be my Lord! It is bigger (than the other two).’ But when it set, he said: ‘O my people! Indeed, I disown all that you ascribe as (His) partners.
I have turned my face towards the One Who has originated the heavens and the earth, being an upright believer, and I am not among the polytheists.’

His people argued with him. He said: ‘Do you argue with me concerning Allah while He has guided me? I do not fear whatever (idols) you ascribe to Him (as His) partners, except anything that my Lord may wish. My Lord comprehends all things in (His) knowledge. Will you then not remember?

How should I fear the partners you ascribe when you do not fear ascribing partners to Allah, for which He has not sent down any authority to you? So which of the two parties has a greater right to safety if (only) you know?’
Those who believe and do not mix up their belief with wrongdoing, it is they who shall have safety (in the Hereafter), and they are the (rightly) guided.
That was Our argument We gave to Ibrahim (Abraham) against his people. We raise in degrees whomsoever We please. Indeed, your Lord is All-Wise, All-Knowing.

And We bestowed upon him Ishaq (Isaac) and Ya‘qub (Jacob), and blessed all (of them) with guidance. And We had guided Nuh (Noah) before, and from his offspring, Dawud (David), Sulayman (Solomon), Ayyub (Job), Yusuf (Joseph), Musa (Moses) and Harun (Aaron). Thus, do We reward the virtuously excellent.
And (We guided) Zakariyya (Zachariah), Yahya (John), ‘Isa (Jesus) and Ilyas (Elias). All (of them) were among the righteous.
And (We also guided) Isma‘il (Ishmael), al-Yasa‘ (Elisha), Yunus (Jonah) and Lut (Lot). And We graced all (of them) over the nations,
and from among their forefathers, their offspring and their brethren. We chose them and guided them to a straight path.

That is Allah’s guidance, with which He guides whomsoever He pleases among His servants. And if they were to ascribe partners (to Allah), whatever (good) they were doing would have been removed from them.
They are the ones whom We gave the Book, the (sound) judgement, and prophethood. So if these (pagans) disbelieve in them, We have already entrusted them to a people who will never deny them.
Those were the people whom Allah had guided. So, follow their (path of) guidance. Say: ‘I do not ask you any reward for it. It is nothing but a reminder for all the nations.’

They did not pay due regard to the Reverence of Allah when (denying prophethood,) they said: ‘Allah did not reveal anything to any human.’ Say: ‘Who revealed the Book that Musa (Moses) brought as a light and a guidance for the people, which you make into (separate) parchments that you display, while you conceal much (of it)? And you were taught (through that) what you had not known before, neither you nor your fathers.’ Say: ‘Allah.’ Then, leave them alone to play around in their impious discourse.

Blessed is this Book (which) We have sent down, confirming what was revealed before it, so that you may warn (the people of) the Mother of Cities (i.e. Mecca) and those around it. Those who believe in the Hereafter believe in it and are watchful of their prayers.

Who could be more unjust than someone who fabricates a lie against Allah or says: ‘It has been revealed to me,’ although nothing was revealed to him, and he who says: ‘I will reveal something equal to what Allah has revealed?’ If only you could see when the wrongdoers are in the agonies of death, and the angels stretch out their hands (to them saying): ‘Give up your souls! Today, you shall be recompensed with the punishment of humiliation for you have been speaking untruth against Allah, and for you have been arrogant towards His revelations.’

(Then Allah will say:) ‘Now you have come to Us alone, just as We created you the first time, and you have left behind whatever We had bestowed upon you. And We do not see your intercessors with you, whom you claimed that they were (Our) partners in your matters. Surely, all links between you have been cut (today). And what you have been claiming has abandoned you.
Indeed, Allah is the Splitter of the grain and the fruit stone. He brings out the living from the dead, and He brings the dead out from the living. That is Allah! So, how are you misled?
(He is) the Splitter of the dawn. He has made the night for rest, the sun, and the moon (to travel) with calculation. That is the finely-measured design of the All-Mighty, the All-Knowing.
And He is the One Who made the stars for you so you can navigate through them in the darkness of land and sea. Indeed, We have distinguished the signs for the people having (astronomical) knowledge.

He is the One Who produced you from one living soul (i.e. a single cell). Then (assigned you) a place to stay (in life) and a place (to be laid) for rest. Indeed, We have explained the signs to people who understand deeply.

He is the One Who sends down water from the clouds. Then, with it, We bring out the buds of all plants. Thereby, We bring out the vegetation, from which We produce the grain in closely packed rows. And from the palm trees (We bring out) clusters of dates hanging low, and gardens of grapes, olives and pomegranate, (which from many aspects look) similar and dissimilar. Look upon its fruit as it grows and ripens. Indeed, there are signs for the people who believe in all these things.

They make the jinn partners of Allah, though He has created them, and they falsely attribute sons and daughters to Him without any knowledge. Glory be to Him! And Exalted is He above what they ascribe.
The Originator of the heavens and the earth! How could He have a child when He has no spouse? Whereas He created all things, and He is All-Knowing of everything.
That is Allah, your Lord; there is no god except Him, the Creator of everything. So worship Him, and He is the Guardian over everything.
Visions cannot comprehend Him, but He comprehends all visions. And He is the All-Subtle, the All-Aware.
Indeed, clear insights have come to you from your Lord. If anyone sees (them) clearly, it will be to his benefit. And whoever remains blind it will be to his loss. And I am not a watcher over you.
Thus, We diversify the styles of Our Communications so they may exclaim: ‘You have studied (from somewhere),’ and We make this (Qur’an) clear to the people who possess knowledge.
Follow what has been revealed to you by your Lord. There is no god except Him—and turn away from the polytheists.
If Allah had willed, they would not have ascribed partners (to Him). And We have not made you a watcher over them, nor are you an advocate for them.

Do not abuse whatever (idols) they worship besides Allah, lest they vindictively insult Allah out of ignorance. That is how We have made the conduct of every community seem alluring to them. Then to their Lord shall be their return, and He will inform them of what they had been doing.

And they swear by Allah their most solemn oaths that if a sign came to them, they would most certainly believe in it. Say: ‘The signs are only with Allah.’ And, (O Muslims,) what will make you realise that even if those (signs) were to come, they would (still) not believe.
We shall turn their hearts and their eyes away from (accepting the truth) as they did not believe in it the first time. And We will leave them wandering, confused in their rebellion.

Even if We had sent down angels to them and the dead had spoken to them, and We had gathered all things right in front of them, they would (still) not believe unless Allah so willed. But most of them are ignorant.

Thus, for every prophet, We have made an enemy—the Satans from among humans and jinn, who inspire one another with adorned words of deception. Had your Lord willed, they would never have done it. So leave them with what they fabricate,
so that the hearts of those who do not believe in the Hereafter may incline towards it, be pleased with it and earn (the evil of) what they commit.

(Say:) ‘Should I seek a judge besides Allah, while it is He Who has revealed to you the Book, clearly explained?’ Those to whom We have given the Book know that it has been revealed from your Lord with the truth, so do not be among the doubters.
The word of your Lord is fulfilled in truth and justice. No one can change His words. And He is the All-Hearing, All-Knowing.
If you obey most of those on the earth, they will lead you astray from the way of Allah. They follow nothing but assumptions, and they are merely guessing (and lying).
Indeed, your Lord knows best those who stray from His way, and He knows best the rightly guided.
So eat from (the meat of) that (permissible animal) over which Allah’s Name is invoked (at the time of slaughter) if you are (true) believers in His revelations.

Why should you not eat from (the meat of) that (animal) over which Allah’s name is invoked (at the time of slaughter), while He has already explained to you whatever He has forbidden you, except for what you are highly compelled (to eat)? Indeed, many people mislead (others) with their desires without (any concrete) knowledge. Indeed, your Lord is He Who knows the transgressors well.
Abandon the outward sins and the inward ones. Indeed, those who commit sins shall be recompensed for what they used to commit.

And do not eat from (the meat of) that (animal) over which Allah’s name has not been invoked (at the time of slaughter). It is indeed a disobedience (contravening the Sacred Law). Indeed, the devils inspire their intimate friends to argue with you. And if you obey them, you will also become polytheists.

Can the one who was dead, then We gave him life and provided him with a light, with which he walks among the people (to enlighten them), be comparable to the one (trapped) in manifold darkness, which he cannot escape? Thus, whatever (misdeeds) the disbelievers used to do were made alluring to them.
Thus, We have made the powerful ones the criminals in every town so that they may devise their (evil) plots therein. But they only plot against themselves, though they are unaware.

And when a revelation comes to them, they say: ‘We will never believe unless we are given the like of what was given to the messengers of Allah.’ Allah knows best where to place His message. Soon, humiliation before Allah and severe punishment shall befall the guilty because of the plots they used to devise.

Whoever Allah intends to guide, He expands his breast for Islam. But whoever He intends to leave astray, He makes his breast narrow and tight, as if he were climbing up into the sky. Thus, Allah lays (spiritual) defilement on those who do not believe.
This is your Lord’s path—perfectly straight. We have clearly explained the revelations for a people mindful (of advice).
For them shall be the Abode of Peace with their Lord, and He shall be their Guardian because of what they have been doing.

On the Day when He would assemble them all together, (He shall say:) ‘O company of jinn! You have misled many of the humans.’ Their friends from among the humans will say: ‘Our Lord! We benefited from one another, and (now) we have reached our term which You had appointed for us.’ He will say: ‘The Fire is your abode, to remain in it forever, except what Allah may wish. Indeed, your Lord is All-Wise, All-Knowing.’
That is how We make the wrongdoers allies of one another because of what they used to earn.

(Allah will ask:) ‘O company of jinn and humans! Did messengers not come to you from yourselves, proclaiming My revelations to you and warning you of the meeting of this Day of yours?’ They will say: ‘We testify against ourselves.’ The life of this world had deceived them, and they would bear witness against themselves that they had been disbelievers.
This (sending of messengers) is because your Lord would never destroy towns unjustly while their people were unaware (of the truth).
For everyone, there are ranks according to what they have done. And your Lord is not negligent of what they do.
Your Lord is All-Sufficient, the Lord of Mercy. If He wishes, He can remove you and make whomever He pleases to succeed you in your place, just as He raised you from other people’s offspring.
Indeed, whatever you are promised will surely come, and you cannot escape it.
Say: ‘O my people! Work according to your ability; I, (too), am working (accordingly). You will soon realise who will have (a good end of) the ultimate abode. Indeed, the unjust will never prosper.’

They dedicate to Allah a share of the crops and cattle He has created and say: ‘This belongs to Allah,’ as they claim, ‘and this belongs to our partners.’ But what is for their partners does not reach Allah, and what is for Allah reaches their partners. Evil indeed is the judgement they make!

Likewise, their partners have made the killing of their children fair-seeming to many of the polytheists so that they may destroy them and confuse them in their religion. Had Allah willed, they would not have done it. So, leave them with what they fabricate.

And they say: ‘These cattle and crops are prohibited; no one can eat them except whom we please,’ as they claim. And there are cattle whose backs are forbidden and cattle over which they do not invoke Allah’s name (during slaughter), falsely attributing these (regulations) to Him. Soon, He will recompense them for what they used to fabricate.

They (also) say: ‘Whatever is in the wombs of these cattle is reserved for our males and forbidden to our wives. But if it is stillborn, then they are all partners in it. He will soon repay them for their (false) attribution (to Him). Indeed, He is All-Wise, All-Knowing.

Certainly, ruined are those who kill their children foolishly without knowledge and who forbid what Allah has provided them, fabricating lies against Allah. They have indeed gone astray and are not guided.

He is the One Who produces gardens including climbing and non-climbing plants, date palms and crops with diverse fruits, olives, and pomegranates—similar and dissimilar. Eat the fruits of these crops when they grow and pay its due on the day of its harvest. And do not be wasteful. Indeed, Allah does not like the wasteful.
And (He produces) the livestock, (some) for burden and (some) for slaughter. Eat what Allah has provided you. And do not follow in the footsteps of Satan. He is indeed your clear enemy.

(Allah has created) eight animals in (four) pairs: a pair of sheep and a pair of goats. Say (to them): ‘Is it the two males that He has forbidden or the two females, or that which the wombs of the two females contain? Tell me with (sound) knowledge if you are truthful.’

And a pair of camels and a pair of cattle. Say: ‘Is it the two males that He has forbidden or the two females, or that which the wombs of the two females contain? Were you present when Allah gave you this command?’ So, who is a greater wrongdoer than he who fabricates a lie against Allah to mislead the people without any knowledge? Indeed, Allah does not guide unjust people.

Say: ‘I do not find in what has been revealed to me anything forbidden for an eater to eat except carrion, or flowing blood, or flesh of swine, for that is surely impure, or a sinful offering over which any name other than Allah has been invoked during slaughter. But if someone is compelled by extreme necessity, without wilfully disobeying or exceeding the limit, then surely your Lord is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.’

And for the Jews, We forbade every animal with claws. And We forbade the fat of cattle and sheep, except what is carried by their backs or (mixed with) the internal organs, or what is attached to the bones. Thus, We recompensed them for their rebellion, and indeed, We are Truthful.
If they reject you, say: ‘Your Lord is the Possessor of an expansive mercy, yet His punishment will not be averted from the wicked people.’

The polytheists will say: ‘If Allah had willed, we would not have ascribed partners (to Him), nor our fathers, nor would we have forbidden anything.’ In the same way, those who were before them had continually rejected (the truth) until they tasted Our punishment. Say: ‘Do you have any (revealed) knowledge that you can produce before us? You follow nothing except (false) assumptions, and you do nothing but lie.’

Say: ‘To Allah belongs the conclusive argument. If Allah had wished, He would have surely guided you all.’* [* Allah has distinguished truth from falsehood, explaining the consequences of both. Then, He gives everyone the freedom to choose his way and holds him accountable for his doings in the Hereafter. This freedom makes everyone worthy of reward or liable for punishment according to his deeds.]

Say: ‘Bring your witnesses who may testify that Allah has forbidden this.’ So if they (falsely) testify, do not testify with them, and do not follow the desires of those who have rejected Our revelations, and those who do not believe in the Hereafter and have set up equals with their Lord.

Say: ‘Come! I will recite (to you) what your Lord has forbidden you from. That you should not ascribe anything as a partner to Him; that you should be virtuous to (your) parents; that you should not kill your children fearing poverty—We will provide for you and for them—that you should not come near indecencies, the outward among them or the inward; and that you should not kill any soul that Allah has made inviolable, except through lawful means. That is what He has commanded you so that you may understand.’

And do not come near the orphan’s property, except in the best manner, till he reaches maturity; and observe fully the measure and the balance with justice. We do not task a soul beyond its capacity. And when you speak, be just, even if it be (against) a relative, and fulfil the covenant of Allah. That is what He has commanded you, so perhaps you may become mindful (of advice).

And indeed, this is My path, perfectly straight—so follow it, and do not follow (other) ways, for they will separate you from His way. That is what He has commanded you, so perhaps you may become God-conscious.

Then We gave the Book to Musa (Moses), to complete (Our blessings) upon those who are virtuous, and as an exposition of all things, and as a guide and a mercy, so that they may believe in the meeting with their Lord.
And this Book that We have revealed is a blessed one. So follow it and be God-conscious so that you may be shown (His) mercy.
Lest you (pagans) say: ‘The Book was revealed only to two communities before us (i.e. the Jews and the Christians), and we were indeed unaware of their teachings.’

Or lest you say: ‘If the Book had been revealed to us, we would have been better guided than they.’ Now, manifest proof, guidance, and mercy have come to you from your Lord. So, who is more unjust than he who rejects Allah’s revelations and turns away from them? We shall soon recompense those who turn away from Our revelations with a terrible punishment because of what they used to evade.

Are they waiting for the (very) angels to come to them, or your Lord to come (Himself), or some (special) signs of your Lord to come? The day when some (special) signs of your Lord do come, belief shall not benefit any soul that did not believe beforehand and did not earn some goodness in its belief. Say: ‘Wait! We (too) are waiting.’
Indeed, those who split up their religion and become sects, you have nothing to do with them. Their case rests only with Allah. Then, He will inform them about what they used to do.
Whoever brings a good deed shall receive ten times the like of it, and whoever brings an evil deed shall not be recompensed except the like of it. And they shall not be wronged.
Say: ‘Indeed, my Lord has guided me to a straight path, the upright religion, the religion of Ibrahim (Abraham), the man of pure faith. And he was not among the polytheists.’
Say: ‘Indeed my prayer, my ritual sacrifices, my life and my death are (all) for Allah, the Lord of all worlds.
He has no partner, and that is what I have been commanded (to follow). I am the first of those who submit (to Allah).’

Say: ‘Shall I seek a Lord other than Allah, while He is the Lord of everything?’ No soul commits evil but against itself, and no bearer shall bear the burden of another. Then, to your Lord is your (ultimate) return. And He will inform you regarding that over which you used to differ.’

He is the One Who made you successors on the earth and raised some of you in ranks above others, so He may test you regarding what He has given you (as a trust). Indeed, your Lord is Swift in retribution. And indeed, He is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.
Alif, Lam, Mim, Sad.

(This is) a Book revealed to you. So, let there be no constriction in your breast on its account (because of the unpleasant response of the disbelievers)—so you may warn (them) with it. And (it is) a Reminder for the believers.
(Believers!) Follow what has been sent down to you from your Lord, and do not follow (the self-proclaimed) guardians besides Him. How seldom do you accept advice?
How many towns have We destroyed? Our punishment came upon them at night or while they were taking a midday nap.
When Our punishment overtook them, they could say nothing but cry: ‘Indeed we were wrongdoers.’
We shall certainly question those to whom the messengers were sent, and We shall surely ask the messengers (too).
Then We shall surely relate to them (all their affairs) from (Our) knowledge, for We had never been absent.
And the weighing (of deeds) on that Day is true. As for those whose scales (of good deeds) are heavy, they are the ones who shall be prosperous.
As for those whose scales (of good deeds) are light, they are the ones who have ruined their souls, for they used to treat Our revelations wrongfully.
And We have surely established you on the earth and provided in it (different) means of livelihood for you. How seldom do you give thanks?
Indeed, We created you, then We shaped you. Then We said to the angels: ‘Prostrate yourselves before Adam.’ So they (all) prostrated except Iblis. He was not among those who prostrated.
He said (to Iblis): ‘What prevented you from prostrating when I commanded you?’ He said: ‘I am better than him. You created me from fire, while You created him from clay.’
He said: ‘Get down from here. It is not for you to be arrogant here. Get out! You are indeed among the disgraced.’
He (Iblis) said: ‘Grant me respite until the Day when people will be resurrected.’
He (Allah) said: ‘You are indeed among the respited ones.’
He (Iblis) said: ‘As You have led me astray, I will surely sit in wait for them on Your straight path.
Then I will surely come to them from their front, and from their rear, and from their right, and from their left. And You will not find most of them grateful.’
He said: ‘(O Iblis!) Be gone from here, blameful (and) banished. Whoever among them follows you, I shall surely fill Hell with you all.’
(Then He said:) ‘O Adam! Live you and your wife in the Garden (i.e. Paradise), and eat from wherever you wish, but do not go near this tree, lest you should be among the wrongdoers.’

Then Satan whispered to them to expose to them what was hidden from them of their nakedness. He said: ‘Your Lord has only forbidden you from this tree lest you should become angels, or lest you become among the immortals.’
He swore to them both, (saying:) ‘I am indeed your well-wisher.’

So he led them on by deception. So when they tasted from the tree, their private parts were exposed to them. And they both began to cover themselves with the leaves of the Garden. Then their Lord called them: ‘Did I not forbid you from that tree and tell you that Satan is indeed an open enemy to you?’
They said: ‘Our Lord! We have wronged ourselves. And if You do not forgive us and have mercy on us, we shall surely be among the losers.’
He said: ‘Descend! Some of you (will be) enemies of one another. And there will be a residence for you on the earth and a provision for some time.’
He said: ‘Therein you shall live, and therein you shall die, and from there you shall be raised (on the Day of Resurrection).’

O Children of Adam! We have indeed sent down to you garments to cover your private parts and for adornment. Yet the garment of God-consciousness—that is the best. That is one of Allah’s signs, so perhaps they may become mindful (of advice).

O Children of Adam! Let not Satan tempt you (too) as he brought your parents out of Paradise, stripping them of their garments to expose their private parts to them. Indeed, he sees you—he and his tribe (members)—from where you cannot see them. Indeed, We have made the satans allies of those who do not believe.

Whenever they commit a shameful act, they say: ‘We found our forefathers practising it, and Allah has commanded us to do it.’ Say: ‘Indeed, Allah does not command indecency. Do you attribute to Allah what you do not know?’

Say: ‘My Lord commands justice. So set your faces (towards Him) on every occasion (and at every place) of prayer. And worship Him, devoting your obedience sincerely to Him. Just as He originated you, so shall you return (to life again).’
A group He has guided, and a group deserves to go astray—they have taken devils as their guardians instead of Allah, and they think they are guided.
O Children of Adam! Put on your adornments at every place of worship. And eat and drink (as We have permitted), but do not be excessive (or wasteful). Indeed, Allah does not like the wasteful.

Say: ‘Who has forbidden the adornment of Allah that He has produced for His servants and the good things from (His) provision?’ Say: ‘They are (permissible) for the believers in the life of this world, and exclusively (for them) on the Day of Resurrection.’ Thus, We clarify the revelations for people who understand.

Say: ‘My Lord has forbidden only indecencies, both the apparent among them and the hidden, sinfulness, unjust aggression and that you should ascribe partners to Allah for which He has not sent down any authority, and that you should attribute to Allah what you do not know.’
For each community, there is an appointed term. When their time comes, they shall not delay it by a single hour, nor shall they advance it.

O Children of Adam! If messengers come to you from among yourselves, relating to you My revelations, then whoever becomes God-conscious and reforms himself, there shall be no fear on them, nor shall they grieve.
As for those who reject Our revelations and look down on them arrogantly, they shall be the inhabitants of the Fire; they shall remain in it forever.

Who is more unjust than the one who fabricates a lie against Allah or rejects His revelations? It is they whose portion of (good and evil), as decreed in the Book, shall reach them, until when Our messengers (i.e. the angels of death) come to them to take them away, they will say (to them): ‘Where are those (idols or devils) whom you used to worship (or call upon) besides Allah?’ They will say: ‘They have abandoned us.’ And they will testify against themselves that they were disbelievers.

He will say: ‘Enter (into the Fire), along with the communities who have passed away before you, from among the jinn and humans.’ Every time a community enters (the Hellfire), it will curse its sister (community) until they all gather inside. The last of them (i.e. the followers) will say about the first of them (i.e. the leaders): ‘Our Lord! They (i.e. the leaders) are the ones who led us astray, so give them a double punishment of the Fire.’ He will say: ‘It is double for each (of you), but you do not know.’
And the first of them (i.e. the leaders) will say to the last of them (i.e. the followers): ‘You cannot have (any) advantage over us. So taste the punishment for what you had been earning.’

Indeed, those who reject Our revelations and look down on them arrogantly, the gates of Heaven shall not be opened for them, nor shall they enter Paradise until the camel passes through the eye of a needle. And thus do We recompense the guilty.
They shall have Hell for their bed, and there shall be sheets (of fire) over them. And thus do We recompense the wrongdoers.
But those who believe and do righteous deeds—We do not task a soul beyond its capacity. It is they who are the inhabitants of Paradise; they shall remain there forever.

We will remove whatever rancour lies within their breasts. And rivers will flow beneath them. And they will say: ‘All praise belongs to Allah, Who guided us to this (place). We would never have found the way (to this place) if Allah had not guided us. The messengers of our Lord had certainly brought the truth.’ And a call would be made (to them): ‘This is your Paradise, (which) you have been given as inheritance for what you used to do.’

The people of Paradise will call out to the people of Hell: ‘We have indeed found what our Lord had promised us to be true. Have you (also) found what your Lord had promised (you) to be true?’ They will answer: ‘Yes.’ Then a caller will announce among them: ‘The curse of Allah be upon the wrongdoers—
those who bar (others) from the way of Allah and seek to make it crooked, and they disbelieve in the Hereafter.’

There will be a barrier between them (i.e. Paradise and Hell). And on the Heights (of that barrier) will be some people who will recognise each of them by their marks. They will call out to the people of Paradise: ‘Peace be upon you!’ They will not have entered it, though they would be eagerly hoping so.
When their eyes will be turned towards the inmates of the Fire, they will say: ‘Our Lord, do not place us among the wrongdoing people.’

And the people of the Heights will call out to certain men whom they will recognise by their marks: ‘Your accumulation of wealth (or being greater in number) did not avail you, nor did your behaving arrogantly.
Are these (believers) the ones you swore Allah would not bestow mercy upon?’ (Now these people are being told:) ‘Enter Paradise! You shall have no fear, nor shall you grieve.’

The people of Hell will call out to the people of Paradise: ‘Pour on us some water, or (give us) any of the sustenance Allah has granted you.’ They will say: ‘Allah has indeed forbidden both (of these bounties) to the disbelievers,

those who took their religion for play and amusement, and whom the life of the world had deceived.’ So today, We forget them, as they forgot the meeting of this Day of theirs, and as they used to deny Our revelations.
We have indeed brought them a Book which We have expounded with knowledge, as a guidance and a mercy for a people who believe.

Do they wait for anything except the fulfilment of this (warning)? The Day when (the moment of) its fulfilment comes, those who had forgotten it before will say: ‘The messengers of Our Lord had certainly brought the truth. Are there any intercessors for us (now) who can intercede on our behalf? Or could we be sent back to do other than what we used to do?’ They have surely ruined their souls, and whatever (false beliefs and idols) they used to invent will abandon them.

Indeed, your Lord is Allah, Who created the heavens and the earth in six periods and then settled (His Might) on the Throne (i.e. enforced His command and power over the universe). He causes the night to cover the day, pursuing it rapidly. And (He created) the sun, moon and stars (all) subjected to His command. Surely, all creation and command belong to Him. Blessed is Allah, the Lord of all worlds.
Supplicate your Lord humbly and secretly. Indeed, He does not like the transgressors.
Do not cause corruption on the earth after it has been set in order. And supplicate Him with fear and hope. Indeed, Allah’s mercy is close to the virtuously excellent.

He is the One Who sends the winds bearing good news before (the showering of) His mercy. When they have gathered up the heavy clouds, We drive them towards a dead land, where We cause rain to fall. Then We bring out with it all kinds of crops. Thus shall We bring out the dead (to life). Perhaps you may be mindful (of advice).

As for the fertile land, its vegetation springs out (abundantly) by the will of its Lord. And as for that which is infertile, it does not come out except hardly. Thus do We vary the signs for people who are grateful.
