Indeed, We sent Nuh (Noah) to his people. So he said: ‘O my people! Worship Allah! You have no god besides Him. Indeed, I fear for you the punishment of a tremendous Day.’
The influential leaders of his people said: ‘Indeed, we see you in clear misguidance.’
He said: ‘O my people! I am not misguided. Rather, I am a messenger from the Lord of all worlds.
I communicate to you the messages of my Lord and advise you sincerely, and I know from Allah what you do not know.
Do you find it astonishing that a reminder from your Lord has come to you through a man from among yourselves to warn you so that you may become God-conscious, and perhaps you may be bestowed mercy?’
But they rejected him, so We saved him and those with him on the Ark. And drowned those who rejected Our revelations. They were indeed blind people.
And to (the people of) ‘Ad, (We sent) their (fellow) brother Hud. He said: ‘O my people! Worship Allah! You have no god besides Him. Will you not then be conscious (of Him)?’
The influential leaders of his people who disbelieved said: ‘Indeed we see you are foolish, and indeed we consider you to be among the liars.’
He said: ‘O my people! There is no foolishness in me. Rather, I am a messenger from the Lord of all worlds.
I communicate to you the messages of my Lord, and I am a trustworthy well-wisher for you.

Do you find it astonishing that a reminder from your Lord has come to you through a man from among yourselves to warn you? Remember when He made you successors after the people of Nuh (Noah) and gave you great stature; then remember Allah’s bounties so that you may be prosperous.’
They said: ‘Have you come to us (that) we should worship Allah alone, and give up what our fathers have been worshipping? Then bring us what you threaten us with if you are among the truthful.’

He said: ‘‘The punishment and wrath of your Lord have already become due against you. Do you dispute with me regarding the names (of idols) that you have named—you and your forefathers, for which Allah has not sent down any authority? So wait! I am also waiting along with you.’
Then We saved him and those with him by a mercy from Us and cut off the last (remnants) of those who rejected Our revelations. And they would never have believed.

And to (the people of) Thamud, We (sent) their (fellow) brother Salih. He said: ‘O my people! Worship Allah! You have no god besides Him. A clear proof has come to you from your Lord. This She-camel of Allah is (sent) as a sign for you. So leave her to graze freely in Allah’s land. And do not cause her any harm, for then a painful punishment will seize you.

Remember when He made you successors after (the people of) ‘Ad and settled you in the land. You built castles on its open plains and carved houses out of the mountains. So remember Allah’s bounties, and do not behave wickedly in the land, causing corruption.’

The influential leaders of his people, who were arrogant, said to the abased, who believed among them: ‘Do you honestly think that Salih has been sent by his Lord?’ They said: ‘Surely we believe in what he has been sent with.’
The arrogant said: ‘Indeed, we are strong deniers of what you believe in.’
So they slew the She-camel and revolted against the command of their Lord. Then they said: ‘O Salih! Bring us what you threaten us with if you are among the messengers.’
Then the earthquake overtook them, and by the following morning, they were lying dead in their homes.
So he (Salih) turned away from them and said: ‘O my people! I indeed conveyed to you the message of my Lord and advised you sincerely, but you do not like well-wishing mentors.’
And (likewise, We sent) Lut (Lot) when he said to his people: ‘What! Do you practise a shameful act of indecency such as none in the world has ever committed before you?
Surely, you come to males with sexual desire instead of females. Indeed, you are a people who transgress all bounds!’
But the answer of his people was none other than to say: ‘Expel them from your town. They are a people who seek to keep themselves pure and chaste.’
So We saved him (Lot) and his family (and his followers)—except his wife; she was among those who stayed behind.
Then We poured upon them a rain (of stones). So observe how (disastrous) was the end of the evildoers!

And to the people of Madyan ([Midian], We sent) their (fellow) brother Shu‘ayb. He said: ‘O my people! Worship Allah! You have no god besides Him. A clear proof has surely come to you from your Lord. So observe fully the measure and the balance, and do not undervalue the goods of the people. And do not cause corruption in the land after it has been set in order. That is better for you if you are (true) believers.

And do not lie in ambush on every path, frightening and hindering those who believe in Him from the way of Allah, seeking to make it crooked. And remember when you were few, and then He multiplied you. And observe how (disastrous) was the end of the corruptors.
If a group among you have believed in what I have been sent with, and another group have not believed, then be patient till Allah judges between us, and He is the best of judges.’

The influential leaders of his people, who were arrogant, said: ‘We shall surely expel you, O Shu‘ayb, and those who believe along with you from our town, or else you shall revert to our religion.’ He said: ‘Even if we are unwilling (to join you)?

Indeed, we would be fabricating a lie against Allah if we reverted to your religion after Allah had delivered us from it. It is inappropriate for us to return to it unless Allah, our Lord, should wish so. Our Lord comprehends all things in (His) knowledge. In Allah, we have put our trust.’ ‘Our Lord! Decide truthfully between us and our people, for You are the best judge.’
The influential leaders of his people, who disbelieved, said (to the weak ones): ‘If you follow Shu‘ayb, you shall surely be losers.’
Then the (overwhelming) earthquake overtook them, and by the following morning, they were lying dead in their homes.
Those who denied Shu‘ayb were (eliminated) as if they had never lived there. Those who denied Shu‘ayb were themselves the losers.
So he turned away from them and said: ‘O my people! I indeed conveyed to you the messages of my Lord and gave you sincere advice.’ Then how should I grieve for a disbelieving people?’
And whenever We sent a prophet to any town, We afflicted its (disbelieving, arrogant) people with suffering and adversity so that they might humble themselves.

Then We replaced (their) adversity with prosperity until they flourished (in multiple numbers) and said: ‘Hardship and affluence (also) befell our forefathers.’ Then, We overtook them suddenly while they were unaware.

If the people of those towns had believed and been conscious of God, We would surely have opened for them (doors of overwhelming) blessings from heaven and the earth. But they rejected (the truth). So We seized them for what they used to earn.
Did the people of (those) towns feel secure that Our punishment would not come upon them by night while they were asleep?
Or did the people of (those) towns feel secure that Our punishment would not come upon them at midday while they were playing around?
Or did they feel secure from Allah’s plan? None feels secure from Allah’s plan except for the losing people.

Does it not serve as guidance to those who inherited the earth after (the destruction of) its (former) inhabitants that—had We willed, We could have punished them (too) for their sins and set a seal on their hearts so that they would not hear (the truth)?

These are the towns whose stories We have narrated to you. Their messengers surely came to them with clear signs, but they were not the ones to believe in what they had already denied. Thus does Allah set a seal upon the hearts of the disbelievers.
We did not find in most of them any (loyalty to their) covenant. And We found most of them to be defiant.
Then, after them, We sent Musa (Moses) with Our signs to Pharaoh and his leaders, but they wronged them. So, observe how was the end of the corruptors.
And Musa (Moses) said: ‘O Pharaoh! I am indeed a messenger from the Lord of all worlds.

(I am) duty-bound to say nothing about Allah except the truth. I have certainly come to you with clear proof from your Lord. Therefore, let the Children of Israel go with me (setting them free of your slavery).’
He said: ‘If you have brought a sign, then produce it if you are among the truthful.’
So, he (Moses) threw his staff down. Behold! It was a large snake, clear to all.
Then, he pulled out his hand (out of his collar). Behold! It was (shining) white to the viewers.
The influential leaders of Pharaoh’s people said: ‘‘He is indeed an expert magician.
He wants to expel you from your land. (Pharaoh asked:) ‘So, what do you advise?’ ’’
They said: ‘Let him and his brother wait (for a while) and send collectors to the cities,
to bring you every expert magician.’
And the magicians came to Pharaoh. They said: ‘Shall we receive a (suitable) reward if we are the ones to be victorious?’
He (Pharaoh) said: ‘Of course; you shall indeed become among the closest to me.’
They (the magicians) said: ‘Musa (Moses)! Will you throw (first), or shall we be the ones to throw?’
He (Moses) said: ‘You throw.’ So when they threw (their strings and sticks on the ground), they deceived the eyes of the people and frightened them, producing a tremendous magic.’
And We revealed to Musa (Moses): ‘(Now) throw down your staff.’ And behold, it was swallowing up whatever they had faked.
So, the truth was established, and whatever they had done was proved to be false.
So they were defeated then and there, and became utterly humiliated.
And the magicians were made to fall, prostrating.
They said: ‘We believe in the Lord of all worlds.
(The) Lord of Musa (Moses) and Harun (Aaron).’

Pharaoh said: ‘Have you believed in Him before I permit you? This is indeed a plot you have secretly devised in this city so that you may expel its people from it. Soon, you will know (the consequences).
Surely, I shall cut off your hands and your feet on alternate sides, and then, most certainly, I shall crucify you all.’
They said: ‘Indeed, we are to return to our Lord.

And you are vindictive towards us only because we have believed in the signs of our Lord when they came to us (clearly).’ ‘Our Lord! Shower upon us patience and grant us to die as Muslims (i.e. those who submit to Allah).’

And the leaders of Pharaoh’s people said: ‘Will you leave Musa (Moses) and his people to cause disorder in the land, and to abandon you and your gods?’ He (Pharaoh) said: ‘We shall slaughter their sons and keep their women alive, and surely we are dominant over them.’

Musa (Moses) said to his people: ‘Seek help from Allah and be patient. Surely, the earth belongs to Allah, and He gives its inheritance to whomever He wills among His servants. And the final outcome will be in favour of those who are God-conscious.’

They said: ‘We were persecuted before you came to us and (also) after you came to us.’ He (Moses) said: ‘Perhaps your Lord will destroy your enemy and make you successors in the land, and then He will see how you behave.’
And indeed, We afflicted the people of Pharaoh with drought and shortage of crops; perhaps they might be mindful (of advice).

But whenever something good came to them, they would say: ‘This is due to us.’ And if any evil afflicted them, they would attribute it to the ill luck of Musa (Moses) and those with him. Look! Indeed, their ill fortune is only from Allah, but most of them do not realise (it).
And they said (to Moses): ‘Whatever sign you may bring to us to bewitch us with, we are not going to believe you.’
So We sent upon them the flood, the locusts, the lice, the frogs and the blood—(all) as distinct signs. But they (still) behaved arrogantly and were (excessively) sinful people.

And when the wrath fell upon them, they would say: ‘Musa (Moses)! Pray for us to your Lord by the covenant He has made with you. If you remove the wrath from us, we will certainly believe in you and let the Children of Israel go along with you.’
But when We removed the wrath from them, until a (promised) term they were to reach, they (again) broke their promise.
So We took vengeance upon them and drowned them in the sea because they rejected Our Signs. And they were careless of those (signs).

Then We made the people who were oppressed (i.e. the Children of Israel) to inherit the eastern parts of the land (i.e. the Levant) and its western parts (i.e. Egypt), which We had showered with blessings. And (in this way) the fairest word of your Lord was fulfilled for the Children of Israel because they endured (sufferings) patiently. So We demolished all that Pharaoh and his people had made and what they used to construct (among the buildings and gardens).

And We carried the Children of Israel across the sea, where they came upon a people clinging to their idols for worship. They said: ‘O Musa (Moses)! Make for us a god like the gods they have.’ He said: ‘You are surely an ignorant people.
Indeed, what they are engaged in is bound to perish, and what they have been doing is false.’
He said: ‘Shall I seek for you a god other than Allah, while He has graced you over all (other) nations?’

And (remember) when We delivered you from the people of Pharaoh, who subjected you to terrible torment, slaughtering your sons and sparing your women. And in that (life) was a grievous trial from your Lord.

And We appointed for Musa (Moses) thirty nights and completed them with ten (more). Thus, the appointed term of his Lord (for the meeting) was completed in forty nights. And Musa (Moses) said to his brother Harun (Aaron): ‘Be my successor among my people (in my absence), and set things right, and do not follow the way of the mischief-makers.’

When Musa (Moses) came to Our appointment and his Lord spoke to him, he said: ‘My Lord, show me (Yourself) so that I may look at You.’ He said: ‘You cannot (bear to) see Me. But look at the mountain. If it remains firm in its place, then you will see Me.’ So when his Lord manifested His (Divine) Glory to the mountain, He made it crumble to dust, and Musa (Moses) fell down fainting. And when he recovered, he said: ‘Glory be to You! I turn to You in repentance. And I am the first of the believers.’
He said: ‘O Musa (Moses)! I have chosen you above (all other) people with My messages and My speech. So take hold of what I have given you and be among the grateful.’

And We inscribed for him on the Tablets (of the Torah) admonition, and an exposition of all (necessary) things. (And We said:) ‘Hold on to them firmly and command your people to take the best of (what is in) them. I will soon show you the abode of the transgressors.

I will turn away from My revelations those who act arrogantly on the earth without justice. Even if they were to see every sign, they would not believe in it. And if they were to see the path of guidance, they still would not adopt it as (their) path. And if they were to see the way of error, they would adopt it as (their) way. That is because they rejected Our revelations and remained negligent of them.’
And those who deny Our revelations and the meeting of the Hereafter, their deeds have come to nothing. Should they be rewarded except for what they used to do?

And while he was away, the people of Musa (Moses) took up (for worship) a calf (made) from their ornaments—a mere body that made sounds like a cow. Did they not see that it could neither speak to them nor guide them to the (right) path? Yet they took it up (for worship). And they were wrongdoers.

And when they felt ashamed of what they had done and realised that they had really gone astray, they said: ‘If our Lord does not have mercy upon us and forgive us, we shall surely be among the losers.’

And when Musa (Moses) returned to his people angry and aggrieved, he said: ‘How evil is the course you have followed after me? Were you so keen to bring your Lord’s judgement forward?’ He put down the Tablets (of the Torah) and seized his brother by the head, pulling him towards himself. He (Aaron) said: ‘O son of my mother! Indeed, people considered me weak, and they were about to kill me (on my forbidding). So let not the enemies rejoice over me, and do not count me among the wrongdoing people.’
He (Moses) said: ‘O my Lord! Forgive me and my brother and admit us into Your Mercy, and You are the most Merciful of the merciful.’
Indeed, those who took up the calf (for worship), surely their Lord’s wrath, and disgrace in worldly life will overtake them. Thus do we recompense the fabricators (of lies).
As for those who commit evil deeds, repent after that, and become (true) believers—surely, thereafter, your Lord shall be Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.
And when the anger subsided from Musa (Moses), he picked up the Tablets, whose text contained guidance and mercy for those who fear for the sake of their Lord.

And Musa (Moses) chose seventy men of his people for Our appointment. So when the earthquake overtook them, he said: ‘My Lord! If You had willed, You could have destroyed them and me long ago. Will You (now) destroy us for what the fools among us have done? It is only Your trial by which You leave to stray whomever You will and guide whomever You please. You are our Master, so forgive us and have mercy upon us. And You are the Best of forgivers.

And ordain for us what is good in this world and the Hereafter. Indeed, we have turned to You (in repentance).’ He said: ‘(As for) My punishment, I will afflict with it whom I wish, but My mercy embraces all things. I shall ordain this (mercy) for those who are God-conscious, pay the alms due and believe in Our revelations.

(They are) the ones who follow the Messenger, the Prophet, untutored (by any worldly teacher)*. (He is the one) whom they find described with them in the Torah and the Injil (Gospel). He commands them whatever is good and forbids them from whatever is evil. He makes all good things lawful for them and makes all impure things unlawful for them. He relieves them from their burdens and (removes) the shackles which had bound them. So only those who believe in him, revere him, support him (in his cause) and follow the light sent down with him—it is they who will be successful.’ [* The word ‘ Ummi ’ refers to the special status of the Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him), that he did not receive any formal education from any human, nor did he study any book or scripture. He still knows far more than the entire creation through the divine knowledge bestowed upon him directly from Allah Almighty. Allah taught him directly whatever he did not know before. This word also refers to his innocence and purity, like the innocence and purity of a newborn baby, despite his raising in an idolatrous and polytheistic society.]

Say: ‘O humankind! I am the Messenger of Allah to you all. To Him belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. There is no god except Him. He gives life and causes death. So believe in Allah and His Messenger, the Prophet, untutored (by any worldly teacher), who believes in Allah and His Words, and follow him so that you may be rightly guided.
And among the people of Musa (Moses) are some who guide (others) with the truth and act justly according to it.

We divided them (i.e. Children of Israel) into twelve tribal communities. And We revealed to Musa (Moses) when his people asked him for water: ‘Strike the rock with your staff.’ Then, twelve fountains gushed out from it. All the people came to know their drinking place. And We shaded them with clouds, and We sent down manna and quails upon them (and said to them:) ‘Eat of the good things We have provided you.’ And they did not harm Us, but they used to wrong only themselves.

And (remember) when it was said to them: ‘Settle in this town* and eat from it wherever you wish, and say: ‘We repent (for all of our sins),’ and enter the gate prostrating, We will forgive you your sins, (and) We shall increase (the reward of) those who excel in virtue. [* The town here refers to Jerusalem, or Ariha (Jericho), or a Palestinian city in the West Bank, or the land of Syria, or the whole of Palestine.]

But the wrongdoers among them substituted a different word from the one they were told. So, We sent down a punishment (i.e. a plague) upon them from heaven because they used to transgress persistently.

And ask them about the (people of the) town situated on the seaside,* who violated the Sabbath, when their fish would come to them on the Sabbath day (i.e. Saturday), appearing clearly on the surface of the water (towards the shore). On (other) days, when they were not observing the Sabbath, they (i.e. the fish) would not come to them. Thus, We tested them because of the transgressions they used to commit. [* Some reports identify it as a town called Ayla (Eilat), located in the Gulf of Aqaba between Midian and Sinai. Others identify it as Tiberias or a town in Midian.]

And (remember) when a group of them said (to their preachers): ‘Why do you admonish a people whom Allah is going to destroy or punish with a severe punishment?’ They (i.e. the preachers) said: ‘(We do it) just to be free from your Lord’s blame, and (with the hope) that they may perhaps become God-conscious.’
So when they ignored (the warning) they had been reminded of, We saved those who forbade evil and overtook those who did wrong with dreadful punishment because of their persistent disobedience.
When they arrogantly persisted in doing what they were forbidden, We said (to them): ‘Be like apes, disgraced and rejected.’

And when your Lord declared that, until the Day of Resurrection, He would certainly send (other) people against them, who would inflict terrible sufferings on them. Indeed, your Lord is swift in retribution. And indeed, He is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.
We dispersed them into (separate) communities over the earth. Some of them were righteous, and some of them were otherwise. And We tested them with blessings and misfortunes that they might return.

Then, a generation succeeded them, inheriting the Book. They would grab the (transitory) gains of this lower world and say: ‘We will be forgiven.’ And if similar (transitory) gains were to come their way (again), they would grab them too. Was not the covenant of the Book taken from them that they shall not attribute anything to Allah except the truth? And they were well-versed in its teaching. And (know that) the abode of the Hereafter is better for those who are God-conscious. Do you not then understand?
As for those who hold fast to the Book and establish the prayer—indeed, We do not waste the reward of the reformers.

And when We raised the mountain above them as if it were a canopy, they thought it would fall upon them. (So We said to them:) ‘Hold firmly to what We have given you, and remember what is in it, so that you may become God-conscious.’

And (remember) when your Lord took (the pretemporal covenant) from the Children of Adam and brought forth (all) their descendants from their loins, and made them bear witness over themselves. (He said to them:) ‘Am I not your Lord?’ They said: ‘Yes indeed! We bear witness.’ Lest you should say on the Day of Resurrection: ‘Indeed we were unaware of this (testimony).’

Or (lest) you should say: ‘It was our forefathers who ascribed partners (to Allah) before (us), and we were only (following in their footsteps as) descendants (who came) after them. So, will You destroy us because of what the falsifiers have done?’
Thus, We make the revelations clear so they may return (to the truth).
(O Prophet!) Relate to them the account of a man to whom We gave Our signs, but he abandoned them. So, Satan made him his follower, and he became one of the deviants.

Had We willed, We would surely have elevated him with these (signs), but instead, he inclined towards the earth and followed his low desires. So his example is that of a dog: if you attack him, he will loll out his tongue, and if you leave him alone, he will (still) loll out his tongue. Such is the parable of the people who reject Our revelations. So recount these stories (to the people); perhaps they may reflect.
How evil is the parable of the people who rejected Our revelations and wronged themselves?
Whoever Allah guides, he is rightly guided, and whoever He leaves to stray, so they are the ones who are the losers.

We have indeed created for Hell many of the jinn and humans: they have hearts with which they do not understand; they have eyes with which they do not see; and they have ears with which they do not hear. They are like cattle; rather, they are further astray. It is they who are heedless.

To Allah belong the Most Beautiful Names, so call (and supplicate) Him by them. And leave those who deviate regarding (the attributes of) His Names. Soon, they shall be recompensed for what they used to do.
And among those whom We created is a community which guides (people) with the truth and acts justly according to it.
As for those who reject Our revelations, We shall gradually draw them (into destruction) in a way they cannot realise.
And I will grant them (a temporary) respite. Indeed, My Scheme is firmly effective.
Have they not reflected that there is no insanity in their Guide (and that) he is only a clear Warner?
Have they not contemplated the dominions of the heavens, the earth, and all that Allah has created, and perhaps their end has already drawn near? So what discourse after this will they believe?
Whomever Allah leaves to stray, there is no guide for him. And He leaves them alone, wandering confused in their defiance.

They ask you about the Hour: ‘When will it arrive?’ Say: ‘Its knowledge is only with my Lord. None but He shall reveal it at its proper time. It will be heavy upon the heavens and the earth. It will not overtake you but very suddenly.’ They ask you as if you were eager to find out. Say: ‘Its knowledge is only with Allah, but most people do not know.’

Say: ‘I do not own any benefit for myself, nor any harm, except what Allah may wish. If I had known the Unseen (of my own accord), I would have acquired abundant benefit, and no trouble would have (ever) touched me. I am only a Warner and a Bearer of glad tidings to a people who believe.’

He is the One Who created you out of one living soul and made its mate from it, that he might incline to her. So when he covers her, she bears a light burden and passes (some time) with it. When it grows heavy, they both pray to Allah, their Lord: ‘If You give us a healthy child, we will surely be among the grateful.’
But when He gives them a healthy child, they ascribe partners to Him regarding what He has given them. But Allah is highly exalted above the partners they ascribe to Him.
Do they ascribe (to Him) partners who cannot create anything? Rather, they are created themselves,
and they can neither help them nor can they help themselves.
(O idolaters!) If you call upon them for guidance, they will not respond to you. It is the same to you whether you call upon them or whether you remain silent.
(O idolaters!) Indeed, those whom you worship besides Allah are creatures like you. So call upon them! Let them answer you if you are truthful (in your claims).

Do they have feet with which they walk? Do they have hands with which they hold? Do they have eyes with which they see? Do they have ears with which they hear? Say: ‘(O idolaters!) Invoke your partners (which you ascribe to Allah), then plan against me and do not grant me any respite.
Indeed, my Protector is Allah, Who sent down the Book, and He takes care of the righteous.
Those (idols) you worship besides Him can neither help you nor can they help themselves.’
(O idolaters!) If you call upon them for guidance, they will not hear (you). You see them looking at you, but they do not see.
Take (the course of) pardoning, enjoin the good and pay no heed to the ignorant.
And if a temptation from Satan provokes you, seek refuge with Allah. Indeed, He is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.
Those who are God-conscious, when a visitation from Satan touches them, they remember (Allah), and behold, they perceive (through the eye of their heart).
But as for their (fellow) brothers (i.e. the followers of Satan), those (devils) draw them further into error and then do not spare (them any harm).

When you do not bring them a (fresh) revelation, they say: ‘Why do you not choose (to forge) it?’ Say: ‘I only follow what is revealed to me from my Lord. These are insights from your Lord, and a guidance and a mercy for a people who believe.’
And (O believers!) when the Qur’an is recited, listen to it and remain silent. Perhaps you may be bestowed mercy.
And remember your Lord within your heart humbly and fearfully, without being loud of voice, in the morning and the evening. And do not be among the negligent.
Indeed, those who are (stationed) in the presence of your Lord are not arrogant to worship Him. They keep glorifying Him and prostrating to Him.

(Esteemed Messenger!) They ask you about (the distribution of) the spoils of war. Say: ‘The spoils belong to Allah and the Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him). So be conscious of Allah and settle the matters (of disagreement) nicely among yourselves. And obey Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) if you are (true) believers.’
The believers are only those who, when Allah is mentioned, their hearts tremble (with reverence). And when His revelations are recited to them, they increase their faith—and they trust in their Lord.
(They are those) who establish the prayer and spend out of what We have provided them (in charity).
They are the ones who are the true believers. They shall have (high) ranks in the presence of their Lord, forgiveness and generous provision.
(Esteemed Prophet!) For it was your Lord Who brought you out from your home for a just cause, though a group of the believers were indeed reluctant.
They disputed with you about the just cause even after it became clear (to them). (It was) as if they were being driven towards death while they could see themselves.

And (remember) when Allah promised you (victory over) one of the two (enemy) groups, (saying:) ‘It is for you.’ You wished the unarmed one to be yours (for that was easy to encounter). But Allah desired to establish the truth through His Words and to cut off the roots of the (invading) disbelievers,
so that He may establish the truth (firmly) and nullify falsehood, though the guilty might dislike it.
And (remember) when you were calling upon your Lord for help, He responded to your supplication, (saying:) ‘I am going to reinforce you with a thousand angels coming in ranks upon ranks.’
Allah did not make it but as good news (of victory), and that your hearts may feel peace through it. And victory comes only from Allah. Indeed, Allah is All-Mighty, All-Wise.

And (remember) when He caused drowsiness to overcome you (on the night before the battle) as (a feeling of) security from Him, and He sent down water upon you from the sky to purify you with it, and to remove from you Satan’s pollution (of suspicions), and to fortify your hearts and to make (your) steps firm and steady.

And (remember) when your Lord revealed to the angels: ‘I am with you; so make the believers stand firm. I shall cast fear into the hearts of the (invading) disbelievers. So, strike (them) above the necks and strike each of their fingertips (during the battle).’

That is because they defied Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him). And whosoever defies Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him), Allah is indeed severe in retribution.
That (punishment) is yours, so taste it! And (know) that for the disbelievers, there is the punishment of Hell (as well).
O believers! When you encounter an army of disbelievers advancing for battle, do not turn your backs (in retreat) from them.

And whoever turns his back (to flee) from them on such a day, unless it is a diversion in the battle or a (strategic) withdrawal to join another troop, shall certainly earn the wrath of Allah. And his refuge shall be Hell, an evil destination!

So you did not kill them, but it was Allah who killed them. And (Esteemed Prophet,) you did not throw (the handful of sand and pebbles at your enemies) when you threw (at the outset of the battle of Badr); instead, it was Allah Who threw, that He might try the believers with a beautiful trial from Himself. Indeed, Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.
That is (what you are granted), and Allah indeed weakens the strategy of the disbelievers.

(O Meccan disbelievers!) If you were seeking victory, then the (decision of) victory has already come to you. If you stop here, it would be better for you. And if you return (to fighting), We shall also return. And your troops, though greater in number, shall avail you nothing. And (know) that Allah is with the believers.
O believers! Obey Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him), and do not turn away from him while you are listening (to him).
And do not be like those who say: ‘We hear,’ but they do not listen.
Indeed, the worst of all creatures in Allah’s sight are those who are (wilfully) the deaf and the dumb who do not understand.
Had Allah known any virtue in them, He would have surely made them hear. But even if He had made them hear, they would still have turned away, not taking any notice (of the truth).

O believers! Respond to (the call of) Allah and the Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) whenever he calls you to that which gives you (everlasting) life. Know that Allah intervenes between a man and (the desires of) his heart, and towards Him, you will all be gathered.
And guard yourselves against affliction (of temptation) which shall befall not only those among you who are wrongdoers. And know that Allah is severe in retribution.

Remember when you were few (in Mecca), oppressed in the land, constantly fearing that the (powerful) people might snatch you away, then (after the migration to Medina) He sheltered you, strengthened you with His help and provided you with the good things so that you might be grateful.
O believers! Do not betray Allah and the Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him), and do not betray your trusts knowingly.
Know that your possessions and your children are only a test (and temptation) and that Allah—with Him is an excellent reward.

O believers! If you become conscious of Allah, He will grant you a criterion (to distinguish between right and wrong), and He will absolve you of your misdeeds and forgive you. And Allah is the Lord of Mighty Grace.
And (remember) when the disbelievers were devising plans against you to confine you, or kill you, or expel you. They plotted, and Allah foiled their plots. And Allah is the Best of planners.
When Our revelations are recited to them, they say: ‘We have heard already. If we want, we (too) can say something similar. These are nothing but the (fictional) stories of the ancients.’
And when they said: ‘O Allah! If this is the truth from You, rain down stones upon us from the sky, or bring us a painful punishment.’
But (Esteemed Prophet!) Allah would never punish them while you are among them, nor would Allah punish them while they seek forgiveness.

What (other excuse) do they have that Allah should not punish them while they are barring (the believers) from the Sacred Mosque when they are not its (rightful) custodians? Its (rightful) custodians are only those who are God-conscious. But most of them do not know.
Their prayer at the (Sacred) House is only whistling and clapping. So, taste the punishment for what you used to disbelieve in.

Indeed, those who disbelieve, spend their wealth to hinder (people) from the way of Allah. And they will keep on spending it; then it will be a source of regret for them, then they shall be overcome. And the disbelievers shall be gathered towards Hell,
so that Allah may separate the evil from the good, and make the evil (dominating) one upon another, then pile them up altogether, and place them in Hell. It is they who are the losers.

Say to the disbelievers: ‘If they stop (their transgression), what has already passed shall be forgiven for them. But if they repeat the same, then the precedent of the ancients has already passed (before them).’

And fight them (against their aggression) until there is no more persecution and the religion becomes totally (free) in compliance with Allah’s command (of peace and justice). And if they abstain (from causing terror and disorder), then Allah is indeed All-Seeing of whatever they do.
And if they turn away (from complying with Allah’s command), then know that Allah is your Protector—an Excellent Protector and an Excellent Helper!

And know that whatever you take (legitimately) as spoils of war, a fifth of it is for Allah and the Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him), and (his) close relatives, the orphans, the needy and the travellers if you believe in Allah and what We sent down to Our (exalted) Servant on the Day of Distinction, the day when the two armies encountered (each other on the battlefield of Badr). And Allah is All-Capable of everything.

And (remember) when you were on the nearer bank (of the valley towards Medina), and they (i.e. the army of the Meccans) were on the farther bank (of the valley), and the (mercantile) caravan (of Abu Sufyan) was in a lower place than you. Had you mutually made an appointment (to fight), you would have surely reached there at different times (but Allah coincided your encounter)—so that Allah might conclude a matter which was bound to be fulfilled, (and) that whoever perishes should perish with clear evidence, and whoever survives should survive with clear evidence. And Allah is indeed All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

And (remember) when Allah showed them (i.e. the enemy troops) to you in your dream as being few. Had He shown them to you as being many, you would have surely lost hope and disputed about the matter (of war), but Allah saved (you from being disheartened). Indeed, He is the All-Knowing of what is in the breasts.

And (also remember) when He made them appear to you, at the time of your encounter, to be few in your eyes, and He made you appear to be few in their eyes, so that Allah may conclude a matter that was bound to be done. And to Allah, all matters are returned.
O believers! When you face a troop (in battle), stand firm and remember Allah frequently so that you may prosper.

And obey Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him), and do not dispute (among yourselves), for then you will lose heart, and your power will be gone. And be patient; indeed, Allah is with the patient.
And be not like those (pagans) who left their homes boastfully, only to be seen by people and to bar (other people) from Allah’s path. And Allah comprehends whatever they do.

And (remember) when Satan made their deeds fair-seeming to them and said: ‘No one among humankind shall overcome you today, and I am indeed your protector.’ But when the two troops saw each other, he withdrew upon his heels, saying: ‘Indeed I disown you. Surely, I see what you do not see. Truly, I fear Allah, and Allah is Severe in retribution.’
And (remember) when the hypocrites and those possessing sickness in their hearts said: ‘Their Religion has deceived them.’ But whoever trusts in Allah, Allah is indeed All-Mighty, All-Wise.
If only you could see when the angels take away the souls of the disbelievers, striking their faces and their backs, saying: ‘Taste the punishment of the burning (of Hell).
That is for what your hands have sent forth, and because Allah is never unjust to (His) servants.’
(They are) like the people of Pharaoh and those before them, who denied Allah’s signs. So Allah seized them for their sins. Indeed, Allah is Strong (and) Severe in retribution.
That is because Allah never changes a blessing that He has bestowed upon a people unless they change their condition by themselves. And Allah is indeed All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

(It is) like the people of Pharaoh and those before them, who rejected the signs of their Lord; so We destroyed them for their sins. And We drowned the people of Pharaoh, for they were all wrongdoers.
Indeed, the worst creatures in Allah’s sight are those who disbelieve, and they will never believe,

(they are) those among them (i.e. the tribes of al-Nadir and Qurayza) with whom you entered into treaties (for mutual peace and state security), but they violated their treaties every time,* and who do not guard themselves (against evil). [* The tribes of al-Nadir and Qurayza had violated their peace treaties by supporting the Meccan armies during their invasions of Medina and by providing them arms, ammunition and strategic information. It was an act of high treason against the state—as agreed upon in the constitution, mutually signed by all Muslims and Jewish tribes in the form of the ‘Pact of Medina’.]
So if you confront them in battle, deal with them (in such a way) as to scatter those who are (to come) behind them; perhaps they may receive a warning.
If you apprehend treachery from a people, throw back to them (their treaty) on equal terms. Indeed, Allah does not like the treacherous.
Let the disbelievers not suppose that they have won (or outstripped Allah’s will). Indeed, they cannot frustrate (His power).

And prepare against them whatever (human resources and economic and military) strength you can, and warhorses, to frighten thereby the enemy of Allah, and your enemy and others besides them (from attacking you), whom you do not know but Allah knows them. And whatever you spend (for your defence) in the way of Allah will be repaid to you in full, and you will not be wronged.
If they (i.e. the combatant enemies) incline towards peace, you should also incline towards it and put your trust in Allah. Indeed, He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
And if they intend to deceive you, Allah is surely enough for you. He is the One Who strengthens you (directly) through His help and through the believers (who follow you).

And He united their hearts (in mutual compassion). Had you spent all that is on the earth, you could not have united their hearts, but Allah has united them (despite their long history of conflicts and hostilities). Allah is indeed All-Mighty, All-Wise.
O (Esteemed) Prophet! Sufficient for you is Allah and those of the believers who follow you (steadfastly).

O (Esteemed) Prophet! Urge the believers to fight (in self-defence). If there be twenty steadfast among you (in the event of war), they shall overcome two hundred (combatant enemies). And if there be one hundred of you, they shall overcome one thousand (combatant enemies) from among the disbelievers because they are a people who do not understand.

Now Allah has lightened your burden, for He knows that there is (some degree of) weakness in you. So, if there are one hundred steadfast among you, they shall overcome two hundred (combatant enemies). And if there are one thousand (steadfast) among you, they shall overcome two thousand (combatant enemies) by Allah’s permission. And Allah is with the steadfast.

It is not for a prophet to take prisoners of war until he has completely conquered the battlefield. (O people!) You desire the (temporary and lower) gains of this world, while Allah desires (for you the permanent and superior reward of) the Hereafter. And Allah is All-Mighty, All-Wise.
Had it not been a prior decree (of forgiveness) from Allah, a severe torment would have come upon you for what you had taken (as spoils from the battle or the ransom of prisoners).
So (from now on), consume the spoils you have acquired, for they are lawful and fine. And be conscious of Allah. Indeed, Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.

O (Esteemed) Prophet! Say to those prisoners (of war) in your custody: ‘If Allah finds any goodness in your heart, He will give you (something) better than what was taken away from you. And He will forgive you, and Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.’
And (Esteemed Prophet,) if they intend treachery against you, they have been treacherous with Allah before. But He has granted (you) power over them, and Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

Indeed, those who believed, and migrated (to Medina), and strived with their possessions and their lives in the way of Allah, and those who sheltered and helped (them)—it is they who are guardians of one another. As for those who believed but did not migrate (for Allah), you have no obligation regarding their protection until they emigrate. Yet if they seek your help (against persecution) because of (their) religion, then helping them is your obligation, except against those people with whom you have a treaty (of non-interference). And Allah is All-Seeing of whatever you do.

As for the disbelievers, they are allies (and supporters) of one another. (O believers!) If you do not do the same (by becoming allies and supporters of one another), there will be (endless) persecution on the earth and great corruption.

As for those who believed, emigrated and struggled hard in the way of Allah, and those who provided (them) shelter and help, they are the ones who are the true believers. For them shall be forgiveness and a generous provision.

As for those who believed afterwards, emigrated and struggled hard alongside you, they are (also to be counted) among you. But the blood relatives are more entitled to (inherit from) one another, as ordained by Allah. Indeed, Allah is All-Knowing of everything.
(This is) a (declaration of) acquittal from Allah and His Messenger (addressed) to those (Meccan) polytheists with whom you had made a treaty (of peace and they breached it).

(O breachers of the peace!) You may (still) travel freely in the land for four months (as a truce period) and know that you cannot escape Allah (by frustrating Him). And Allah will disgrace the disbelievers (who persecuted the peaceful civilians).

(This is) a proclamation from Allah and His Messenger to the people on the Day of the Great Pilgrimage that Allah and His Messenger are absolved from all obligations to the (peace breaching) polytheists.* So, if you repent, it would be better for you. But if you turn away, know that you cannot escape Allah. And warn the (persecuting) disbelievers of a painful punishment. [* This proclamation was made as a response to the massacre of the peaceful people of Banu Khuza‘a, who, according to the Treaty of Hudaybiya, were allies of the state of Medina. The Quraysh supported the people of Banu Bakr with arms and men in launching a murderous attack on Banu Khuza‘a. As a result of this act, many innocent people were killed, while others were persecuted, tortured and detained. This act of breaching the Treaty of Hudaybiya absolved the state of Medina from all obligations. Hence, verses 1–16 were revealed in this historical context, referring to a specific event, and do not apply to non-Muslims in general.]

Except for those polytheists with whom you made a treaty and who did not breach any of its terms with you, nor did they support anyone against you. So, fulfil the treaty with them until the end of their term. Indeed, Allah loves the God-conscious.

So when the forbidden months are over, kill the (combatant) idolaters (those who have initiated a war against you) wherever you find them (on the battlefield), or capture them and imprison them, or lie in wait for them at every place of ambush (to besiege them). But, if they repent (after being taken as prisoners of war), and they perform the prayer and give the alms due, then leave their way open (by releasing them). Indeed, Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.

And if any of the polytheists seeks asylum from you, grant him asylum so that he may hear the Word of Allah. Then, convey him to his place of security. That is because they are a people who do not know (the truth).

How can the (treacherous) idolaters have any (valid) treaty with Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him)? But as for those with whom you made a treaty near the Sacred Mosque (at al-Hudaybiya), so long as they remain steadfast with you (in fulfilment of their promise), remain steadfast with them. Indeed, Allah loves the God-conscious.

How (can they have a treaty)? If they prevail over you, they will not observe, in your case, any ties of relationship (or principle of diplomacy) or treaty. They please you with their mouths (by flattering words) while their hearts contain the opposite. And most of them are disobedient.
They have sold the revelations of Allah for a paltry price and have turned from His way. Indeed, evil is what they have been doing.
In the case of a believer, they observe neither any bond of relationship nor any (term of) treaty. It is they who are the transgressors.

But if they repent, establish the prayer and give the alms due, then they are your brethren in faith.* We make the messages clear for people who understand. [* In this case, the Muslims do not need a separate treaty to establish peaceful relations with them. Their embracing Islam will serve as a peace treaty unless they do not betray their commitments.]

If they break their pledges after having made their treaty and openly revile your religion (causing a potential threat to state security), then fight the leaders of militancy (to pre-empt their aggression and invasion). Indeed, they have no (commitment to their) pledges. (This pre-emptive action is needed) so that they might stop (their offensive designs).

Will you not fight the (treacherous) people who broke their pledges (violating the peace treaty), conspired to expel the Messenger ([blessings and peace be upon him] from Mecca) and initiated (hostilities) against you first? Do you fear them? But Allah is most deserving that you should fear Him if you are (true) believers.

Fight those (who breached the Peace Treaty of Hudaybiya and initiated war against you). Allah will punish them by your hands and disgrace them. And He will grant you victory over them and heal the breasts of the believing people (who were tortured by the Meccan leaders of militancy),
and He will remove rage from their hearts. Allah turns mercifully to whomever He pleases, and Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

Do you suppose that you will be left untested, while Allah has not (yet) made known which of you have struggled hard and not taken besides Allah, His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) and the believers someone as (their) intimate confidant? And Allah is All-Aware of what you do.

It is not for the polytheists to maintain the mosques of Allah while they testify to their disbelief against themselves. The deeds of such people have become void, and they shall abide in the Fire forever.

Only they should maintain Allah’s mosques who believe in Allah and the Last Day, who establish the prayer, give the alms due and fear none but Allah. Hopefully, such people will be among the rightly guided.

Do you consider providing water to the Hajj pilgrims and maintaining the Sacred Mosque to be like (the deeds of) someone who believes in Allah and the Last Day and struggles hard in the way of Allah? They are not equal in Allah’s sight, and Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.

Those who believe and migrate (from their homelands) and struggle hard in the way of Allah with their possessions and their lives are much higher in rank in Allah’s sight. And they are the ones who are successful.
Their Lord gives them the glad tidings of Mercy from Himself and (His) good pleasure and Gardens, wherein they shall have eternal blessings.
They will reside in them forever. Indeed, with Allah is the mighty reward.

O believers! Do not take your fathers and brothers as trusted allies (against the Muslims) if they choose disbelief over faith. And those of you who take them as trusted allies are the ones who are the wrongdoers.

(Esteemed Prophet!) Say: ‘If your fathers, your children, your brothers, your spouses, your tribe, the possessions which you have acquired, the business whose decline you may fear and the dwellings which you find pleasing are more beloved to you than Allah, His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) and striving in His way, then wait until Allah issues His command (of torment). And Allah does not guide the disobedient people.’

Allah had helped you (believers) in many situations (and especially) on the day of Hunayn when you took pride in your large number, but it did not benefit you in any way. The earth became narrow upon you despite its expanse; then you turned back to flee.

Then Allah sent down His tranquillity upon His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) and upon the believers and sent down armies (of angels) you could not see. And He punished the (combatant) disbelievers. That is the recompense of the disbelievers.
But after (all of this), Allah turns mercifully to whomever He pleases. Indeed, Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.

O believers! The polytheists are indeed unclean. So let them not come near the Sacred Mosque after this year.* If you fear poverty (due to loss of trade), Allah will soon enrich you out of His Grace if He wishes. Indeed, Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise. [* It was from the year 9 AH onwards.]

Fight (the treacherous people) among those given the book who do not believe in Allah, nor the Last Day, nor do they forbid what Allah and His Messenger have forbidden, nor do they practise the true religion (instead, they have breached the Treaty of Medina by committing an act of treason against the state), until they pay the indemnity tax (al-Jizya) with a willing hand*, and they submit to the command of the state.** [* Jizya legally refers to an indemnity tax received from non-Muslim combatants who surrendered to the Muslim State through a treaty, demanding their security, indemnity from military services and exemption from prescribed alms, payable to the state. These non-Muslims were not citizens of the state; instead, they were security seekers after surrendering from the battlefield. They used to contribute tax to military defence for their exemption from army services, whereas, in contrast, Muslim citizens would offer their personal services for the protection of the state whenever invaded by their enemies. The Jizya tax would also exempt those non-Muslims from Zakat and Kharaj taxes (trade and agricultural taxes), which were obligatory for Muslims. This particular category of non-Muslims, being non-citizens, were known as ‘Treaty People’ (Ahl al-Dhimma) or ‘Covenant Holders’. The Islamic State would provide them with all rights of security, equality and freedom against the tax, known as Jizya, which means reward or compensation. Women, children, the elderly, the clergy, the poor, the sick and those unable to work were exempt from this tax. Those who opted to join the army were also exempt. Instead, the poor among them were supported financially by the state. The Muslim rulers would refund the Jizya, if they failed to protect their lives or possessions. This kind of taxation system has existed since ancient times; the Old Testament and the New Testament in the Bible provide several references regarding this practice. Islam did not introduce it; instead, Islam introduced many humanitarian reforms in this old existing tradition among the nations. This term does not entail disgrace, inequality or humiliation of non-Muslims, nor does it uniformly apply to any non-Muslim citizen of a Muslim state. ** This verse refers to a retributive or punitive action taken against certain tribes, such as Qurayza and al-Nadir, who, in violation of the terms of alliance and the constitution, had supported the Meccan armies during their invasions of Medina, particularly in the battle of the confederates (al-Ahzab) by mobilizing them and providing them arms, ammunition and strategic information. Such punitive action against any party who breached the peace treaty and acted treacherously was repeatedly demanded in various articles of the constitution of Medina, jointly signed by the Jews and the Muslims years before.]

And the Jews say: ‘‘Uzayr (Ezra) is the son of Allah,’ and the Christians say: ‘The Messiah is the son of Allah.’ That is the utterance of their mouths. They resemble the words of those who disbelieved before. May Allah destroy them! How deluded they are!

They have taken their rabbis and monks as lords besides Allah and also the Messiah, son of Maryam (Mary), though they were commanded to worship only One God. There is no god except Him. His Glory is far above any partners they ascribe (to Him).
They wish to extinguish the Light of Allah with their mouths, and Allah refuses but to bring His Light to its fullness, though the disbelievers dislike it.
He is the One Who has sent His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) with guidance and the religion of truth so that He may elevate it over all religions, though the polytheists dislike it.

O believers! Indeed, many of the rabbis and monks falsely consume the wealth of people and bar (them) from the way of Allah. As for those who treasure up gold and silver and do not spend it for the cause of Allah, inform them of a painful punishment.

The day when it (i.e. their treasure) shall be heated in the Fire of Hell, their foreheads, their sides, and their backs shall be branded with it: ‘This is what you treasured up for yourselves. Now taste what you have been treasuring.’

Indeed, the number of months, according to Allah’s decree, is twelve months. It is (laid down) in Allah’s Book since the day He created the heavens and the earth. Out of them, four (Dhu al-Qa‘da, Dhu al-Hijja, Muharram and Rajab) are sacred. That is the upright calculation. So do not wrong yourself during these months (by getting involved in warfare). And fight all the (combatant) polytheists (in case they attack you) as they fight you all together. And know that Allah is with the God-conscious.

Indeed, postponing (the sacred months to continue fighting) is an increase in disbelief, by which the disbelievers are (further) led astray. They make it lawful one year, and forbid it another year, to reconcile (outwardly) with the number Allah has made inviolable. But in doing so, they permit what Allah has forbidden. Their evil deeds have been made fair-seeming to them. And Allah does not guide the disbelieving people.

O believers! What excuse do you have when it is said to you, ‘Go forth in the way of Allah’, and you sink heavily to the ground? Are you so pleased with the life of this world instead of the Hereafter? Yet the provision of the life of this world, compared with the Hereafter, is insignificant.
If you do not move forth, He will punish you with a painful torment and replace you with other people, and you will not harm Him in the least. And Allah is All-Capable of everything.

(O people!) If you do not help him (i.e. the Esteemed Prophet), Allah has already helped him when the disbelievers drove him out (of Mecca, and he was) the second of the two (emigrants). When the two of them (i.e. the Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) and Abu Bakr) were in the cave (of Mount Thawr), he (the Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him) said to his companion (Abu Bakr): ‘Do not worry, surely Allah is with us.’ Then Allah sent down His tranquillity upon him and strengthened him with armies (of angels), which you could not see, and He made the word of the disbelievers the lowest. And the word of Allah is the highest, and Allah is All-Mighty, All-Wise.
Go forth, lightly or heavily, and strive with your possessions and your persons for the cause of Allah. That is better for you if you know.

Were it an accessible gain or a short journey, they would certainly have followed you, but the distance seemed too far for them. Yet they will swear by Allah: ‘If we had been able to, we would surely have gone forth with you.’ They are (just) ruining themselves. Allah knows that they are indeed liars.

May Allah excuse you (O Esteemed Prophet)! Why did you grant them leave (to stay behind from the expedition to Tabuk) before it became clear to you who spoke the truth, and you had (also) ascertained the liars?
Those who believe in Allah and the Last Day will not ask you for exemption from striving with their possessions and their persons. And Allah is All-Knowing of the God-conscious.
Only those ask you (for exemption) who do not believe in Allah and the Last Day and whose hearts are in doubt. So they keep wavering in their doubt.

And if they (i.e. the hypocrites) had (really) desired to go forth, they would have surely made some preparations for it. But Allah did not like their going forth (for the expedition). So, He held them back, and it was said (to them): ‘Stay back with those who stay back (i.e. women, children and the infirm).’

Had they gone forth with you (O believers), they would have only added to your trouble. And they would have surely spread rumours in your midst, seeking to cause friction and confusion between you. And among you are some who listen for them (to spy on you). And Allah is All-Knowing of the wrongdoers.

(O Esteemed Prophet!) Indeed, they (the hypocrites) had sought to cause turbulence earlier and turn things upside down for you until the truth came, and the command of Allah prevailed (manifestly), though they disliked (it).
Among them, there are some who say: ‘Grant me leave (to stay at home) and do not put me to trial.’ They have already fallen into trial. And indeed, Hell surrounds the disbelievers.
If good fortune comes to you, it troubles them, but if calamity befalls you, they say: ‘Indeed, we had taken our precautions beforehand.’ And they turn away, rejoicing.
Say: ‘Nothing will ever befall us except what Allah has decreed for us. He is our Master, and let the believers put their trust in Allah.’

Say (to the hypocrites): ‘Do you anticipate for us except one of the two most wonderful things (i.e. victory or martyrdom)? But we anticipate for you that Allah shall afflict you with a punishment from Himself or through our hands. So wait! We (too) are waiting along with you.’
Say: ‘Spend willingly or unwillingly; it shall never be accepted from you, for you have indeed been transgressing people.’

Nothing prevents their charities from being accepted, except that they disbelieve in Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him), and do not perform prayer but lazily (and unwillingly). And they do not spend (in charity) but reluctantly.
So let not their wealth nor their children impress you. Allah only wants to punish them through these things in this worldly life, and their souls will depart while they are disbelievers.
(O believers!) They swear by Allah that they are part of you, but they are not part of you. Instead, they are cowardly people.
If they could find a refuge, or a cave, or a hole to enter (into the earth), they would turn towards it with great haste.
Some of them blame you regarding (the distribution of) charitable offerings. If they are given some of it, they are pleased, but if they are not given some of it, they are enraged.

(It would have been better) if they had been pleased with what Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) gave them, and had said: ‘Allah is sufficient for us; Allah will give us out of His grace, and so will His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him). Indeed, to Allah we turn in hope.’

The prescribed charities (i.e. zakat) are only for the poor, the needy, the officials employed to collect them, and those whose hearts are (to be) reconciled, and for (the freedom of) the captives, and for the debtors, and (those) in the way of Allah and for the travellers. (This is) an obligation from Allah, and Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

Among them are those who hurt the Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him) and say: ‘He is an ear (i.e. believes everything he hears).’ Say: ‘(He is) an ear that is good for you; he believes in Allah, trusts the believers and is a mercy for those who believe among you. As for those who hurt the Messenger of Allah, they shall have a painful punishment.’

They (the hypocrites) swear to you by Allah that they might please you. But Allah, as well as His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him), has a greater right that they should please Him if they are (true) believers.
Do they not know that whoever opposes Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him), surely for him shall be the Fire of Hell, to remain there forever? That is the ultimate disgrace.
The hypocrites fear that a (Qur’anic) Chapter will be revealed against them, informing them about what is in their hearts. Say: ‘Keep mocking. Allah will indeed disclose what you are fearing.’
If you ask them, they will certainly say: ‘We were just chatting and joking around.’ Say: ‘Were you mocking Allah, His revelations and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him)?
Do not make excuses. You have disbelieved after you had believed.’ If We pardon a group of you, We shall punish another group for they have been guilty.

The hypocrites, both men and women, are all the same: they enjoin evil, forbid good and keep their hands shut (i.e. they remain reluctant to spend in the way of Allah). They have forgotten Allah, so He has forgotten them. The hypocrites are indeed the disobedient ones.

Allah has promised the hypocrites, both men and women, and the disbelievers, the Fire of Hell, to remain in it forever. It is enough for them; Allah has cursed them, and there shall be a lasting punishment for them.

(O hypocrites! You are) like those (disbelievers) before you. They were stronger than you in might and more abundant in wealth and children. So they enjoyed their share (of worldly pleasures). You, too, have enjoyed your share, just like those (disbelievers) before you enjoyed their share. And you have indulged (impiously) in idle talk as they indulged. They are the ones whose deeds have become void in this world and the Hereafter. And it is they who are the losers.

Has the news of those before them not come to them—the people of Nuh (Noah), ‘Ad and Thamud, and the people of Ibrahim (Abraham) and the inhabitants of Madyan (Midian) and the overthrown towns? Their messengers came to them with clear proofs (but they rejected them). Allah would not have wronged them, but they had been unjust to themselves.

The believing men and the believing women are supporters of one another, enjoining good and forbidding evil, establishing the prayer, giving the alms due and obeying Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him). It is they upon whom Allah will have mercy. Indeed, Allah is All-Mighty, All-Wise.

Allah has promised the believing men and the believing women Gardens with rivers flowing beneath them to stay there forever and pleasant dwellings in the Gardens of Eternity. And the greatest of all is Allah’s good pleasure. That is the supreme success.
O Prophet! Strive hard against the disbelievers and the hypocrites and be unyielding to them (against their aggressive designs). And their abode shall be Hell, and it is an evil destination.

They (the hypocrites) swear by Allah that they did not say (anything blasphemous). But surely they have uttered the word of disbelief and renounced faith after submitting (to Allah). They had planned what they could not achieve, and they became vengeful only because Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) had enriched them out of His Grace. Yet, if they repent, it would be better for them. But if they turn away, Allah will punish them with a painful torment in this world and the Hereafter. And they shall have on the earth neither any protector nor any helper.
Among them are those who pledged to Allah (saying): ‘If He gives us out of His grace, we will surely spend in charity (i.e. the alms due), and we will surely be among the righteous.’
But when He gave them (wealth) out of His grace, they were miserly with it and turned away, being disregardful (of their pledge).
So He caused hypocrisy to develop in their hearts (due to their miserliness) until the day when they would meet Him, as they broke their promise to Allah and because they used to tell lies.
Do they not realise that Allah knows their hidden (thoughts) and their secret talks, and that Allah is the Most Knowing of all unseen things?

As for those (hypocrites) who slander the believers regarding charities, those who donate freely and those who can give only a little with great effort, they ridicule such people. Allah shall punish them for their ridiculing. And there shall be a painful punishment for them.

(It does not matter) whether you seek forgiveness for them or do not seek forgiveness for them, even if you seek forgiveness for them seventy times (out of your kindness and mercy), Allah shall never forgive them. That is because they rejected Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him). And Allah does not guide the transgressing people.

(These hypocrites are) those who stayed behind (from participating in the expedition to Tabuk with the Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him) and were happy for their sitting back, opposing (the command of) Allah’s Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him), and they hated to strive with their possessions and their persons for the cause of Allah. And they said: ‘Do not go out (with the Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him) in this heat.’ Say: ‘The Fire of Hell is much hotter if they could understand.’
So let them laugh a little; they will weep much as a reward for what they used to earn.

If Allah brings you back (from the battlefront of Tabuk) to a group of them and they seek your permission to go forth (for any further expeditions), say (to them): ‘By no means shall you ever go forth with me, and by no means shall you ever fight an enemy with me. You were pleased to sit back the first time (i.e. the most crucial moment); therefore, sit (this time again) with those who remain behind.’
And do not ever offer the (funeral) prayers for any of their dead, nor stand by their grave. Indeed, they defied Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) and died rebellious.
And let neither their possessions nor their children impress you. Allah desires only to punish them through these (things) in this world and that their souls may depart while they are disbelievers.

And when a (Qur’anic) Chapter is revealed, (saying:) ‘Believe in Allah and struggle along with His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him),’ the affluent among them ask you for leave and say: ‘Let us remain with those who are sitting back (among the women and children).’
They are pleased to be among those who stay behind, and their hearts have been sealed. So they do not understand.

But the Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) and the believers with him strive hard with their possessions and their persons. They are the ones who shall have all the blessings, and it is they who shall be prosperous.
Allah has prepared for them Gardens with rivers flowing beneath them; they will remain in them forever. That is the supreme success.

Some of the Bedouins (also) came to make excuses, asking to be granted an exemption for them, while those who lied to Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) stayed back. Soon, a painful punishment shall befall the disbelievers among them.

There is no blame on the weak, nor on the sick, nor on those who do not find anything to spend, so long as they are sincere to Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him). There is no reason to blame the virtuous. And Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.

Nor (is there any blame) on those who came to you (O Prophet) to provide them with riding animals (so that they may join you in the expedition). (But) you said: ‘I cannot find any mount for you.’ So they turned back while their eyes were overflowing with tears of sorrow because they did not find any means to spend.
The blame is only on those who though they are wealthy. They are pleased to remain with those who stay behind. Allah has sealed their hearts, so they do not understand.

(O believers!) They will make excuses to you when you return to them (from the expedition of Tabuk). Say: ‘Do not make excuses; we shall never believe you. Allah has already informed us about your (state of) affairs. Allah and His Messenger will watch your actions now. Then, you will be returned to the Knower of the Unseen and the seen. And He will inform you concerning what you used to do.’

They will swear to you by Allah when you return to them so that you may turn aside from them (by accepting their excuses). So leave them alone. Surely, they are unclean (by their intentions and actions). And their abode shall be Hell, a recompense for what they have been earning.
(O believers!) They will swear to you so that you may be pleased with them. But (even) if you are pleased with them, Allah shall not be pleased with the transgressing people.

The Bedouins are more stubborn in disbelief and hypocrisy and the least likely to know the limits (of the commandments) that Allah has revealed to His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him), and Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

(O believers!) And among the Bedouins are those who regard what they spend (in charity) as a fine, and they wait for a change in fortune for you. Upon them shall be an evil change of fortune. And Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

Yet among the Bedouins are (also) those who believe in Allah and the Last Day. And they regard what they spend as (a means to attain) nearness to Allah and the blessings of the Messenger. Behold! It shall surely be (a means of attaining) nearness for them. Allah will make them enter into His mercy. Indeed, Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.

As for the foremost, the ones who are the first among the Emigrants (from Mecca) and the Helpers (from Medina), and those who followed them in (acts of) excellence, Allah is well pleased with them, and they are (all) well pleased with Him. And He has prepared for them Gardens with rivers flowing beneath them, to abide in them forever. That is the supreme success.

(O believers!) Some of the Bedouins who dwell (in the deserts) around you are hypocrites, and some of the town people (of Medina) have grown bold in hypocrisy. You do not know them, but We know them well. We shall punish them twice. Then, they will be returned to (face) a grievous punishment (in the Hereafter).
(There are) others who have confessed their sins, having mixed up righteous deeds with others that are evil. May be Allah will accept their repentance. Indeed, Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.

(O Prophet,) take charity from their possessions, cleansing them and purifying them thereby, and bless them with your prayer. Indeed, your blessing will be a comfort for them. And Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.
Do they not know that Allah is the One Who accepts repentance from His servants and receives (their) charities and that Allah is the One Who is the Most-Clement, the All-Merciful?

And say: ‘Perform your deeds (as you wish). Allah will see your deeds, as will the Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) and the believers. And you will be brought back to the Knower of the Unseen and the seen. Then He will inform you of what you used to do.’
And (there are) others who are waiting (in suspense) for the command of Allah: either He will punish them, or He will turn to them mercifully. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

And (among the hypocrites are also) those who built a mosque to cause harm, (to promote) disbelief, (to create) a divide between the believers and as a base for those who have waged war against Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him). They will surely swear: ‘We intended nothing but good.’ And Allah testifies that they are indeed liars.

(O Prophet!) Do not ever stand (to pray) therein. Truly, the mosque founded upon God-consciousness from the first day is worthier of your standing in it (for prayer). There are people in it who love to purify themselves. And Allah loves those who purify themselves.

Is the one who lays the foundation of his building upon God-consciousness and (the pursuit of His) good pleasure better, or the one who lays the foundation of his building upon the edge of a collapsing bank, which collapses with him into the Fire of Hell? And Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.
The building they have built (in the name of a mosque) will always be a source of doubt (and hypocrisy) in their hearts till their hearts are torn apart. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

Indeed, Allah has purchased from the believers their lives and their possessions in exchange for Paradise to be theirs. They fight (against the aggressors) in the way of Allah, slaying (during the war) and being slain. A true promise binding upon Him in the Torah and the Gospel (the Injil) and the Qur’an. And who is truer to his promise than Allah? So rejoice over the bargain you have made with Him. Indeed, that is the supreme success.

(The believers are) those who repent, who worship, who praise (Him), who fast (or are the travellers upon Allah’s path), who bow, who prostrate (in prayer), who enjoin good, who forbid evil and who observe the limits set by Allah. And give glad tidings to such believers.

It is not fitting for the Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him) and the believers to ask pardon for the polytheists, even though they are near relatives after it has become clear to them that they shall be inhabitants of the Hellfire.

And Ibrahim’s (Abraham’s) asking pardon for his (fostering) father was owing only to a promise he had made to him. But when it became clear to him that he was an enemy of Allah, he acquitted himself from him (and never prayed for him afterwards). Indeed, Ibrahim (Abraham) was very compassionate and forbearing.
Allah would never leave a people to stray after He has guided them until He makes them clear what they should be mindful of. Indeed, Allah is All-Knowing of everything.

Indeed, to Allah belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. He gives life and causes death. And besides Allah, you have neither guardian nor helper (who can protect you against Allah’s command).

Indeed, Allah turned mercifully towards the Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him), the Emigrants and the Helpers, who followed him in the hour of adversity (at the expedition of Tabuk), after (the situation emerged wherein) the hearts of a group of them had almost wavered. Then He turned towards them in mercy. Indeed, He is Most Compassionate (and) All-Merciful to them.

And (Allah also turned in mercy) to the three who had remained behind, when the earth, despite its vastness, became narrow upon them, and their souls (also) closed in upon them, and they realised that there was no refuge from Allah except in Him. Then He turned to them in mercy that they might turn (to Him) in repentance. Indeed, Allah is Most-Clement, All-Merciful.
O believers! Be conscious of Allah and stay with the Truthful.

It was not (suitable) for the people of Medina and the Bedouins around them to hold back from the Messenger of Allah (blessings and peace be upon him) by failing to accompany him during his campaigns and expeditions) and prefer their own lives to his life. That is because no thirst, fatigue or hunger afflicts them in the way of Allah, nor do they take any step enraging the (transgressing) disbelievers, or gain any advantage over an enemy, but a righteous deed is written on its account to their credit. Indeed, Allah does not waste the reward of the excellently virtuous.
