Their messengers said to them: ‘We may be human beings like yourselves, but Allah bestows (His) grace upon whomever He pleases among His servants. And it is not for us to bring you authority except by Allah’s permission. And in Allah, let the believers put their trust.

Why should We not trust in Allah, seeing that He has guided us in our ways? And surely, We will patiently endure whatever harm you inflict upon us. And in Allah let all the trusting (people) put their trust.’
The disbelievers said to their messengers: ‘We shall surely expel you from our land, or you shall revert to our creed.’ So their Lord revealed to them: ‘We shall surely destroy the wrongdoers,
and We shall surely settle you in the land after them. This (promise) is for whoever fears standing in My presence and fears My warning (of torment).’
And they (i.e. the messengers) asked for victory (against the disbelievers), and every stubborn tyrant has failed,
with Hell lying ahead of him, (where) he shall be given oozing pus to drink.
He will try to gulp it down but can hardly swallow it. Death will encroach on him from every side, but he will not die. And there is (yet) more intense punishment ahead of him.

The example of those who disbelieve in their Lord: Their deeds are like ashes over which the wind blows intensely on a stormy day (causing them to scatter). They will have no control over anything they have earned. That is the farthest error.
Do you not see that Allah created the heavens and the earth with a truthful purpose? If He wills, He can remove you (all) and bring a new creation,
and that is not a great matter for Allah.

They will appear before Allah together. Then the weak (followers) will say to the arrogant (leaders): ‘Indeed we were your followers. So will you rescue us from Allah’s punishment in any way?’ They will say: ‘If Allah had guided us, we too would have guided you. It is the same to us whether we become impatient or remain patient; there is no escape for us.’

And Satan will say (to his followers), once the judgement is passed: ‘Indeed, Allah promised you (and it was) a true promise, whereas I (too) made you a promise, but I failed you. And I had no authority over you, except that I called you (towards falsehood), and you responded to me (for your own interest). So, do not blame me; blame yourselves. I cannot respond to your distress calls, nor can you respond to my distress calls. Surely, I deny your taking me as Allah’s partner previously. As for the wrongdoers, surely there shall be a painful punishment for them.

Those who believe and perform righteous deeds shall be made to enter Gardens with rivers flowing beneath them. They will remain in them forever by the leave of their Lord. Their greeting therein will be: ‘Peace!’
Have you not considered how Allah makes comparisons? A good word is like a good tree: its roots are firm (in the earth), and its branches are (high) in the sky.
It gives its fruit every season by the leave of its Lord. And Allah makes such comparisons for people so that they may be mindful of advice.
And the example of a bad word is that of a rotten tree: uprooted from the earth’s surface, having no stability.
Allah reassures those who believe with the firm word (of truth) in the life of this world and the Hereafter, and Allah leaves the wrongdoers to stray. And Allah does whatever He pleases.
Have you not seen those who have exchanged the blessing of Allah with disbelief (or ingratitude) and caused their people to dwell in the abode of destruction?
(That is) Hell, where they shall enter to burn, and it is an evil abode.
And they have set up equals to Allah, to lead (people) astray from His way. Say: ‘Enjoy yourselves (for a while), for indeed your destination is towards the Fire.’

Tell My believing servants to establish the prayer and to spend (in charity) out of what We have provided them, secretly and openly, before there comes a day wherein there will be neither any bargain nor (any worldly) friendship.

Allah is (the One) Who created the heavens and the earth, sent down water from the clouds, and brought forth crops for your sustenance with it. And He has subjected the ships for your service so that they may sail through the sea by His command. And He has (also) subjected the rivers for you.
And He has subjected the sun and the moon for you, constantly rotating (in their respective orbits). And He has (also) subjected the night and the day for you.

And He has provided you with everything you demanded from Him (for your needs). If you count the blessings of Allah, you will not be able to number them. Indeed, human being is most unjust (and) ungrateful.
And (recall) when Ibrahim (Abraham) said: ‘O my Lord, make this city (Mecca) a secure sanctuary, and save me and my children from worshipping idols.
My Lord! These (idols) have indeed led many people astray! So whoever follows me certainly belongs to me, but as for anyone who disobeys me, You are surely Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.

Our Lord! I have settled (some) of my children (i.e. Isma‘il [Ishmael]) in a barren valley (of Mecca) near Your Sacred House, our Lord, that they may establish the prayer. So cause the hearts of (the believing) people to incline towards them, and provide them with fruits, that they may be grateful.
Our Lord! Indeed, You know whatever we conceal and whatever we reveal. And nothing is hidden from Allah on the earth or in the sky.
All praise belongs to Allah Who, despite (my) old age, bestowed upon me Isma‘il (Ishmael) and Ishaq (Isaac). Indeed, my Lord is the Hearer of supplications.
My Lord! Make me a maintainer of the prayer and among my offspring (too). Our Lord, accept my supplication.
Our Lord! Forgive me, my parents and the believers on the Day when the reckoning is held.’
And do not think Allah to be unaware of what the wrongdoers are doing. He is only delaying them for a Day when (their) eyes will stare (in horror),
running forward with their heads upraised (and necks outstretched). Their glance will not return to them, and their hearts will be vacant (having been drained of strength and hope).

And warn the people of the Day when the punishment shall overtake them, then the wrongdoers will say: ‘Our Lord, grant us respite for a little while. We will respond to Your call and follow the messengers.’ (They will be told:) ‘Did you not use to swear before that there would be no end for you (in the worldly life)?’
You settled in the dwellings of those who had wronged themselves (before). And it was made clear to you how We had dealt with them, and We have (also) cited examples for you.
Indeed, they devised their schemes, but (the undoing of) their schemes lies with Allah, even if their schemes were such to dislodge the mountains.
Therefore, do not think Allah would fail to keep His promise to His messengers. Indeed, Allah is All-Mighty, All-Capable of Retribution.
(It will be) on the Day when the earth will be transformed into a different earth and the heavens (as well). And all will appear before Allah, the One, the Supreme.
And you will see the guilty on that Day, bound together in chains,
their garments (will be) made of sulphur (or coal-tar pitch), and the Fire will cover their faces.
(All will be judged,) so that Allah may recompense every soul for what it has earned. Indeed, Allah is Swift in Reckoning.
This (Qur’an) is a proclamation for humankind so that they may be warned by it and know that He is indeed the One God, and that the possessors of intellect may accept admonition.
Alif, Lam, Ra. These are the Verses of the Book and a Manifest Qur’an.
The disbelievers will often wish (in the Hereafter) that they had been Muslims.
Leave them to eat and enjoy themselves, and let false hope distract them, for soon they will know.
We have never destroyed any town, but it had a known decree.
No nation can bring their time forward, nor can they delay it.
And they say: ‘O you to whom the Reminder has been revealed! You are indeed insane.
Why do you not bring us the angels if you are truthful?’
(Tell them:) We do not send down the angels, but with (the command of execution of) the judgement. And then they will not be granted any respite.
Indeed, We have revealed the Reminder, and surely, We are its Protectors.
We indeed sent (messengers) before you to former communities.
And never did a messenger come to them, but they ridiculed him.
That is how We pave the way for mockery and disbelief to enter the hearts of the guilty.
They do not believe in this (Qur’an), although the precedent of the ancients (destroyed before them) has already passed.
If We were to open a gateway to heaven for them so that they could continually ascend through it,
they would (still) say: ‘Indeed, our eyes are hallucinated; rather, we are a bewitched people!’
Indeed, We have set up constellations (of stars) in the sky (in the form of galaxies) and adorned them for the onlookers.
And We have protected them from every outcast Satan (i.e. every destructive force),
except for the one who steals a hearing, then a discernible flame (such as a shooting star or meteor) follows him.
As for the earth, We have spread it out and set firm mountains in it. And We caused to grow in it every well-balanced thing.
And We have placed in it (various) means of livelihood for you and all those (creatures) to whom you do not provide sustenance.
And there is not a thing (on this earth), but its means of sustenance are with Us, and We do not send it down except in a precise measure.
And We send the fertilising winds, then send down water from the clouds, so that We provide it for you to drink. And you are not the keepers of its resources.
Indeed, it is We Who give life and cause death, and We are the Inheritors (i.e. the Owners of everything).
And We indeed know those of you who were predecessors, and We indeed know the successors.
Indeed, your Lord is the One Who will gather them (on the Day of Resurrection). Indeed, He is All-Wise, All-Knowing.
Indeed, We created human being from sounding dried clay, moulded from ageing, dark mud.
As for the jinn, We created them earlier from scorching smokeless fire.
And (remember) when your Lord said to the angels: ‘Indeed I am going to create a human from sounding dried clay, moulded from ageing, dark mud.
So when I have proportioned him and breathed My Spirit into him, then fall down in prostration before him.’
So the angels prostrated, all of them together,
but Iblis (did not). He refused to be with those who prostrated.
He (Allah) said: ‘O Iblis! What excuse do you have that you are not with those who have prostrated?’
He (Iblis) said: ‘I cannot be the one to prostrate before a human being whom You have created from dried clay, formed from ageing, dark mud.’
He (Allah) said: ‘Get out from here (i.e. Paradise). For surely you are accursed.
And, indeed, the curse shall be upon you till the Day of Judgement.’
He (Iblis) said: ‘My Lord! Grant me respite till the Day they are resurrected.’
He (Allah) said: ‘You are indeed among those granted respite,
till the Day of the known moment.’
He (Iblis) said: ‘My Lord! As You have caused me to go astray, I will surely glamorise (evil) for them on the earth, and I will surely lead them all astray,
except for those among them who are Your exclusively chosen servants.’
He (Allah) said: ‘This is a path (leading) straight to Me.
Surely you shall have no control over My servants—except those deviators who follow you.
Indeed, Hell shall be the promised place for them all.
It has seven gates; for each gate, there is a separate (allotted) portion of them.’
Surely, the God-conscious will be in (the middle of) gardens and springs.
(They will be told:) ‘Enter them in peace, being secure.’
And We shall remove whatever bitterness lies within their breasts; (they shall be sitting) as brothers, upon couches, facing one another.
No tiredness will touch them, nor will they (ever) be expelled from it.
Inform My servants that I am indeed the Most-Forgiving, the All-Merciful,
and that My punishment is indeed the most painful torment.
And inform them about the guests of Ibrahim (Abraham),
when they entered upon him and greeted (him with): ‘Peace!’ He (Ibrahim) said: ‘We are indeed afraid of you.’
They (the angels) said: ‘Do not be afraid. Indeed, we give you glad tidings of a knowledgeable son.’
He (Ibrahim) said: ‘Do you bring me glad tidings when old age has already overtaken me? So what glad tidings have you brought me?’
They said: ‘We have brought you glad tidings in truth, so do not be among the despairing.’
He (Ibrahim) said: ‘Who despairs of his Lord’s mercy, except those who are astray?’
He (Ibrahim) said: ‘Then what is your (next) mission, O messengers?’
They said: ‘We have been sent to a guilty people.
(They shall perish) except the family of Lut (Lot). We will indeed save them all,
except his wife; we have ordained, she will indeed be among those who remain behind (to perish).’
So when the messengers came to Lut’s family,
he (Lut) said: ‘Indeed, you are an unfamiliar people.’
They said: ‘Rather, we have come to you with (the punishment) that they used to doubt.
And we have come to you with (the judgement of) truth, and surely, we are truthful.
Set out with your family (and followers) during the darkness of the night, and you should follow behind them, and none of you should turn around. And keep proceeding as you are commanded.’
We decreed this command to him, that these (people of Sodom and Gomorrah) shall be uprooted by the morning.
And the people of the town came (to Lut’s house) rejoicing.
He said: ‘Surely, these are my guests, so do not embarrass me (regarding them).
And fear (the wrath of) Allah and do not shame me.’
They said: ‘(O Lut,) did we not forbid you from (protecting) all the people?’
He (Lut) said: ‘Here are my (people’s) daughters. If you desire to do something (then marry them, instead of committing indecency).’
(O Prophet!) By your life, they wandered blindly in their (lustful) drunkenness.
So the blast overtook them at sunrise.
Thus, We turned their town upside down and rained stones of baked clay upon them.
There are indeed signs in that (destruction) for those who can anticipate.
Indeed (the ruins of) these (destroyed towns) are still there on the highway.
Most surely, in this (account), there is a sign for the believers.
Indeed, the inhabitants of al-Ayka (the Woodland) were wrongdoers.
So We took vengeance upon them, and indeed (the ruins of) both (towns of Sodom and al-Ayka) are (visible) on an open highway.
Indeed, the inhabitants of al-Hijr (the Rocky Plain, also) denied the messengers.
And We had given them Our signs, but they turned away from them.
They used to carve out homes in the mountains, feeling secure.
So the blast overtook them in the morning.
And what they used to earn could not benefit them.
And We did not create the heavens, the earth and whatever is between them but with a true purpose. And the Hour is surely coming. So forbear with a graceful forbearance.
Indeed, your Lord is the All-Creator, the All-Knowing.
And indeed, We have given you the seven oft-repeated verses (i.e. Sura al-Fatiha) and the Glorious Qur’an.

Do not extend your glance towards (the worldly pleasures) that We have provided to some (disbelieving) groups of them, nor grieve for them. And lower your wing (of grace and affection) to the believers (to make them feel content).
And (O Prophet!) say: ‘Indeed, I am the manifest warner,
(carrying a warning) similar to what We sent to those who made divisions (in the Scriptures and the community),
who (now) reduce the Qur’an into fragments (by accepting some verses and rejecting others).’
So, by your Lord, We shall indeed question them all,
concerning what they used to do.
So proclaim what you have been commanded (to convey) and ignore the polytheists.
Indeed, We suffice you against those who ridicule (your message).
Those who set up another god besides Allah—they will soon come to know.
We certainly know that your heart is distressed by what they say.
So glorify your Lord with praise and be among those who prostrate,
and (continue to) worship your Lord until what is certain comes to you.
Allah’s judgement is coming! So do not seek to hasten it. Glory be to Him. He is exalted above any partners they ascribe (to Him).

He sends down the angels with the Spirit of His Command (i.e. revelation and Prophethood) on whom He pleases among His servants (saying): ‘Warn (the people) that there is no god except Me. So be conscious of Me.’
He created the heavens and the earth with a true purpose. He is above any partners they ascribe (to Him).

He created human being from a fertilised egg (i.e. a zygote*), yet he is an open opponent. [* In Arabic, the word ‘ al-nutfa ’ is mentioned, and it refers to a zygote, which is a mixture of male and female gametes. It is the new single cell formed after the union of a sperm and ovum.]
And He created cattle: you have warmth in them* and (other) benefits, and you get food from some of them. [* That is through the garments made from their wool and leather.]
And there is (an aesthetic) beauty in them for you when you drive (them) back from the pasture in the evening and when you drive (them) out in the morning to graze.
And they carry your heavy loads to lands you could not reach except by straining yourselves. Indeed, your Lord is Most Kind, All-Merciful.
And (He has created) horses, mules and donkeys for you to ride and as means of attraction. And He creates (many other animals) that you do not (yet) know.
And it is for Allah to show the straight path, and there are some deviating (ways). If He wished, He could have guided you all.
He is the One Who sends down water from the sky, from which you get some drink, and some of it (is for) plantation, upon which you graze your cattle.
He causes to grow for you thereby the crops, olives, palm trees, grapevines and fruits of all kinds. There is indeed a sign for people who reflect.
And He has subjected for you the night, the day, the sun and the moon. And the stars have (also) been subjected (for you) by His command. There are indeed signs in that for people who understand.
And whatever He has created for you on the earth, of different colours (and characteristics is also for your benefit). There is indeed a sign for people who accept admonition.

And He is the One Who has subjected the sea that you may eat fresh meat (i.e. seafood) out of it, and extract from it ornaments (i.e. gems and pearls) that you wear (as jewellery). And you see the ships penetrating through it, and (this is so) that you may seek His Grace and give thanks.
And He has cast firm mountains in the earth lest it shakes beneath you. And (He has made) the rivers and pathways, so you may be guided (to reach your destination).
And (there are) landmarks (to help navigate during the day), and (at night) they are guided by the stars.
So can the One Who creates be like the one who cannot create? Will you not then be mindful of advice?
And if you were to count the blessings of Allah, you would never be able to count them. Indeed, Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.
And Allah knows whatever you hide and whatever you disclose.
And those (idols) they worship besides Allah do not create anything and are themselves created.
(They are) dead, not alive, and they are not (even) aware of when they (i.e. their worshippers) will be resurrected.
Your God is (only) One God. Those who do not believe in the Hereafter—their hearts are deniers, and they are arrogant.
There is no doubt that Allah knows whatever they conceal and whatever they reveal. Indeed, He does not like the arrogant.
And when it is said to them: ‘What has your Lord revealed?’ They say: ‘Stories of the ancient people.’
(This is so) that, on the Day of Resurrection, they may bear their entire burdens, as well as the burdens of those whom they mislead without knowledge. Beware! Evil is what they bear!

Those before them had also schemed, but Allah demolished their constructions from the foundation. So the roof collapsed upon them from above, and the punishment overtook them from where they could not perceive.

Then, He will disgrace them on the Day of Resurrection and say: ‘Where are My (so-called) partners, on whose account you used to oppose (Me and the believers)?’ Those given knowledge will say: ‘Indeed, today, the disgrace and the distress are upon the disbelievers.’

Those whose souls the angels take away while they are wronging themselves will then offer submission (and say): ‘We were not committing any evil (in our life)!’ ‘Yes! Indeed Allah is All-Knowing of what you were doing!
So, enter the gates of Hell to remain in it forever. So surely, evil is the (final) abode of the arrogant.’

And it will be said to those who were God-conscious: ‘What did your Lord send down?’ They will say: ‘Goodness.’ For those who were excellent in goodness in this world, there will be a beautiful reward, and the abode of the Hereafter (for them) will be far better. The abode of the God-conscious is surely wonderful.
(These are) the Gardens of Eternity, which they will enter, with rivers flowing beneath them. There, they shall have whatever they desire. That is how Allah rewards those who are God-conscious.
Those whose souls the angels take away while they are in a state of (goodness and) purity, they will say (to them): ‘Peace be upon you! Enter Paradise for what you have been doing.’
Are they only waiting for the angels to come upon them, or should your Lord’s command come? Those who were before them did the same. Allah did not wrong them, but they used to wrong themselves.
So the evil (consequences) of what they had earned afflicted them. And the very thing they used to ridicule surrounded them (with death).

And those who ascribe partners to Allah say: ‘If Allah had wished, we would not have worshipped anything besides Him—not us, nor our fathers—nor would we have forbidden anything without (permission from) Him.’ Those who were before them did the same. Is anything incumbent upon the messengers except the clear proclamation?

We raised a messenger in every nation (to preach the message): ‘Worship Allah and keep away from false gods.’ So there were some of them whom Allah guided, and there were others among them who were deserving of error. So, travel through the land and observe how was the end of the deniers!
Though you are eager for their guidance, Allah does not guide those who mislead (others). And they will have no helpers.
And they swear by Allah with their most solemn oaths that Allah will not resurrect those who die. Yes, indeed! It is a promise binding upon Him, but most people do not know.
(It is) so that He may clarify to them what they differ about and that the disbelievers may know they were liars.
Our word to a thing, when We intend it (to come into existence), is only to say to it: ‘Be!’ and it is.

And those who migrated for the sake of Allah after being persecuted, We will indeed settle them in a good place in this world. And surely the reward of the Hereafter is much greater, if only they knew.
They are the ones who are patient and put their trust in their Lord!
And We did not send (any messenger) before you, except men to whom We had revealed. Ask the People of the Reminder if you do not know.

(We sent them) with clear proofs and Scriptures. And (Esteemed Prophet,) We have revealed the Reminder (i.e. the Qur’an) to you so that you may clearly explain to the people what has been sent down to them; perhaps they may reflect.
Do those who have plotted evil deeds feel secure that Allah will not cause the earth to swallow them, or the punishment will not overtake them (suddenly) from where they do not perceive?
Or that He will not seize them during their journeys, then they will not be able to escape?
Or that He will not seize them with gradual attrition (striking them with fear)? Indeed, your Lord is Most-Kind, All-Merciful.
Have they not considered that whatever Allah has created casts its shadow to the right and the left, prostrating to Allah in utter humility?
To Allah prostrates whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth from the living creatures, as do the angels, who are not arrogant.
They fear their Lord above them and do what they are commanded.
And Allah has said: ‘Do not take two gods. Truly, He is only One God. So you should be afraid of Me alone.’
To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth. And to Him belongs the everlasting devotion. Will you then be conscious of something other than Allah?
And whatever blessing you possess comes from Allah. Then, whenever hardship afflicts you, to Him (alone), you cry (for help).
Yet, when He removes the hardship from you, a group of you attribute partners to your Lord,
showing ingratitude for what We have given them. So enjoy for a while, for you will soon know.
And they dedicate to those (idols) whom they do not know a share of what We have provided them. By Allah! You will indeed be questioned about (the lies) you used to fabricate.
And they attribute daughters to Allah—Glory be to Him—while for themselves is what they desire (i.e. sons).
And whenever one of them is given the news of (the birth of) a baby girl, his face darkens as he suppresses his rage.
He hides from the people because of the evil of the news he has been given. Shall he keep it in disgrace or bury it (alive) in the dust? Indeed, evil is the judgement they make!
For those who do not believe in the Hereafter, there is an evil example. And the finest exalted example belongs only to Allah. And He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.

If Allah were to punish people (immediately) for their wrongdoing, He would not leave a single moving creature on the earth, but He would reprieve them till an appointed time. So, when their time arrives, they cannot defer (it) by a moment, nor can they advance (it).

And they attribute to Allah what they dislike (for themselves), and their tongues utter the lie that the best (things) belong to them. Undoubtedly, for them shall be the Fire, and they will be hastened into it (to be abandoned therein).

By Allah! We have certainly sent (messengers) to (many) nations before you. But Satan made their deeds fair-seeming to them. So, he is their guardian today. And there is a painful punishment for them.
And We did not reveal the Book to you, except that you might clarify to them what they differ on, and as a guidance and a mercy for a people who believe.
Allah sends down water from the sky, and revives the earth with it after its death. There is indeed a sign in that for people who listen.

And surely in the cattle, there is a lesson for you: We give you to drink from what is in their bellies—produced through compounding some intestinal content and blood—pure milk that is pleasant and nourishing for the drinkers.
And from the fruits of date palms and grapes, you derive sweet juice (or vinegar) and other pleasant foods. There is indeed a sign in that for people who understand.
Your Lord inspired the bees (saying:) ‘Make hives in the mountains, the trees and whatever they build.

Then eat from (the flowers of) all kinds of fruit, and follow the ways made easy (for you) by your Lord.’ Then comes out from their bellies a liquid (i.e. honey) of different colours, in which there is a cure for the people. Therein is indeed a sign for people who reflect.

And Allah has created you, then He causes you to die. And some among you are brought back to the frail old age, such that he may know nothing after (having possessed) some knowledge. Indeed, Allah is All-Knowing, All-Capable.

Allah has given some of you an advantage over others in provision. Those who have been given an advantage are unwilling to pass their provision to the slaves they possess so that they become equal in this regard. So, do they reject Allah’s blessing (in this way)?

Allah made for you spouses from among yourselves and gave you sons and grandchildren from your spouses. And He provided you with all the good things. Will they still believe in falsehood and disregard Allah’s blessings?
And they worship besides Allah those (idols) which have no power to provide them anything from the heavens and the earth, nor are they capable (of doing such a thing).
So do not draw comparisons for Allah. Indeed, Allah knows, and you do not know.

Allah draws a parable: a servant enslaved having no power over anything, and the (other) one whom We have blessed with good sustenance from Ourselves and he spends out of it secretly and openly—can they be equal? All praise belongs to Allah. But most of them do not know.

And Allah draws (another) parable of two men: one of them is dumb, incapable of doing anything, and he is a burden to his master; wherever he sends him, he does not achieve any good. Can he be equal to someone who commands justice and is (steady) on a straight path?
And to Allah belongs the Unseen of the heavens and the earth. And the matter of the Last Hour is just like the blinking of an eye, or even quicker. Indeed, Allah is All-Capable of everything.
And Allah has brought you out from the wombs of your mothers (in such a state) that you did not know anything. And He gave you hearing, sight and hearts, so that you may give thanks.
Have they not seen the birds that are made to fly through the air in the sky? Nothing sustains them (in the air) but Allah. There are indeed signs in this (law of aerodynamics) for the believers.

And Allah has made for you a place of rest in your homes. He has made for you (portable) dwellings (i.e. tents) from the skins of cattle, which you find easy to carry on the day of your travelling and on the day of your camping. And from their wool, and their fur and their hair, (He has given you) household stuff and other provisions for a time.

Allah has made shade for you from what He has created. And He has prepared for you the places of retreat and shelter in the mountains. And He has made for you the garments that protect you from heat and armours that protect you in your fighting. That is how Allah completes His blessings upon you, so that you may submit (to Him).
But (Esteemed Prophet), if they turn away, you only have to deliver the message in clear terms.
They recognise the blessing of Allah, yet they deny it. And most of them are disbelievers.
And on the Day when We shall raise a witness from every nation—those who disbelieve will not be permitted (to speak), nor will they be allowed to make amends(or excuses).
And when the wrongdoers shall see the punishment, it shall not be lightened for them, nor shall they be granted respite.

And when those who ascribe partners (to Allah) shall see their associate gods, they will say: ‘Our Lord! These are our associate gods whom we used to worship besides You.’ But they will reply to them: ‘Most surely, you are liars!’
And they will offer total submission to Allah on that Day, and what they used to fabricate will depart from them.
Those who disbelieve and bar others from the way of Allah, We shall add (more) punishment to their punishment for all the corruption they used to cause.

And on the Day when We shall raise in every nation a witness against them from among themselves, We shall bring you (Esteemed Prophet) as a witness against all of them. And We have revealed the Book to you explaining everything clearly, and as a guidance, mercy and glad tidings for those who submit themselves (to God).
Indeed, Allah enjoins justice, benevolence and giving to relatives, and He forbids indecency, wrongdoing and aggression. He advises you so that you may be mindful of advice.
And fulfil Allah’s covenant when you have pledged it. And do not break your oaths after affirming them and having made Allah a surety over yourselves. Indeed, Allah knows whatever you do.

Do not be like the woman who unravels her yarn, breaking it (to pieces) after (spinning it to) strength, by making your oaths a means of deception among yourselves, so one community may become more affluent (and wealthier) than another community. Allah only tests you by this. And He will most surely clarify for you what you used to differ about on the Day of Resurrection.

And if Allah had willed, He would certainly have made you a single nation, but He leaves to stray whom He wills and guides whom He wills. And you will surely be questioned concerning what you used to do.

And do not use your oaths as a means of deception among yourselves, lest any foot should slip after being firmly placed, and lest you should taste the penalty for barring others from the way of Allah. And there shall be a great punishment for you.
And do not sell Allah’s covenant in exchange for a small gain (i.e. some worldly benefit). Indeed, what is with Allah is better for you if (only) you know.
Whatever is with you will finish, but whatever is with Allah is everlasting. We will surely give the patient their reward by the best of what they used to do.
Whoever acts righteously, male or female, and is a (true) believer, We shall certainly revive them with a good life and pay them their reward according to the best of what they used to do.
So when you recite the Qur’an, seek refuge in Allah from the accursed Satan.
Indeed, he has no control over those who believe and put their (complete) trust in their Lord.
His control (or influence) is only over those who befriend him (or take him as their guardian) and those who make him a partner (to Allah).

And when We replace one verse with another verse—and Allah knows best what He reveals—they say: ‘You are only a fabricator.’ Instead, most of them do not know (the reason for the abrogation or the wisdom behind its substitution).
Say: ‘The Holy Spirit (Gabriel) has brought it (i.e. the Qur’an) down from your Lord with the truth to fortify those who believe, and as a guidance and glad tidings for those who submit.’
And indeed, We know that they say: ‘It is only a human being who teaches him.’ The language of the person whom they falsely refer to is non-Arabic, while this (Qur’an) is (in) clear Arabic.
As for those who do not believe in Allah’s revelations, Allah will not guide them, and there is a painful punishment for them.
Only those (people can) fabricate falsehood who do not believe in Allah’s revelations. And it is they who are liars.

Whoever renounces faith in Allah after (affirming) his belief, except for one who is forced while his heart is content with faith (as before). But whosoever opens his breast to disbelief, upon such (people) shall be Allah’s wrath, and there is a great punishment for them.
That is because they preferred this worldly life to the Hereafter. Indeed, Allah does not guide the disbelieving people.
They are the ones whose hearts, and their hearing, and their sight have been sealed by Allah. They are the ones who are neglectful (of their end in the Hereafter).
No doubt, they are the ones who, in the Hereafter, will be the losers.

Then, your Lord (will forgive) those who migrated after being persecuted (to renounce Islam) and struggled hard and remained patient. Your Lord, most surely, after that, is Most Forgiving, All-Merciful.
(Remember) the Day when every soul will come pleading for itself, and every soul will be recompensed (fully) for what it has done, and they will not be wronged.

And Allah gives the example of a town that was secure and peaceful. Its provision came in abundance from every quarter. But (the people of) this (town) became ungrateful to Allah’s blessings. So Allah made it taste the garment of hunger and fear for what its people had been doing.
A messenger had certainly come to them from among themselves, but they rejected him. So, the punishment overtook them while they were wrongdoers.
So eat from what Allah has provided you as lawful and pure (sustenance), and give thanks for Allah’s blessing, if it is He whom you worship.

He has only forbidden you carrion (i.e. the lawful animal that dies by itself without slaughtering), blood, the flesh of swine, and animals over which any name other than Allah’s has been invoked at the time of slaughter. But whoever is compelled by extreme necessity, not wilfully disobeying nor exceeding the limit, indeed, Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.
And do not say, asserting falsely with your tongues, ‘This is lawful, and this is forbidden’, to fabricate lies against Allah. Indeed, those who fabricate falsehood against Allah shall not prosper.
(They may have) a little enjoyment, but there will be a painful punishment for them (in the Hereafter).
And as for the Jews, We had prohibited for them what We have recounted to you earlier, and We did not wrong them, but they used to wrong themselves.
Then indeed, your Lord, concerning those who commit evil out of ignorance and then repent after that and reform (themselves)—most surely, after this, your Lord will be Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.
Indeed, Ibrahim (Abraham) was a nation (in himself and a model of excellence), devoutly obedient to Allah, most upright, and he was not from the polytheists.
(And he was) grateful (to Allah) for His blessings. He chose him (for His special favours) and guided him to the straight path.
And We blessed him with goodness in this world. And in the Hereafter, he shall indeed be among the righteous.
Then We revealed to you (saying): ‘Follow the faith of Ibrahim (Abraham), the upright, and he was not among the polytheists.’
And the Sabbath was ordained only for those who disputed about him (i.e. Abraham). And your Lord will surely judge between them on the Day of Resurrection, concerning what they used to differ.

Invite (people) to the way of your Lord with wisdom and courteous advice, and dispute with them (if needed) in the most gracious manner. Indeed, your Lord knows best those who have strayed from His way, and He knows best those who follow the right way.
(O believers!) If you have to retaliate, make your response equivalent to what you have been made to suffer. But if you adopt patience, that is surely better for the patient.
So remain patient, and your patience is only with Allah’s help. And do not grieve over them, nor be distressed for what they are plotting.
Indeed, Allah is with those who are God-conscious and those who are virtuously excellent.

Glory be to Him Who took His (esteemed) servant (Muhammad (blessings and peace be upon him) on a journey by night from the Sacred Mosque to the Farthest Mosque, whose surroundings We have blessed, so that We may show him some of Our signs. Indeed, He is the One Who is All-Hearing, All-Seeing.
We gave Musa (Moses) the Book and made it a guide for the Children of Israel (saying:) ‘Do not take any guardian apart from Me.’
(You are) descendants of those whom We carried (on the Ark) with Nuh (Noah). Indeed, he was an incredibly grateful servant.
We warned the Children of Israel in the Book: ‘You will certainly cause corruption on the earth twice, and most certainly you will become extremely arrogant, perpetrating great tyranny.’

So when the promise of the first of the two (prophecies) came to pass, We aroused against you Our servants, possessing great might. So they ravaged your homes (to kill you). And it was a warning to be fulfilled.
Then We gave you a turn against them, helped you with wealth and children, and made you greater in number.

(We said to them:) ‘If you act righteously, you will do good to your souls, but if you commit evil, it will be for them. So when (the occasion of) the second promise came (We raised another people against you), so that they may shame your faces (i.e. disgrace the notables of the Israelites) and enter the Temple (of Jerusalem) as they entered it the first time, and utterly destroy whatever they overtake.

Perhaps your Lord will have mercy upon you (after your repentance), but if you again turn (to rebellion), We (too) will return (with the punishment). And We have made Hell a prison for the disbelievers.’
Indeed, this Qur’an guides to what is most upright and gives glad tidings to the believers—who perform righteous deeds—that there is a great reward for them.
And as for those who do not believe in the Hereafter, We have prepared a painful punishment for them.
Human being prays for evil as he prays for good, and man is overly hasty.

We made the night and the day (as) two signs. Then We effaced the sign of the night (by darkening it) and made the sign of the day sight giving, that you may seek grace from your Lord, and that you may know the number of years and the calculation (of time). And We have elaborated everything distinctly.

We have attached (out of Our pre-eternal knowledge), for every human being, his record of deeds to his neck (to be performed by him with free choice). And We shall bring it out for him on the Day of Resurrection, as a wide-open book he will encounter.
(It will be said to him:) ‘Read your book (of deeds)! Today, your (own) soul is sufficient as a reckoner against yourself.’

Whoever accepts guidance is guided only for (the benefit of) his soul, and whoever goes astray goes astray only to its detriment. No bearer shall bear the burden of another. And We do not punish (a people) until We have sent a messenger (to them).

And whenever We intend to destroy a town, We send Our command to its elites (to reform themselves and obey God), but they (continue to) act disobediently therein. Thus, the Word comes due against that town, and We utterly destroy it.
And how many generations have We destroyed since Nuh (Noah)? And your Lord is sufficient as the One (Who is) All-Aware, All-Seeing of His servants’ sins.
Whoever desires this momentary life, We expedite for him in it whatever We wish for whomever We wish. Then We assign Hell for him, where he shall burn, being blameful and rejected.
And whoever desires the Hereafter and strives for it with an effort due to it, and he is a (truthful) believer—they are the ones whose striving shall be appreciated.

(O Prophet!) We extend (Our help) to all (of them)—these and those (i.e. the ambitious of this world and the aspiring of the Hereafter)—out of the bounty of your Lord. And the bounty of your Lord is not confined (to one group alone).
See how We have exalted some of them over others, and the Hereafter is surely much superior in degrees and far greater in excellence.
(O listener!) Do not set up another god with Allah, lest you sit down blameful, forsaken.

Your Lord has decreed that you shall worship none except Him and that you be kind and virtuous to parents. If either of them or both of them reach old age in your care, do not say to them (so much as) ‘Uff!’ (i.e. any word expressing annoyance, impatience or displeasure). And do not be harsh with them but speak to them a generous word.
And lower to them the wing of humility out of kindness. And say: ‘My Lord! Have mercy on them, just as they raised me (mercifully) when I was small.’
Your Lord knows best what is in your hearts. If you are righteous, He is indeed Most-Forgiving to the penitent.
Give the relatives their (due) right, and the needy and the traveller (as well). But do not spend (your money) wastefully.
Indeed, the wasteful are the brothers of Satan. And Satan is most ungrateful to his Lord.
And if you have to overlook them (for their lack of means), seeking the mercy of your Lord, which you will (soon) expect, then (at least) speak to them a gentle word.
Do not keep your hand chained to your neck (i.e. do not be miserly), nor open it all together (i.e. do not overspend), lest you sit down blamed, regretful.
Indeed, your Lord expands the provision for whom He wishes and straitens (it for whom He wills). Surely, He is All-Aware, All-Seeing of His servants.
And do not kill your children fearing poverty. We provide for them and for you. Indeed, their killing is a great sin.
And do not go near adultery. It is an indecent act and an evil path.

And do not kill any soul whom Allah has forbidden, except lawfully. And whoever is killed unjustly, We have certainly given his heir the authority (of obtaining legal retribution). But he must not commit any excess in (the retributive) killing, for he indeed enjoys the support (of the Law).
And do not go near the orphan’s property, except in the most virtuous manner, until he attains maturity. And fulfil the promise. Indeed, all promises are accountable.
Give full measure when you measure, and weigh with an accurate scale. That is better and more virtuous in outcome.
And do not follow (blindly) what you do not know (to be the truth). Indeed, the hearing, the seeing and the heart—all of these shall be held accountable.
And do not walk proudly on the earth. Indeed, you can neither tear up the earth nor reach the mountains in height.
The evil of all these (actions) is detestable in the sight of your Lord.
This is from the wisdom that your Lord has revealed to you. And do not set up another god with Allah, lest you should be thrown into Hell, condemned, rejected.
Did your Lord prefer you with sons, while He adopted (for Himself) daughters from among the angels? Indeed, you utter a terrible saying.
And We have indeed explained (the facts) in this Qur’an in various ways so that they may accept admonition. But it increases them only in aversion.
Say: ‘If there were (other) gods alongside Him as they claim, then surely they would have been able to seek a path to the Possessor of the Mighty Throne.
Glory be to Him. He is greatly exalted above whatever they say!’

The seven heavens glorify Him, the earth, and whoever is in them. And there is not a single thing, but it glorifies His praise. But you do not understand their glorification. Indeed, He is All-Forbearing, All-Forgiving.
And (O Prophet), when you recite the Qur’an, We put a hidden barrier between you and those who do not believe in the Hereafter.

And We have placed coverings over their hearts that prevent them from understanding it, and (there is) a heaviness in their ears (that prevents them from listening to it). And when you mention your Lord alone in the Qur’an, they turn their backs in aversion.
We know best what they listen for when they listen to you, and when they have secret talks, when the wrongdoers say: ‘(If you follow him,) you will be following just a bewitched man.’
See how they strike comparisons for you. So they have gone astray and cannot find the (right) way.
And they say: ‘What! When we become bones and dust, shall we surely be raised as a new creation?’
Say: ‘(Even) if you turn into stones or iron,

or some other creature that is more difficult (to resurrect) in your minds! (You will still be brought to life again).’ Then they will say: ‘Who will bring us back (to life)?’ Say: ‘He Who originated you the first time.’ So they will shake their heads at you and say: ‘When will it be?’ Say: ‘Perhaps it is soon.’
The Day when He calls you, you will respond by praising Him, and you will think that you remained (in the world) for only a little while.
Tell My servants to speak what is more virtuous. Indeed, Satan provokes ill-feeling between them. Indeed, Satan is an open enemy to human being.
Your Lord knows you best. If He pleases, He will have mercy upon you. If He wants, He will punish you, and We have not sent you as a guardian over them.
Your Lord knows best who is in the heavens and the earth. And indeed, We have exalted some Prophets over others, and We gave Dawud (David) the Zabur (Psalms).
Say: ‘Call upon those you consider (to be gods) besides Him. They have no power to remove harm from you nor transfer it (to someone else).

Those (angels) whom they call upon are (themselves) seeking a means to approach their Lord—whoever of them is nearer—and they hope for His mercy and fear His punishment.’ Indeed, your Lord’s punishment is much to be feared.
There is not a town (of evildoers), but We will destroy it before the Day of Resurrection or punish it with a most severe torment. That has been written in the Book.

Nothing prevents Us from sending (miraculous) signs, except that the ancients had denied them (previously). We gave Thamud the she-camel (of Salih) as an eye-opener, but they wronged her. And We do not send the signs except to instil fear (as a deterrence).

When We said to you: ‘Indeed, your Lord encompasses all humankind (with His power and knowledge). And We did not make the vision that We showed you except as a test for the people, and the Accursed Tree in the Qur’an (as well). And We instil fear in them, but it only increases them in great rebellion.’
And when We said to the angels: ‘Prostrate before Adam.’ They (all) prostrated except Iblis. He said: ‘Shall I prostrate before someone whom You have created from clay?’
He (further) said: ‘Do you see this one whom You have honoured above me? If You reprieve me till the Day of Resurrection, I will surely destroy his progeny, except a few.’
Allah said: ‘Be gone! Whoever of them follows you, the Hellfire shall indeed be a recompense for you (all)—an ample reward.

So incite whomsoever of them you can with your (tempting) voice, and rally against them with your cavalry and your infantry. And be their partner in wealth and children. And make (false) promises to them!’ And Satan promises them nothing but delusion.
‘As for My (faithful) servants, you shall have no authority over them. And your Lord can take care of them well enough.’
Your Lord is the One Who makes the ships sail smoothly for you across the sea, that you may seek His grace. Indeed, He is All-Merciful to you.

Whenever affliction befalls you at sea, those you worship desert (you), except for Him. But when He delivers you safely to the land, you (again) turn away (from Him). And human being is very ungrateful.
Do you feel secure that He will not cause the coast to swallow you, or He will not send upon you a tornado of pebbles? Then, you will not find any guardian for yourselves.

Do you feel secure that He will not take you back into it again, and He will not send upon you a violent storm and drown you for your being ungrateful? Then you will not find for yourselves any redresser against Us in the matter.

And indeed, We have honoured the children of Adam. We carried them in the land and the sea, provided them with all the good things and favoured them above many We have created with an appropriate preference.

(Remember) the Day We shall call every group of people along with their leader. So whoever is given his book in his right hand, it is they who shall read their book (with delight), and they shall not be wronged so much as a thread of a date stone.
But whoever was blind in this (world) will be blind in the Hereafter and even more astray from the (right) way.

And they had aspired to tempt you (to turn) away from what Allah has revealed to you, so that you may fabricate against Us something other than that, and then they would certainly have taken you as a friend.
Had We not firmly fortified you (with the strength of truth and divine wisdom), you might have gone near to incline a little bit towards them.

In that case, We would surely have made you taste double in this life and double after death. And then you would not have found any helper against Us. (But it could never happen because of Our protection).

And they had (also) sought to unsettle you in the land (of Mecca) to expel you from it, (but Allah Himself caused you to migrate by His Divine Plan). But in that case, they would not have remained after you (had left) except a little.
Such was (Our) custom regarding those We had sent from among Our messengers before you, and you will not find any change in Our custom.
Establish the (ritual) prayer from the declining of the sun till the darkening of the night, and (observe particularly) the dawn recitation. Indeed, the dawn recitation is witnessed (by angels).
And during a part of the night, offer an additional prayer as an act of extra devotion for you. Surely, your Lord will raise you to a praiseworthy station.
And say: ‘My Lord! Make me enter with an honourable entrance, and make me depart with an honourable departure, and grant me a favourable authority from Your Presence.’
And say: ‘The truth has come, and falsehood has vanished. Indeed, falsehood is bound to vanish.’
And We send down in the Qur’an that which is a healing and a mercy for the believers. And it increases the wrongdoers in nothing but loss.
And whenever We bestow favour upon human being, he becomes disregardful and turns aside arrogantly. But whenever harm befalls him, he loses hope.
Say: ‘Each acts according to his character.’ Your Lord knows best who is better guided on the way.
And they ask you about the spirit. Say: ‘The spirit is from (the realm of) the Command of my Lord, and you have not been given knowledge but a little.’
If We wished, We could have certainly taken away what We have revealed to you. Then you would not find for yourself any guardian against Us.
But on account of mercy from your Lord (We have not taken it away from your community). Indeed, His grace upon you (O Prophet) has (always) been abundant.
Say: ‘If (all) humans and jinn were to come together to bring the equivalent of this Qur’an, they could not produce the like of it, even if they supported one another.’
And surely, We have repeatedly employed for the people in this Qur’an all kinds of examples. Yet most people refuse everything except disbelief.
And they say: ‘We shall never believe you (O Muhammad s) until you make a spring gush out for us from the ground.
Or until you have a garden of date palms and grapes, and you should cause streams to flow, gushing out through it.
Or until you cause the heaven to fall upon us in pieces, just as you have claimed, or until you bring Allah and the angels right in front of us.

Or until you have a house of gold, or you ascend into heaven. And we will not believe your ascension until you bring us down a Book we can read.’ Say: ‘Glory be to my Lord! Am I anything but a human being (and) a Messenger?’
And nothing has prevented (these) people from believing when the guidance has come to them, except that they say: ‘Has Allah sent a human as a messenger?’
Say: ‘If there were angels on the earth, walking around as residents, We would have certainly sent down to them from heaven an angel as a messenger.’
Say: ‘Allah is sufficient as a Witness between me and you. Indeed, He is All-Aware, All-Seeing of His servants.

Whomever Allah guides is rightly guided, and whomever He leaves to stray, you will never find for them any guardians apart from Him. And on the Day of Resurrection, We shall gather them (scrambling) upon their faces—blind, dumb and deaf. Their refuge shall be Hell. Whenever it dies down, We shall intensify the blazing flame for them.’
That is their recompense because they disbelieved in Our Signs and said (mockingly): ‘What! When we are reduced to bones and dust, will we surely be resurrected as a new creation?’

Do they not realise that Allah, Who created the heavens and the earth, is capable of creating the like of them? And He has appointed for them a term in which there is no doubt. Yet the wrongdoers refuse everything except disbelief.
Say: ‘Were you to possess the (countless) treasures of my Lord’s mercy, you would hold back for fear of spending. And humans are ever miserly.
Indeed, We gave Musa (Moses) nine clear signs. So ask the Children of Israel. When he came to them, Pharaoh said to him: ‘O Musa (Moses), most surely, I think you are bewitched.’

He said: ‘You certainly know that no one could have sent down these (clear proofs) as eye-openers, except the Lord of the heavens and the earth. And, O Pharaoh, I most surely think that you are doomed.’
He (Pharaoh) desired to exterminate them from the land (of Egypt). So We drowned him and all those with him.
And after him, We said to the Children of Israel: ‘Take up (your) residence in the land. And when the promise of the Hereafter comes, We shall gather you as a mixed crowd.’
And with the truth, We have revealed it (i.e. the Qur’an), and with the truth, it has come down. And (O Prophet!) We have not sent you except as a Bearer of glad tidings and as a Warner.
(It is) a Qur’an, We have divided it into distinct parts so that you may recite it to the people in intervals. And We have sent it down in successive revelations.
Say: ‘Believe in it or do not believe.’ Indeed, those who were given knowledge before it, when it is recited to them, fall on their faces in prostration.
And they say: ‘Glory be to our Lord. Indeed, our Lord’s promise is fulfilled.’
And they fall down upon their faces weeping, and it (i.e. the Qur’an) increases them in humility.

Say: ‘Invoke Allah or invoke al-Rahman (the Most Compassionate). Whichever (Name) you call, to Him belong the Most Beautiful Names.’ And be not loud in your prayer, nor too quiet in it; instead, seek a middle course between the two.

And say: ‘All praise belongs to Allah, Who has never taken any son nor does He have any partner in the kingdom; nor has He (taken) any helper out of weakness.’ And proclaim His Greatness with a magnification (worthy of Him).
All praise belongs to Allah, Who revealed the Book to His (Esteemed) Servant and did not allow any deviation to be in it.

(He has made it) perfectly upright, to warn (the disbelievers) of severe punishment from Him and to give glad tidings to the believers who perform righteous deeds that there shall be a beautiful reward for them,
wherein they shall stay forever,
and to warn those who say: ‘Allah has taken a son.’
They have no knowledge about this, nor did their forefathers. It is a grievous utterance that comes out of their mouths, and they say nothing but a lie.
Yet (O Prophet), perhaps you will consume yourself with grief for their sake if they do not believe in this message (i.e. the Qur’an).
Indeed, We have made whatever is on the earth an adornment for it, that We may test them as to which of them is best in conduct.
And We shall surely turn whatever is upon it into a barren plain.

(O Prophet!) Have you considered that the Companions of the Cave and the Valley of al-Raqim (or Inscription) were among Our wonderful signs?* [* The story of the Companions of the Cave is also known in the Christian tradition as the Seven Sleepers of Ephesus. This was a group of believing Christian youths who hid inside a cave outside the city of Ephesus around 250 AD. They fled to Allah, protecting their faith and seeking refuge from their king (the Roman emperor) named Daqyanus (Decius). The pious youths slept for 300 solar years, which equates to 309 lunar years. It is one of the most evident examples of non-prophetic miracles ( karamat ) in the Qur’an.]
When the youths took refuge in the cave, they said: ‘Our Lord! Grant us mercy from Your presence and make us incline to the right course concerning our affair.’
So We cast (a veil) over their ears (causing them to fall dead asleep) in the cave for several years.
Then We raised them (again) so that We may show (them) which of the two groups had better calculated the period they had stayed (in the cave).
We relate their story to you exactly (as it happened). They were (pious) youths who believed in their Lord, and We increased them in guidance.

We fortified their hearts when they stood up boldly (before their king). They said: ‘Our Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth. We will never worship any (false) god besides Him, for then we shall have certainly said an outrageous thing.
These people of ours have taken gods besides Him. Why do they not bring any clear evidence to support them? So who could be more unjust than someone who fabricates a lie against Allah?’

(So those young men said to one another:) ‘When you have separated yourselves from them and what they worship besides Allah, take refuge in the cave. Your Lord will unfold His mercy for you and will make for you an easy way out in your affair.’

You would have seen the sun as it rose, declining from their cave to the right, and when it set (you would have seen it) turning away from them to the left while they were in an open space within it. That is among the signs of Allah. Whomever Allah guides is rightly guided, and whomever He leaves to stray, you shall never find for him any guardian who guides (to the right path).

You would have thought them awake while they were asleep. And We turned them over, to the right and the left, while their dog was (lying) stretching out its forelegs at the entrance (of the cave). Had you come upon them, you would surely have turned away to flee from them and would surely been filled with fear of them.

Even so, We raised them (from sleep) that they might question one another. One of them said: ‘How long have you stayed (here)?’ Some replied: ‘We have stayed a day or a part of a day.’ They said (to one another): ‘Your Lord knows best how long you have stayed. Send one of you to the town with this money of yours. Let him observe which of them has the purest food and bring you some provisions from there. Let him be polite (and careful), and by no means make anyone aware of you.
Indeed, if they come to find you out, they will stone you (to death) or force you to return to their faith. And then you will never be saved.’

So in this way, We brought them to the people’s attention so that they may know that Allah’s promise (of the Resurrection) is true, and as for the Last Hour, there is no doubt about it. As they (i.e. the town’s people) were disputing among themselves in their matter, they said: ‘Construct a building over them; their Lord knows best about them.’ Those who prevailed over their affair said: ‘We shall build a place of worship over them.’

Some will say: ‘(The Companions of the Cave) were three, and the fourth of them was their dog.’ And others will say: ‘(They were) five, and the sixth of them was their dog.’ (They are) guessing at the Unseen. Yet others will say: ‘(They were) seven, and the eighth of them was their dog.’ Say: ‘My Lord knows best their number, and none (truly) knows about them except a few.’ So do not dispute concerning them except with a clear argument. And do not question any of them about them.
And do not say of anything: ‘I will surely do it tomorrow,’
except (adding): ‘If Allah wills so.’ And remember your Lord when you forget. And say: ‘Perhaps my Lord will guide me to what is closer to right conduct than this.’

They remained in their cave for three hundred years, adding nine more (to that number).* [* It may be 300 years, according to the Gregorian Calendar, which equals 309 years of the Islamic Lunar Calendar.]

Say: ‘Allah knows best how long they stayed.’ To Him belongs the Unseen of the heavens and the earth. How wonderful His seeing and how wonderful His hearing! They have no guardian besides Him. And He does not make anyone a partner in His judgement.
Recite what has been revealed to you from your Lord’s Book. None can change His Words. And you will never find any refuge besides Him.

Keep yourself patiently in the company of those who call upon their Lord morning and evening, desiring His Countenance (i.e. His pleasure). And do not turn your eyes away from them, desiring the attractions of this world’s life, nor follow the one whose heart We have made negligent of Our remembrance, who follows his low desires and whose affair exceeds (all) bounds.

And say: ‘(This is) the truth from your Lord. So whoever wishes, let him believe, and whoever wishes, let him disbelieve.’ Indeed, We have prepared for the wrongdoers a fire, the curtains of which will surround them (from all sides). And if they plead for relief, they will be showered with (boiling) water, like molten copper, which will burn (their) faces. What a dreadful drink! And what a terrible resting place!
As for those who believe and perform righteous deeds, We surely do not waste the reward of those who are excellent in conduct.

For such, there shall be the Gardens of Eternity, with rivers flowing beneath them. They will be adorned therein with bracelets of gold, and they shall wear green robes of fine silk and brocade interwoven with gold. They will be comfortably seated therein upon soft couches. What a blessed reward! And what a fantastic resting place!
Tell them the parable of two men: for one of them, We made two gardens of grapevines, and We surrounded them both with date palms and placed corn fields between them.
Both gardens produced their fruits and did not fall short in any way. And We caused a river to gush through them.
And he possessed (abundant wealth and) fruits. So he said to his companion, as he argued with him: ‘I am greater than you in wealth and stronger in number (i.e. in family and followers).’
And he went into his garden wronging himself (by way of arrogance). He said: ‘I do not think that this will ever perish.
And I do not think that the Hour will ever come. And even if I am returned to my Lord, I will surely find a resort better than this.’

His companion said to him while conversing with him: ‘Do you disbelieve in Him Who created you (i.e. your father Adam) from dust, and then (developed you) from a zygote*, and then fashioned you as a man? [* In Arabic, the word ‘al-nutfa’ is mentioned, and it refers to a zygote, which is a mixture of male and female gametes. It is the new single cell formed after the union of a sperm and ovum.]
But He is Allah, my Lord, and I do not ascribe any partner to my Lord.
And why did you not say when you entered your garden: ‘(This is) what Allah has willed! There is no power except in Allah!’ If you see that I am lesser than you in wealth and children,
perhaps, my Lord will grant me (something) better than your garden and send down upon your garden thunderbolts from the sky so that it becomes a flattened plain.
Or its water may sink so deep into the ground that you will never be able to obtain it (again).’

(And so it happened.) His fruits were (entirely) destroyed, and he began to wring his hands over what he had invested while it had collapsed on its trellises, and he was saying: ‘I wish I had not ascribed any partner to my Lord.’
He had no group to help him besides Allah, nor could he help himself.

In this situation, the only protection* is that of Allah, the real (God). He is the best in rewarding and the best in requiting. [* The word ‘al-Walaya’, in common usage, designates a state of friendship, attachment and protection. In contrast, ‘ al-Wilaya ’ denotes a spiritual or political authority or guardianship, which comes from one’s relationship with a higher authority.]

Draw an example of this worldly life for them: (It is) like the water We send down from the sky. Then the vegetation of the earth absorbs it (to grow). Then it (i.e. the vegetation) becomes dry, broken into pieces, which the winds scatter (afterwards). And Allah holds power over everything.
Wealth and children are the attractions of the worldly life. However, the everlasting righteous deeds are better in reward with your Lord and better in hope.
On the Day when We shall make the mountains move away, and you will see the earth an open plain, and We shall gather them, We shall not leave any of them out.

And they will be presented before your Lord in rows. (It will be said to them:) ‘Indeed, you have come to Us as We created you the first time. Rather, you claimed that We would never appoint for you a time for the fulfilment of the promise.’

The record of deeds will be laid (open). Then, you will see the evildoers fearful of what is in it. They will say: ‘Oh, woe to us! What kind of record is this that does not omit anything, small or big, but numbers them all.’ And they will find present whatsoever they had done. And your Lord does not deal with anyone unjustly.

And (remember) when We said to the angels: ‘Prostrate before Adam (in veneration).’ So they prostrated except Iblis. He was one of the jinn, so he deviated from the command of his Lord. Would you still take him and his offspring as guardians besides Me, though they are your enemies? How evil an exchange for the wrongdoers!
I did not make them witnesses to the creation of the heavens and the earth, nor their own creation. And I do not take the misleaders as assistants.
On the Day, He will say: ‘Call those whom you claimed as My partners.’ They will call upon them, but they will not respond to them, and We will cause a destructive separation between them.
The evildoers will see the Fire and will realise that they are about to fall into it, and they will find no means of escape from it.
Indeed, We have presented in this Qur’an every (kind of) illustration for people. But human being is the most contentious of all creatures.

Nothing prevents people from believing in their Lord when guidance has come to them and asking forgiveness, except that the fate of the earlier people overtakes them or their punishment confronts them face to face.

We do not send the messengers but as bearers of glad tidings and as warners. And those who disbelieve, dispute falsely to refute the truth thereby. And they take My revelations and what they are warned about in mockery.

Who is more unjust than he who has been reminded of the signs of his Lord, but he turns away from them and forgets what his hands have sent forth? Indeed, We have placed coverings over their hearts, lest they should understand it, and a deafness in their ears. And if you invite them to (the path of) guidance, they will never (let themselves) be guided.

And your Lord is All-Forgiving, the Possessor of Mercy. Were He to punish them because of what they had committed, He would have hastened the punishment for them. But they have a fixed time (when) they shall not find a refuge besides Him.
And those are the towns that We destroyed when they acted unjustly. And We had fixed a time for their destruction.
And (remember) when Musa (Moses) said to his young companion: ‘I shall not stop until I reach the junction of the two seas, even if I keep travelling for ages.’
So when they reached the (location of the) junction of the two (seas), they forgot their fish (there), which made its way into the sea and swam away.
But when they had gone further, he told his young companion: ‘Bring us our meal. Surely, we have encountered much fatigue during this journey of ours.’

He (the disciple) said: ‘Did you notice? When we rested at the rock, I forgot the fish (there), and none but Satan made me forget to mention it (to you). And it made its way into the sea amazingly (after returning to life).’
He (Moses) said: ‘That’s (the place) we were looking for.’ So they turned back, retracing their footsteps carefully.
They found a (unique) servant from among Our servants (there, i.e. al-Khidr), whom We had granted special mercy from Ourselves and whom We had taught special knowledge from Our presence.
Musa (Moses) said to him: ‘May I follow you that you teach me some of the right guidance you have been taught (especially)?’
He (al-Khidr) said: ‘Indeed, you will not be able to be patient with me!’
How could you be patient upon something you do not comprehend in knowledge?’
He (Moses) said: ‘If Allah pleases, you will find me patient, and I will not violate any of your instructions.’
He (al-Khidr) said: ‘If you follow me, then do not question me about anything until I mention it to you (myself).’
So they went on till they boarded the boat, (and) he (al-Khidr) made a hole in it. He (Moses) said: ‘Did you make a hole in it to drown its people? Surely, you have done a grievous thing.’
He (al-Khidr) said: ‘Did I not tell you that you will not be able to be patient with me?’
He (Moses) said: ‘Do not take me to task that I forgot, nor make me suffer much hardship on account of what I have done.’

So they travelled on until they came across a young boy. He (al-Khidr) killed him. He (Moses) said: ‘Did you kill an innocent soul who had slain no other soul? Indeed, you have done a terrible thing!’
He (al-Khidr) said: ‘Did I not tell you that you will not be able to be patient with me?’
He (Moses) said: ‘If I question you about anything after this, do not keep me in your company. You have already received sufficient excuse on my part.’

So they moved on until they came to the people of a town. They asked its people for food, but they refused to show them any hospitality. There, they found a wall which was about to collapse. So he (al-Khidr) erected it. He (Moses) said: ‘Had you wished, you could have taken a wage for it.’
He (al-Khidr) said: ‘This is the parting between you and me. Now, I shall inform you about the interpretation of what you could not bear patiently.
As for the boat, it belonged to some poor people working at sea. I wanted to make it defective, for ahead of them was a king, coming to seize every (serviceable) boat by force.
As for the young boy, his parents were believers, and we feared he would trouble them with rebellion and disbelief.
So we desired that their Lord would give them in exchange one better than him in purity and closer in mercy.

As for the wall, it belonged to two orphan boys in the town. And under it, there was a treasure belonging to them. And their father was a righteous man. So your Lord desired that they should reach their maturity and take out their treasure (themselves). (This was) your Lord’s kindness. And I did not do it of my own accord. This is the interpretation of what you could not bear patiently.’
And they ask you about Dhu al-Qarnayn. Say: ‘I shall relate to you something of his account.’
We had established his power in the land and granted him the resources to achieve everything (necessary for his role).
So he travelled on a particular route,

until he reached the place (towards the west) where he perceived the sun setting. He found it (seemingly) setting into a spring of murky hot water.* And he found some people (living) nearby. We said: ‘O Dhu al-Qarnayn, you may either punish them or treat them kindly.’ [* This spring of murky hot water is possibly a reference to the black sea.]
He said: ‘As for him who does wrong, we shall punish him. Then he shall be returned to his Lord. And He will punish him with a dreadful torment.
But as for the one who believes and acts righteously, he shall have a beautiful reward. And We shall speak to him a gentle word of our command.’
Then (after consolidating his conquests in the west) he travelled on another route,
until he reached the place (towards the east) where he perceived (the rising of) the sun. He found it (seemingly) rising over a people for whom We had not made any shelter from it.
So it was. And We comprehended whatever he possessed in (Our) knowledge,
then he followed another route,
until he reached (a place) between two barriers. He found beside them a people who could hardly understand a word.
They said: ‘O Dhu al-Qarnayn! Indeed, Gog and Magog are making trouble in the land. Should we pay you a tribute, provided you build a barrier between them and us?’
He said: ‘Whatever my Lord has established me with is better (than what you are offering). So, help me with some workforce. I will put up a fortified barricade between you and them.
Bring me blocks of iron.’ Then, when he had levelled up the space between the two top sides, he said: ‘Blow!’ When he had turned it into fire, he said: ‘Bring me some molten copper to pour over it.’
So they (Gog and Magog) could neither cross over it nor make a tunnel through it.

He (Dhu al-Qarnayn) said: ‘This is a mercy from my Lord (to hold back the terrors of Gog and Magog from the people). But when my Lord’s promise (of the Last Hour) comes, He will (demolish this barrier and) level it to the ground (or reduce it to dust particles). And my Lord’s promise always comes true.’
On that Day, We shall let them surge like waves over one another. Later, the Trumpet will be blown, and We will gather them all.
And on that Day, We shall present the Hellfire, fully exposed to the disbelievers,
those whose eyes were covered from My remembrance and could not hear.
Do the disbelievers suppose they may take My servants as (their) guardians besides Me? Indeed, We have prepared Hell as a reception for the disbelievers.
Say: ‘Shall We inform you about the biggest losers in (respect to their) deeds?
(They are) those whose efforts in this world are misguided while they think they are doing good work.’
They are the ones who disbelieve in the signs of their Lord and the meeting with Him. So their deeds have come to nothing. On the Day of Resurrection, We shall not give them any weight.
That is their recompense—Hell—for they disbelieved and took My revelations and My Messengers in mockery.
Indeed, those who believe and perform righteous deeds shall have the Gardens of Paradise as (their) reception (and hospitality),
abiding in them forever, from where they will never wish to move.
Say: ‘If the ocean were ink for the Words of my Lord, the ocean would surely run dry before the Words of my Lord are finished, even if we brought another like it to help.’

Say: ‘I am only a human being like you. It has been revealed to me that your God is (only) One God. So whoever hopes for the meeting with his Lord, let him perform righteous deeds and not associate anyone in the worship of his Lord.’
Kaf, Ha, Ya, ‘Ayn, Sad.
(This is) an account of the mercy of your Lord upon His servant Zakariyya (Zachariah),
when he called upon his Lord (secretly) in a low voice.
He said: ‘My Lord! Indeed, my bones have become feeble, and my head has turned white with age, yet in supplicating You, my Lord, I have never been disappointed (or unblessed).
Indeed, I fear (what) my relatives (will do) after me while my wife is barren. So grant me an heir from Your presence.
He should inherit from me and inherit from the House of Ya‘qub (Jacob), and make him, my Lord, well-pleasing (to You).’
(The angel said:) ‘O Zakariyya (Zachariah)! Indeed, We give you the good news of a son whose name is Yahya (John). We have not given such a name to anyone before.’
He said: ‘My Lord! How shall I have a son when my wife is barren and I have already become extremely old?’
He (the angel) said: ‘‘So shall it be. Your Lord says: ‘It is easy for Me, just as I created you before when you were nothing.’’’
He said: ‘My Lord! Grant me a sign.’ He said: ‘Your sign is that you will not (be able to) speak to people for three full (days and) nights (though in sound health).’
Then he came out to his people from his worshipping chamber and conveyed to them through signs that they should glorify (Allah) morning and evening.
(We later said:) ‘O Yahya (John)! Hold on to the Book (i.e. the Torah) firmly. And We granted him wisdom (and prophethood) while he was still a child,
and (We granted him) compassion and purity from Our presence; and he was God-conscious.
And he was kind to his parents, and he was neither arrogant nor disobedient.
Peace be upon him, the day he was born, the day he dies, and the day he will be raised alive!
And mention Maryam (Mary) in the Book when she withdrew from her family to an eastern place.
So, she secluded herself from them. Then We sent Our Spirit (the Angel Gabriel) to her, who appeared before her as a perfect human.
She said: ‘I seek refuge from you in the Most-Compassionate (Lord) if you are conscious (of Allah).’
He (Gabriel) said: ‘I am only a messenger from your Lord so that I may bestow upon you a pure son.’
She said: ‘How shall I have a son when no man has ever touched me, nor have I been unchaste?’
He (Gabriel) said: ‘So shall it be. Your Lord says: ‘It is easy for Me. And so that We may make him a sign for humankind and a mercy from Us. And it is a matter (already) decided.’
So, she conceived him, then withdrew with him to a distant place.
And the pains of childbirth drove her to the trunk of a palm tree. She said: ‘Oh, I wish I had died before this and become a thing completely forgotten from memory!’
Then he called her from below her (saying): ‘Do not worry. Surely, your Lord has made a spring to flow beneath you.
Shake towards you the trunk of the palm tree; it will deliver fresh ripe dates to you.
