So eat and drink and cool (your) eyes. Then, if you see any human being, say (to him by gestures): ‘Indeed, I have vowed a fast (of silence) to the Most-Compassionate (Lord), so I shall not speak to any human being today.’
Then, she came to her people carrying him. They said: ‘O Maryam (Mary), you have certainly come up with a strange thing!

O sister of Harun (Aaron)*! Your father was not an indecent man, nor was your mother an unchaste woman.’ [* She was not a biological sister of Prophet Aaron, the brother of Moses. The name of Aaron signified purity and righteousness among the Jews of that time. So the title ‘Sister of Aaron’ meant to indicate that Mary was like Aaron in righteousness, as she was his sibling in purity and virtue. It may also refer to being a pious daughter from the noble lineage of Prophet Aaron.]
Then she pointed to him (i.e. Jesus). They said: ‘How can we speak to one who is yet a child in the cradle?’
He (the infant Jesus) said: ‘I am indeed a servant of Allah. He has given me the Book and made me a prophet.
And He has made me blessed, wherever I may be. And He has enjoined upon me prayer and almsgiving (i.e. charity) as long as I live,
and to be kind to my mother. And He has not made me domineering and graceless.
Peace be upon me the day I was born, the day I will die, and the day I will be raised to life again!’
That is ‘Isa, the son of Maryam (i.e. Jesus, the son of Mary). (This is) the saying of the truth, concerning which they doubt.
It is not for Allah to take a son. Glory be to Him! When He has decided a matter, He only says to it: ‘Be!’ and it is.
(‘Isa [Jesus] said:) ‘And indeed, Allah is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him. This is a straight path.’
But the factions differed among themselves. So suffering will come to those who disbelieve when the witnessing of a tremendous Day (takes place).
How well they will hear and how well they will see on the Day when they come to Us, although the wrongdoers today are in clear error.
Warn them of the Day of Regret, when the matter will have been decided, while they are yet in a state of negligence and do not believe.
Indeed, We inherit the earth and whatever is upon it. And to Us, they shall be returned.
And mention Ibrahim (Abraham) in the Book (i.e. the Holy Qur’an). Indeed, he was a man of truth (and) a prophet.
When he said to his (foster) father: ‘O my father! Why do you worship what can neither hear nor see, nor can it avail you in any way?
O my father! Indeed knowledge has come to me which has not come to you. So follow me; I shall guide you to the right path.
O my father! Do not worship Satan. Indeed Satan is disobedient to the Most-Compassionate (Lord).
O my father! Indeed I fear that a punishment from the Most-Compassionate (Lord) will befall you, and you will become an ally of Satan.’
He (Azar) said: ‘Ibrahim (Abraham)! Are you renouncing my gods? If you do not stop, I shall surely stone you. So keep away from me for a long time.’
He (Abraham) said: ‘Peace be upon you! I will pray to my Lord to forgive you. Indeed, He is gracious to me.
I dissociate myself from you and whatever you worship besides Allah. I will pray to my Lord. Hopefully, I will never be disappointed in praying to my Lord.’
So when he (Abraham) detached himself from them and what they worship besides Allah, We gave him Ishaq (Isaac) and Ya‘qub (Jacob), and We made each of them a prophet.
And We bestowed upon them Our mercy and blessed them with a truthful (and) honourable mention.
And mention Musa (Moses) in the Book. Indeed, he was a purified and chosen devotee (of Allah) and was a messenger (and) a prophet.
And We called to him from the right side of the Mount and brought him close for intimate communication.
And We granted him, out of Our mercy, his brother Harun (Aaron), as a prophet (to assist him in his mission).
And mention Isma‘il (Ishmael) in the Book. Indeed, he was truthful to (his) promise and was a messenger (and) a prophet.
He used to enjoin the prayer and almsgiving upon his household, and he was pleasing to his Lord.
And mention Idris in the Book. Indeed, he was a most truthful person (and) a prophet.
And We raised him to an exalted station.

They are the ones whom Allah has blessed among the prophets from the progeny of Adam, and from (the progeny of) those (believers) whom We carried with Nuh ([Noah] on the Ark), and from the progeny of Ibrahim (Abraham) and Isra’il (Jacob), and from (the progeny of) those whom We had guided and chosen. When the revelations of the Most-Compassionate (Lord) were recited to them, they would fall down, prostrating and weeping.
Then came after them an evil generation that neglected the prayer and followed (their) base desires. So they will soon encounter the perdition (in the Ghayy Valley of Hell),
except for those who repent, believe and act righteously. It is they who shall enter Paradise and shall not be wronged in the least.
(They will dwell in) the Gardens of Eternity, which the Most-Compassionate (Lord) has promised to His servants in the (realm of the) Unseen. Indeed, His promise shall come to pass.
They shall not hear in it any vain discourse, but only: ‘Peace!’ And they shall have in it their provision, morning and evening.
That is Paradise, which We will give as an inheritance to those of Our servants who are God-conscious.

And (Jibra’il [Gabriel] said): ‘We do not descend except by the command of your Lord. To Him belongs whatever is before us, whatever is behind us and everything in between. And your Lord is not forgetful.
(He is) the Lord of the heavens, the earth and whatever is between them. So worship Him (alone) and be steadfast in His worship. Do you know anyone who could be His namesake?’
Man says: ‘What? Once I am dead, shall I truly be brought out alive?’
Does human being not remember that We had created him before when he was nothing?
So, by your Lord, We shall surely gather them and the devils. Then, We shall surely cause them to be present around Hell (scrambling) on their knees.
Then, from every group, We shall surely drag out whichever of them was the most rebellious against the Most-Compassionate (Lord).
Again, surely, We know best those most deserving to be thrown into Hell.

And there is none of you but will pass over it*. (This is) a decree of your Lord, certainly determined. [* This refers to the bridge extended over Hell, known as ‘al-Sirat.’ Both the believers and the disbelievers will have to cross this bridge. The believers will cross it safely at a speed corresponding to the strength of their faith, whereas the disbelievers and the hypocrites will be unable to cross it and will consequently fall into Hell.]
Then We will deliver those who are God-conscious and leave the wrongdoers in it, falling on their knees.
When Our clear revelations are recited to them, the disbelievers say to the believers: ‘Which of the two groups (i.e. yours or ours) is superior in station and better in company.’
And how many a generation have We destroyed before them who were superior in worldly riches and outward glitter!

Say: ‘Whoever remains upon misguidance, the Most-Compassionate (Lord) shall prolong his life until they see what they have been warned about—either punishment (in this life) or the Hour (of the judgement). Then they will come to know who was in a bad position and (who was) weaker as an army.
And Allah increases in guidance those who are rightly guided. And the lasting deeds of righteousness are better with your Lord in reward and better in return.
Have you seen the one who rejects Our revelations and says: ‘I shall certainly be granted (plenty of) wealth and children’?
Has he come to know the Unseen or received a pledge from the Most-Compassionate (Lord)?
No, indeed! We shall record what he says and keep prolonging his punishment (endlessly).
We shall take over from him what he claims, and he will come to Us alone (without wealth and children).
They have taken gods besides Allah so that they may be (a source of) strength and honour for them.
No, indeed! They (i.e. the false gods) shall soon disown their worship, and they will become their opponents.
Do you not realise that We sent the devils upon the disbelievers? They keep inciting them cunningly (to commit wrongs).
So be not in haste towards them (for their punishment). We are just counting down their days.
On the Day, We will gather the God-conscious towards the Most-Compassionate (Lord) as an honoured delegation.
And We will drive the guilty as a thirsty herd towards Hell.
(On that Day,) none will possess the (power of) intercession except the one who has (already) taken a promise from the Most-Compassionate (Lord).
They say: ‘The Most-Compassionate (Lord) has taken a child!’
You have indeed asserted a terrible thing!
The heavens can almost burst apart at it, the earth can split asunder, and the mountains can crumble to pieces,
(protesting) that they attribute a son to the Most-Compassionate (Lord)!
It is not befitting for (the Glory of) the Most-Compassionate (Lord) to take a child.
There is none in the heavens and the earth but will come to the Most-Compassionate (Lord) as a servant.
Indeed, He has (accurately) counted them (all) and numbered them precisely.
And each of them shall come to Him on the Day of Resurrection, all alone.
As for those who believe and perform righteous deeds, the Most-Compassionate (Lord) will endear them (to His creation).
Indeed, We have made this (Qur’an) easy in your tongue (i.e. in Arabic) so, with it, you may give glad tidings to the God-conscious and warn the contentious people through it.
And how many a generation We destroyed before them! Can you trace any of them or (even) hear the slightest murmuring sound from them?
Ta-Ha.
(Esteemed Prophet!) We have not revealed the Qur’an to you that you should be distressed.
(We revealed it) only as a reminder for the one who fears (his Lord).
(It is) a revelation from the One Who created the earth and the highest heavens.
The Most-Compassionate (Lord) established (His authority) over the Throne (befitting His Glory).
To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth, whatever is in between them and whatever is under the ground.
Whether you speak loudly (or quietly), He certainly knows what is secret and what is even more hidden.
Allah—there is no god except Him. To Him belong the most Beautiful Names.
And has the story of Musa (Moses) come to you (O Prophet)?

When he saw a fire (on his way back to Egypt from Madyan), he said to his family: ‘Stay here! Indeed, I have seen a fire. Perhaps I may bring you a flaming brand from it or find some guidance at the fire.’
So, when he approached it, he was called: ‘O Musa (Moses)!
Indeed, I am your Lord! So take off your sandals. Indeed, you are in the sacred valley of Tuwa.
I have chosen you (as My messenger), so listen to what is revealed.
Indeed, I am Allah. There is no god but Me. So worship Me and establish the prayer for My remembrance.
Indeed, the Hour is coming. I would keep it hidden so that every soul may be recompensed for what it strives for.
So let not avert you from it, the one who does not believe in it, and follows his base desires, lest you should perish.
(Allah asked:) ‘What is that in your right hand, O Musa (Moses)?’
He said: ‘It is my staff. I lean upon it, and with it, I beat down leaves (making them fall) upon my sheep. And I have other uses for it.’
He said: ‘Throw it down Musa (Moses).’
So he threw it down. And behold, it was a snake moving fast.
He said: ‘Pick it up and do not fear. We shall restore it to its original state.
Now enclose your hand to your armpit. It will emerge (shining) white without any blemish. (This is yet) another sign.
(We are doing this) so that We can show you some of Our greater signs.
Go to Pharaoh. He has indeed transgressed all bounds.’
He said: ‘My Lord! Expand my breast for me.
And make my task easy for me.
And remove the hitch from my tongue (granting me clarity of speech),
so that people may understand my discourse (easily).
And appoint for me a minister from my family,
my brother Harun (Aaron).
Increase my strength through him,
and make him my associate in my task.
So that we may glorify You much,
and remember You much.
Indeed, You are always watching over us.’
He said: ‘Musa (Moses)! Your request has been granted.
And indeed, We had shown you favour before,
when We inspired to your mother that which was inspired, (saying:)

‘Put him in the chest, then place it into the river. Then the river will throw him on the shore. An enemy of Mine and an enemy of his (i.e. Pharoah) shall pick him up (from there).’ And I blessed you with (an exceptional) love from Me that you might be brought up under My (caring) eyes.

When your sister walked up (to Pharaoh’s family) and said: ‘Shall I direct you to someone who can nurse him?’ Then We brought you back to your mother, that her eyes might be cooled, and she should not grieve. Later, you killed a man (unintentionally), but We saved you from sorrow. Then We tried you with various trials. Then, you stayed among the people of Madyan (Midian) for years. Then you came (here) as ordained, O Musa (Moses)!
I have chosen you for Myself (i.e. the task of Messengership).
Go ahead, you and your brother (Aaron), with My Signs and do not tire in remembering Me.
Go, both of you, to Pharaoh. Indeed, he has rebelled.
Speak to him a gentle word. Perhaps he may accept admonition or fear (My wrath).’
Both of them said: ‘Our Lord! Indeed, we fear that he will torture us or become (more) rebellious.’
He said: ‘Do not fear. Indeed, I am with you both; I hear and I see.

So both of you go to him and say: “Indeed we are two messengers of your Lord. Therefore, let the Children of Israel go with us. And do not persecute them (anymore). Indeed, We have brought you a sign from your Lord. Peace be upon him who follows guidance!
Indeed, it has been revealed to us that punishment will surely come upon him who rejects (the truth) and turns his back (on us).”’
He (Pharaoh) said: ‘Who is the Lord of you both? O Musa (Moses)!’
He (Moses) said: ‘Our Lord is He Who gave everything its distinctive form and then guided it (according to its potential).’
He (Pharaoh) said: ‘What about the previous generations?’
He (Moses) said: ‘Their knowledge is with My Lord in a Book. My Lord neither makes any error nor forgets.’

(He is) the One Who made the earth a habitable cradle for you and made therein ways and means (of livelihood) for you and sent down water from the sky. Then, through it, We have brought out various pairs of vegetation.
Eat and graze your cattle. There are indeed signs in this for those who possess intelligence.
From it (i.e. the soil of the earth) did We create you, and into it shall We return you, and from it shall We bring you out another time.
And indeed, We showed him (i.e. Pharaoh) all Our signs, but he rejected (them) and refused (to believe).
He said: ‘Have you come to us to expel us from our land with your magic, O Musa (Moses)?
So we shall confront you with magic like it. Therefore, set a time (for the contest) between us and you, which neither we shall break nor you, at a central location.’
He (Moses) said: ‘Your appointment will be on the Day of Adornment (i.e. the day of Annual Festival), and let the people be gathered at mid-morning.’
Then Pharaoh turned away (to consult his cabinet). He put together his strategy and arrived (at the arena).
Musa (Moses) said to them (i.e. the sorcerers): ‘Woe to you! Do not fabricate a lie against Allah, lest He should destroy you with a punishment. Whoever fabricates (lies) will lose surely.’
So they debated their plan among themselves and kept their confidential discourse secret.
They (the sorcerers) said: ‘These two are only magicians who want to expel you from your land with their magic and abolish your exemplary tradition.
So, gather your strategy and line up in rows (for the contest). Indeed, he will prosper today who gains the upper hand.’
They (the magicians) said: ‘O Musa (Moses)! Either you should throw (first), or we shall be the first to throw.’
He (Moses) said: ‘Rather you throw (first).’ Then, behold, their ropes and sticks appeared to him by their magic (as though) they were moving around fast.
So Musa (Moses) concealed the alarm he felt in his mind.
We said: ‘Do not worry. Indeed, you will have the upper hand.

Throw down what is in your right hand. It will swallow up whatever they have produced. What they have produced is only a magician’s trick. And the magician will not be successful wherever he may come.’
Then, the magicians were cast down in prostration. They said: ‘We believe in the Lord of Harun (Aaron) and Musa (Moses).’

He (Pharaoh) said: ‘Do you profess faith in him before I permit you? He is indeed your chief who has taught you magic! I shall surely cut off your hands and your feet from opposite sides. And I shall surely crucify you on the trunks of palm trees. So you shall surely know which of us is more severe and more lasting in punishing.’

They (the magicians) said: ‘We will never prefer you over the clear proofs that have come to us, nor over Him Who has created us. So decide whatever you (are determined to) decide. You can only decide about the life of this world.
Indeed, we have believed in our Lord so He may forgive us our sins and that magic you compelled us to perform. And Allah is the Best and the Everlasting.’
Whoever comes to his Lord as an evildoer will certainly have Hell, where he will neither die nor live.
But whoever comes to Him as a believer, having performed righteous deeds, for such shall be the highest ranks (in Paradise),
the Gardens of Eternity with rivers flowing beneath them. They will abide in them forever. And that is the reward of the one who purifies (himself).

And We indeed revealed to Musa (saying): ‘Set forth with My servants by night. Then, strike out for them a dry path through the sea. Do not be afraid of being overtaken and have no fear (of getting drowned).’
Then, Pharaoh pursued them with his troops. So the sea covered them with (the waves) that overwhelmed them.
Pharaoh misled his people and did not guide (them correctly).
O Children of Israel! We saved you from your enemy. And We made a covenant with you on the right side of the Mount (Tur). And We sent down to you the manna and the quails.
(We said to you:) ‘Eat from the good things We have provided you, but do not exceed the limits therein, lest My wrath should befall you. And anyone upon whom My wrath befalls is certainly destroyed.’
Indeed, I am Most-Forgiving for the one who repents, becomes (a true) believer, acts righteously and then keeps following guidance.
(Allah asked:) ‘What has caused you to come (to Mount Sinai for Our meeting) in such haste, ahead of your people, O Musa (Moses)?’
He said: ‘They are coming closely upon my tracks, and I have hurried to You, my Lord, that You may be pleased!’
He said: ‘Indeed, We have tested your people in your absence, and Samiri has led them astray.’

So, Musa (Moses) returned to his people, wrathful and saddened. He said: ‘O my people! Did your Lord not make a gracious promise to you? Did the time (of its fulfilment) seem too long for you? Or did you desire that the wrath of your Lord should descend upon you? So you broke your promise to me.’
They said: ‘We did not deliberately break (our) promise to you, but we were laden with the weight of people’s (golden) ornaments. So we threw them (into the fire), and Samiri also threw it.’
Then, he (Samiri) produced for them (from the molten jewellery) a statue of a calf, with a lowing sound (of a cow). Then they said: ‘This is your god and Musa’s god, but he forgot (to mention).’
Could they not see that it (i.e. the calf) would not respond to them with speech, and that it did not possess for them (any) harm or benefit?
And Harun (Aaron) had already told them: ‘O my people! You are only being tested by it. And surely your Lord is the Most-Compassionate. So follow me and obey my command.’
They said: ‘We shall not cease to be its devotees until Musa (Moses) returns to us.’
He (Musa [Moses]) said: ‘O Harun (Aaron)! What prevented you when you saw them going astray,
from coming after me (to inform me)? Did you disobey my command?’
He (Aaron) said: ‘O son of my mother! Do not hold my beard or my head. I was scared you would say: ‘You have caused division among the Children of Israel, and you did not wait for my word.’’
He (Musa [Moses]) said: ‘What was your purpose, O Samiri?’

He said: ‘I saw what they had not seen (i.e. the Archangel Gabriel). I took a handful (of dust) from the messenger’s footprints (from when he visited you), and then I threw it (in the gold casting for the calf). That is how my soul prompted me.’

He (Musa [Moses]) said (to Samiri): ‘Get away (from here). It shall be for you (a punishment) in this life to keep saying: ‘Don’t touch me!’ And there is yet another promise (of punishment), which you will never be allowed to escape. Now, look at your god, which you have kept on worshipping. We shall surely burn it down and then scatter it into the sea like dust.
(O my people!) Indeed, your God is only Allah. There is no god except Him. He comprehends everything in (His) knowledge.’
(Esteemed Prophet!) In this way, We relate to you the news of what happened before. Indeed, We have given you a Reminder (i.e. the Qur’an) from Our presence.
Whoever turns away from it shall bear a heavy burden on the Day of Resurrection,
remaining in it forever. What a terrible burden for them on the Day of Resurrection!
(It is) the Day when the Trumpet will be blown. And We shall gather the guilty on that Day (blind), with blued eyes.
They will whisper among themselves: ‘You have stayed only for ten (days in the world).’
We know best what they will say when the fairest of them in conduct will say: ‘You stayed (there) for no longer than a day.’
They ask you about the mountains. So tell (them): ‘My Lord will scatter them like dust.
Then He will leave it (i.e. the earth) as a flat, barren land.
In it, You will see neither curvature nor unevenness (i.e. any valley or hill).’
On that Day, they will follow the inviter. There will be no escape from following him. And all voices shall be humbled before the Most-Compassionate (Lord), and you will hear nothing but a murmur.
On that Day, intercession will not benefit (anyone) except the one to whom the Most-Compassionate (Lord) has granted permission and was pleased with his word.
He knows what is before them and what is behind them, but they do not encompass Him in knowledge.
All faces shall be humbled before the Ever-Living, the All-Sustaining. And surely, he who bears (the burden of) injustice will certainly fail.
And whoever performs righteous deeds and is a believer shall fear neither injustice nor deprivation.
Thus, We have sent it down as an Arabic Qur’an. And We have distinctly explained the warning in it, so that they may be God-conscious, or it may induce remembrance for them.
So exalted is Allah, the Real Sovereign, the Ultimate Truth. And be not in haste with the Qur’an before its revelation to you is completed. And say: ‘My Lord! Increase me in knowledge.’
And indeed, We made a pact with Adam earlier, but he forgot. And We did not find any intention (of disobedience) in him.
When We said to the angels: ‘Prostrate yourselves before Adam.’ They prostrated, except Iblis. He refused.
Then We said: ‘O Adam! This is indeed an enemy of yours and your wife’s. Therefore, do not let him expel you both from Paradise; in that case, you may face hardship.
Indeed, you shall neither be hungry in it nor naked.
And indeed, you shall neither be thirsty in it nor suffer from the sun.’
Then Satan whispered to him and said: ‘O Adam! Shall I show you the Tree of Eternity and a kingdom that will never diminish?’

Then they both ate from it, and their private parts became exposed to them. So they began to stitch the leaves of Paradise over themselves. And (in this way) Adam failed to observe his Lord’s command. So, he missed the mark (and thus could not achieve eternal life).* [* Adam’s act of eating from the tree took place before he was blessed with prophethood. This incident took place in Paradise, whereas he was honoured with the blessing of prophethood after coming down from heaven and settling on the earth, as indicated in the next two verses: 122–123.]
Then his Lord chose him (for His proximity), turned to him (with mercy) and guided (him to repentance).
He said: ‘Get down from here, all together, being enemies of one another! And if guidance should come to you from Me, then whoever follows My guidance will not go astray, nor will he be miserable.
And whoever turns away from My remembrance, there shall be indeed a depressing life for him. And We shall raise him blind on the Day of Resurrection.’
He will say: ‘My Lord! Why have You raised me blind, though I used to see (before)?’
He will say: ‘This is how it is. Our revelations came to you, but you forgot them. And thus, you are forgotten today.’
And thus, We recompense the one who exceeds the limits and does not believe in the revelations of his Lord. And indeed, the punishment of the Hereafter is more severe and more lasting.
Does it not guide them (to see) how many generations We have destroyed before them in whose dwellings they walk around? There are indeed signs in that for those who possess reason.
Had it not been for a previous word from your Lord and an appointed term (for their punishment), the inevitable (calamity) would have happened.

So be patient with what they say. And glorify the praise of your Lord before sunrise and before sunset. And glorify Him in the hours of the night and at the ends of the day, so that you may be pleased (with the reward).

Do not extend your glance towards what We have provided certain groups of them to enjoy as the splendour of this worldly life so that We may test them through it. And the provision of your Lord (in the Hereafter) is better and more lasting.
And command your family for prayer and be steadfast in observing it yourself. We do not ask you for any provision. It is We Who provide for you. And the Ultimate End belongs to God-consciousness.
And they say: ‘Why does he not bring us a (visible) sign from his Lord?’ Has there not come to them a clear proof (i.e. the Qur’an), which is (mentioned) in the earlier Scriptures?

Had We destroyed them with a punishment before this (revelation), they would certainly have said: ‘Our Lord! Why did You not send a messenger to us, for then we would have followed Your revelations before we became disgraced and humiliated?’
Say (to them): ‘Each (of us) is waiting. So keep waiting! For you will soon come to know who the people of the straight path are and who are rightly guided.’
The (time of) reckoning for the people has drawn closer, yet they are turning away heedlessly.
Whatever new Reminder comes to them from their Lord, they only listen to it playfully,
with their hearts (totally) distracted. Those who are unjust secretly whisper: ‘Is not this (man) just a human being like yourselves? Will you surrender to (his) magic while you see clearly?’
He said: ‘My Lord knows every word (spoken) in the heaven and the earth. And He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.’
Yet they said: ‘(These Reminders are) confused dreams! Rather, he has fabricated it! Rather, he is a poet! Let him bring us a sign like the former (messengers) were sent (with signs).’
Not a (single) town We destroyed before them ever believed (after receiving the signs). Will these (pagans) then now believe?
We did not send (the messengers) before you, except men to whom We revealed. So ask the People of the Reminder if you do not know.
And We did not make them bodies (of such nature) that would not eat food, nor were they to abide forever.
Then We fulfilled the promise to them. So We delivered them and whomever We willed, and We destroyed the transgressors.
Indeed, We have (now) revealed to you a Book in which there is your Reminder. Will you still not understand?
And how many towns engaged in wrongdoing have We demolished and (then) raised other people after them?
So, when they felt Our Might (coming upon them), see how they tried to escape.
(The angels said:) ‘Do not try to escape (now)! Return to the pleasures you were given to enjoy and to your dwellings. So that you may be questioned!’
They said: ‘Woe to us! We have, indeed, been wrongdoers!’
This remained their cry until We made them a cut-off field, extinct (like burnt ashes).
We did not create the heavens, earth, or whatever is between them just for play.
Had We wished to have some entertainment, We would have indeed found it in Our presence if We were to do (so).
Rather, We cast truth against falsehood, and it crushes its head. And behold, it (i.e. falsehood) vanishes away. And woe to you for what you allege (about Allah).
To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth. And those who remain in His presence are neither arrogant to worship Him, nor do they get tired.
They glorify (Him) night and day (and) they never take a momentary break.
Have they taken gods from the earth, who raise (the dead) alive?
Had there been gods in them (i.e. the heavens and the earth) other than Allah, they would surely have collapsed. So, Glory be to Allah, the Lord of the Throne, above what they attribute to Him.
He cannot be questioned about what He does, but they (all) shall be questioned (about their actions).

Have they taken gods besides Him? Say: ‘Produce your evidence! This is a reminder for those with me and a reminder for those before me.’ Instead, most of them do not know the truth, so they are disregardful.
We never sent any messenger before you, but We revealed to him: ‘There is no god except Me.’ So worship Me.
And they say: ‘The Most-Compassionate (Lord) has taken (angels as His) children.’ Glory be to Him. Instead, they are (His) honoured servants.
They do not precede Him in speech, but they act by His command.
He knows what is before them and what is behind them, and they do not intercede except for him with whom He is pleased. And they (themselves) are apprehensive in fear of Him.
Whosoever among them says, ‘I am a god besides Him’, we shall reward such (a person) with Hell. That is how We recompense the wrongdoers.

Have the disbelievers not considered that the heavens and the earth were joined together (at one time), and then We split them apart? And We originated (the life of) all living organisms (on earth) from water. Will they still not believe (after learning these facts mentioned in the Qur’an)?

And We set firm mountains in the earth, lest it should shake with them (while rotating in its orbit). And We made on this (earth) broad ways so that they might find routes (to different destinations).

We made the upper spheres of the earth a secure roof.* Yet they are disregardful of its signs. [* These upper spheres protect the earth’s inhabitants from the harmful waves and radiation coming from space.]
He is the One Who created the night and the day, the sun and the moon—each moving in an orbit.
And We have not granted eternity to any human before you (in this world). So, if you die, will they live forever?
Every soul is sure to taste death, and We try you (all) with evil and with good as a test. And to Us shall you be returned.

And whenever the disbelievers see you, they only take you for mockery, (saying:) ‘Is this the one who speaks (ill) of your gods?’ But it is they who deny the remembrance of the Most-Compassionate (Lord).
Human being was created hasty (and impatient by his disposition). I shall soon show you My signs. So do not ask Me to hasten (the punishment).
And they say: ‘When will this promise (of punishment) be fulfilled if you are truthful?’

If only the disbelievers knew that a time would come when they would not be able to stop the Fire from (burning) their faces and their backs, nor would they be helped (then they would not seek to hasten it).
Instead, it will come upon them suddenly and confound them. So, neither will they be able to avert it nor be granted respite.
And certainly messengers were mocked before you. But those who ridiculed them were overtaken by what they used to mock.
Say: ‘Who can guard you by night and day from (the punishment of) the Most-Compassionate (Lord)?’ Instead, they are disregardful of their Lord’s remembrance.
Do they have gods besides Us who can defend them (from the punishment)? They can neither help themselves nor will they be given protection against Us.

Instead, We provided enjoyment to these (Meccans) and their fathers until they lived on for a long time. Do they not see how We are shrinking the land (under their control) from its borders? So, are they the ones who will prevail?
Say: ‘I only warn you through revelation.’ But the deaf will not hear the call whenever they are warned.
Yet if a (minor) breath of your Lord’s punishment touches them, they will surely say: ‘Woe to us! Truly, we have been wrongdoers.’

And We shall set up the scales of justice on the Day of Resurrection. And no soul shall be wronged in the least. Even if it be the weight of a grain of mustard seed, We shall produce it, and We suffice as Reckoners.
And indeed, We gave Musa (Moses) and Harun (Aaron) the standard (to distinguish between right and wrong), a light and a reminder for those who are God-conscious.
Those who fear their Lord without seeing, and they are apprehensive of the Hour.
This (Qur’an) is a blessed Reminder that We have revealed. Are you (all) going to deny it?
We had indeed bestowed upon Ibrahim (Abraham) rectitude of his conduct long before, and We knew him.
When he said to his (foster) father and his people: ‘What are these idols to which you are so devoted (in worship)?’
They said: ‘We found our fathers worshipping them.’
He said: ‘Surely, you and your fathers have been in clear error.’
They said: ‘Have you brought us the truth, or are you just joking?’
He said: ‘Instead, your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, Who brought them into existence. And I am among those who bear witness to that.
By Allah, I shall surely plan against your idols after you have gone away, turning your backs.’
Then he broke them all into pieces—except the biggest of them—so they might return to it.
They said: ‘Who has done this to our gods? Surely, he is among the wrongdoers.’
Some people said: ‘We heard a young man talking about them. He is called Ibrahim (Abraham).’
They said: ‘Bring him before the eyes of the people so that they may bear witness (against him).’
They said: ‘Was it you who did this to our gods, O Ibrahim (Abraham)?’
He said: ‘Surely, this one—the chief of them—has done it. So ask them (i.e. the broken idols) if they can speak.’
Then they turned to themselves and said (to one another): ‘Indeed, it is you who are the wrongdoers.’
Then they were made to hang their heads (in shame). They said, ‘You certainly know that they cannot speak.’
He said: ‘Do you then worship something besides Allah, which can neither benefit you nor harm you?
Shame on you and on what you worship besides Allah! Do you not yet understand?’
They said: ‘Burn him and help your gods if you are to take action!’
We said: ‘O fire, be (comfortingly) cool and safe for Ibrahim (Abraham)!’
They had intended an evil plot against him, but We made them the biggest losers.
And We rescued him and Lut* towards the land We had blessed for all nations. [* Prophet Lut was the nephew of Prophet Ibrahim, the son of his brother Haran, from Mesopotamia (Iraq).]
And We gave him (i.e. Abraham) Ishaq (Isaac), and Ya‘qub (Jacob) as an added gift, and each of them We made righteous.

We made them leaders, guiding (people) by Our command. And We revealed to them the performance of virtuous deeds, the maintenance of prayers and the giving of charity (alms). And they were (always) Our devoted worshippers.

And (as for) Lut, We bestowed upon him (the wisdom of) judgement and knowledge, and We delivered him from the town whose people used to commit acts of indecency. Indeed, they were shameless people with an evil character.
And We caused him to enter Our Mercy. Indeed, he was among the righteous.
And (remember) Nuh (Noah), when he called (his Lord) long before. So We responded to him and rescued him and his family from the great disaster.
And We helped him against the people who rejected Our Signs. Indeed, they were evil people. So We drowned them all.

And (remember) Dawud (David) and Sulayman (Solomon) when they both gave the judgement concerning the crop field when the sheep of a tribe entered into it by night (and destroyed it). And We were witness to their decision.

And We gave its better understanding to Sulayman (Solomon). And to all, We blessed sound judgement and knowledge. And We made the mountains and the birds subservient to glorify (Our praises) together with Dawud (David). And We were the doers (of such things).
And We taught him the art of making (armoured) garments for you to protect you in your battle. Will you then be grateful?
And (We made) the stormy wind (subservient) for Sulayman (Solomon), which pursued its course by his command towards the land which We had blessed. And We are the knower of all things.
And among the devils (some were made subservient) who dove for him (into the seas) and accomplished tasks other than that. And We were watchful over them.
And (remember) Ayyub (Job) when he called his Lord: ‘Indeed suffering has befallen me, and You are the Most Merciful of the merciful.’
So We responded to him and removed his suffering. And We gave him (back) his family, along with others like them, as a mercy from Us and a reminder for the worshippers.
And (remember) Isma‘il (Ishmael), Idris (Enoch) and Dhu al-Kifl; each of them was among the patient.
And We admitted them into Our mercy. Indeed, they were among the righteous.

And (remember) Dhu al-Nun*, when he went away angrily, thinking that We would not put him to hardship (on his journey). Then he called (Us) in the darkness: ‘There is no god except You. Glory be to You! I have indeed been among those who wrong (their souls).’ [* His name is Yunus b. Matta (Jonah), and he was the Prophet who was swallowed by a whale.]
So We responded to him and delivered him from grief. And that is how We save the believers.
And (remember) Zakariyya (Zachariah) when he called to his Lord: ‘My Lord! Do not leave me alone (i.e. childless), though You are the best of inheritors.’

So We responded to him and gave him Yahya (John), and We cured (the infertility of) his wife for him. Indeed, they were always quick in (performing) good deeds. And they would supplicate Us, in hope and in fear, and they were always humble before Us.
And (remember Mary) who guarded her chastity. So We breathed in her Our Spirit, and made her and her son a sign for all nations.
Indeed, this community of yours is one community. And I am your Lord. So worship Me (alone).
And they fragmented their religion between themselves (into sects). All (of them) will return to Us.
So, whoever does righteous deeds and is a believer, there shall be no denial of his effort, and We are indeed writing (it) down for him.
And it is forbidden for (the people of) any town which We have destroyed (to return to the world). They shall never return,
till the time when Gog and Magog are let loose and race down from every elevated place,

and the true promise draws near (to its fulfilment), behold, the eyes of the disbelievers shall stare in horror, (and they will cry out:) ‘Woe to us! We have certainly been heedless to this (day)! Rather, we have been wrongdoers!’
Indeed, you and what you worship besides Allah shall be the fuel of Hell. You will (all) enter it.
Had they been (true) gods, they would not have entered it. All of them will remain in it forever.
For them, there shall be groaning in it, and they shall not hear anything (else) in it.
Indeed, those for whom (the promise of) the best reward has gone forth from Us, it is they who will be kept away from it (i.e. Hell).
They will not hear (even) its slightest murmur. And they will remain forever in what their souls desire.
The Greatest Horror will not disturb them, and the angels will greet them, (saying:) ‘This is your Day, which you were promised.’
On that Day, We shall roll up the sky like the rolling of writing scrolls. As We began the first creation, so shall We bring it back—a promise (binding) upon Us. Indeed, We are going to do it.
And indeed, We wrote in al-Zabur (the Psalms) after the Reminder that My pious servants shall inherit the earth (in the Hereafter).
There is, indeed, in this, a clear message (of achieving the objective) for a worshipful people.
And (Esteemed Prophet,) We did not send you but as a mercy for all (creatures of) the worlds.
Say: ‘It is revealed to me that your God is only One God. So, are you going to submit (to Him)?’
But if they turn away, say: ‘I have proclaimed the message to you all equally. And I do not know (of my own accord) if what you have been promised is near or far.
Indeed, He knows whatever is spoken openly, and He knows whatever you conceal.
And I do not know (of my own accord) if this (delaying of the punishment) is a trial for you and an enjoyment for a while.’
He (the Esteemed Prophet) said: ‘My Lord! Pass the true judgement! Our Lord is the Most-Compassionate, the One Whose help is sought against what you ascribe (to Him).’
O people! Be conscious of your Lord. Indeed, the (violent) quake of the Hour is a terrible thing.

On the Day you see it, every breastfeeding mother will think no more of (her baby) that she was suckling. And every pregnant woman will miscarry her burden. And you will think people are drunk, though they will not be drunk. But Allah’s punishment will be that severe.
And among the people are those who dispute regarding Allah without any knowledge and who follow every rebellious Satan,
for whom it has been decreed that whoever takes him for a friend, he shall lead him astray and conduct him towards the punishment of the Blaze.

O people! If you are in doubt about the Resurrection, then (remember that) We (initially) created you from dust (i.e. your chemical genesis was from the extract of clay).* Then (your biological genesis was) from a fertilised egg (i.e. a zygote**). Then, from a clinging mass (i.e. an embryo).*** And then from a lump of flesh looking chewed (i.e. the foetus in the early stages of its development), partly shaped and partly unshaped (having some parts recognisable and others unrecognisable), so that We may manifest (Our power) to you. And We cause whom We are pleased to stay in the wombs till a specified term. Then We bring you out as an infant. Then (We rear you) so that you may attain maturity. And among you, some are caused to die (before old age). And among you, some are brought back to the most senile age so that he knows nothing after (having possessed) some knowledge. Likewise, you see the earth completely sterile (appearing lifeless), yet when We shower water upon it, it stirs (with freshness), swells (with fertility) and produces all kinds of beautiful pairs of plants. [* Dust comprises minerals, metals, salts and other chemical elements such as calcium, sodium, phosphorus, potassium, silicon, chlorine, iodine, fluorine, magnesium, zinc, sulphur, iron, etc. These and other clay elements also form a balanced diet essential for nourishment. The animals whose meat and milk we consume graze upon vegetation. The grains, vegetables and fruits that constitute our diet benefit from the soil they grow in and absorb all its chemicals.   ** In Arabic, the word ‘al-nutfa’ is mentioned, and it refers to a zygote, which is a mixture of male and female gametes. It is the new single cell formed after the union of a sperm and ovum.   *** The word ‘alaqa’ in this verse describes the human embryo from days 7–24, which clings to the endometrium of the uterus in the same way that a leech clings to the skin. Just as the leech derives blood from the host, the human embryo derives blood from the decidua or pregnant endometrium. It is remarkable how much the embryo of 23–24 days resembles a leech. No microscopes were available in the 7th century, so none would have known that the human embryo at this stage was clinging to the endometrium and had a leech-like appearance.]
That is because Allah is the Absolute Reality. And it is He Who gives life to the dead, and He is All-Capable of everything.
And the (Final) Hour is coming; there is no doubt about it. And Allah will resurrect those who are in the graves.
Among the people are those who dispute about Allah without knowledge, guidance or an enlightening Book,
turning aside to lead (others) astray from Allah’s way. For such, there is disgrace in this world. And on the Day of Resurrection, We shall make him taste the punishment of the burning.
That is due to what your hands have sent ahead and because Allah is never unjust to the servants.

And among the people are those who worship Allah upon the edge (of faith): if good fortune befalls him, he is content with it; but if a trial afflicts him, he turns back (entirely). He loses this world and the Hereafter. That is indeed the manifest loss.
He worships besides Allah that which neither harms him nor benefits him. Indeed, that is the farthest error.
He worships someone whose harm is closer than his benefit. What an evil patron and what an evil companion!
Allah will surely admit those who believe and perform righteous deeds into Gardens with rivers flowing beneath them. Certainly, Allah does whatever He wills.

Whoever thinks that Allah will not help him (i.e. the Esteemed Prophet) in this world and the Hereafter, let him stretch a rope to the ceiling and then hang himself. And let him see if his scheme does away with (the cause of) his rage.
Thus, We revealed this (Qur’an) as clear verses. And indeed, Allah guides whomever He wills.

Indeed, the believers, the Jews, the Sabaeans, the Christians, the Magians (or Zoroastrians) and the idolaters—Allah will indeed judge between them on the Day of Resurrection. Surely Allah is witness over all things.

Have you not realised that whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth prostrates to Allah: the sun, the moon, the stars, the mountains, the trees, the animals and many human beings? But for many the punishment has become due. And whomever Allah disgraces will find none who may bring him honour. Indeed, Allah does whatever He wills.
These are two opposing groups who dispute about their Lord. As for those who disbelieve, garments of fire will be cut out for them, and boiling liquid will be poured over their heads,
by which whatever is in their bellies, along with (their) skins, will be melted.
And for them shall be iron rods (with which they will be struck).
Whenever they desire to leave it due to the intense suffering, they will be turned back into it (and told): ‘Taste the punishment of the burning!’

Surely, Allah will admit those who believe and perform righteous deeds to the Gardens with rivers flowing beneath them. They will be adorned with bracelets of gold and pearls. And their garments in it will be of silk.
And they will be guided to the purest speech and directed to the path of the All-Praised.

Indeed, those who disbelieve and block (others) from Allah’s way and from the Sacred Mosque (the Ka‘ba), which We have assigned for all the people who reside therein and the visitors alike, and whoever desires to violate (its sanctity) by some wrongdoing, We shall make him taste a painful punishment.

And (recall) when We assigned for Ibrahim (Abraham) the site of the House (of Allah for its construction, saying:) ‘Do not ascribe any partners to Me and purify My House for those who circulate (the Ka‘ba), and those who bow and prostrate themselves.’
And proclaim the Hajj (the Pilgrimage) among people: they will come to you on foot and upon every lean camel from all deep and distant places,

so that they may witness (some) benefits for them and mention Allah’s Name during specified days over the livestock He has provided them (to sacrifice). Then eat of them and feed the poor (and) the needy.
Then let them accomplish their shaving and cleansing acts (upon removing the ihram), fulfil their vows (or complete the remaining rituals of the Pilgrimage) and circumambulate the Ancient House.

That (is what has been commanded). And whoever venerates Allah’s sanctities—it is better for him in the sight of his Lord. And the cattle are made lawful to you except for what is explicitly recited to you (from the prohibitions). Therefore, avoid the filth of idols and avoid false words,

while being persons of pure faith and devotion to Allah, without associating any partners with Him. Whoever ascribes partners to Allah, it is as though he has fallen from the sky, then the birds snatch him away, or the wind carries him away to a place far and wide.
That (is how it is). And whoever venerates Allah’s signs (i.e. the sacred rituals, places and commands)—indeed, that is from the God-consciousness of the heart.
You have utility in them until a fixed time. Then, their lawful place of sacrifice is near the Ancient House.

And for every community, We have prescribed a sacrificial rite that they may invoke Allah’s name over the livestock He has provided them (at the time of their slaughtering). Your God is the One God. So to Him, you should submit. And give glad tidings to the humble,
whose hearts tremble with reverence when Allah is mentioned, and who are patient through whatever befalls them, and who maintain the prayer and spend (in charity) out of what We have provided them.

And the sacrificial animals (i.e. camels and cows, etc.), We have made them for you among the signs of Allah. There is (much) good for you in them. So invoke Allah’s name over them as they line up for sacrifice. Once they have fallen upon their flanks (after being slaughtered), eat from them and feed the needy—those who are content and those who beg. Thus have We subjected them for your benefit so that you may give thanks.

It is neither their flesh nor their blood that reaches Allah. Instead, it is your God-consciousness that reaches Him. That is how He has subjected them to your benefit. So that you may proclaim Allah’s greatness for what He has guided you to. And give glad tidings to the virtuously excellent.
Indeed, Allah defends those who believe. Verily, Allah dislikes every ungrateful traitor.
Permission (to fight in self-defence) is granted to those upon whom war is imposed, for they have been wronged. And Allah is indeed All-Capable of helping them.

They are the ones who were unjustly expelled from their homes only because they said: ‘Our Lord is Allah.’ Had Allah not defended the people from one another, many monasteries, churches, synagogues and mosques would have been destroyed, where Allah’s Name is mentioned abundantly. And Allah will surely help those who help His cause. Indeed, Allah is All-Strong, All-Mighty.
(They are) those who, if We granted them power in the land, will establish the prayer, give the alms due, enjoin good and forbid evil. And to Allah is the end of all affairs.
And if they deny you (now), then (already) the people of Nuh (Noah) have denied before them, and ‘Ad and Thamud (too).
And (similarly) the people of Ibrahim (Abraham) and the people of Lut (Lot).

And the inhabitants of Madyan ([Midian] did the same thing). And Musa (Moses) was also denied. But I granted respite to the disbelievers. Then, I overtook them (with punishment). So how terrible was My retribution!
How many towns have We destroyed while their inhabitants were wrongdoers, lying fallen on their roofs, and (their) deserted wells and lofty palaces (also)?

Have they (the Meccans) not travelled through the land so that they may have hearts by which they may understand or ears by which they may hear? Indeed, it is not the eyes that turn blind, but the hearts become blind, which are in the breasts.
They demand you to hasten the punishment, but Allah shall never break His promise. Indeed, a single day in the sight of your Lord is like a thousand years by your calculation.
How many towns did I grant respite while their inhabitants were wrongdoers? Then, I overtook it (with punishment). And towards Me is the destination.
Say: ‘O humankind! I am only a clear warner to you.’
Those who believe and perform righteous deeds shall have forgiveness and honourable sustenance.
But (as for) those who strive in (refuting) Our revelations, seeking to defeat (Us), they shall be the inmates of the Hellfire.

And We did not send any messenger or prophet before you except that when he would recite, Satan would cast (doubts in people’s minds) about his recitation. But Allah nullifies whatever Satan casts. Then Allah confirms His revelations (in the hearts and minds of the believers), and Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

(It is) so that Allah may make these (false doubts) which Satan casts (into their minds) a trial for those in whose hearts is a disease (of hypocrisy) and those whose hearts are hardened (with disbelief). Indeed, the wrongdoers are in extreme defiance.

And that those who have been given knowledge may realise that it (i.e. the recited revelation) is the truth from your Lord, and thus believe in it (by rejecting Satan’s interjections) and that their hearts may humble before Him. Indeed, Allah guides the believers to the straight path.
Yet those who disbelieve will persist in (their) doubt about it until the Hour overtakes them suddenly or the punishment of a barren day comes upon them.
On that Day, all sovereignty shall belong to Allah. He will judge between them. Then those who believe and perform righteous deeds shall be in Gardens of Bliss.
And those who disbelieve and reject Our revelations, for them shall be a humiliating punishment.
And those who migrated in Allah’s way, and were then slain or died, Allah shall indeed provide them with a beautiful provision. Indeed, Allah is the best of providers.
He will surely admit them into a place they will be well pleased with. And indeed Allah is All-Knowing, All-Forbearing.
That is so. And whoever retaliates in equal measure to what he was made to suffer and is then oppressed again, Allah will surely help him. Indeed, Allah is All-Excusing, All-Forgiving.
That is because Allah merges the night into the day and merges the day into the night. And indeed, Allah is All-Hearing, All-Seeing.
That is because it is Allah (alone) Who is the Truth, and whatever else they worship besides Him is false. And indeed, it is Allah Who is the All-Exalted, the All-Great.
Have you not seen that Allah sends down water from the sky so the earth becomes fresh and green? Indeed, Allah is All-Gracious All-Aware.
To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And indeed, Allah is the Self-Sufficient, the All-Praised.

Have you not seen that Allah has made subservient to you whatever is on the earth, and (that) the ships sail in the sea by His command, and that He sustains the sky (through a cosmic system) lest it should fall on the earth, except (when He wills, they will collide) by His leave? Indeed, Allah is Most-Kind (and) All-Merciful towards people.
And He is the One Who brought you to life, then He causes you to die, then He will bring you to life (again). Indeed, humans are very ungrateful.

For every nation, We have prescribed a ritual act of devotion which they have to perform. Therefore, they should not argue with you regarding the matter (of Allah’s command). And invite (them) to your Lord. Indeed, you are on the straight (path of) guidance.
If they argue with you, say: ‘Allah knows best whatever you are doing.
Allah will judge between you on the Day of Resurrection concerning what you used to differ about.’
Do you not know that Allah knows whatever is in the heavens and the earth? That is surely (written) in a Book. That is indeed easy for Allah.
And they worship besides Allah that for which He has sent down no authority and about which they have no knowledge. And the wrongdoers shall have no helper (on the Day of Judgement).

When Our enlightening verses are recited to them, you perceive denial on the faces of those who disbelieve. It is almost as if they would attack those who recite our verses to them. Say: ‘Shall I tell you what is far worse than that? The Fire which Allah has promised to those who disbelieve. And it is an evil destination!’

O people! An illustration is being given; listen to it (carefully). Indeed, those (idols) you worship besides Allah cannot create (even) a fly, even if they were to come together for that (purpose). And if the fly snatches something from them, they could not rescue that from it. How helpless are the seeker (i.e. the idol worshippers) and the sought (i.e. the idol gods)?
And they did not regard Allah with the proper regard due to Him. Indeed, Allah is All-Strong, All-Mighty.
Allah chooses messengers from both angels and men. Indeed, Allah is All-Hearing, All-Seeing.
He knows what is before them (i.e. their future) and what is behind them (i.e. their past). And to Allah, all matters are returned.
O believers! Bow down and prostrate yourselves. And worship your Lord, and do (what is) good, so that you may prosper.

And strive hard in the way of Allah with a striving worthy of Him. He has chosen you and not placed upon you any hardship in religion, the faith of your father Ibrahim (Abraham). He named you Muslims in the past (scriptures) and in this (Book, the Qur’an). So that the (final) Messenger may be a witness over you, and you may be bearers of witness over humankind. So, establish the prayer, pay the mandatory charity and hold fast to Allah. He is your Guardian—an excellent Guardian and an excellent Helper.
Indeed, the believers have prospered.
Those who are humble in their prayers,
who turn away from idle talk,
who give the alms (i.e. mandatory charity),
who guard their private parts (protecting their chastity),
except with their wives or whom their right hands possess (i.e. the female captives in their lawful custody), for then they are not blameworthy;
but whoever seeks beyond that, they are the transgressors;
and those who keep their trusts and their pledges,
and who are (persistently) observant of their prayers,
these are the ones who will be the heirs;
who shall inherit Paradise, abiding therein forever.

Indeed, We created human being from an extract (i.e. the chemical ingredients) of clay.* [* Dust comprises minerals, metals, salts and other chemical elements such as calcium, sodium, phosphorus, potassium, silicon, chlorine, iodine, fluorine, magnesium, zinc, sulphur, iron, etc. These and other clay elements also form a balanced diet essential for nourishment. The animals whose meat and milk we consume graze upon vegetation. The grains, vegetables and fruits that constitute our diet benefit from the soil they grow in and absorb all its chemicals.]

Then, We placed him as a fertilised egg (i.e. a zygote*) in a secure abode (of the mother’s womb). [* In Arabic, the word ‘ al-nutfa ’ is mentioned, and it refers to a zygote, which is a mixture of male and female gametes. It is the new single cell formed after the union of a sperm and ovum.]

Then We made that zygote a clinging mass.* Then We developed that clinging mass into a lump of flesh (chewed with teeth). Then, out of this chewed lump, We developed a structure of bones and clothed the bones with flesh (and muscle). Later, We developed it (gradually) into a new form. So blessed is Allah, the Best of creators. [* The word ‘ alaqa ’ in this verse describes the human embryo from days 7–24, which clings to the endometrium of the uterus in the same way that a leech clings to the skin. Just as the leech derives blood from the host, the human embryo derives blood from the decidua or pregnant endometrium. It is remarkable how much the embryo of 23–24 days resembles a leech. No microscopes were available in the 7th century, so none would have known that the human embryo at this stage was clinging to the endometrium and had a leech-like appearance.]
Then, after that, you will surely die.
Then you will indeed be raised on the Day of Resurrection.

And certainly, We have created above you seven tracks.* And We have never been heedless of (Our) creation. [* Seven tracks may refer to the seven heavens, being above one another, according to the description of traditional cosmology. It may also refer to the paths of the five visible planets, including the sun and the moon, or the celestial spheres in which the sun and the planets move, traditionally known as ‘ aflak ’. Another possible meaning could be the seven magnetic fields around the vast atmospheric expanse of the earth for safeguarding the cosmic system.]
And We sent down water from the sky in a measured manner and settled it in the earth (shaping it into preliminary forms of oceans). And indeed, We have the power to make it disappear (as vapours).
Then, with it, We caused gardens of date palms and grapevines to grow for you. There are many (other) fruits in it for you, and you eat from them.
There is another tree that grows forth from (the region of) Mount Sinai, which produces oil and seasoning for those who eat.

And there is indeed a lesson for you in the cattle: We make you drink (milk prepared from some constituents) of what is in their bellies. And in them are many (other) advantages for you, and you eat (some) of them (as well).
And upon them and ships, you are carried (to different destinations).
Indeed, We sent Nuh (Noah) to his people. And he said: ‘O my people, worship Allah! You have no god besides Him. Will you not then be conscious (of Him)?’

The chiefs of his people who disbelieved said: ‘He is nothing but a human being like you. He desires to gain superiority over you. If Allah had pleased, He would have surely sent down angels (instead). We have never heard of such a thing from our forefathers.
He is just a madman. So bear with him for a while.’
He said: ‘My Lord! Help me as they have rejected me.’

So We revealed to him: ‘Make the Ark under Our eyes and according to Our revelation. So when Our command comes, and the oven gushes out (the stream of water), board into it two pairs of every kind of animal (male and female). And (take) your family, except those against whom the word has already been issued. And do not address Me concerning those who are wrongdoers. They shall indeed be drowned.’
So when you and those with you have settled on the Ark, say: ‘All praise be to Allah, Who has saved us from the wrongdoing people.’
And say: ‘My Lord! Land me with a blessed landing, for You are the best of those who bring to the shore.’
There are indeed signs in all of this. And indeed, We have always been testing (people).
Then We raised after them another generation.
And We sent them a messenger from among themselves (saying): ‘Worship Allah. You have no god other than Him. Will you not then be conscious of Him?’

Said the chiefs of his people, who disbelieved and denied the meeting of the Hereafter, and to whom We had given abundance of wealth in this world’s life: ‘This is just a human being like you. He eats what you eat and drinks what you drink.
If you obey a human being like yourselves, you will indeed be losers.
Does he promise you that once you are dead and become dust and bones, you shall be brought out (alive again)?
Far-fetched, far-fetched, is what you are promised!
There is nothing but our life in this world. We die, and we live (in this world only). We shall not be raised again.
He is just a man who has fabricated lies against Allah, and we will not believe in him.’
He said: ‘My Lord! Help me as they have rejected me.’
He (Allah) said: ‘In a little while, they will become regretful.’
Then, the dreadful Blast seized them rightfully, and We turned them into rubbish. So away with the unjust people.
Then, after them, We raised other generations.
No nation can advance its term, nor can it delay.

Then We sent Our messengers successively. Whenever a messenger would come to its community, they would deny him. So, We caused them to follow one another and turned them into folktales. So away with the disbelieving people!
Then We sent Musa (Moses) and his brother Harun (Aaron) with Our signs and a manifest authority,
to Pharaoh and his chiefs—but they acted arrogantly, and they were oppressive people.
So they said: ‘Shall we believe in two humans like ourselves while their people are our slaves?’
They rejected both of them, so they (also) became among those destroyed.
We gave Musa (Moses) the Book; perhaps they might follow the right direction.
And We made the son of Maryam (Mary) and his mother a (great) sign. And We gave them shelter on a highland, a place of comfort, watered by a flowing spring.
O messengers! Eat good things and act righteously. Indeed, I am All-Knowing of what you do.
Indeed, this—your community—is one community, and I am your Lord. Therefore, be conscious of Me.
But they divided their matter (i.e. their religion) into sects based upon different scriptures. Each group is pleased with what (portion) it has.
So leave them in their overwhelming ignorance for a while.
Do they think, since We have provided them with wealth and children,
that We are eager to honour them with all kinds of good? No! They are not aware (of the reality).
Indeed, those who are apprehensive in fear of their Lord,
and those who believe in the Signs of their Lord,
and those who do not ascribe partners to their Lord,
and those who give (in charity) whatever they can, while their hearts are trembling with fear for they are returning to their Lord—
it is they who hasten towards good deeds and always take the lead in them.
And We do not task any soul except to the extent of its capacity, and with Us is a Book that speaks the truth, and they shall not be wronged.
However, their hearts are in overwhelming ignorance regarding it. Yet they have other (evil) deeds besides what they continue to do.
But as soon as We overtake their affluent ones with punishment, they cry for help.
Do not cry out today! Indeed, you will not be helped by Us.
Certainly, My revelations were recited to you, but you used to turn back on your heels,
being arrogant to this (Book), talking nonsense in your night sessions.
Have they not contemplated the word (of Allah), or has something come to them which did not come to their forefathers?
Or could they not recognise their Messenger, so they deny him?
Or do they say: ‘There is madness in him?’ Instead, he has brought them the truth, but most of them are resentful of the truth.

Had the truth followed their low desires, the heavens and the earth would have surely perished along with all those in them. Rather, We have brought them their reminder, but they are disregardful of their reminder.
Do you ask them for any payment? Yet your Lord’s recompense is better, and He is the best of providers.
Surely, you invite them to a straight path.
Indeed, those who do not believe in the Hereafter, most certainly deviate from the path.
Were We to show them mercy and remove whatever evil was upon them, they would surely persist, wandering confused in their rebellion.
We already overtook them with punishment, yet they were not submissive to their Lord, nor did they humble themselves (to receive His mercy).
When We open a door of severe punishment upon them, behold, they will fall into utter despair.
He is the One Who made for you (your) ears, eyes and hearts. How seldom are you thankful!
He is the One Who created you on the earth, and to Him shall you be gathered.
He is the One Who gives life and causes death. And to Him belongs the alternation of the night and the day. Do you not then understand?
But they say just like what the ancients said.
They say: ‘What, when we are dead and become dust and bones, shall we be resurrected?
Surely, we have heard such promises, and (so did) our forefathers before. These are just the fairy tales of the ancients.’
Say: ‘To whom does the earth belong and whoever is upon it, if you know?’
They will (promptly) say: ‘To Allah.’ Say: ‘Will you not then be mindful of advice?’
Say: ‘Who is the Lord of the seven heavens and the Lord of the Great Throne?’
They will (promptly) say: ‘To Allah.’ Say: ‘Will you not then be conscious of Him?’
Say: ‘In Whose Hand is the dominion of all things and Who provides shelter (to everything), and against Whom no shelter can be provided if you know?’
They will (promptly) say: ‘To Allah.’ Say: ‘How then are you deceived?
Instead, We have brought them the truth, but they are surely liars.

Allah has never taken any child, nor is there any god besides Him. Otherwise, each god would have taken away what he created, and some of them would have tried to dominate one another. Glory be to Allah above what they ascribe.
The Knower of the Unseen and the seen! He is above what they ascribe as partners (to Him).
Say: ‘My Lord! If You are going to show me (the punishment) that they are promised,
my Lord, then, do not include me among the wrongdoing people.’
Indeed, We are able to show you (the punishment) that We have promised them.
Repel evil (conduct) with what is best. We know very well what they attribute (to Us).
And say: ‘My Lord! I seek refuge in You from the evil incitements of the devils.
And I seek refuge in You, my Lord, lest they should be present with me.’
Till, when death comes to one of them, he says: ‘My Lord! Send me back (to the world),
so that I may act righteously regarding what I neglected.’ By no means! These are (mere) words that he utters. And behind them stands a barrier till the Day they are resurrected.
So when the trumpet is blown, there shall be no family ties between them on that Day, nor will they ask about each other.
As for those whose scales weigh heavy (with good deeds), they are the ones who will be successful.
As for those whose scales weigh light, they will be the ones who have ruined themselves (and) will remain in Hell forever.
The Fire will scorch their faces, and they will be lying in it with mouths deformed and teeth bulging out.
(They will be told:) ‘Were My revelations not recited to you, but you used to deny them?’
They will say: ‘Our Lord! Our misfortune overwhelmed us, and we were a people gone astray.
Our Lord! Take us out of this. Then if we revert (to denial), we will indeed be wrongdoers.’
He (Allah) will say: ‘Go away into it and do not speak to Me.
Indeed, there was a group of My servants who would say: ‘Our Lord! We have believed, so forgive us and have mercy upon us. You are the Best of the merciful.’
But then you took them in mockery until they made you forget My remembrance. And you always used to laugh at them.
Indeed, I have rewarded them today for having been patient. They are surely successful.’
He (Allah) will say: ‘How many years did you remain on earth?’
They will say: ‘We remained for a day or part of a day; yet ask those who keep count.’
He will say: ‘You remained just for a little while if only you had known!
Did you then suppose that We had created you aimlessly and that you would not be returned to Us?
So, exalted is Allah, the True Sovereign. There is no god except Him, the Lord of the Noble Throne.
And whoever worships another god along with Allah, for which he has no proof, his reckoning is indeed with his Lord. Surely, the disbelievers will not prosper.
And say: ‘My Lord! Forgive and have mercy, for You are the Best of the merciful.’
(This is) a chapter, which We have revealed and made (its rulings) obligatory, and We have revealed in it clear communications so that you may be mindful of advice.

As for the fornicating woman and the fornicating man, strike each of them a hundred stripes. And let not compassion for them overcome you in Allah’s law if you believe in Allah and the Last Day. And let a group of believers witness their punishment.

A fornicator will not marry anyone except a fornicatress or an idolatress. And as for the fornicatress, none will (like to) marry her except a fornicator or an idolater. And this act (of premarital intercourse) is forbidden to the believers.
And those who accuse chaste women (of adultery) and then do not bring four witnesses, strike them eighty stripes and never accept any testimony from them. And they are the transgressors,
except those who repent after that and reform (themselves), for Allah is Most Forgiving, All-Merciful.

And those who accuse their wives (of adultery), but have no witnesses except themselves, then the testimony of one of them shall be four testimonies, swearing by Allah, that he is indeed among the truthful.
And the fifth (is an oath to say) that Allah’s curse shall be upon him if he is among the liars.
And (similarly,) it shall avert the punishment from her by her testifying with four testimonies, swearing by Allah that he is indeed among the liars.
And the fifth (is an oath to say) that Allah’s curse shall be upon her if he (the husband) is among the truthful.
Were it not for Allah’s grace and His mercy upon you (then a great affliction would have befallen you). Indeed, Allah is Most-Clement, All-Wise.

Indeed, those who fabricated the false accusation were a group among yourselves. Do not consider this to be bad for you. Instead, it was a good thing for you. Each of them will bear (the onus of) the sin he has earned. And as for him who undertook its major part among them, he shall have a great punishment.
When you (first) heard this (rumour), why did not the believing men and the believing women think well of their people and declare: ‘This is an obvious slander’?
Why did they (the slanderers) not bring four witnesses to it? So when they could not produce the witnesses, they were the liars in Allah’s sight.
Were it not for Allah’s grace upon you and His mercy in this world and the Hereafter, a great punishment would have indeed afflicted you for your having engaged (in vain discourse) concerning it,

when you passed it from one tongue to the other and spoke with your mouths something which you did not know, supposing it to be a light matter while it was an extremely grave (thing) in Allah’s sight.
And why, when you heard it, did you not say: ‘It is not for us to speak about such a thing. (O Allah!) Glory be to You! This is a heinous slander!’
Allah warns you lest you should ever repeat anything like this if you are (true) believers.
Allah clarifies the revelations for you, and Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

Indeed, those who desire that indecency be spread among the believers will have a painful punishment in this world and the Hereafter. And Allah knows (the intentions of such people), while you do not know.
Were it not for Allah’s grace upon you and His mercy (you would have suffered from a grievous punishment). But Allah is All-Kind, All-Merciful.

O believers! Do not follow in the footsteps of Satan. And whoever follows in Satan’s steps (should know that) he indeed prompts you to indecency and evil. And were it not for Allah’s grace and His mercy upon you, none of you would ever have been pure (from the stain of such slander). But Allah purifies whomever He pleases, and Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

Let the people of grace and bounty from you not swear against giving (charity) to the relatives, the needy and the emigrants in Allah’s way. They should pardon and overlook (their mistake). Do you not love that Allah should forgive you? And Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.
Indeed, those who accuse chaste believing women who are unaware (of indecency) shall be cursed in this world and the Hereafter. And there shall be a great punishment for them,
on the Day when their tongues, their hands, and their feet testify against them concerning whatever they used to do.
On that Day, Allah will pay them their just due in full. And they shall know that Allah is the Manifest Truth.

Wicked women are for wicked men, and wicked men are for wicked women. Virtuous women are for virtuous men, and virtuous men are for virtuous women.* These (chaste people) are innocent of what they (the slanderers) say (about them). They shall have forgiveness and honourable provision. [* So, considering the purity of the Holy Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him), imagine how chaste and pure his wives would be, for Allah has chosen them for him.]
O believers! Do not enter houses other than yours until you ask permission and greet their residents. That is better for you. Perhaps you may become mindful of advice.

But if you do not find anyone therein, do not enter until you have been given permission. And if you are asked to go back, then go back. That is more decent for you, and Allah is All-Knowing of what you do.

There is no blame upon you for entering those houses where no one lives (i.e. public places), where there is something of benefit for you. And Allah knows whatever you reveal and whatever you conceal.
Tell the believing men to lower their gaze and guard their private parts. That is more decent for them. Allah is indeed well aware of what they do.

Tell the believing women to lower their gaze, guard their private parts and not display their charms except what (usually) appears. Moreover, let them draw their head-coverings over their bosoms and not reveal their charms (to anyone) except to their husbands, or their fathers, or their husband’s fathers, or their sons, or their husband’s sons, or their brothers, or their brother’s sons, or their sister’s sons, or their fellow women, or their slave girls, or male attendants lacking sexual desire or children not yet aware of (the attraction of) women’s private parts. And let them not strike their feet such that their hidden charms become known. And repent to Allah, O believers, so that you may prosper.
Marry off the singles among you and those of your male and female slaves who are fit for marriage. If they are poor, Allah will enrich them out of His Grace. And Allah is All-Embracing, All-Knowing.

Those who do not have the means to marry should keep themselves chaste until Allah enriches them out of His Grace. As for those seeking an emancipation contract from among your slaves, make a contract with them (accordingly) if you know any good in them. Give them some of Allah’s wealth, which He has given you (to help them gain freedom). And do not force your slave girls into prostitution when they wish to remain chaste to seek short-term gains in the worldly life. If anyone forces them (into it), then after their coercion, Allah is indeed All-Forgiving, All-Merciful.
Indeed, We have sent down to you clarifying revelations and a descriptive example of those who have passed before you and advice for the God-conscious.

Allah is the Light of the heavens and the earth. His Light is like a niche in which there is a lamp (and) the lamp is in a glass. The glass seems like a glittering star, kindled from a blessed olive tree. It is neither Eastern nor Western (rather universal in its luminosity). Its oil would almost shine, even if no fire touched it. Light upon light! Allah guides whomever He pleases to His Light. And Allah draws such parables for people, and Allah is All-Knowing of everything.* [* The likeness of Allah’s Light refers to the light of Prophet Muhammad (blessings and peace be upon him). The niche refers to his sacred breast wherein the lamp of Prophethood is glowing. This lamp of Prophethood is placed in a glass, which is the radiant heart of the Prophet Muhammad (blessings and peace be upon him). This glass is shining like a glittering star. This lamp is lit with the sacred olive tree, which may refer to the blessing of Divine revelation or the blissful genealogical tree of the Prophets and the Messengers (peace be upon them). The blessing of Prophethood or the guidance of Divine revelation is neither Eastern nor Western. Instead, it is a universal and timeless guidance.
