This lamp of Muhammad’s Prophethood is almost shining due to its inherent qualities even before revelation had touched him. This Light of Prophethood radiates under the Light of the Holy Essence of God. In such a way, Allah guides people to understand the reality of His Light. ‘Abd Allah b. ‘Abbas (may God be pleased with both of them), ‘Abd Allah b. ‘Umar (may God be pleased with both of them), Ka‘b al-Ahbar, Sa‘id b. Jubayr, Abu al-‘Aliya and al-Dahhak b. Mazahim have described these meanings of the Verse of Light. ‘Abd b. Humayd, Ibn Jarir al-Tabari, Ibn al-Mundhir, Ibn Abi Hatim al-Razi, Abu Mansur al-Maturidi, al-Tabarani, Abu al-Layth al-Samarqandi, al-Sulami, Abu Ishaq al-Tha‘labi, Makki b. Abi Talib al-Muqri, al-Baghawi, al-Qadi ‘Iyad, Ibn ‘Atiyya, Ibn al-Jawzi, Fakhr al-Din al-Razi, al-Qurtubi, Ibn Hayyan, Ibn al-Qayyim, Abu Hafs al-Hanbali, al-Suyuti, al-Qastallani, al-Alusi and many other exegetes have reported the above-mentioned meanings. The parable of the light may also refer to the light in the heart of the believers. The niche can be the breast of the believers, the lamp can be the Qur’an and faith, and the glass can be the heart of the believers. Many other such interpretations have also been given for the Verse of Light.]
(That light shines) in the houses (of worship) which Allah has ordered to be raised (high in esteem), and wherein His Name is remembered (abundantly). He is glorified therein, morning and evening.

(Blessed with this light are those) men whom neither trading nor selling can distract from the remembrance of Allah, the maintenance of prayer and the giving of alms due. They fear a Day when the hearts and the eyes shall turn about (with terror),
(hoping) that Allah may reward them for the best of what they have done and increase them from His grace. And Allah provides for whomever He pleases without measure.

As for the disbelievers, their deeds are like a mirage in a desert plain, which the thirsty man supposes to be water. When he comes upon it, he finds it to be nothing. Instead, there he finds Allah. So He will pay him his account in full. And Allah is swift in reckoning.

Or (the disbelievers’ deeds are like) the manifold darkness in a deep sea, covered by waves upon waves, and (further) clouds above them. (These are) manifold (layers of) darkness one above another. When someone brings his hand out, one can hardly see it. And whoever Allah has not blessed with light will have no light (anywhere).

Have you not seen that whoever is in the heavens and the earth glorifies Allah, as do the birds spreading their wings? Each indeed knows its (own way of) prayer and glorification. And Allah is All-Knowing of what they do.
To Allah belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. And towards Allah is the destination.

Have you not seen that Allah drives the clouds, composes them together, then piles them into layers upon layers, so you see the rain coming out from its midst? And He sends down hail from the sky out of the mountains (of clouds). And He strikes with it whomever He wills and averts it from whomever He wills. The flash of its lightning almost takes away the sight.
(And) Allah alternates the night and the day. There is indeed a lesson for those who have insight.

Allah created every living creature from water. Among them are some who creep upon their bellies; among them are some who walk upon two feet; and among them are some who walk upon four. Allah creates whatever He wills. Indeed, Allah is All-Capable of everything.
Indeed, We have sent down clarifying signs. And Allah guides whomever He pleases to a straight path.
They (the hypocrites) say: ‘We believe in Allah and the Messenger, and we obey.’ Then, a group of them turn away after that, and they are not believers.

When they are called to Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) so he may judge between them, behold, a group of them turn away (from accepting the authority of the Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him).
But if justice is on their side, they come to him submissively.

Is there a disease in their hearts? Or do they have doubts? Or do they fear that Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) will be unjust to them? Instead, it is they who are the wrongdoers.

The only response of the (true) believers, when summoned to Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) so he may judge between them, should be to say: ‘We hear and we obey.’ It is they who shall be successful.
And whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him), and fears Allah and is conscious of Him, it is they who shall be victorious.

And they swear by Allah with their most solemn oaths that if you command them, they will surely go forth. Say: ‘Do not swear! (Only) honourable obedience (is required from you). Allah is indeed well aware of what you do.’

Say: ‘Obey Allah and obey the Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him).’ But if you turn away (from obedience), then (know that) he is responsible only for the duty placed upon him, and you are responsible for the duty placed upon you. If you obey him, you shall be (rightly) guided. And nothing is incumbent upon the Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) except the clear communication (of the Divine message).

Allah has promised those among you who believe and perform righteous deeds that He will surely make them successors upon the earth, as He made successors those before them. And He will most certainly establish for them their faith, which He has chosen for them. And He will most surely change them from a state of fear to (one of) security. They shall worship Me, not ascribing any partners to Me. And whoever is ungrateful after that are the ones who are the transgressors.
Establish the prayer, give the alms due and obey the Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) so that you may be granted mercy (from Allah).
Let it never occur to you that the disbelievers can escape on the earth (from Allah’s power). Their refuge shall be the Fire. And it is indeed an evil destination.

O believers! Let your (male and female) slaves and those who have not yet reached puberty ask your permission (to come in) at three times: Before the dawn prayer, when you lay your clothes aside at noon (in summer) and after the night prayer. These are three times of privacy for you. Beyond these (timings), there is no blame upon you nor them in visiting one another freely. In this way, Allah makes the revelations clear to you. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

When the children among you reach puberty, let them ask permission (at all times) just as those (elders) who asked permission before them. In this way, Allah makes His revelations clear to you. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

(As for) older women who no longer anticipate marriage, there will be no sin upon them if they lay their outer garments aside without displaying any charm (of theirs). But if they restrain themselves (by not removing additional outer coverings), it is better for them. And Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

There is no blame upon the blind, nor is there blame upon the lame, nor any blame upon the sick, nor upon yourselves, whether you eat from your own houses or the houses of your fathers, or the houses of your mothers, or the houses of your brothers, or the houses of your sisters, or the houses of your paternal uncles, or the houses of your paternal aunts, or the houses of your maternal uncles, or the houses of your maternal aunts, or those (houses) whose keys are in your possession or those of your friends. There will be no blame on you whether you eat together or separately. So, when you enter (your) houses, greet each other with a salutation from Allah, a greeting of blessing and goodness. In this way, Allah clarifies the revelations for you so that you may understand (His commands).

Indeed, the believers are only those who (firmly) believe in Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him). And when they are with him in a communal matter, they do not depart until they have asked his permission. Indeed, those who ask your permission are the ones who (truly) believe in Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him). So when they ask your permission for (attending) some of their (personal) affairs, give permission to whomever of them you please, and seek forgiveness for them from Allah. Indeed, Allah is Most Forgiving, All-Merciful.

(O people!) Do not regard calling the Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) among you to be like the call of one of you to another. Indeed, Allah knows those among you who quietly slip away under the shelter of others. So let those who go against his orders beware lest a trial should visit them (in this world), or a painful punishment should befall them (in the Hereafter).

Behold! Indeed, to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth. He certainly knows what you are up to. On the Day they are brought back to Him, He will inform them about what they have done. And Allah is All-Knowing of everything.
Blessed is He Who sent down the Criterion (i.e. the Qur’an) upon His (esteemed) servant, that he may be a warner to the whole world.

He (is the One) Who owns the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, Who did not take a son, Who has no partner in (His) Kingdom and Who created everything and then designed it with precisely programmed measure.

Yet they have taken gods besides Him, who do not create anything and have themselves been created, and who have no control over their own harm or benefit, and have no control over (their own) death, life or resurrection.
The disbelievers say: ‘This is nothing but a lie which he has fabricated, and other people have helped him in (forging) it.’ So they have indeed come out with wrongdoing and falsehood.
And they say: ‘(This Qur’an comprises) the fables of the ancients, and they are dictated to him morning and evening.’
Say: ‘He Who knows every secret in the heavens and the earth has sent it down. Surely, He is the Most Forgiving, All-Merciful.’
And they say: ‘What is the matter with this Messenger that he eats food and goes around in the markets? Why has an angel not been sent down to him to be a warner along with him?
Or (why is not) a treasure thrown to him, or (why does) he (not) have a garden from which he may eat?’ And the wrongdoers say: ‘You are just following a bewitched man (who is deprived of reason).’
Look how they draw comparisons for you, so they go astray and cannot find the (right) way!
Blessed is He Who will grant you better than that if He pleases—gardens with rivers flowing beneath them. Moreover, He will give you palaces.
Instead, they reject the Hour, and We have prepared a blazing Fire for the deniers of the Hour.
When it (i.e. the Fire of Hell) sees them from a distant place, they will hear it raging forcefully and roaring.
And when they are thrown into a narrow place inside it, bound together (in chains), they will pray for (their instant) annihilation.
(They will be told:) ‘Do not pray today for a single annihilation but pray for many annihilations.’
Say: ‘Is that better, or the everlasting Paradise promised to the God-conscious, which will be their reward and destination?
Therein, they will have whatever they wish, abiding forever. That is a promise from your Lord which will indeed be fulfilled.’

On the Day when He shall gather them along with what they used to worship besides Allah, He will say: ‘Were it you (i.e. the false gods) who led these servants of Mine astray, or did they stray from the way (on their own)?’

They will say: ‘Glory be to You. It was not right for us to take any guardians besides You! But You made them and their fathers enjoy (the comforts of the world) until they forgot the Reminder. And they were a ruined people.’

(The idolators will be told:) ‘They (the false gods) have denied your claims. So you shall neither be able to avert (the punishment) nor find (any) help.’ And whoever among you commits wrong, We shall make him taste a terrible punishment.

We never sent any messengers before you who did not eat food and walk in marketplaces. And We have made some of you a means of trial for others (to see) if you can be patient. And your Lord is All-Seeing.

And those who do not hope for Our meeting say: ‘Why have not the angels been sent down upon us? Or why do we not see our Lord?’ Indeed, they are full of arrogance within themselves and have become highly defiant.
The Day they will see the angels, there will be no good news for the guilty that Day. And they (the angels) will say: ‘(Paradise is) totally prohibited (for you).’
And (then) We shall proceed to whatever work they have done, so We shall make it scattered dust.
On that Day, the inhabitants of Paradise will be in a better abode and an excellent resting place.
The Day when the sky will be split open with the clouds, and the angels will be sent down descending (in ranks),
the true kingdom on that Day shall belong to the Most-Compassionate (Lord), and it will be a hard day for the disbelievers.
The Day when every wrongdoer will bite his hands (in frustration), saying: ‘I wish I had followed the path (of truth) with the Messenger!
Oh, woe to me! I wish I had not taken so and so for a friend!
Indeed, he led me astray from the Reminder after it had reached me, and Satan has always been one to desert humankind.’
And the Messenger will say: ‘My Lord! Indeed, my people have taken this Qur’an as something to be abandoned.’
And that is how We make for every Prophet an enemy from among the evildoers. And your Lord is sufficient as a Guide and Helper.

And the disbelievers say: ‘Why was the Qur’an not revealed to him all at once?’ Thus (We have sent the Qur’an down in intervals) so that We may keep strengthening your heart with it. We have recited it (to you gradually) in a measured pace.
And they do not bring you a single example, but We bring you the truth and the best explanation (in reply to them).
Those who will be dragged upon their faces towards Hell will be in the worst place and are (now) farthest from the right way.
Indeed, We gave Musa (Moses) the Book, and We made his brother Harun (Aaron) accompany him as a minister (for his assistance).
Then We said: ‘Go, both of you, to the people who have rejected Our signs.’ Then, We destroyed them utterly.
And the people of Nuh (Noah), We drowned them when they denied the messengers and made them a sign for humankind. And We have prepared for the wrongdoers a painful punishment.
And (We also destroyed) ‘Ad and Thamud, the inhabitants of al-Rass (i.e. the water pit) and many (other) generations between them.
And to each (of them), We gave examples (as warnings), and each (of them) We eradicated with utter destruction.
Indeed, they must have passed by the town (of Sodom), which had been showered with a terrible rain (of stones). Have they not seen it? Yet they did not hope to be resurrected.
And when they see you, they do nothing but make fun of you, (saying): ‘Is this the one whom Allah has sent as a messenger?
Indeed, he was about to lead us astray from our gods if we had not stood firmly by them. And soon they will realise when they see the punishment who is further astray from the (right) path.
Have you seen him who takes his desire as his god? Is it your duty to become a guardian over him?
Do you think that most of them listen or understand? They are just like cattle. Instead, they are further astray from the (right) way.
Have you not considered how your Lord extends the shade (till noon)? Had He willed, He could have made it (remain) stationary. Then We made the sun an indicator over it.
Then, We withdraw it towards Ourselves, drawing little by little.
And He is the One Who made the night for you as a covering and sleep as a (means of) rest. And He made the day for rising again (so that you may earn a livelihood).
And He is the One Who sends the winds as good news heralding His mercy. And We send down from the sky purifying water,
so that We may revive a dead land with it and provide it as a drink to many of the cattle and humans We have created.
Indeed, We distribute it among them so they may be mindful of advice. But most people refuse (to be anything) but ungrateful.
Had We wished, We would have certainly raised a warner in every town.
So do not obey the disbelievers, but strive against them a great striving through this (Qur’an).
And He is the One Who has merged the two seas: this one sweet and fresh, and that one salty and bitter. And between the two, He has made a barrier and a forbidden hindrance.
He is the One Who created the human being from water, then made him kin with ties of blood and marriage. And your Lord is All-Capable.
Yet they worship (things) besides Allah, which can neither benefit them nor cause them harm. And the disbeliever is always a helper (of Satan) against his Lord(’s command).
We did not send you (O Prophet) except as a giver of glad tidings and a warner.
Say: ‘I do not ask you any reward for this (message), but whoever wishes should take the path to his Lord.’
Put your trust in the Living One, Who never dies. And glorify (Him) with His praise. And He suffices as One All-Aware of His servants’ sins.

He (is the One) Who created the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them in six periods, then He established (His authority) on the Throne (as befitting His Majesty). The Most-Compassionate (Lord), so ask about Him, someone who is well aware (of Him).

When they are told, ‘Prostrate yourselves before the Most-Compassionate (Lord),’ they say: ‘What is the Most-Compassionate (Lord)? Shall we prostrate ourselves before whatever you command us?’ So, it increases their aversion.
Blessed is He Who made vast mansions in the sky (in the form of galaxies) and made in it a (light-generating and heat-releasing) lamp (i.e. the sun) and a shining (light-reflecting) moon.
And He is the One Who made the night and the day in succession (of each other), for one who desires to be mindful or desires to be thankful.
And the servants of the Most-Compassionate (Lord) are the ones who walk humbly upon the earth, and when the ignorant address them (indecently), they only respond with words of peace and gentleness.
And (they are) those who spend the night for their Lord, prostrating and standing (in worship).
And those who say: ‘Our Lord! Turn away from us the punishment of the Hellfire. Indeed, its punishment is most terrible (and everlasting).
Indeed, it is an evil abode and a (terrible) place to stay.
And (they are) those who, when they spend, are neither wasteful nor miserly, and they maintain moderation between these (two extremes).

And (they are) those who do not worship another god besides Allah, do not kill the soul that Allah has made inviolable, except in the lawful way, and do not commit adultery. And whoever does that shall face retribution.
The punishment shall be multiplied for him on the Day of Resurrection. And he shall abide in it with humiliation forever,
except those who repent, believe and perform righteous deeds. For them, Allah will replace their evil deeds with good deeds. And Allah is Most Forgiving, All-Merciful.
And whoever repents and acts righteously has truly turned to Allah with due penitence.
As for those who do not give false testimony, and when they come across some idle talk, they pass by (it) with dignity.
(They are) those who, when reminded of the signs of their Lord, do not turn a deaf ear and a blind eye to them,
and those who say: ‘Our Lord! Grant us comfort in our spouses and our offspring, and make us a leading model for the God-conscious.’
These (noble servants) shall be rewarded with elevated mansions (in Paradise) for their patience. And they shall be received there with greetings and (salutations of) peace,
abiding there forever. What an excellent place to settle and reside!

Say (to the disbelievers): ‘My Lord would not care for you even if your worship does not exist. But you have indeed rejected (the truth), so this (rejection) will (forever) continue as a permanent torment (for you).’
Ta, Sin, Mim.
These are the Verses of the Manifest Book.
(Esteemed Prophet!) You will perhaps consume yourself with grief that they are not becoming believers.
If We had wished, We could have sent down upon them a sign from the heavens at which their necks would remain bowed in utter humility.
There did not come to them any new reminder from the Most-Compassionate (Lord), except that they turned away from it.
They have indeed denied (the truth). So, the news of what they have been ridiculing will soon come to them.
Have they not looked at the earth, how much of every splendid kind of vegetation We have caused to grow in it?
There is a sign in that, but most of them are not believers.
Indeed, your Lord is the All-Mighty, the All-Merciful.
And (remember) when your Lord called out to Musa (Moses), (saying:) ‘Go to the wrongdoing people,
the people of Pharaoh. Will they not become God-conscious?’
He (Moses) said: ‘My Lord! I fear that they will reject me,
my breast will constrict (from the stress), and my tongue will not express (itself) fluently. Therefore, send (Your Messenger Gabriel) to Harun ([Aaron] to make him my helper).
They also have a charge against me, and I fear they may kill me.’
He (Allah) said: ‘No (they will not). Go, both of you, with Our signs. We shall be with you, listening (to everything).
So both of you approach Pharaoh and say: ‘We are indeed messengers of the Lord of the worlds.
(We are commanded to say:) ‘Send with us the Children of Israel.’’
He (Pharaoh) said: ‘Did we not raise you among us as a child, and did you not stay several years of your life in our care?
Then you did the act of yours which you did, and you are one of the ungrateful.’
He (Moses) said: ‘I did it, but I was unaware (that a single blow would take his life).
So, I fled from you, as I was afraid of you. Then, my Lord granted me divine wisdom and made me among the messengers.
And that blessing you are recounting to me, it was because you had enslaved the Children of Israel.’
Pharaoh said: ‘And what is the Lord of the worlds?’
He (Moses) said: ‘The Lord of all the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, if you have sure faith.’
He (Pharaoh) said to those around him: ‘Do you not hear?’
He (Moses) said: ‘Your Lord and the Lord of your forefathers!’
He (Pharaoh) said: ‘Indeed your messenger, who has been sent to you, is surely crazy.’
He (Moses) said: ‘The Lord of the east and the west and whatever is between them, if you can understand.’
He (Pharaoh) said: ‘If you take any other god besides me, I will most surely imprison you.’
He (Moses) said: ‘And what if I bring you something very clear?’
He (Pharaoh) said: ‘Bring it, then, if you are among the truthful.’
So he (Moses) threw down his staff; behold, it was a clear serpent.
Then he drew out his hand (after putting it under his armpit), and behold, it was radiant white to the viewers.
He (Pharaoh) said to the chiefs (who stood) around him: ‘This is indeed a very knowledgeable magician.
He wants to expel you from your land by his sorcery. So, what would you advise?’
They said: ‘Leave him and his brother for a while and send heralds to the cities.
They shall bring you every expert magician.’
So the magicians were brought together at the appointed time of a fixed day.
And the people were told: ‘Will you gather (for this occasion)?
Perhaps we may follow the magicians if they are the victors!’
So when the magicians came, they said to Pharaoh: ‘Shall we indeed receive a reward if we are the victors?’
He (Pharaoh) said: ‘Of course! Then indeed you shall be among those near (to me, by entering my inner court).’
Musa (Moses) said to them (the magicians): ‘Throw down what you are going to throw.’
So they threw down their ropes and sticks and said: ‘By the might of Pharaoh, we shall surely be the victors.’
Then, Musa (Moses) threw down his staff; behold, it was swallowing up what they had faked.
Then, the magicians were made to fall, prostrating.
They said: ‘We believe in the Lord of worlds,
the Lord of Musa (Moses) and Harun (Aaron).’

He (Pharaoh) said: ‘Do you believe in Him before I permit you? He (Moses) is indeed your chief who has taught you magic. So you shall soon know (about your end). I will surely cut off your hands and your feet from opposite sides, and I will surely crucify you all.’
They said: ‘No harm (in it). Indeed, we are to return to our Lord.
Indeed, we hope our Lord will forgive us our sins for being the first of the believers.’
Then We revealed to Musa (Moses), (saying:) ‘Leave with My servants by night, for you shall indeed be pursued.’
Then Pharaoh sent heralds to the cities.
(Announcing:) ‘These (Israelites) are indeed a small gang.
And indeed, they are enraging us.
And indeed, all of us are alert.’
So We expelled them (i.e. Pharaoh and his people) from gardens and springs,
and (from) treasures and noble dwellings.
Thus it is, and We made the Children of Israel inherit them.
Then they pursued them at sunrise.
So when the two parties saw each other, the companions of Musa (Moses) said: ‘Indeed we are overtaken.’
He (Moses) said: ‘Certainly not! Indeed, my Lord is with me. He will guide me (to the way out).’
Then We revealed to Musa (Moses): ‘Strike the sea with your staff.’ So, it split (into parts). And each part was as if it were a great mountain.
So We brought the others (i.e. Pharaoh and his people) closer.
We saved Musa (Moses) and all those with him.
Then We drowned the others (i.e. Pharaoh and his people).
There is indeed a sign in that, but most of them were not believers.
And indeed, your Lord is the All-Mighty, the All-Merciful.
And recite to them the story of Ibrahim (Abraham).
When he told his (foster) father and his people: ‘What are you worshipping?’
They said: ‘We worship idols, and We will always remain devoted to them.’
He (Abraham) said: ‘Do they hear you when you call (them)?
Or do they bring you any benefit or cause you any harm?’
They said: ‘Rather we found our fathers doing likewise.’
He (Abraham) said: ‘Have you (ever) realised what you have been worshipping,
you and your ancestors?’
They are indeed my enemies. But the Lord of all worlds,
Who created me—He guides me.
And He is the One Who gives me to eat and gives me to drink.
And when I get sick, He is the One Who cures me.
And Who will cause me to die, then He will bring me to life (again).
And Who, I hope, will forgive me my faults on the Day of Judgement.
My Lord! Grant me unerring judgement (and wisdom), and join me with the righteous.
And bless me with an honourable reputation among the later generations.
And place me among the heirs of the Garden of Bliss.
Forgive my (foster) father, for he is from those who have gone astray.
Do not disgrace me on the Day all will be resurrected,
the Day when neither wealth nor children will benefit (any person),
except for him who appears before Allah with a sound heart.’
And Paradise will be brought near the God-conscious,
and the Hellfire will be displayed to the deviant.
Then, they shall be told: ‘Where are those (idols) you used to worship,
besides Allah. Are they helping you or helping each other?’
Then they shall be toppled into it—they (the idols) and the deviant (people),
and the troops of Iblis altogether.
There, they will say, disputing (with their idols):
‘By Allah, indeed we were in manifest error,
when we made you equal with the Lord of all worlds.
None caused us to go astray but the guilty.
So now we have no intercessors,
nor any sympathetic friend.
If we could return (to the world) once again, we would be among the believers.’
There is indeed a sign in that, but most of them were not believers.
And indeed, your Lord is the All-Mighty, the All-Merciful.
The people of Nuh (Noah) rejected the messengers.
When their (kinship) brother Nuh (Noah) said to them: ‘Will you not be God-conscious?
Indeed, I am a trustworthy messenger (sent) to you.
So be God-conscious and obey me.
I do not ask you for any reward for this (message). My reward lies only with the Lord of all worlds.
So be God-conscious and obey me.’
They said: ‘Shall we believe in you while the lowest people (of our society) follow you?’
He said: ‘What knowledge do I have about what they used to do?
Their account is only with my Lord if you could perceive (what is true honour)!
And I am not going to drive the believers away.
I am just a manifest warner.’
They said: ‘If you do not stop (your preaching), O Nuh (Noah), you shall indeed be stoned (to death).’
He said: ‘My Lord! Indeed, my people have rejected me.
So judge between me and them, and deliver me and the believers who are with me!’
So We delivered him and those with him in the fully laden Ark.
Then We drowned the rest afterwards.
There is indeed a sign in that, but most of them are not believers.
And indeed, your Lord is the All-Mighty, the All-Merciful.
(The people of) ‘Ad rejected the messengers,
when their (kinship) brother Hud said to them: ‘Will you not be God-conscious?
Indeed, I am a trustworthy messenger (sent) to you.
So be God-conscious and obey me.
And I do not ask you for any reward for this (message). My reward lies only with the Lord of all worlds.
Do you frivolously build on every prominent place a monument?
You construct castles as if you will live (in the world) forever.
And whenever you seize (someone for punishment), you seize like ruthless tyrants.
So be God-conscious and obey me.
And be conscious of Him, Who has provided you with (all the good things) that you know.
He has aided you with (an abundance of) cattle and children,
and gardens and springs.
Indeed, I fear for you the punishment of a grievous day.’
They said: ‘It is the same to us whether you warn us or be not among the warners.
This is nothing but the traditions of the ancients.
And we will never be punished.’
So they rejected him (i.e. Hud), and We destroyed them. There is indeed a sign in that, but most of them are not believers.
Indeed, your Lord is the All-Mighty, the All-Merciful.
(The people of) Thamud rejected the messengers,
when their (kinship) brother Salih said to them: ‘Will you not be God-conscious?
Indeed, I am a trustworthy messenger (sent) to you.
So be God-conscious and obey me.
I do not ask you for any reward for this (message). My reward lies only with the Lord of all worlds.
(Do you think that) you will be left secure (forever) in whatever you have here:
In gardens and springs,
and farms and date palms, having stalks laden with ripe fruits?
And you skillfully hew houses in the mountains.
So be God-conscious and obey me.
And do not obey the dictates of the extravagant,
who cause corruption in the land and do not set matters aright.’
They said: ‘Indeed, you are one of the bewitched.
You are just a human being like us. So bring us some sign if you are among the truthful.’
He (Salih) said: ‘This is a she-camel. She shall have her turn to drink (water), and you shall have your turn to drink on known days.
And do not touch her with evil intention, lest the punishment of a terrible day overtakes you.’
But they hamstrung her, and then they became regretful (of killing her).
So the punishment overtook them. There is indeed a sign in that, but most of them are not believers.
Indeed, your Lord is the All-Mighty, the All-Merciful.
The people of Lut (Lot) rejected the messengers,
when their (kinship) brother Lut (Lot) said to them: ‘Will you not be God-conscious?
Indeed, I am a trustworthy messenger (sent) to you.
So be God-conscious and obey me.
And I do not ask you for any reward for this (message). My reward lies only with the Lord of all worlds.
Do you come only to males, among all people (to satisfy your sexual desire),
abandoning your wives whom your Lord has created for you? Instead, you are a transgressing people.’
They said: ‘If you do not stop this, O Lut (Lot), you will surely be among the expelled.’
He said: ‘Indeed, I detest your conduct.
My Lord! Save me and my family from what they are doing.’
So We saved him and all his family,
except an old woman who remained behind.
Then We destroyed the others.
And We rained upon them a rain (of stones). So how devastating was the rain of the warned!
There is indeed a sign in that, but most of them are not believers.
Indeed, your Lord is the All-Mighty, the All-Merciful.
The People of al-Ayka (the forest dwellers) rejected the messengers,
when Shu‘ayb said to them: ‘Will you not be God-conscious?
Indeed, I am a trustworthy messenger (sent) to you.
So be God-conscious and obey me.
And I do not ask you for any reward for this (message). My reward lies only with the Lord of all worlds.
Give full measure. And do not be among those who cause loss to others.
Weigh (things) with accurate scales.
And do not cheat the people in (weighing) their goods. And do not act corruptly upon the earth, causing mischief.
And be conscious of Him Who created you and the earlier generations.’
They said: ‘You are simply one of the bewitched.
You are nothing but a human being like us. And we indeed consider you to be among the liars.
So make pieces of the heaven fall upon us if you are among the truthful.’
He (Shu‘ayb) said: ‘My Lord knows best what you are doing.’
So they rejected him, and then the punishment of the day of the shady cloud overtook them. No doubt, that was the punishment of a terrible day.
There is indeed a sign in that, but most of them are not believers.
And indeed, your Lord is the All-Mighty, the All-Merciful.
Indeed, this (Qur’an) is a revelation of the Lord of all worlds.
The Trustworthy Spirit (Gabriel) has descended with it,
upon your heart so that you may be among the warners.
(It has been revealed) in a clear Arabic language.
Indeed, it is (foretold) in the scriptures of the ancients.
Is it not enough proof for them that the learned men of the Children of Israel (already) recognise it?
Had We revealed it to some non-Arab,
who would have recited it to them (in fluent Arabic), they would still not have believed in it.
This is how We cause (denial) to pass through the hearts of the guilty.
They will not believe in it until they see the painful punishment.
So, it will come upon them suddenly while they are unaware.
Then they will say: ‘Shall we be granted respite?’
Do they seek to hasten Our punishment?
Just think: if We let them enjoy (themselves) for some years,
then (the torment) which they have been promised comes upon them,
then that (respite) which they enjoyed would not avail them.
We have never destroyed any town without having (sent) warners to it,
as a reminder (from Us). And We were never unjust.
And the devils have not descended with it (i.e. the Qur’an).
Neither is it befitting for them, nor are they capable (of it).
Indeed, they are strictly barred from (even) hearing (it).
So do not invoke another god along with Allah, lest you should be among the punished.
And warn the nearest of your kinsfolk.
And lower your wing (of kindness) to the believers who follow you.
But if they disobey you, say: ‘I am absolved of what you do.’
And put your trust in the All-Mighty, the All-Merciful,
Who sees you when you stand (for prayer),
and your moving over and over among those who prostrate themselves (before Allah).
Indeed, He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
Shall I inform you upon whom the devils descend?
They descend upon every sinful liar,
who readily lends an ear (to Satanic whispers), and most of them are liars (when passing on information to people).
As for the poets, only the deviants follow them.
Have you not seen that they wander in every valley (of imagination)?
And that they say what they do not do?

Except for those (poets) who believe, perform righteous deeds, remember Allah abundantly and defend themselves (by inspiring zeal and courage) after they were oppressed. And the wrongdoers will soon know to what evil end they will return.
Ta, Sin. These are the verses of the Qur’an, the manifest Book.
(It is) a guidance and glad tiding for the believers,
those who establish prayer, give alms due and firmly believe in the Hereafter.
As for those who do not believe in the Hereafter, We have made their (evil) deeds fair-seeming to them. So they are wandering blindly.
It is they who shall have a terrible punishment and are the ones who will be the biggest losers in the Hereafter.
Indeed, you are receiving the Qur’an from the presence of One All-Wise, All-Knowing.
And (remember) when Musa (Moses) said to his family: ‘Indeed I have perceived a fire! I shall bring you some (good) news from it or bring you a flaming firebrand so that you may warm yourselves.’
When he came to it, he was called: ‘Blessed is He Who is (unveiling His Light) in the fire and Who is around it. And glory be to Allah, the Lord of all worlds.
O Musa (Moses)! Indeed, it is I, Allah—the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.

Throw down your staff!’ So when he saw it moving fast like a snake, he turned to retreat without looking back. (Allah said:) ‘O Musa (Moses), do not fear. Indeed, the messengers should have no fear in My presence,
except for someone who has done wrong. But if he later mends his evil way with goodness, then I am Most Forgiving, All-Merciful.
Put your hand inside your bosom. It will come out white without any harm. (These are) among the nine signs (that you will show) to Pharaoh and his people. Indeed, they are a transgressing lot.’
But when Our signs came to them as eye-openers, they said: ‘This is clear magic.’
So they denied these (signs), although their hearts were convinced by them. (But they still did it) wrongfully and arrogantly. So see how was the end of the corruptors!
Indeed, We gave knowledge to Dawud (David) and Sulayman (Solomon). And they said: ‘All praise belongs to Allah, Who has preferred us over many of His believing servants.’

Sulayman (Solomon) inherited from Dawud (David), and he said: ‘O people! We have been taught the language of the birds, and we have been given (an abundance) of everything. This is indeed the manifest grace (of Allah).’
(Once,) Sulayman’s (Solomon’s) troops were gathered for him, comprising jinn, humans and birds, and they were held (for his service) in ordered ranks.
When they came upon the valley of ants, an ant said: ‘O ants! Enter your dwellings, lest Sulayman (Solomon) and his troops crush you while they are unaware.’

So he (Solomon) smiled, laughing at her words, and said: ‘My Lord! Inspire me to give thanks for Your blessing You have bestowed upon me and my parents, and that I may do righteous deeds which You are pleased with, and make me enter, by Your mercy, among Your righteous servants.’
(One day) he inspected the birds and said: ‘Why do I not see the hoopoe? Or is he (really) absent?
I will surely punish him with a terrible punishment, or I shall slaughter him unless he brings me an evident reason (for his absence).’
But he (the hoopoe) did not stay for long (before he turned up) and said: ‘I have found out something you did not know. And I have come to you with firm news from (the land of) Sheba.
I have found a woman (there) ruling over them, and she has been given a share of all things and has a magnificent throne.
I found her and her people prostrating to the sun instead of Allah. And Satan has made their (evil) deeds alluring to them. Thus, he has turned them from the path (of Allah), so they are not guided.
Will they not prostrate to Allah, Who brings out the hidden in the heavens and the earth, and He knows whatever you hide and whatever you disclose?
Allah—there is none worthy of worship except Him, the Lord of the Mighty Throne.’
He (Solomon) said: ‘We shall see if you have told the truth or if you are among the liars.
Go with this letter of mine and deliver it to them, then turn away from them and see what (answer) they send back.’
She (the Queen of Sheba) said: ‘O chiefs! Indeed, a noble letter has been delivered to me.
It is from Sulayman (Solomon), and it (begins) in the Name of Allah, the Most-Compassionate, the All-Merciful.
(It states:) ‘Do not (try to) rise against me and come to me fully submitting (to Allah).’
She said: ‘O chiefs! Advise me regarding my matter. I never decide any matter unless you are present.’
They said: ‘We are people of strength and possess great (military) power, but the command is yours. So think what you would command.’
She said: ‘Indeed when kings invade a town, they ruin it and reduce the most honourable of its people to the most disgraced. And that is how they will act.
So I shall send them a gift and observe what the envoys bring back.’

When he (i.e. the envoy) came to Sulayman (Solomon), he (Solomon) said: ‘Do you offer me wealth? What Allah has given me is far greater than what He has given you. Instead, you are rejoicing over your gift.

Go back to them (with your gifts). We shall come upon them with such troops which they can never face. And we shall expel them from that (land) disgracefully. Moreover, they will be dishonoured (by becoming prisoners).’
He said: ‘O Chief Counsellors! Which of you will bring me her throne before they come to me in (full) submission?’
A giant among the jinn said: ‘I will bring it to you before you rise from your place. Indeed, I am strong enough (and) trustworthy for this (task).’

(Then) the one who had (special) knowledge of the Book said: ‘I will bring it to you before the blink of your eye.’ So, when he saw it (i.e. the throne) placed next to him, he said: ‘This is by the grace of my Lord, to test me whether I give thanks or be unthankful. And whoever is grateful shows gratitude only for his own sake. And whoever is ungrateful (should know that) my Lord is indeed All-Sufficient, All-Honoured.’
He (Solomon) said: ‘Change the appearance of her throne for her. We shall see whether she has the sense to recognise or she is among those who do not recognise.’
So when she came, it was said (to her): ‘Is your throne like this?’ She said: ‘It looks to be the same. We were (already) informed before this (miracle). And we had submitted.’
So whatever she used to worship besides Allah had prevented her (from the right path). Indeed, she belonged to a disbelieving people.

It was said to her: ‘Enter the compound of the palace.’ So when she saw it, she supposed it to be a pool of water and bared her lower legs. He said: ‘It is a compound of the palace paved with crystal slabs.’ She said: ‘My Lord! Indeed, I have wronged myself. (Now) I submit with Sulayman (Solomon) to Allah, the Lord of the worlds.’
And, indeed, We sent to (the people of) Thamud their (kinship) brother Salih, (saying:) ‘Worship Allah.’ Then, they became two groups quarrelling (with each other).
He said: ‘O my people! Why do you seek to hasten the evil before the good? Why do you not seek forgiveness from Allah so you may be shown mercy?’
They said: ‘We have met evil fortune because of you and those with you.’ He said: ‘Your evil fortune is with (the command of) Allah. Instead, you are (only) a people being tested.’
In the city, nine persons (leading their families or gangs) were causing corruption in the land and not letting matters be reformed.

They said: ‘Let us swear together by Allah that we shall attack him and his family by night. Then we will surely tell his heirs that we did not witness the murder of his family, and we are indeed truthful.’
So they devised a plot, and We (too) devised a (counter) plan, but they were not aware.
See then how was the end of their plotting, as We destroyed them and their people together.
So, their houses are there, lying ruined because of their wrongdoing. There is indeed a sign in that for people who know.
We saved those who believed and were God-conscious.
And (remember) Lut (Lot), when he said to his people: ‘Do you commit (this) indecency while you see (with your eyes open)?
Do you approach men with sexual desire instead of women? No! You are a senseless people.’
But the answer of his people was none other but to say: ‘Expel the family of Lut (Lot) from your town! They are trying to keep themselves pure (from our sexual practices).’
So We saved him and his family, except his wife. We ordained her to be among those who remained behind (for torment).
Then We rained upon them a rain (of stones), so evil was the rain of those who were warned!
Say: ‘All praise belongs to Allah. And peace be upon His servants whom He has chosen. Is Allah better, or the partners they associate (with Him)?’

Or (ask them): ‘Who created the heavens and the earth and sent down water for you from the sky, by which We cause beautiful gardens to grow, whereas you could never cause their trees to grow? Was it another god besides Allah? But they are a people who (still) set up equals (to Allah).’

Or (ask them): ‘Who made the earth a place of settlement, made rivers flowing through it, raised firm mountains for it and made a barrier between the two seas (i.e. the fresh and salty water)? Was it another god besides Allah? But most of them do not know.’

Or (ask them): ‘Who answers the call of the distressed when he invokes Him, and removes his suffering, and makes you successors of the earth? Is it another god besides Allah? How little do you reflect!’

Or (ask them): ‘Who guides you in the darkness of land and sea, and Who sends the winds as glad tidings ahead of His mercy? Is it another god besides Allah? Exalted is Allah, above what they associate (with Him).’

Or (ask them): ‘Who brings the creation into being, then returns it (to its former state)? And Who gives you provision from the sky and the earth? Is it another god besides Allah? Say: ‘Produce your evidence if you are truthful.’
Say: ‘No one in the heavens and the earth knows the Unseen (of his own accord) except Allah. And they are not (even) aware when they will be resurrected.’
Nay! Does their knowledge comprehend the Hereafter? No, they are just in doubt about it. Instead, they are blind to it.
The disbelievers say: ‘What! When we and our fathers become dust, shall we be brought out (from our graves alive)?
We have indeed been promised this and our fathers before, but these are only myths of the ancients.’
Say: ‘Travel in the land, then see how was the end of the guilty.’
And (O Prophet!) Do not grieve over them, and do not be distressed for what they plot.
They say: ‘When will this promise be fulfilled if you are truthful.’
Say: ‘Perhaps a part of that (torment) you seek to hasten is close behind you.’
Indeed, your Lord is gracious to humankind, but most of them do not give thanks.
And your Lord indeed knows well what their breasts conceal and what they reveal.
Nothing is hidden in the heavens and the earth, but it is (recorded) in a Manifest Book.
Indeed, this Qur’an recounts to the Children of Israel most of what they differ about,
and it is indeed a guidance and a mercy for the believers.
Indeed, your Lord will decide between them by His command. And He is the All-Mighty, the All-Knowing.
So put your trust in Allah. Indeed, you firmly stand on the manifest truth.
Indeed, you cannot make the dead (i.e. the disbelievers) hear, nor can you make the deaf hear the call (of truth) when they turn their backs (and withdraw from you).
Nor can you lead the blind out of their misguidance. You can make none hear (the truth) except those who believe in Our Commandments and submit.
And when the Word (of the final judgement) comes upon them, We shall bring out a beast for them from the earth telling them that the people had no firm faith in Our revelations.
On that day, We shall gather from every community a group of those who rejected Our revelations, and they will be driven in ranks.
When they come (before their Lord), He will say (to them): ‘Did you reject My revelations, while you did not comprehend them in knowledge? What is it that you used to do?’
And the Word (of the final judgement) shall pass against them because of their wrongdoing. So they will not be able to speak.
Do they not see We made the night that they may rest in it and the day to provide visibility? There are indeed signs in that for people who believe.
The Day when the trumpet will be blown, all those in the heavens and all those on the earth will be terrified, except whom Allah pleases (to spare). And all will come to Him in utter humility.

You see the mountains, which you suppose are firmly fixed, while they will float away like passing clouds. (This is) the work of Allah, Who has perfected everything. He is indeed well aware of what you do.
Whoever comes with a good deed shall receive (a reward) better than it. They shall be secure from terror on that day.
And whoever comes with an evil deed—their faces shall be cast down into the Fire. (They will be told:) ‘Are you rewarded for anything except what you have been doing?’

(Esteemed Prophet say:) ‘Indeed, I have been commanded to worship only the Lord of this city (i.e. Mecca), Who has made it sacred (and inviolable), and to Him belongs everything. And I have been commanded that I should be among those who (fully) submit (to Allah).
And that I should recite the Qur’an.’ So, whoever is rightly guided is guided only for (the benefit of) himself. And whoever goes astray, then say: ‘I am just one of the warners.’
And say: ‘All praise belongs to Allah. He will soon show you His signs, and you will recognise them.’ And your Lord is not negligent of what you do.
Ta, Sin, Mim.
These are the Verses of the Manifest Book.
We recite to you in truth some part of the story of Musa (Moses) and Pharaoh for people who believe.

Indeed, Pharaoh had exalted himself in the land and divided its people into factions, oppressing one group among them, slaughtering their sons and sparing their women. Indeed, he was among the corruptors.
We desired to bestow a favour upon those oppressed in the land, to make them leaders and the inheritors (of the kingdom).
(We desired) to establish them in the land and make Pharaoh, Haman and their armies see (the fulfilment of) what they were apprehensive of.

So We inspired Musa’s mother (saying): ‘Nurse him, but when you fear for him, put him (afloat) into the river and do not fear nor grieve. Indeed, We shall bring him back to you and make him among the messengers.’
Then Pharaoh’s family picked him up, that he might be an enemy and a (cause of) sorrow to them. Indeed, Pharaoh, Haman and their armies were sinners.

Pharaoh’s wife said (to Pharaoh): ‘(This baby seems to be) a source of comfort for me and you. Do not kill him. Perhaps he may benefit us, or we may adopt him as a son.’ Yet, they did not realise (what they were doing).
And the heart of Musa’s mother became empty (of patience). And she would have almost disclosed it if We had not strengthened her heart so that she might be among the believers (in Allah’s plan).
And she said to his sister: ‘Follow him.’ So she watched him from a distance, but they were not aware.

And We had forbidden him to be suckled from the wet nurses since before. So she (i.e. Moses’ sister) said: ‘Shall I show you a household that will take care of him for you, and they will be his well-wishers?’
Thus, We returned him to his mother so she might feel comforted and not grieve. And she would know that Allah’s promise is true, though most of them do not know.
So when he reached maturity and was firmly established (in life), We gave him wisdom and knowledge. And thus do We reward the virtuously excellent.

(One day,) he entered the city (in Egypt) at a time of being unnoticed by its people. He found two men fighting there, one from among his followers (i.e. the Children of Israel) and the other from his enemies (i.e. the people of Pharaoh). The one from his followers sought his help against the one from his enemies. So Musa (Moses) punched him and put him to death. He said: ‘This is Satan’s doing. Indeed, he is an enemy (who) openly misguides.’
He said: ‘My Lord! I have wronged myself. Forgive me.’ So He forgave him. Indeed, He is the Most-Forgiving, the All-Merciful.
He said: ‘My Lord! As You have blessed me, I shall never be a supporter of the guilty.’

He rose early in the morning, fearful and vigilant in the city, when the man who had sought his help the day before suddenly cried out to him (once again). Musa said to him: ‘You are a clear troublemaker.’

As he desired to strike the man who was an enemy to them both, he said: ‘Musa (Moses)! Do you want to kill me as you killed a person yesterday? You only want to be a tyrant in the land, and you do not want to be among the reformers.’

Then a man came running from the remotest part of the city. He said: ‘Musa (Moses)! The authorities are conspiring against you, that they might kill you. So leave (this city). Surely, I am among your well-wishers.’
So he left from there, fearing (and) waiting (for Allah’s help). He said: ‘My Lord! Save me from the wrongdoing people.’
As he turned towards Madyan (Midian), he said: ‘I hope my Lord will guide me to the right way.’

When he arrived at the well of Madyan, he found a crowd of people watering (their flocks). He noticed two women keeping (their flocks) back beside them. He said: ‘What is the matter with you two?’ They said: ‘We cannot water (our flocks) until the shepherds take their flocks away, for our father is a very old man.’
So he watered (their flocks) for them. Then he went back to the shade and said: ‘My Lord! I am surely in (desperate) need of any good You may send down to me.’

Then one of the two (girls) came to him, walking modestly. She said: ‘Indeed, my father invites you so he may reward you for watering (the flocks) for us. So when he came to him (i.e. Shu‘ayb) and told him (his) story, he said: ‘Do not fear. You are secure from the wrongdoing people.’
One of the two girls said: ‘Father! Hire him. Indeed, the best (man) you can hire is the strong, the trustworthy.’

He said (to Moses): ‘Indeed I desire to marry you to one of these two daughters of mine, on the condition that you hire yourself to serve me for eight years. But if you complete ten, then that will be up to you. And I do not want to be hard on you. God-willing, you will find me to be among the righteous.’
He (Moses) said: ‘This is (an agreement) between you and me. Whichever of the two terms I complete, there will be no unfairness towards me. And Allah is a Guardian over what we say.’

So when Musa (Moses) completed the term and set out with his family, he perceived a fire on the side of the Mount (Tur). He said to his family: ‘Wait (here)! Indeed, I see a fire. Maybe I will bring you some news from there, or (I may bring you) a firebrand so that you may warm yourselves.’
So when he came to it, he was called from the right bank of the valley, at the blessed site through the tree: ‘Musa (Moses)! Indeed, I am Allah, the Lord of worlds.

Throw down your staff!’ But when he saw it moving and slithering like a snake, he turned back, retreating, without looking behind. (Allah said:) ‘Musa (Moses)! Come forward and do not fear. Indeed, you are among those who are secure.

Put your hand inside the opening of your garment; it will emerge white without any disease, and keep your arms pressed close to your sides to remove fear. These are two proofs from your Lord to Pharaoh and his council. They are indeed a transgressing people.’
He said: ‘My Lord! Indeed, I have killed one of their men, so I fear they will kill me.
And my brother, Harun (Aaron)—he is more eloquent than me in speech. So send him with me as a helper to verify me, for I fear they would reject me.’

He (Allah) said: ‘We will strengthen your arm through your brother. And We will grant you both a dominating authority, so they shall not be able to touch you both. With (the help of) Our signs, you and those who follow you shall be the victors.
So when Musa (Moses) came to them with Our clear signs, they said: ‘This is nothing but fabricated magic. And We have never heard of such a thing among our forefathers.’
And Musa (Moses) said: ‘My Lord knows best who brings guidance from Him, and whose shall be the good end in the final abode. Indeed, the wrongdoers will not prosper.’

And Pharaoh said: ‘O chief councillors! I know of no other god for you but myself. So, kindle for me a fire, O Haman, to bake clay bricks. Then build me a tower so that I may take a look at Musa’s God. And most surely, I think he is among the liars.’
He and his forces behaved arrogantly in the land with no right and thought they would not be returned to Us.
So We seized him and his forces and threw them into the sea. And see how (terrible) was the end of the tyrants!
So We made them (such) leaders who invite (others) only to the Fire. And on the Day of Resurrection, they will not be helped.
And We caused them to be followed by a curse in this world. And on the Day of Resurrection, they will be among the repugnant.
Indeed, We gave Musa (Moses) the Book after We had destroyed the earlier generations, as (a set of) eye-openers for the people, and as guidance and mercy so that they might become mindful of advice.
And you were not (present) at the western side (of Mount Sinai) when We revealed the commandment to Musa (Moses), nor were you among the witnesses (of these events).

But We brought forth many generations (successively after Moses). Then, long ages passed over them. And you were not living among the people of Madyan reciting to them Our verses, but We are the senders (of the prophets with news of the Unseen).

And you were not on the side of the Mount when We called out (to Moses). But (We have sent you as) a mercy from your Lord that you may warn a people to whom no warner has come before you, so that they may become mindful of advice.

And (We did so) lest a disaster strikes them because of what their hands have sent forth—they would say: ‘Our Lord! Why did You not send a messenger to us so that we could have followed Your revelations and been among the believers?’

But when the truth came to them from Us, they said: ‘Why has he not been given (signs) like those given to Musa (Moses)?’ Did they not disbelieve in what Musa (Moses) was given before? They said: ‘Two magicians supporting each other.’ And they said: ‘We refuse to believe any of them.’
Say: ‘Then produce some (other) book from Allah, which is better in guidance than both of them, so that I may follow it if you are truthful.’

But if they do not answer you, then know that they follow only their desires. And who is more astray than one who follows his base desires without any guidance from Allah? Indeed, Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.
Indeed, We have caused the Word to reach them so that they may become mindful of advice.
Those to whom We gave the Book before it—they are the ones who believe in it.
When it is recited to them, they say: ‘We believe in it. It is surely the truth from our Lord. Indeed, we were the ones who submitted (even) before it (came).’
These (believers) will be given their reward two times for having been patient. They repel evil with good and spend (in charity) from what We have provided them.
And when they hear vain talk, they turn away from it and say: ‘Our deeds belong to us, and your deeds belong to you. Peace be upon you! We do not seek (the company of) ignorant people.’
You cannot guide whomever you wish, but Allah guides whomever He wills. And He knows best who are (rightly) guided.

They say: ‘If we follow the guidance with you, we shall be snatched away from our land.’ Have We not established a secure Sanctuary (i.e. Mecca) for them, where fruits of all kinds are brought as a provision from Us? But most of them do not know.

How many a town have We destroyed who were spoiled by their (high standard of) living? So these are their (ruined) houses that were never inhabited after them except a few. And We are the (sole) Inheritors.

And your Lord would never destroy the towns until He had raised a messenger in their mother city to recite to them Our Verses. And We would never destroy the towns except when their people were wrongdoers.

And whatever things you have been given are only the (temporary) enjoyments of the life of this world and its glamour. But whatever lies with Allah is better and more lasting. Do you not then understand?

Can the person to whom We have given a good promise, which he will receive, be like him whom We have provided the (momentary) enjoyment in the life of this world, then, on the Day of Resurrection, he shall be among those who are summoned (to account for their deeds)?
And on the Day, He will call out to them and say: ‘Where are My partners whom you used to claim?’

Those (deviant leaders) against whom the decree (of torment) will be passed will say: ‘Our Lord! These (followers) are the ones whom we caused to deviate. We led them into deviance, for we were deviant ourselves. We disassociate ourselves (from them) before You. It was not us whom they worshipped. (Rather they worshipped their lusts).’

It will be said (to the disbelievers): ‘Invoke your (self-made) partner-gods (for help)!’ So they will invoke them, but they will not respond to them. And they will see the punishment, wishing they had followed guidance!
The Day when He will call out to them and say: ‘What response did you give to the messengers?’
On that Day, all information shall be blacked out for them so they cannot question one another.
As for him who repents, believes and acts righteously, perhaps he will be among the successful.
And your Lord creates whatever He wishes and chooses. They have no choice. Glory be to Allah, and Exalted is He above any partners they ascribe (to Him).
And your Lord knows what their breasts conceal and what they reveal.
And He is Allah—there is no god except Him. All praise belongs to Him in this world and the Hereafter. (Final) judgement belongs to Him. And to Him, you shall be returned.
Say: ‘Tell me: If Allah were to make the night perpetual over you till the Day of Resurrection, what god besides Allah could bring you light? Will you not then listen?’
Say: ‘Tell me: If Allah were to make the day perpetual over you till the Day of Resurrection, what god besides Allah could bring you night, in which you could rest? Will you not then see?’
Out of His mercy, He has made for you the night and the day that you may rest therein and seek from His grace so that you may be grateful.
On that Day, He will call out to them and say: ‘Where are my partners whom you used to claim?’
And We shall draw from every community a witness and say: ‘Produce your evidence.’ Then they will know that all truth belongs to Allah, and what they used to fabricate will depart from them.

Indeed, Qarun (Korah) belonged to the people of Musa (Moses), but he rebelled against them. And We had given him so many treasures that their keys would be a heavy burden for a group of strong men. When his people said to him: ‘Do not be proud (of your wealth). Indeed, Allah does not like the boastful.

Rather seek (the reward of) the abode of the Hereafter through what Allah has given you, and do not neglect your rightful share of this world. And be good (to others) as Allah has been good to you. And do not seek to spread corruption in the land. Indeed, Allah does not like the corruptors.’

He said: ‘I have indeed been given (all) this (wealth) because of the knowledge (and expertise) I possess.’ Did he not know that Allah had already destroyed before him many generations who were far mightier than him in power and greater in accumulating (wealth)? And the guilty will not be questioned about (their excuse for) their sins.

So he emerged before his people in his adornment. Those who desired the life of this world said: ‘We wish we had the likes of what Qarun (Korah) has been given! Indeed, he is a possessor of great fortune.’
But those gifted with knowledge said: ‘Woe to you! Allah’s reward is better for someone who believes and acts righteously. But no one will receive it except the patient.’
So We caused the earth to swallow him and his house. And he had no group that could help him apart from Allah, nor could he defend himself.

The next morning, those who had longed to be in his place the day before began to say: ‘Alas! It seems Allah provides abundantly to whom He wills among His servants and tightens it. Had Allah not been gracious to us, He would have also caused us to be swallowed. Alas! It seems the disbelievers will not prosper.’
That is the Abode of the Hereafter. We grant it to those who desire neither dominance upon the earth nor corruption. And the good end belongs to those who are God-conscious.
Whosoever brings a good deed, he shall receive (a reward) better than it. But whoever produces evil deed—those who commit misdeeds shall not be rewarded except for what they used to perform.
Indeed, the One Who has ordained the Qur’an for you will surely bring you back to the place of return (i.e. Mecca). Say: ‘My Lord knows best who has come with guidance and who is in manifest error.’
You did not hope that the Book would be delivered to you. But it was a mercy from your Lord. So do not ever be an advocate of the disbelievers (to protect them from My torment).
Do not ever let them bar you from (conveying) Allah’s revelations after they have been sent down to you. Invite (all) to (the way of) your Lord, and never be among the polytheists.

And do not worship another god besides Allah. There is no god except Him. Everything is bound to perish except Him (i.e. His Pre-Eternal Being). All authority belongs to Him, and to Him, you (all) shall be returned.
Alif, Lam, Mim.
Do people think they will be left once they say, ‘We believe,’ and they will not be tried?
Indeed, We tested those before them. So Allah shall surely distinguish those who are truthful and expose the liars.
Do those who commit evil deeds think they will surpass Us? Evil indeed is the judgement they make!
Whoever hopes to meet Allah (should know that) Allah’s appointed time will indeed come. And He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
Whosoever strives hard (in Allah’s cause) strives only for his own sake. Indeed, Allah is Self-Sufficient (and not in need) of (His) creation.
As for those who believe and perform righteous deeds, We shall most surely absolve them of their misdeeds. And We shall most certainly reward them the best of what they used to do.

And We have commanded human being to be good to his parents. But if they urge you to associate with Me what you have no knowledge of, then do not obey them. To Me will be your return, and I will inform you of what you used to do.
And those who believe and perform righteous deeds, We shall surely admit them into (the company of) the virtuous.

There are some among the people who say: ‘We believe in Allah.’ But if such a person is made to suffer in Allah’s cause, he takes the persecution by the people as Allah’s punishment. Yet, if some help comes from your Lord, they will surely say: ‘We were indeed with you.’ Does not Allah know best what is (hidden) in the breasts of all creatures?
Allah shall indeed distinguish those who have (truly) believed, and He will certainly expose the hypocrites.
And those who disbelieve say to the believers: ‘Follow our path, and we shall bear (the burden of) your wrongs.’ Yet they will never be the bearers of any of their wrongs. Indeed, they are liars.

But most surely, they shall carry their burdens and (many) other burdens along with their burdens. And they will indeed be questioned on the Day of Resurrection concerning what they used to fabricate.
Indeed, We sent Nuh (Noah) to his people. And he stayed among them for a thousand years, less fifty. Then, the flood overtook them while they were wrongdoers.
Thus, We saved him and the companions of the Ark. And We made it a sign for the worlds.
And (remember) Ibrahim (Abraham) when he said to his people: ‘Worship Allah and be conscious of Him. That would be better for you if only you knew.

You worship only idols besides Allah and produce lies (about them). Those you worship besides Allah have no control over your sustenance. Therefore, seek all (your) sustenance from Allah, worship Him and be grateful to Him. To Him, you (all) shall be returned.
If you reject (the truth), then (other) communities have (also) rejected (it) before you. The Messenger’s only duty is to communicate (the message) in clear terms.’
Have they not seen how Allah originates the creation and then brings it back (again)? That is indeed easy for Allah.
Say: ‘Travel through the earth and observe how He originated the creation.’ Then, Allah shall bring the next genesis (of the Hereafter) into being. Indeed, Allah is All-Capable of everything.
He punishes whomsoever He wills and shows mercy to whomsoever He wills. And to Him, you shall be returned.
You can neither frustrate (Him) on the earth nor in the sky, and you have neither any guardian besides Allah nor any helper.
Those who disbelieve in Allah’s revelations and His meeting—they have despaired of My mercy. And for them, there shall be a painful punishment.
But the only answer of his (i.e. Abraham’s) people was that they said: ‘Kill him or burn him.’ Then Allah saved him from the fire. There are indeed signs in that for people who believe.

And he (Abraham) said (to his people): ‘You have taken idols (for worship) besides Allah, only for the sake of mutual friendship among yourselves in the life of the world. Then, on the Day of Resurrection, you will disown one another, and you will curse one another. And your abode will be the Fire, and you will not have any helpers.
Lut (Lot) believed in him (i.e. Abraham). And he (Abraham) said: ‘I am migrating towards my Lord. Indeed, He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.’

And We granted him Ishaq (Isaac) and Ya‘qub (Jacob), and We established prophethood and the Book among his descendants. And We gave him his reward in this world; and in the Hereafter, he will most surely be among the righteous.
And (remember) Lut (Lot) when he said to his people: ‘Indeed, you are committing an indecency none in the world has ever committed before you.

What! Do you come upon males (for your sexual desire), commit highway robberies, and practise immorality openly in your gatherings?’ But the only response of his people was that they said: ‘Bring upon us Allah’s punishment if you are among the truthful.’
He said: ‘My Lord! Help me against the corrupt people.’
And when Our messengers came to Ibrahim (Abraham) with the good news, they said: ‘We are indeed going to destroy the people of this town. Its people are indeed wrongdoers.’

He (Abraham) said: ‘Lut (Lot) is indeed in it.’ They said: ‘We know better who is in it. We shall surely save him and his family, except his wife. She will be among those who are going to stay behind.’

And when Our messengers came to Lut (Lot), he was upset on their account and felt distressed for their sake. But they said: ‘Do not fear, nor grieve! We shall deliver you and your family, except your wife. She will be among those who are going to stay behind.
We are indeed going to bring down upon the people of this town a punishment from heaven because they have been transgressors.’
Indeed, We have left some (ruined remains) of it as a clear sign for people who understand.
To Madyan, (We sent) their (kin) brother Shu‘ayb. So he said: ‘O my people! Worship Allah and hope for the Last Day. And do not act wickedly upon the earth, causing corruption.’
But they rejected him, so a severe earthquake overtook them. And in the early morning, they became lifeless bodies lying in their homes.

And (We destroyed) ‘Ad and Thamud (as well). It is undoubtedly apparent to you from their (ruined) dwellings. Satan made their deeds look charming to them, so he turned them away from the path (of truth), though they were wise and discerning.
And (remember) Qarun (Korah), Pharaoh and Haman. Indeed, Musa (Moses) came to them with clear proofs, but they behaved arrogantly in the land, yet they could not outstrip (Us).

So We seized each (of them) for his sin. Among them were those upon whom We sent a violent pebble storm. And among them were those whom the sudden roaring blast overtook. And among them were those whom We caused the earth to swallow. And among them were those whom We drowned. It was not Allah who wronged them, but it was they who used to wrong themselves.
The example of those who adopt protectors besides Allah is like the spider that makes a house (for itself). And indeed, the weakest of all houses is the spider’s house, if only they knew!
Allah indeed knows what they worship besides Him. And He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.
And these are the parables; We draw them for people, but none understands them except the people of knowledge.
Allah created the heavens and the earth for a true purpose. There is indeed a sign in this for the believers.

(Esteemed Prophet!) Recite what has been revealed to you of the Book and establish the prayer. Indeed, prayer prevents indecencies and wrongs. And the remembrance of Allah is (even) greater. And Allah knows whatever (deeds) you perform.

(Believers!) Do not argue with the People of the Book except in the most graceful manner, barring such of them who act wrongfully. And say: ‘We believe in what has been revealed to us and revealed to you. And our God and your God is only One. And to Him, we (fully) submit.’

Similar (to the past messengers), We have revealed the Book to you (O Prophet). So (the faithful among) those to whom We have given the Book believe in it. And among these (inhabitants of Mecca), some (also) believe in it. And none rejects Our revelations except the disbelievers.
You have never recited any Scripture before this, nor did you ever transcribe it with your right hand, for then the falsifiers would have been suspicious.
Rather, it is (preserved as) manifest revelations in the breasts of those gifted with knowledge. And none rejects Our revelations except the wrongdoers.
They say: ‘Why were some (miraculous) signs not sent down upon him from his Lord?’ Say: ‘The (miraculous) signs are with Allah alone, and I am simply a clear warner.’
Is it not enough for them that We have revealed to you the Book (which is) recited to them? There is indeed mercy in it and a reminder for people who believe.
Say: ‘Allah suffices as a Witness between you and me. He knows whatever is in the heavens and the earth. And those who believe in falsehood and disbelieve in Allah, it is they who are the losers.’
And they ask you to hasten the punishment. Had the time not been specified, the punishment would have surely come to them. And indeed, it will overtake them suddenly while they are unaware.
They challenge you to hasten the punishment. Indeed, Hell will besiege the disbelievers.
On the Day the punishment will overwhelm them from above them and from beneath their feet, He will say: ‘Taste what you used to do!’
O My servants who believe! Indeed, My earth is vast. Therefore, Me alone should you worship.
Every soul shall taste death. Then you shall be brought back to Us.

And those who believe and perform righteous deeds, We will surely settle them in lofty dwellings of Paradise, (graced) with streams flowing beneath them, remaining therein forever. How excellent is the reward of the righteous workers,
those who are patient and put their trust in their Lord!
How many creatures do not carry their provision, but Allah provides for them and you (as well)? And He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
If you ask them: ‘Who created the heavens and the earth and subjected the sun and the moon?’ they will surely say: ‘Allah.’ How, then, do they turn away?
Allah expands (the means of) provision for whom He pleases among His servants and straitens it for whom (He wills). Indeed, Allah is All-Knowing of everything.

If you ask them: ‘Who sends down water from the sky, then gives life to the earth with it after its death?’ they will surely say: ‘Allah.’ Say: ‘All praise belongs to Allah!’ But most of them have no understanding.
The life of this word is nothing but diversion and play. As for the abode of the Hereafter, it is indeed the (real) life if only they knew.
Whenever they board a ship, they call upon Allah, devoting (their) faith exclusively to Him. But, once He has delivered them safely to land, behold, they start ascribing partners (to Him again).
So let them be ungrateful for what We have granted them. And let them enjoy themselves. Soon, they will know (the truth).

And do they (i.e. the Meccan disbelievers) not see that We have made (the city of Mecca) a secure sanctuary, whereas people are being snatched away all around them? Would they still believe in falsehood and be ungrateful towards the blessing of Allah?
Who is more unjust than one who fabricates a lie against Allah or denies the truth after it has reached him? Is not the (final) abode of the disbelievers in Hell?
As for those who strive hard for Us, We shall surely guide them in Our ways. And Allah is indeed with the virtuously excellent.
Alif, Lam, Mim.
The Romans have been defeated,
in a land nearby. But after their defeat, they will become victorious,
within a few years. All command belongs to Allah, before and after. And on that day, the believers will rejoice,
upon Allah’s help. He helps whom He pleases. And He is the All-Mighty, the All-Merciful.
(This is) Allah’s promise. Allah does not break His promise, but most people do not know.
They know just an outward aspect of the worldly life. But they are neglectful about the Hereafter.

Do they not reflect within themselves? Allah did not create the heavens, the earth and whatever is between them except for a true purpose and a fixed term. And yet most people disbelieve in the meeting with their Lord.

Have they not travelled in the land and seen how was the end of those before them? They were greater than them in strength. And they cultivated and developed the land more than these (Meccans) have ever developed it. Their messengers came to them with clear proofs. Allah would never have wronged them, but it was they who used to wrong themselves.
Then the end of those who committed evil deeds was most evil, for they denied the revelations of Allah and used to mock them.
Allah originates the creation, then He repeats it, and then to Him, you will be returned.
On the Day the Hour sets in, the guilty shall despair.
And they will have no intercessors among those partners they ascribed (to Allah), and they will then disbelieve in their associate gods.
And on the Day the Hour sets in, they shall be separated (from one another).
As for those who believed and performed righteous deeds, they shall be made to rejoice in a Garden.
While those who disbelieved and denied Our signs and the meeting of the Hereafter, they shall be brought to punishment.
So glorify Allah when you enter the evening and rise in the morning.
And to Him belongs all praise in the heavens and the earth, and at nightfall and when you are in the late afternoon.
He brings the living out of the dead and brings the dead out of the living. And He gives life to the earth after its death. Likewise, you (too) shall be raised (from the dead).
Among His signs is that He created you from dust. Then, behold, you are humans scattered (all over the world).

And among His signs is that He created spouses for you from yourselves so that you may find comfort in them. And He established love and compassion between you. Indeed, there are signs in this for people who reflect.
And among His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the diversity of your languages and colours. There are indeed signs in this for the knowledgeable.
And among His signs is your sleep by night and day, and your pursuit of His grace. There are indeed signs in this for people who listen.

And among His signs is that He shows you lightning, arousing fear and hope. And He sends water down from the sky. Then He revives the earth after its death. There are indeed signs in it for people who apply reason.
