And among His signs is that the heavens and the earth persist by His command. Then, when He calls you out of the earth with a single call, behold, you will come forth.
And to Him belongs whoever is in the heavens and the earth. All are submissive to Him.

It is He Who originates the creation and then returns it—which is even easier for Him. To Him belongs the most exalted description in the heavens and the earth. And He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.

He sets forth for you an example relating to yourselves. Do you have among your slaves any partners (whom you may allow to share) in whatever We have provided you so that you may become equal in its respect and that you may fear them as you fear each other? That is how We make the signs clear for people who understand.
Rather, the wrongdoers merely follow their desires without any knowledge. Who then can guide those whom Allah led astray? And they shall have no helpers.

So, set your face (and heart) steadfastly to the faith in an upright and pure state—the natural way made by Allah upon which He has originated humankind. Let there be no change in the creation of Allah; that is the upright religion. But most people do not know.
(Always) keep turning to Him in repentance. Be conscious of Him, establish the prayer, and be not among the polytheists.
(And do not be like) those who divided their religion and split into sects—each faction rejoicing in what they have.

When something terrible happens to people, they supplicate their Lord, turning to Him in repentance. But as soon as He gives them a taste of His Mercy, a group of them begins to associate (partners) with their Lord (in worship),
being ungrateful for what We have given them. So enjoy yourselves for a while. You shall soon come to know.
Have We sent down any authority to them that speaks (in favour) of what they associate with Him?
When We make people taste some mercy, they become prideful of it. If something bad happens to them for what their hands have sent forth, they instantly fall into utter despair.
Have they not seen that Allah expands (His) provision for whomever He wishes and tightens (it for whomever He wills)? There are indeed signs in this for people who believe.
So give the close relative his right, as well as the poor and the (needy) traveller. That is better for those who seek Allah’s pleasure. And it is they who are successful.

Whatever you lend out in usury so that it may increase at the expense of people’s wealth will not gain value in Allah’s sight. But whatever you give in alms due (i.e. the mandatory charity) seeking Allah’s pleasure—it is they who receive a manifold increase.

Allah is He Who created you, and then He nourished you. Then He causes you to die, and then He will bring you to life. Is there any of your associate gods who can do any of that? Glory be to Him, and Exalted is He above what they ascribe to Him.

Corruption has appeared in the land and the sea because of what people’s hands have earned. So that He may make them taste some (of the consequences) of what they have done; perhaps they may return (to the right path).
(Esteemed Prophet!) Say: ‘Travel upon the earth, and then observe how was the end of those before you! Most of them were polytheists.’
So set your face to the upright religion before a day comes from Allah that cannot be averted. On that day, they will separate from one another.
Whoever disbelieves shall bear (the burden of) his disbelief. And those who act righteously only prepare (pleasing abodes) for themselves,
so that He may reward those who believe and perform righteous deeds out of His Grace. Indeed, He does not like the disbelievers.

Among His signs is that He sends the winds as bearers of good news so that He may make you taste His mercy, and that the ships may sail by His command and that you may seek of His grace, and perhaps you may give thanks (to Him).

(Esteemed Prophet!) Indeed, We sent messengers before you to their (respective) people, and they came to them with clear proofs. Then, We took vengeance upon those who were guilty. And it was incumbent upon Us to help the believers (out of Our grace).

Allah is the One Who sends the winds. Then, they raise a cloud. Then He spreads it out in the sky as He pleases and forms it into fragments. Then you see the rain coming out from its midst. Then, when He causes it to fall upon whom He pleases among His servants, they (instantly) rejoice,
though previously, before it was sent down upon them when they had been in despair.
So observe the effects of Allah’s mercy—how He revives the earth after its death! Indeed, He is the Reviver of the dead. And He is All-Capable of everything.
And if We send a (harsh) wind (over their crops) and they see them turning yellow, they will indeed deny (old favours) right after.
Indeed, you cannot make the dead (i.e. the disbelievers) hear, nor can you make the deaf (i.e. the deniers) hear the call when they turn their backs (upon you).
Nor can you lead the blind out of their misguidance. You can make none hear (the truth) except those who believe in Our revelations while they (fully) submit (to Allah).

Allah is the One Who created you from a state of weakness. Then He gave (you) strength after weakness. Then, after (your) strength, He gave (you) weakness and old age. He creates whatever He wills. And He is the All-Knowing, the All-Capable.
On the Day the Hour arrives, the guilty will swear that they had stayed (in the world) only for an hour. In this way, they were (always) deluded.
Those gifted with knowledge and faith will say: ‘Certainly you stayed according to Allah’s Book until the Day of Resurrection. So here is the Day of Resurrection, but you did not know(it was true).’
So on that day, the excuses of the wrongdoers will not benefit them, nor will they be allowed to make amends.

We have indeed set for people in this Qur’an every kind of illustration. And if you bring them any sign (according to their demand), the disbelievers will still say: ‘You are nothing but false claimers.’
That is how Allah seals the hearts of those who do not (want to) know (the truth).
So be patient! Allah’s promise is indeed true. And do not let those who are not certain (in Allah’s promise) disquiet you.
Alif, Lam, Mim.
These are the Verses of the Wise Book,
guidance and mercy for the virtuous,
those who establish the prayer, pay the alms due, and have sure faith in the Hereafter.
It is they who are upon guidance from their Lord. And it is they who will be successful.

But among the people are those who buy idle discourse only to lead (others) astray from Allah’s way without knowledge and to take it in mockery. For such (people), there shall be a humiliating punishment.
Whenever Our revelations are recited to him, he turns away arrogantly as if he did not hear them, as if there was deafness in his ears. So give him tidings of a painful punishment.
Surely those who believe and perform righteous deeds will have Gardens of Bliss,
abiding in them forever. A true promise of Allah. And He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.

He created the heavens without pillars, as you see, and placed firm mountains inside the earth lest it should shake with you (during its rotation). And He spread in it every kind of animal. And We sent down water from the sky and caused every generous kind (of vegetation) to grow in it.
This is Allah’s Creation. Now show Me what others besides Him have created. Instead, the wrongdoers are in clear error.

Surely, We blessed Luqman with wisdom, saying: ‘Be thankful to Allah. And whoever gives thanks gives thanks only for his (own) good. And whoever is thankless—then indeed Allah is All-Sufficient, All-Praised.’
And when Luqman told his son, as he was counselling him: ‘O my son! Never associate any partners with Allah. Ascribing partners (to Allah) is indeed the worst injustice.’

We have commanded human being regarding his parents (to honour them). His mother carried him with strain upon strain, and his weaning took two years: ‘So be grateful to Me and your parents. To Me will be the final return.

But if they urge you to associate (partners) with Me that you do not know about, then do not obey them. Yet keep their company in this world in a kind manner. But follow the way of those who turn to Me in true devotion. Then to Me will be your return, and I shall inform you what you used to do.’

(Luqman continued:) ‘My son! Even if a deed were the weight of a mustard seed (hidden) in a rock, or (high above) in the heavens or (deep) in the earth, Allah will produce it (on the Day of Judgement). Indeed, Allah is All-Attentive, All-Aware.
My son! Establish the prayer, encourage what is good, forbid what is wrong and be patient through whatever befalls you. That is indeed a course worthy of high resolve.
Do not turn your face arrogantly away from people, nor walk exultantly on the land. Indeed, Allah does not like whoever is arrogant (or) boastful.
Be moderate in your pace. Lower your voice. Indeed, the most hateful of all voices is the braying of donkeys.’

Have you not seen that Allah has subjected for you whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth, and that He has showered His blessings upon you outwardly and inwardly? Still, some people dispute about Allah without knowledge, without guidance, and without an enlightening scripture.

And when it is said to them: ‘Follow what Allah has revealed,’ they reply: ‘We will rather follow what we found our forefathers following.’ What! Even if Satan is calling them to the punishment of the Blaze.
And whoever completely submits himself to Allah and is excellently virtuous has grasped the firmest handhold. And with Allah rests the outcome of all matters.

(O Prophet!) Whoever disbelieves, let not his disbelief sadden you. To Us will be their return, and We will inform them about what they have done. Indeed, Allah is All-Knowing of what is (hidden) in the breasts.
We grant them enjoyment for a little while, then We will force them towards a harsh punishment.
If you ask them: ‘Who created the heavens and the earth?’ They will surely say: ‘Allah.’ Say: ‘All praise belongs to Allah.’ Yet most of them do not know.
To Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth. Indeed, Allah is the All-Sufficient, the All-Praiseworthy.
And if all the trees on the earth were pens, and the ocean refilled with seven more oceans (were ink), the Words of Allah would not be exhausted. Indeed, Allah is All-Mighty, All-Wise.
The creation and resurrection of you (all) is only like that of a single soul (for Him). Indeed, Allah is All-Hearing, All-Seeing.

Do you not see that Allah causes the night to merge into the day and causes the day to merge into the night, and that He has subjected the sun and the moon, each orbiting for a specified term? And Allah is well aware of what you do.
That is because Allah is the Ultimate Reality. And whatever they worship besides Him is falsehood. And Allah is the All-Exalted, the All-Great.
Do you not see that the ships sail upon the sea by Allah’s blessing, that He may show you some of His signs? Most surely, there are signs in it for every patient (and) grateful (person).

When a wave overwhelms them like mountains, they cry out to Allah with exclusive devotion to Him. But when He delivers them (safely) towards the land, only some of them are relatively grateful. And no one rejects Our signs except a treacherous (and) ungrateful person.

O people! Be conscious of your Lord and fear the day when no parent shall be able to expiate for his child, nor will a child be able to expiate for its parent in any way. Indeed, Allah’s promise is true. So let not the life of this world deceive you, nor let the Arch-Deceiver (Satan) deceive you about Allah.

Indeed, with Allah lies the knowledge of the Hour. He sends down the rain and knows what is in the wombs. No soul knows what it will earn tomorrow, and no soul knows in what land it will die. Indeed, Allah is All-Knowing, All-Aware.
Alif, Lam, Mim.
The revelation of the Book, there is no doubt in it, is from the Lord of all worlds.
Do they say: ‘He has fabricated it?’ No! It is the truth from your Lord so that you may warn a people to whom no warner has come before you—that perhaps they may be guided (to the right path).

Allah is the One Who created the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them in six periods. Then He established (His authority) over the Throne (befitting His Glory). Apart from Him, you have neither any protector nor intercessor. Will you not then become mindful (of advice)?
He governs every affair from the heavens to the earth. Then it all ascends to Him in a day whose measure is a thousand years by your counting.
That is the Knower of the Unseen and the seen—the All-Mighty, the All-Merciful,
Who perfected everything that He created and originated the creation of humankind from clay.
Then, He made his progeny from an extract of an ignoble fluid.
Then, He proportioned him and breathed into him from His spirit. Then He made for you ears, eyes and hearts. Yet you hardly give thanks.
Still, they say (mockingly): ‘When we are lost in the earth, shall we certainly be (raised) in a new creation?’ Instead, they disbelieve in the meeting of their Lord.
Say: ‘The angel of death, who has been given charge of you, will take you (i.e. your soul) away. Then to your Lord, you shall be returned.’

And if you could see when the guilty, hanging their heads (in shame) in the presence of their Lord, (will confess:) ‘Our Lord! We have seen and heard; therefore, send us back (to the world) so that we may act righteously. Indeed, we are (now) convinced.’
And had We willed, We could have given each soul its guidance, but the Word from Me is confirmed (against the defiant): ‘I will indeed fill up Hell with the (guilty) jinn and humans all together.’
So taste (the punishment), for you forgot the meeting of this day of yours. We (too) have forgotten you. And taste the everlasting punishment for what you used to do.
The (true) believers in Our revelations are only those who, when reminded of them, fall into prostration and glorify their Lord’s praise, and are not arrogant.
Their sides abandon their beds, calling upon their Lord out of fear and hope. And they spend (in charity) from what We have provided them.
No soul knows what has been kept hidden for them of comfort to the eyes as a reward for what they used to do.
Is one who believes like the one who is a transgressor? They are not equal.
Those who believe and perform righteous deeds will have the Gardens of Eternal abode as a hospitality for what they used to do.
As for those who disobeyed, their refuge is the Fire. Whenever they desire to come out of it, they will be driven back into it and told: ‘Taste the torment of the Fire which you used to deny.’
We will surely make them taste some of the minor punishment (of this world) before the greater punishment (of the Hereafter). Perhaps they may return (to the right path).
Who is a greater wrongdoer than the one who is reminded of his Lord’s signs and then turns away from them? Indeed, We shall take vengeance upon the guilty.
And We certainly gave Musa (Moses) the Book. So be not in doubt regarding his meeting (with Him). And We made him a guide for the Children of Israel.
We raised leaders among them, guiding (the people) according to Our command when they patiently endured and firmly believed in Our revelations.
Surely your Lord will decide between them on the Day of Resurrection about what they used to differ.
Does it not guide them: how many generations We have destroyed before them, amidst (the ruins of) whose dwellings they walk? There are indeed signs in it. Will they not then listen?
Do they not see that We carry rainwater to the barren land and produce (various) crops through it, from which they and their cattle eat? Do they not observe?
And they say: ‘When is this victory (of yours coming) if you are truthful?’
Say: ‘On the Day of victory, faith will not benefit those who disbelieved (in this world), nor will they be given respite.’
So turn away from them and wait, and they (too) are waiting.
O Prophet! Remain mindful of Allah (as ever) and do not yield (to the suggestions of) the disbelievers and the hypocrites. Indeed, Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.
And keep following what is revealed to you by your Lord. Indeed, Allah is All-Aware of what you do.
And maintain your trust in Allah. Allah suffices as a Guardian.

Allah does not place two hearts in a person’s breast. Nor does He consider your wives whom you repudiate by zihar* (to be unlawful for you) as your (real) mothers. Nor does He consider your adopted sons as your (actual) sons. These are only words from your mouths, but Allah speaks the truth. And He (alone) guides the way. [* Zihar was a pre-Islamic Arabian practice in which a man could repudiate his wife by saying: ‘You are to me as my mother’s back (zahr) .’ By declaring this, the wife was deprived of her marital rights but was not yet free to contract another marriage. Islam abolished this unjust practice.]

Call them (i.e. your adopted sons) after their (real) fathers’ (names). That is more equitable in the sight of Allah. But if you do not know their fathers, then they are your brethren in faith and close associates. There is no blame for what you did by mistake, barring what your hearts may deliberately intend. And Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.

The Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him) is closer to the believers than their (own) souls,* ** while his wives are their mothers. The blood relatives are more entitled to inherit from one another according to the Book of Allah than (the rest of) the believers and the emigrants unless you show kindness to your close friends (through bequest). All this is written in the Book. [* Or: The Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him) has a greater right and authority over the believers than they have over themselves. ** Or: The Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him) should be considered and felt more beloved by the believers than their own selves.]

And (remember) when We took a pledge from the prophets, as well as from you, and Nuh (Noah), Ibrahim (Abraham), Musa (Moses) and ‘Isa (Jesus), the son of Maryam (Mary). We took from them a solemn pledge (to call the people towards the upright religion),
so that He may question the truthful about their truthfulness. And He has prepared for the disbelievers a painful punishment.

O believers! Remember Allah’s blessing upon you when the (enemy) armies massed upon you. Then We sent against them a strong wind and troops (of angels) whom you could not see. And Allah is All-Seeing of whatever you do.

When they invaded you from above (i.e. the higher side of the eastern valley) and from below (i.e. the lower side of the western valley), and when the eyes got rolled (with fear), and the hearts rose to the throats, and you started thinking many (conflicting) things about Allah.
At that stage, the believers were put to the test and were most violently shaken.
And (remember) when the hypocrites and those with sickness in their hearts said: ‘Allah and His Messenger promised us nothing but delusion.’

And when a group of them said: ‘O people of Yathrib! (This is) not a place for you to stay (safely), so retreat.’ Another group of them began to seek permission from the Prophet, saying: ‘Our houses are lying exposed (to the enemy),’ although they were not exposed. They just wanted to run away.
If they had been invaded from all sides of the city and asked to commit treachery, they would have committed it and would not have hesitated except very little.
Yet they had already pledged to Allah earlier that they would not turn their backs (in retreat). And a pledge to Allah must be called to account.
Say: ‘Fleeing will not benefit you (at all). If you manage to escape death or slaughter, you will not be granted enjoyment except for a little while.’
Say: ‘Who is the one that can protect you from Allah if He intends to cause you harm or desires to grant you mercy?’ They will not find for themselves any protector or helper besides Allah.
Surely, Allah knows those among you who discourage others and those who say to their brothers (secretly): ‘Come and join us (in staying behind), and they hardly take part in the fighting.’

(They do so) being miserly towards you. So when a situation of danger comes, you see them staring at you with their eyes rolling like someone fainting at death. But when the panic is over, they afflict you with sharp tongues in their greed for worldly gains (from the spoils). These people have never believed. So Allah has rendered their deeds void. And that is easy for Allah.

They (still) think that the allied forces have not yet withdrawn. And if the allied forces were to come (again), they would wish they were in the desert among the Bedouins, asking for news about you. And if they were among you, they would not fight except very little.
Indeed, in the Messenger of Allah, you have an excellent example for whoever looks forward to Allah and the Last Day and remembers Allah abundantly.

And when the believers saw the allied forces, they said: ‘This is what Allah and His Messenger had promised us. And Allah and His Messenger spoke the truth.’ And this only increased them in faith and submission.

Among the believers are men who have proven true what they pledged to Allah. Some of them have fulfilled their pledge, and some of them are still waiting, and they have not changed (their commitment) in the least.

(All of this is) so that Allah may reward the truthful for their truthfulness and punish the hypocrites if He so wills, or He may relent towards them (and accept their repentance). Indeed, Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.

Allah drove back the disbelievers with their rage, for they failed to achieve any success. And Allah was Sufficient for the believers in the battle (against the allied forces). And Allah is All-Strong, All-Mighty.

And He brought down from their strongholds those who had backed them (i.e. the Meccan aggressors) from the People of the Book (i.e. the Banu Qurayza—who acted treacherously in breach of their peace treaty with the Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him) and the Muslims). And He cast horror into their hearts. You killed a (treacherous) group among them and took another group as war captives.
And He passed on to you their land, their houses, their possessions and territory where you had not even stepped in (before). And Allah is All-Capable of everything.
O Prophet! Say to your wives: ‘If you desire the life of this world and its glamour, then come! I will provide you (with suitable compensation), and I will release you graciously.
But if you desire Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) and the abode of the Hereafter, then Allah has surely prepared a tremendous reward for the virtuous amongst you.’
O wives of the Prophet! Whoever of you were to commit gross misconduct, the punishment would be doubled for her. And that is easy for Allah.

But whoever of you remains devoutly obedient to Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) and acts righteously, We shall give her a twofold reward. And we have prepared a generous provision for her.

O wives of the Prophet! You are not like any other women; if you are conscious (of Allah), then be not overly soft in speech (with men), lest one with sickness in his heart may be tempted. But speak in an honourable way.

Stay in your homes and do not display your charm, like the displaying of the former (days of) ignorance. Establish the prayer, pay the alms due and keep obeying Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him). Indeed, Allah only desires to keep all (forms and causes of) impurity away from you, O Members of the (Prophet’s) Family, and purify you with a thorough purifying.
Remember what is recited in your homes from Allah’s verses and wisdom (i.e. the Prophetic sunnah). Indeed, Allah is All-Subtle, All-Aware.

Indeed, the submitting men and the submitting women, the believing men and the believing women, the devout men and the devout women, the truthful men and the truthful women, the steadfast men and the steadfast women, the humble men and the humble women, the charitable men and the charitable women, the fasting men and the fasting women, the men who guard their chastity and the women who guard (their chastity) and the men who remember Allah abundantly and the women who remember (Allah abundantly)—Allah has prepared for them (all) forgiveness and a great reward.

It is not for a believing man nor a believing woman when Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) have decreed a matter that they should have a choice in their affair. And whoever disobeys Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) has undoubtedly strayed into manifest error.

And (remember) when you said to him whom Allah had blessed, and you (too) had blessed: ‘Retain your wife for yourself (in the bond of marriage) and be God-conscious.’ And you had concealed (the secret) within yourself; what Allah would reveal (later). And (out of decency) you were worried about the people (that they might misunderstand the situation). Whereas it is more befitting that you consider Allah’s command fearfully. But when (your adopted son) Zayd gave up his claim upon her (through divorce), We wedded her to you. So, there should be no restriction for the believers in (marrying) the ex-wives of their adopted sons when they have finished their marital relationship with them. And Allah’s command is bound to be fulfilled.

There is no restriction upon the Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him) regarding what Allah has ordained for him. (This has been) the practice of Allah concerning those (prophets) who had gone before. And Allah’s command has been firmly decreed.
Those who deliver the messages of Allah and fear Him, and fear none but Allah, Allah suffices (for them) as a Reckoner.
Muhammad (blessings and peace be upon him) is not the father of any of your men. However, he is the Messenger of Allah and the Last of the Prophets. And Allah is All-Knowing of everything.
O believers! Remember Allah (frequently) with much remembrance.
And glorify Him (every) morning and evening.
He is the One Who sends His blessings upon you, as do His angels, that He may bring you out of darkness into light. And He is All-Merciful to the believers.
Their greeting on the day they meet Him will be ‘Peace’. And He has prepared for them an honourable reward.
O (Esteemed) Prophet! Indeed, We have sent you as a witness, a bearer of glad tidings and a warner,
and a caller to Allah by His leave and an illuminating lamp (i.e. the shining sun).
And give glad tidings to the believers that they shall have a great bounty from Allah.
Do not yield to (the demands of) the disbelievers and the hypocrites. Disregard their hostile attitude and maintain your trust in Allah. And Allah suffices as a Guardian.

O believers! When you marry the believing women and then divorce them before you have touched them, they will have no waiting period for you, which you may count on. But give them a (suitable) provision and release them graciously.

O Prophet! Indeed, We have made lawful for you your wives, to whom you have paid their bridal gifts, and those (bondwomen) in your possession, whom Allah has assigned to you through war. And (you are allowed to marry) the daughters of your paternal uncle, the daughters of your paternal aunts, the daughters of your maternal uncle and the daughters of your maternal aunts, who migrated with you. (Similarly,) any other believing woman who offers herself to the Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him) and the Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him) pleases to take her in marriage. (This permission is) an exclusive privilege for you, not for the rest of the believers. We know what We have made lawful for them concerning their wives and (the bondwomen) in their possession so that there may be no blame upon you. And Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.

It is up to you (O Prophet!) to delay (the turn of) any (wife) whom you may please among them and keep with you (any of them) whom you may please. And there is no blame on you if you seek (to keep with you) someone from whom you have kept your distance (for a while). That makes it more likely that they will be comforted and not feel grieved, and all of them should be pleased with what you have bestowed upon them. And Allah knows what is in your hearts, and Allah is All-Knowing, All-Forbearing.

It is not permissible for you to marry more women after this, nor can you replace any of your present wives with another, even if their (moral and spiritual) beauty pleases you—except whom your right hands possess (through lawful war). And Allah is Watchful over everything.

O believers! Do not enter the houses of the Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him) unless permission is granted to you for a meal—nor (arrive too early as to) linger until the meal is ready. But if you are invited, then enter (on time). And when you have eaten, disperse (at the earliest convenience) without staying there to chat. Indeed, such behaviour would discomfort the Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him). And he is too shy to ask you (to leave), but Allah is never shy from expressing the truth. And when you ask his wives for something, ask them from behind a curtain. That is purer for your hearts and their hearts. And it is not (permissible) for you to annoy the Messenger of Allah (blessings and peace be upon him), nor may you ever marry his wives after him. That would indeed be a grievous offence in the sight of Allah.
Whether you reveal anything or conceal it, Allah is surely All-Knowing of everything.

There is no blame on them (if they appear unveiled) before their fathers, or their sons, or their brothers, or their brother’s sons, or their sister’s sons, or their own women folk or those (bondwomen) in their possession. And be God-conscious. Indeed, Allah is a Witness to everything.

Indeed, Allah and (all) His angels (continuously) invoke blessings upon the Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him). O believers! You, too, invoke blessings upon him and salute him with worthy salutations of peace.
Indeed, those who offend (or insult) Allah and His Messenger, Allah has cursed them in this life and the Hereafter. And He has prepared a humiliating punishment for them.
As for those who abuse (or insult) the believing men and the believing women without having committed (any offence), they will surely bear the burden of false allegation and a flagrant sin.

O Prophet! Ask your wives, your daughters and the women of the believers to draw their cloaks over themselves (when they go out). In this way, it is more likely that they may be recognised (as virtuous women), and they will not be troubled. And Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.

If the hypocrites, those with sickness in their hearts and those who spread false rumours in the city (of Medina) do not cease (their malicious activities), then We will indeed urge you (to take action) against them. Then they will not be able to stay in your neighbourhood (of Medina) except for a short while.

(They are) accursed (and treacherous) people (who are engaged in anti-state and violent conspiracies). Wherever they are confronted, they will be arrested and put to death relentlessly (for their rebellious and anti-peace activities).
(That has been) Allah’s practice regarding those who passed away before. And you shall never find any alteration in Allah’s practice.
People ask you about the Hour. Say: ‘Its knowledge is only with Allah. What shall make you know? Perhaps the Hour is near.’
Indeed, Allah has cursed the disbelievers and prepared a Blazing Fire for them.
They will remain in it forever. They will find neither any guardian nor any helper.
The Day when their faces are turned about in the Fire, they will say: ‘Oh! If only we had obeyed Allah and obeyed the Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him)!’
And they will say: ‘Our Lord, indeed we obeyed our leaders and (the influential) elders. So they led us astray from the (right) way.
Our Lord! Give them a twofold punishment and curse them with a tremendous curse.’
O believers! Do not be like those who slandered Musa (Moses), whereas Allah cleared him of what they alleged. And he was highly honourable in Allah’s sight.
O believers! Be God-conscious and speak an upright word.
He shall rectify for you your deeds and forgive you your sins. Whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) has undoubtedly achieved great success.

Indeed, We offered (Our) trust to the heavens, the earth and the mountains, but they refused to bear this (burden) and were afraid of it. Yet human being undertook it. Indeed, he has proved himself very unfair (in keeping the trust) and highly ignorant (of the consequence of his negligence).

(It is) so that Allah may punish the hypocritical men and the hypocritical women and the polytheist men and the polytheist women, and Allah may turn in mercy to the believing men and the believing women, and Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.
All praise is for Allah, to Whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And to Him belongs all praise in the Hereafter. And He is the All-Wise, the All-Aware.
He knows whatever penetrates the earth and whatever emerges out of it, and whatever descends from Heaven and whatever ascends into it. And He is the All-Merciful, the Most-Forgiving.

The disbelievers say: ‘The Hour will not come to us.’ Say: ‘Yes, by my Lord, it will surely come to you.’ (He is) the Knower of the Unseen. Not (even) the weight of an atom can escape Him in the heavens or on the earth. (There is) nothing smaller than that nor bigger, but it is (mentioned) in a clear Book,
that He may reward those who believe and perform righteous deeds. It is they for whom there shall be forgiveness and a generous provision.
But those who strive hard to undermine Our revelations, presuming to frustrate (Us), it is they for whom there shall be the punishment of painful wrath.
Those gifted with knowledge can see that what has been revealed to you from your Lord is the truth, and (that) it guides to the path of the All-Mighty, the All-Praised.
And the disbelievers say: ‘Shall we show you a man who informs you that when you have been totally torn to pieces, you will be (raised) as a new creation?
Has he fabricated a lie against Allah, or is there a madness in him?’ Instead, those who do not believe in the Hereafter shall (languish) in punishment and (are in) extreme error.

Have they not considered what is in front of them and what is behind them (engulfing them) from the sky and the earth? If We wish, We could make the earth swallow them or cause (deadly) pieces of the sky to fall upon them. Indeed, there is a sign in this for every penitent servant.
And indeed, We granted Dawud (David) a (unique) favour from Us, (commanding:) ‘O mountains, echo God’s praises together with him, and the birds as well.’ And We made iron mouldable for him,
instructing: ‘Make full-length armour, measure well while balancing the links and act righteously. Indeed, I am All-Seeing of whatever you do.’

And for Sulayman (Solomon), (We subjected) the wind: Its morning course was a month’s journey, and its evening course was (also) a month’s journey. And We caused a fountain of molten copper to flow for him. And (We subjected) some of the jinn who would work for him by the command of his Lord. And (We had warned that) whoever of them turned away from Our order, We would make him taste the punishment of the Blaze.

They built for him as many places of worship as he wished, and images and bowls (large) like water reservoirs and cooking pots fixed in the ground. (We commanded:) ‘O family of Dawud (David)! Work thankfully. Only a few of My servants are thankful!’

Then, when We decreed death for him, nothing indicated his death to them except a creature of the earth that kept eating his staff. And, when he collapsed, the jinn realised that if they had known the Unseen, they would not have remained in that humiliating punishment.

Indeed, there was a sign for (the people of) Saba’ (Sheba) in their dwelling place. (There were) two gardens: to the right and the left. (They were told:) ‘Eat from the provision of your Lord and be grateful to Him. (Yours is) a good land and a forgiving Lord!’

But they turned away (from obedience). So We sent upon them a devastating flood and replaced their two gardens with two (other) gardens, bearing bitter fruit, tamarisk bushes and a few thorny trees (here and there).
Thus, We paid them back for their ingratitude. Would We ever punish (anyone in such a way) except the ungrateful?

We had set up between them and the towns (on the Yemen-Syria highway) many other prominent settlements (to facilitate their trade), which We had blessed (with provisions). We had set moderate travel distances in between (for rest). (And We said:) ‘Travel safely between them by night and by day.’

But they said: ‘Our Lord! Place a distance between our journeys,’ and they wronged themselves. So, We turned them into old stories (as a warning) and caused them to disintegrate totally. Indeed, there are signs in this (account) for every extraordinary patient and highly grateful person.
Indeed, Iblis (Satan) proved his opinion true about them. So they (all) followed him except a group of the believers.
He possesses no authority over them, but We only want to distinguish those who believe in the Hereafter from those who doubt it. And your Lord is Watchful over everything.

Say: ‘Call upon those you claim to be (gods) besides Allah. They do not control (even) an atom’s weight in the heavens or the earth. Nor have they any share in either of them. Nor is any one of them a helper to Him.’

No intercession will be of any benefit in His presence except by those to whom He has (already) granted permission. When fear is banished from their hearts, they will ask: ‘What has your Lord said?’ They will say: ‘The truth. And He is the All-Exalted, the All-Great.’
Say: ‘Who provides you sustenance from the heavens and the earth?’ Say: ‘Allah (is the One Who provides). Indeed, (either) we or you (i.e. one of us) must be upon guidance or in manifest error.’
Say: ‘You will not be questioned about what we were guilty of, nor shall we be questioned about what you do.’
Say: ‘Our Lord will bring us together, then He will judge between us with justice. And He is the Greatest Judge, the All-Knowing.’
Say: ‘Show me those whom you associate with Him as partners. No, indeed! (They can never show any such partner). Instead, He (alone) is Allah, the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.’
We have not sent you except as a bearer of glad tidings and a warner to all of humankind, but most people do not know.
And they say: ‘When will this promise (of the Last Day) be fulfilled if you are truthful?’
Say: ‘You (already) have the appointment of a day that you will neither delay nor advance by a single moment.’

The disbelievers say: ‘We will never believe in this Qur’an, nor in what was (revealed) before it.’ If only you could see when the wrongdoers would be made to stand before their Lord, throwing blame at each other. Those who had been oppressed will say to those who were arrogant: ‘Had it not been for you, we would surely have been believers.’
Those who were arrogant will say to those oppressed: ‘Did we hinder you from guidance after it had come to you? Instead, you were guilty (yourselves).’

The oppressed will say to the arrogant: ‘No! (It was your) plotting by night and day when you prompted us to disbelieve in Allah and assign equals to Him.’ They will hide (their) regret when they see the punishment, and We will put iron shackles around the necks of the disbelievers. Will they be rewarded except for what they used to do?
We never sent a warner to any town, but its elite would (always) say: ‘We reject what you have been sent with.’
They said: ‘We have greater wealth and more children (than the believers), and we will never be punished.’
Say: ‘Indeed, my Lord expands provision for whomever He pleases and tightens it (for whom He wills), but most people do not know.’

It is not your wealth nor your children that will bring you closer to Our proximity. But those who believe and act righteously, it is they for whom there will be a manifold reward for what they did, and they will be secure in elevated mansions (of Paradise).
As for those who strive to discredit Our revelations, seeking to undermine them, they will be brought to punishment.

Say: ‘Indeed, my Lord gives provision in abundance to whom He pleases among His servants and restraints it for whom (He wills). And whatever you spend (in charity), He will replace it (in compensation). And He is the best of providers.’
On the day He gathers them all, He will then say to the angels: ‘Were it you whom these (polytheists) used to worship?’
They will say: ‘Glory be to You! You are our sole Guardian, not them! Instead, they used to worship the jinn; most of them had faith in them.’
So, today, none of you has any power to benefit or harm one another. And We shall say to the wrongdoers: ‘Taste the punishment of the Fire which you used to deny.’

When Our clear revelations are recited to them, they say: ‘This is just a man who desires to keep you away from what your fathers used to worship.’ And they (also) say: ‘This (Qur’an) is nothing but a fabricated lie.’ And the disbelievers (further) say about the truth when it has come to them: ‘This is nothing but pure magic.’
We did not give them any scriptures they might have studied, nor did We send them any warner before you.
And those before them had denied (the messengers). And these have not yet attained one-tenth of what We had given them. But they rejected My messengers, so how severe was My response?

Say: ‘I advise you to do one thing only: That you rise for Allah’s sake, in pairs or individually (remaining awake and warned) and then reflect. There is no madness in your companion. He is just a warner to you before (the arrival of) a severe punishment.’
Say: ‘Whatever reward I may have asked you is only for yourselves. My reward lies only with Allah, and He is witness over everything.’
Say: ‘Indeed, My Lord utters the truth. (He is) the Great Knower of the unseen things.’
Say: ‘Truth has come, and falsehood can neither originate (anything) nor does it return.’
Say: ‘If I go astray, my going astray is only against myself. And if I follow the right direction, that is because of what my Lord has revealed to me. Indeed, He is All-Hearing, All-Near.’
If you could see when they are terrified with no escape and are seized from a nearby place,
they will say: ‘We believe in it (now).’ But how could they reach that (faith) at a distant place (from the world),
while they had already disbelieved it before? They are guessing at the Unseen from a place far away.
So a barrier will be placed between them and whatever they desire, as was done with their counterparts before. Indeed, they all were in a deceiving doubt.

All praise belongs to Allah, the Originator of the heavens and the earth, the Maker of the angels as messengers who fly on wings with two, three or four (pairs). He increases in creation whatever He wishes. Indeed, Allah is All-Capable of everything.
Whatever mercy Allah opens for the people, there is none to withhold it. And whatever He withholds, there is none to release it after Him. And He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.

O humankind! Remember Allah’s blessing upon you. Is there any Creator other than Allah who may provide you (sustenance) from the heavens and the earth? There is no god except Him. So, how can you be so deluded?
If they deny you, indeed (other) messengers were (also) denied before you. And (in the end) all matters are returned to Allah.
O humankind! Indeed, Allah’s promise is true. Therefore, let not the worldly life deceive you, nor let the Deceiver deceive you regarding Allah.
Indeed, Satan is your enemy. So, treat him as an enemy. He only invites his followers so that they may become the inhabitants of the Blaze.
(As for) the disbelievers, there is a severe punishment for them. But (as for) those who believe and perform righteous deeds, they shall have forgiveness and a great reward.

Is someone whose evil conduct is made so appealing to him that he regards it as good (like the rightly guided and virtuous conduct)? Indeed, Allah leaves to stray whoever He wills and guides whoever He pleases. So do not let your soul waste away in grief for them. Surely, Allah is All-Knowing of what they do.

It is Allah Who sends the winds, and they cause clouds to rise (together). Then We drive it towards a dead land to water it. Then, with it, We revive the earth after its death. Similar will be the resurrection (of the dead).

Whoever seeks honour, then (let him know that) all honour belongs to Allah. The good words ascend to Him alone, and He uplifts the righteous deed. As for those who plot evil deeds, they will suffer a severe punishment. And the plotting of such people will surely perish.

Allah created you from dust, then from a fertilised egg (i.e. a zygote ), and then He made you mates (i.e. males and females). And no female conceives or delivers except with His knowledge. No older person advances in age, nor is anything decreased from his life, but it is (all recorded) in a Book. That is indeed easy for Allah.

The two seas are not equal: This one is sweet, satisfying and delicious to drink, and the other is salty and bitter. Yet from each of them, you eat fresh meat and extract ornaments you wear. And you see the ships cleaving through it so that you may seek His grace and perhaps be grateful (to Him).

He causes the night to merge into the day and causes the day to merge into the night. And He has subjected the sun and the moon, each orbiting for a specified term. That is Allah, your Lord! All sovereignty belongs to Him. And those (idols) you worship besides Him do not control even the skin of a date stone.

(O polytheists!) If you call upon them, they cannot hear your call. And if they were to hear, they could not respond to you. And on the Day of Resurrection, they will deny your ascribing partners to Allah. And no one can inform you like the One Who is All-Aware.
O humankind! You are the ones in need of Allah. And Allah is the One Who is the All-Sufficient, the All-Praiseworthy.
If He wills, He could eliminate you and bring a new creation.
And that is no great matter for Allah.

No bearer will bear the burden of another. And if a heavily burdened soul calls (another) to carry its burden, none of its load will be carried, even if he were a close relative. You can only warn those who fear their Lord in private and maintain the prayer. Whoever purifies himself only purifies for his own sake. And to Allah is the eventual return.
The blind and the seeing are not equal,
nor the darkness and the light,
nor the shade and the heat,

nor are the living equal to the dead. Indeed, Allah makes whoever He pleases to hear, and you cannot make those in the graves hear.* [* Here, the words ‘man fi al-qubur’ (those in the graves) allude to the disbelievers. It means you are not responsible for making the disbelievers accept your call. The leading exegetes have reported that the Companions and the Followers narrated the same meaning. References: Tafsir al-Baghawi, Zad al-Masir, Tafsir al-Qurtubi, Tafsir al-Khazin, Tafsir Ibn Kathir, Tafsir al-Lubab, al-Durr al-Manthur and Fath al-Qadir.]
You are only a warner.
We have sent you with the truth as a conveyor of glad tidings and a warner. And there was not a nation, but a warner had gone among them.
And if they deny you, those before them had also denied (likewise), (when) their messengers brought them clear proofs, divine scriptures and the enlightening Book.
Then I seized the disbelievers (with the torment). So, how severe was My response?
Do you not see that Allah sends down rain from the sky with which We produce fruits of various colours? In the mountains are streaks of white, red, and diverse shades, and others intensely black.
And among humans, beasts and cattle are also diverse colours. Indeed, only the knowledgeable among His servants fear Allah. Indeed, Allah is All-Mighty, Most-Forgiving.
Surely, those who recite the Book of Allah, establish prayer and spend secretly and openly from what We have provided them hope for a trade that will never perish,
so that He may pay them their rewards in full and increase them out of His grace. Indeed, He is Most-Forgiving, Most-Appreciative.
The Book which We have revealed to you is the truth, confirming what was (revealed) before it. Indeed, Allah is All-Aware (and) All-Seeing of His servants.

Then We made heirs to the Book those We chose from Our servants. Yet among them are some who wrong themselves, some who are average (in rank) and some who are foremost in virtuous deeds by Allah’s will. That is the greatest grace.
(There will be) the Gardens of Eternity, which they will enter, where they will be adorned with bracelets of gold and pearls, and their clothing therein will be of silk.
And they will say: ‘All praise belongs to Allah, Who has removed all sorrow from us. Indeed, our Lord is Most-Forgiving, Most-Appreciative,
Who has settled us in the everlasting home by His grace, wherein no exertion will touch us, nor any fatigue will befall us.’

As for the disbelievers, they will have the Fire of Hell. They will neither be done away with so that they may die nor shall its punishment be lightened for them. Thus, We retribute every ungrateful person.

And there (in Hell), they shall cry: ‘Our Lord, take us out (from here and send us back). We will act righteously, different from what we used to do (before).’ (They will be told:) ‘Did We not grant you long enough life during which whoever wanted to seek advice might reflect? And (moreover,) the Warner had also come to you. Now taste (the consequence of your deeds). So, the wrongdoers shall have no helpers.
Indeed, Allah is the Knower of the Unseen of the heavens and the earth. He is undoubtedly All-Knowing of the things (hidden) in the breasts.

He is the One Who made you successors (of the former peoples) on the earth. So, whoever disbelieves is to face the consequences of his disbelief. The denial of the disbelievers increases nothing for them in the sight of their Lord except (His) displeasure. And the denial of the disbelievers adds nothing to them except loss.

Say: ‘Have you considered your partners whom you worship besides Allah? Show me what part of the earth they have created, or do they have any share in the heavens? Have We given them any scripture so that they may rely upon its evidence? Instead, the wrongdoers promise each other nothing but delusion.’
Allah (alone) sustains the heavens and the earth lest they fall apart. And if they were to fall apart, none could hold them except Him. Indeed, He is All-Forbearing, Most Forgiving.

They swore by Allah with their solemn oaths that if a warner were to come to them, they would surely be better guided than any of the nations. Yet when a warner came to them, it increased them in nothing but aversion.

(They started) behaving more arrogantly in the land and intensifying (their) evil schemes. But evil plotting rebounds only on those who devise it. So, do they await anything except the earlier people’s precedent (of destruction)? You will never find any change in Allah’s practice nor see any diversion in Allah’s course.

Have they not travelled across the earth so they may observe what the end of those (destroyed) before them was? They were far superior to them in might. And Allah is not such that anything in the heavens or the earth could frustrate Him. Indeed, He is All-Knowing, All-Capable.

If Allah were to punish people (at once) for what they have earned, He would not have left a single living being on earth. But He grants them respite till a specified term. And when their time arrives, Allah is All-Seeing of (every action of) His servants.
Ya-Sin.
By the Wise Qur’an,
you are indeed one of the messengers,
upon the straight path.
(This is a revelation) sent down gradually from the All-Mighty, the All-Merciful,
so that you may warn a people whose ancestors were not warned, so they are heedless.
The Word has already proved true against most of them, but they do not believe.
We have put iron shackles around their necks, which (reach) up to (their) chins. So their heads are turned up.
We have placed a barrier before them and a barrier behind them, and then We have veiled them so they do not see.
It is the same for them: whether you warn them or do not warn them, they will not believe.
You (can) only warn the one who follows the Reminder and fears the Most-Compassionate (Lord) without seeing Him. So give him the glad tidings of forgiveness and a generous reward.
Indeed, it is We Who revive the dead and write down what they have sent forth and their effects (which they have left behind). We have counted everything in a clear Record.
And set out for them an example of the people of a town when the messengers came to them.
When (at first) We sent them two (messengers), they rejected both of them, then We reinforced (the two) with the third, and they declared: ‘We (all) have been sent to you (as messengers).’
They said: ‘You are only human beings like us, and the Most- Compassionate (Lord) has not revealed anything. You are just lying.’
They (the messengers) said: ‘Our Lord knows that we have been sent to you.
And we are not responsible except for clear communication (of the message).’
They answered: ‘We take you as an evil fortune. If you do not stop, we shall certainly stone you, and surely a painful punishment will afflict you from us.’
They (the messengers) said: ‘Your evil fortune lies within yourselves. (Why do you take it as an evil omen) when you are reminded (of the truth)? Instead, you are a transgressing people.’
And a person came running from the farthest end of the city. He said: ‘O my people, follow the messengers.
Follow those who do not ask you for any reward, and they are rightly guided.
Why should I not worship Him Who has originated me, and to Whom you shall be returned?
Should I take gods besides Him? If the Most-Compassionate (Lord) intends to cause me any harm, their intercession will not benefit me in any way, nor will they be able to rescue me.
Indeed, I would then be in a clear error.
I do believe in your Lord, so listen to me (attentively).’
(The disbelievers killed him, then) it was said (to him): ‘Enter Paradise!’ He said: ‘Alas! If only my people could know,
for what my Lord has forgiven me and placed me among the honoured ones.’
And after him, We did not send any army (of angels) from the heavens upon his people, nor were We to send down (any punishment to destroy them).
There was just one blast, and they were extinguished (like burnt ashes).
Alas! For the (defiant) servants! No messenger ever came to them, but they used to mock him.
Have they not seen how many generations We have destroyed before them, who would never return to them?
Yet they all will indeed be presented before Us?
And a sign for them is the dead earth. We give it (fresh) life and bring the grain out of it so they eat from it.
We produce in it gardens of date palms and grapevines, and cause springs to gush out of it,
so that they may eat from its fruits, which their hands did not produce. Will they not then give thanks?
Glory be to Him, Who created all things in pairs from what the earth produces, from themselves, and from other things they do not know.
And a sign for them is the night. We strip the daylight from it. And behold! They find themselves in darkness!
And the sun (constantly) moves upon the course appointed for it. That is the measured design of the All-Mighty, the All-Knowing.
As for the moon, We have (also) determined phases (for its motion and rotation) until it appears back (to the dwellers of the earth) like an old dry branch of a palm tree.

It is not allowable for the sun that it should (deviate from its orbit and) overtake the moon, nor can the night precede the day. And all of them (i.e. the stars and the planets) are moving in their respective orbits.
Another sign for them is that We carried their seed (from the progeny of Adam) into the laden Ark (of Noah and saved them).
And like this (Ark,) We created for them (many other things) which they ride.
If We wish, We can drown them, then they will have none to call for help, and they will not be rescued either,
except by a mercy from Us, and for an enjoyment until some time.
When it is said to them: ‘Beware of what is ahead of you (in the Hereafter), and what is behind you (from the remnants of the destroyed nations), so that you may be shown mercy.’
Whenever a sign came to them from among the signs of their Lord, they always turned away from it.

And when they are told: ‘Spend out of what Allah has provided you,’ the disbelievers say to the believers: ‘Shall we feed (someone) whom Allah would have fed if He had wanted?’ You are just in clear error.
And they say: ‘When will this promise (of the Last Hour) be fulfilled if you are truthful?’
But all they are waiting for is a single blast that will overtake them while they are disputing among themselves.
Then they will not be able to make a bequest, nor will they return to their families.
The Trumpet will be blown (again). And behold! They will rush out to their Lord from (their) graves.
They will say: ‘Alas for us! Who has raised us from our place of sleep? This is what the Most-Compassionate (Lord) had promised, and the messengers spoke the truth.’
It will be just a single Blast. And behold! They will all be presented before Us.
Today, no soul shall be wronged in the least, nor shall you be rewarded except for what you used to do.
Indeed, on that Day, the people of Paradise will be busy enjoying themselves.
They and their spouses will be in (cool) shades, reclining on couches.
They shall have fruits there, and they shall have whatever they ask for.
‘Peace!’ (It will be) a word (of greeting) from the All-Merciful Lord.
(And it would be said:) ‘Get apart today, O evildoers!’
‘Did I not command you, O Children of Adam, that you should not worship Satan? He is indeed your open enemy.
And that you should worship Me. This is a straight path.
Yet, he has already misled many of your generations. Did you still not understand?
This is the Hell that you were promised!
Enter it today because of what you used to deny.’
That Day, We will seal their mouths. Their hands will speak to Us, and their feet will bear witness to what they used to earn.
Had We wished, We would have blotted out their eyes. Then, if they had advanced towards the path, how could they see?
If We had wished, We would have deformed (or paralysed) them at their places. Then, they could neither go ahead nor turn back.
And whoever We grant a long life, We cause him to reverse in development (and strength). Do they not yet understand?
We did not teach him poetry, nor is it befitting for him. This (Book) is only a Reminder and a manifest Qur’an.
So he may warn whoever is alive, and the decree (of Allah) may prove true against the disbelievers.
Do they not see that We have created livestock for them, among (all other things) that Our Mighty Hands have generated, and they have control over them?
And We subjected these (animals) to them. So some of them are used for riding and some for food.
And in these (animals), there are other advantages and drinks (derived from them). Will they not then give thanks?
Still, they have taken (idols as) gods besides Allah, hoping they might be helped.
But they cannot help them, and those (idolaters) will be an army for these (idols), presented (together in Hell).
So do not let their words grieve you. We indeed know what they conceal and what they reveal.

Does human being not see that We created him from a fertilised egg (i.e. zygote*)? And even then, he is an open disputant? [* In Arabic, the word ‘ al-nutfa ’ is mentioned, and it refers to a zygote, which is a mixture of male and female gametes. It is the new single cell formed after the union of a sperm and ovum.]
And he draws comparisons for Us and forgets (the truth of) his creation. He says: ‘Who will revive the bones when they have (already) decayed?’
Say: ‘He will revive them Who brought them into existence in the first instance. And He is All-Knowing of every created being.
(He is) the One Who produces fire for you out of the green tree. And behold! You kindle light from it.
Is He Who created the heavens and the earth not able to produce the likes of them (again)?’ Of course (He can)! For He is the Supreme Creator, the All-Knowing.
His command, when He wills something, is to only say to it: ‘Be!’ So it is.
So, Glory be to Him in Whose Hand lies the dominion of all things, and to Whom you shall be returned.
By those (angels) ranged in ranks,
by the ones who drive (the clouds) vigorously,* [* Or: by the angels who firmly restrain people from evil.]
(and) by the ones who recite the Reminder:* [* That is the believers or the angels who recite the Qur’an or the scriptures.]
surely, your God is One,
the Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, and the Lord of the easts.
We have decorated the nearest heaven with the adornment of stars and planets.
And (there is) protection (for them) from every rebellious devil.
They cannot listen to the Highest Assembly (of the angels), for they are repelled (through burning brands) from every side,
being driven away, and there is an everlasting punishment for them.
But whoever (among the devils) tries to snatch a fragment (of information from the angels), a piercing flame pursues him.
So ask them: are they more difficult to create or those (other creatures) We have created (in the heavenly universe)? Surely, We have created them just from sticky clay.
Indeed, you wonder while they ridicule (your message).
And (even) when they are reminded, they never become mindful.
And whenever they see a sign, they incite one another to ridicule.
And say: ‘This is nothing but clear magic.
What! When we are dead and have become dust and bones, shall we then be resurrected?
And our forefathers as well?’
Say: ‘Yes indeed! And you will (also) be humiliated.’
So, it will be only a single blast, and then they will look at once.
And they will say: ‘Oh, woe to us! It is the Day of Retribution.’
(It will be said: ‘Yes!) It is the Day of Final Judgement, which you used to deny.’
(Allah will command:) ‘Assemble all the wrongdoers with their counterparts and those (false gods) they used to worship,
besides Allah, and show them the way to Hell.
And stop them! For they shall be questioned.’
(It will be said to them:) ‘What is the matter with you that you do not help each other (now)?’
Rather, that Day, they will be entirely submissive!
And some of them will turn to others, questioning each other.
They (the misled followers) will say: ‘You came to us from a position of power (swearing an oath of being right).’
They (the leaders) will answer: ‘No! You had disbelieved on your own.
We had no power over you. Instead, you yourselves were already a rebellious people.
So the decree of our Lord against us is correct. We shall indeed taste (the punishment now).
So we caused you to deviate (from the right path), for we were deviant (ourselves).’
So on that Day they shall all share in the punishment.
Indeed, that is how We deal with the guilty.
Whenever it was said to them: ‘There is none worthy of worship except Allah,’ they used to show arrogance.
And (they would) say: ‘Should we abandon our gods for a crazy poet?’
Instead, He has come with the truth and confirmed the (former) messengers.
Indeed, you shall taste the painful punishment.
And you will be rewarded only for what you used to do,
except the chosen (and purified) servants of Allah.
For them, there shall be known provision,
fruits (of every kind), and they shall be highly honoured,
in Gardens of Bliss,
(seated) on couches, facing one another,
served with a cup (of sacred drink) passing around them from a pure flowing spring.
(The drink will be) crystal white (and) delicious to the drinkers.
Therein will be neither any headache nor will they be intoxicated.
And with them will be spouses of modest gazes, with beautiful lustrous eyes,
as if they were eggs (or pearls) carefully protected (from dust).
Then some of those (i.e. the residents of Paradise) will turn to one another, questioning each other.
One among them will say: ‘Indeed, I had a companion (in my worldly life),
who used to say (to me): ‘Are you (also) among those who confirm,
(that) when we are dead and have become dust and bones, we shall be brought (alive again) for retribution?’’
Then he (i.e. one of his companions in Paradise) will say: ‘Would you like to look (at his fate)?’
Then he will look and see him in the middle of Hell.
He will say (to him): ‘By God, you had almost brought me to ruin.
Had it not been for my Lord’s blessing, I (too) would have been among those brought (to Hell).’
(Then he will ask his fellow dwellers of Paradise:) ‘Is it (true) that we are never to die (again),
except for the previous death (which we have already passed through), and that we shall never be punished?’
This indeed is the supreme success.
Let the workers (of righteousness) strive for (success) like this!
Is this a better hospitality or the tree of al-Zaqqum?
We have made this (tree) a trial for the wrongdoers.
Indeed, it is a tree that grows from the bottom of the Hellfire.
Its fruits are as if they were heads of the devils.
They shall eat from it and fill their bellies with it.
On top of that, they shall have a boiling liquid (mixed with pus to drink) after it.
Then, their ultimate return will be to Hell.
Indeed, they had found their forefathers astray.
So they are rushed (to follow) in their footsteps.
And indeed, most of the ancients went astray before them.
And indeed, We had sent warners among them.
So see how was the end of those who had been warned,
except the exclusively chosen (and purified) servants of Allah.
Indeed, Nuh (Noah) called upon Us, and how excellent are We in responding!
And We saved him and (the believers among) his family from great distress.
We made his descendants the only survivors.
And We left for him (an honourable mention) among later generations.
‘Peace be upon Nuh (Noah) throughout the nations!’
Indeed, this is how We reward the virtuous.
Surely, he was among Our faithful servants.
Then We drowned the others.
Indeed, one of those who followed his way was Ibrahim (Abraham).
When he came to his Lord with a submissive, sound heart,
and said to his (foster) father and his people: ‘What are you worshipping?
Do you desire false gods, by perversion, besides Allah?
So, what do you expect from the Lord of all worlds?’
Then he looked up to the stars as an observation.
And said: ‘Indeed, I am sick (of your worshipping these things).’
So, they went away from him, turning (their) backs.
Then he went quietly to their gods and said (mockingly): ‘Do you not eat (from your offerings)?
Why do you not speak?’
Then, he turned upon them, striking (and breaking) them forcefully.
Later, they came running towards him.
He said: ‘Do you worship what you carve (with your hands),
while Allah has created you and whatever you do?’
They said: ‘Build a structure and throw him into the blazing fire.’
They desired to scheme against him, but We made them the lowest (thus defeating them in their plan).
He said (while migrating to Syria): ‘I am going to my Lord. He will certainly guide me.’
‘My Lord, bless me with (a son) among the righteous.’
So We gave him the glad tidings of a gentle son. (And he was Isma‘il [Ishmael]).

When he (Isma‘il) reached the age to work alongside him, he (Ibrahim) said (to him): ‘My dear son! I have seen in a dream that I am sacrificing you. So think, what is your opinion?’ He (Isma‘il) replied: ‘My dear father! Do whatever you are commanded. If Allah pleases, you will find me among the patient.’
So when they both submitted (to Allah’s will) and he laid him on his forehead (for sacrifice),
We called out to him: ‘O Ibrahim (Abraham)!
You have fulfilled the dream!’ Indeed, this is how We reward the virtuously excellent!
Most surely, this was a manifest trial.
Then We ransomed him with a great sacrifice.
And We left for him (an honourable mention) among later generations.
Peace be upon Ibrahim (Abraham)!
That is how We reward the virtuously excellent.
Surely, he was among Our faithful servants.
Then We gave him glad tidings of (the birth of another son) Ishaq (Isaac)—a prophet (and) one of the righteous.
And We bestowed blessings upon him and Ishaq (Isaac). And among their descendants, (some) are virtuous and (some) who openly wrong themselves.
And indeed, We conferred favour upon Musa (Moses) and Harun (Aaron).
And We saved them and their people from great distress.
And We helped them, so they were the ones who became the victors.
We gave the two of them the clarifying Book (i.e. the Torah),
and guided them both to the straight path.
And We left for them (an honourable mention) among the later generations.
Peace be upon Musa (Moses) and Harun (Aaron)!
Indeed, this is how We reward the virtuously excellent.
Surely, they both were among Our faithful servants.
And indeed, Ilyas (Elias) was one of the messengers.
When he said to his people: ‘Will you not be God-conscious?
Do you worship (the idol) Ba‘l and leave the best of the creators,
Allah, your Lord and the Lord of your forefathers?’
But they denied him, so they will surely be presented (for punishment),
except the exclusively chosen (and purified) servants of Allah.
And We left for him (an honourable mention) among the later generations.
Peace be upon Ilyas (Elias)!
Indeed, this is how We reward the virtuously excellent.
Surely, he was among Our faithful servants.
And indeed, Lut (Lot) was among the messengers.
When We saved him and all of his family,
except an old woman among those who stayed behind.
Then We destroyed the others.
And indeed, (O Meccans!) you pass by their ruins in the daylight,
and in the darkness of night (during your travels between Mecca and Syria). Do you not yet understand?
And indeed, Yunus (Jonah) was among the messengers,
when he fled to the overloaded ship.
So he joined (the other passengers) in drawing lots (to save the ship from sinking), but he was the one to be overthrown (into the sea).
Then the whale swallowed him, and he was a self-condemning repentant.
If he had not been among those who glorify (Allah),
he would have surely remained in its belly till the day people will be resurrected.
Then We cast him out upon the open shore while he was sick.
And We caused a gourd plant to grow over him.
Then We sent him to (a city of) a hundred thousand people or more (i.e. Nineveh, in the land of Mosul),
who then believed (in him). So, We granted them prosperity for a while.
Ask them: ‘Are daughters to be for your Lord while sons are to be for them?
Or did We create the angels as females while they were watching?’
Indeed! It is one of their fabrications that they say:
‘Allah has children,’ and indeed, they are liars.
Has He chosen daughters (for Himself) over sons?
What is the matter with you? How do you judge (things)?
Will you not then be mindful of advice?
Do you have (any) clear authority (to prove your accusation)?
Then, produce your scripture if you are truthful.
They also claim that He has kinship with the jinn. Yet the jinn (themselves) know they will be presented (before Him for judgement).
Allah is far above whatever they attribute (to Him).
But the chosen servants of Allah (do not say such things).
Indeed, neither you nor whatever you worship,
you can never tempt anyone against Him,
except someone who is to burn in the Hellfire.
And (the angels say:) ‘There is none among us but has an assigned station (of worship).
We are, indeed, the ones ranged in ranks.
And surely, we are the ones (constantly) glorifying His praise.’
And indeed, they (the pagans of Mecca) used to say:
‘If only we had a Reminder from the ancients,
we would surely have been chosen servants of Allah.’
But they disbelieve in it (now when it has come to them). So they will soon know (their end).
Indeed, Our Word has already gone forth in favour of Our servants, the messengers,
that they will surely be the supported ones.
And indeed, Our forces will be the victors.
So turn away from them for a while,
and watch them. So they will soon see (what happens to them).
Do they seek to hasten Our punishment?
But when it descends on their courtyards, it will be a terrible morning for those (already) warned!
So turn away from them for a while,
and watch them. So they will soon see (what happens to them).
Glory be to your Lord, the Lord of Might. (He is) far above whatever they attribute (to Him).
Peace be upon the messengers!
And all praise belongs to Allah, the Lord of all worlds.
Sad. By the Qur’an, full of Reminder.
(This is the truth) but the disbelievers are steeped in arrogance and opposition.
How many generations have We destroyed before them? And they called out for help when the time for escape had passed.
They think it strange that a warner has come to them from among themselves. So the disbelievers say: ‘This is a magician, a great liar.
Has he reduced the gods to one God? Indeed, this is something extremely astonishing!’
The leaders among them (i.e. the Quraysh) departed (saying): ‘Walk away and stand firm to your gods! Indeed, this is something designed (to usurp power).
We did not hear any such claim in the last religion. It is nothing but a fabrication.
Has the Reminder been revealed (only) to him among us (all)?’ Instead, they are in doubt regarding My Reminder. Nay! They have not yet tasted My punishment.
Do they possess the treasures of your Lord’s mercy—the All-Mighty, the Great Bestower?
Or do they own the kingdom of the heavens, the earth and whatever is between them? (If so,) then let them ascend (into the heavens) by some means.
(They are just) an army bound to be crushed from among the factions.
Before them, the people of Nuh (Noah) and ‘Ad denied (their messengers), and (so did) Pharaoh, who used to impale (his victims).
And (so did) Thamud and the people of Lut (Lot) and the Forest-dwellers (i.e. the people of Shu‘ayb). These were all confederates (against the truth).
Each of them denied the messengers. So, My retribution (against them) became due.
All these (disbelievers) are waiting just for a single blast that will not be postponed (for a while).
And they say (mockingly): ‘Our Lord! Hasten for us our share before the Day of Reckoning.’
Be patient over what they say and remember Our servant Dawud (David), the possessor of power. Indeed, he was persistent in turning (to Allah).
Indeed, We subjected the mountains to glorify (His praises) with him in the evening and after sunrise.
And the birds were (also) flocked together (for him). Each would turn towards him (echoing his praise and seeking to obey his command).
We strengthened his kingdom and blessed him with wisdom and a clear and conclusive way of speaking.
Has the story of the litigants reached you who climbed into his chamber of worship?

When they entered the presence of Dawud (David), he felt nervous because of them. They said: ‘Have no fear. We are two litigants: One of us has wronged the other. So judge between us fairly; do not exceed the bounds of justice; and guide us towards the right path.’
‘Surely, he is my brother. He has ninety-nine sheep, while I have only one sheep. Yet he says: ‘Place her under my care,’ and he overpowers me in (his) conversation.

He (Dawud) said: ‘He has been unjust to you in demanding your sheep in addition to his sheep. And indeed, many partners mistreat each other except those who believe and perform righteous deeds, and very few are they.’ Then Dawud (David) realised that We had tested him. So he asked for his Lord’s forgiveness, fell down in prostration and turned (to Him) in repentance.
So We forgave him that. And indeed, he has (a station of) nearness in Our presence and an honourable position (in the Hereafter).

O Dawud (David)! Indeed, We have made you a ruler in the land. So, judge between the people with justice. And do not follow (your) desire, for it will lead you astray from the path of Allah. Indeed, those who go astray from Allah’s way will suffer a severe punishment because they forgot the Day of Reckoning.
We did not create the heavens, the earth and whatever is between them in vain—as the assumption of the disbelievers. So there is, for the disbelievers, a great suffering of the Hellfire.
Shall We treat those who believe and perform righteous deeds like those who cause corruption on the earth? Or shall We treat the God-conscious like the wicked?
(This is) a blessed Book, which We have revealed to you, so that they may contemplate its verses and those possessing intellect may be mindful of advice.
And We blessed Dawud (David) with (a son named) Sulayman (Solomon). What an excellent servant (he was)! Indeed, he was persistently repentant.
When one evening, swift horses of a fine breed were displayed before him,
he said (remorsefully): ‘I have preferred the love of good (things, i.e. horses) over the remembrance of my Lord, until (the sun) disappeared behind the veil (of the night).’
(He said:) ‘Bring them back to me.’ Then he began to stroke (their) legs and necks (and thus he sacrificed his worldly love for the love of God).
And indeed, We (also) tried Sulayman (Solomon) and placed a (lifeless) body on his throne. Then, he turned to Allah in repentance.
He said: ‘My Lord! Forgive me and grant me a kingdom that shall not befit anyone after me. Surely, You are the Most-Generous Provider.’
Then We made the wind subservient to him, blowing gently by his command wherever he wished.
And (We subjected to him) the jinn (as well as) every builder and diver,
and others, bound together in chains.
(Allah said:) ‘This is Our gift. So (as you wish) give (to others) or withhold (for yourself)—without reckoning.’
Indeed, he has (a station of) nearness in Our presence and an honourable position (in the Hereafter).
Remember Our servant Ayyub (Job), when he called upon his Lord: ‘Satan has afflicted me with distress and suffering.’
(We told him:) ‘Strike (the ground) with your foot. Here is a refreshingly cool spring for washing and drinking.’
And We gave (back to) him his family, with many more like them, as a mercy from Us and a lesson for people of reason.

(And We told him:) ‘Take a small bunch of green kindling in your hand, strike (your wife) with it and do not break your oath.’ Indeed, We found him patient. What an excellent servant (he was)! Indeed, he was persistently repentant.
And remember Our servants Ibrahim (Abraham), Ishaq (Isaac) and Ya‘qub (Jacob)—men of (spiritual) power and vision.
We purified them with the exclusiveness of the remembrance of the (final) abode.
And in Our sight, they are among the elect of the best.
And remember Isma‘il (Ishmael), al-Yasa‘ (Elisha) and Dhu al-Kifl (Ezekiel). Each (of them) was among the elect.
This is a Reminder. And indeed, for all the God-conscious, there will be an honourable destination:
The Gardens of Eternity, whose gates will remain wide open for them.
(They will be) comfortably reclining there (on couches), calling for abundant fruits and drinks.
And with them will be (their) spouses, with modest gazes, well-matched in age.
This is what you are promised for the Day of Reckoning.
This is indeed Our provision, which will never be exhausted.
