This (is for the righteous). As for the rebellious, there shall be the worst destination.
(That is) Hell, where they shall enter (to burn). What an evil place to stay!
This (is the punishment). Let them taste it: boiling liquid and murky pus fluid,
and other kinds (of punishment) resembling in nature.
(The guards of Hell or the inmates will say:) ‘Here is another crowd of people rushing into Hell along with you. There is no welcome for them. They will also burn in the Fire.’
They will say: ‘No! You are not welcome. It is you who prepared (this Hell) for us. What an evil abode!’
They will say (adding): ‘Our Lord! Whoever has prepared this for us, double his punishment in the Fire.’
And they will say (to one another): ‘What is the matter with us that we do not see (those) persons (here) whom we used to count among the evil ones?
Did we ridicule them (unjustly), or have (our) eyes missed them?’
Surely, that is true: the mutual dispute between the people of the Fire.
Say: ‘I am just a warner, and there is no god except Allah, the One, the All-Dominant,
the Lord of the heavens and the earth and what is between them, the All-Mighty, the Most Forgiving.’
Say: ‘This (Day of Reckoning) is momentous news,
from which you are turning away.’
(And say:) ‘I had no knowledge (of my own accord) regarding the Supreme Assembly (of the angels) when they differed (regarding Adam).
All that is revealed to me is that I am just a manifest warner.’
(Remember) when your Lord said to the angels: ‘Indeed, I am going to create a human being from clay.
So, when I have proportioned him and breathed into him My spirit, then fall down in prostration before Him.’
So the angels prostrated (to honour him), all of them together,
except Iblis; he acted arrogantly, so he became among the disbelievers.

He (Allah) said: ‘O Iblis! What has prevented you from prostrating to what I have created with My Own Hands? Did you become arrogant, or are you among the exalted (presuming yourself superior to Adam)?’
(Comparing himself to Prophet Adam) he (Iblis) said: ‘I am better than him. You have created me from fire, while You have created him from clay.’
He (Allah) said: ‘Get out from here (i.e. Paradise). For surely you are accursed.
My curse will remain upon you till the Day of Retribution.’
He (Iblis) said: ‘My Lord! Then grant me respite (to remain alive) till the Day they are resurrected.’
Allah said: ‘(Be off!) Indeed, you are among those granted respite,
till the Day of the known time.’
He (Iblis) said: ‘I swear by Your Honour, I will surely mislead them all,
except Your purified (and chosen) servants among them.’
Allah said: ‘So the truth is (this), and I only speak the truth:
‘I will surely fill Hell with you and those who follow you from them, all together.’
Say (O Prophet!): ‘I do not ask you any reward for this (Qur’an), nor am I among the pretenders.
It is just a Reminder for the whole world.
And you will surely learn its truth after some time.’
The revelation of this Book is from Allah, the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.
Indeed (O Prophet!), We have revealed the Book to you with the truth. So worship Allah, being sincerely devoted to Him in faith.

‘Listen! Exclusive devotion and obedience are only for Allah.’ And those (disbelievers) who take (idols as their) guardians besides Him (claim in false justification of their act:) ‘We only worship them so that they may bring us nearer to Allah.’ Allah Himself will judge between them regarding the matter in which they differ. Indeed, Allah does not guide whoever is a liar and highly ungrateful.
If Allah had desired to beget children (for Himself), He could have chosen whatever He pleased from those He has created. Glory be to Him! He is Allah, the One, the All-Dominant.

He created the heavens and the earth with a truthful purpose. He wraps the night over the day and wraps the day over the night. And He has subjected the sun and the moon, each moving (in its orbit) for a specified time. Behold! He is the All-Mighty, the Most Forgiving.

He created you from a single soul and then made a similar pair from it. And He produced for you eight pairs of cattle. He creates you in the wombs of your mothers (in stages), one phase of development after another, in three-fold (germinal) layers (of darkness).* That is Allah, your Lord; to Him belongs all Power and Authority. There is no god except Him. Then, where are you being turned away? [* The realisation that the human embryo develops in stages was not discussed and illustrated until the 15th century. Leonardo da Vinci drew the first known illustration of a foetus in the uterus in the 15th century. After the microscope was discovered in the 17th century by a Dutch microscopist, Antonie van Leeuwenhoek, the early stages of chick embryo were described for the first time in history. The staging of human embryos was not described until the 20th century. Streeter (1941) developed the first system of staging of the human embryo, which has been now replaced by a more accurate system proposed by O Rahilly (1972). Therefore, the three veils of darkness may refer to: (1) the anterior abdominal wall; (2) the uterine wall; and (3) the amniochorionic membrane. According to another interpretation, the three veils of darkness may refer to: (1) chorion; (2) amnion; and (3) amniotic fluid. But the first interpretation is known to be the most logical from an embryological point of view. (See: Arey L. B. in Development of Anatomy: A Textbook and Laboratory Manual of Embryology; Moor, K. L. in The Developing Human Clinically Oriented Embryology; and O Rahilly in R. in Guide to the Staging of Human Embryo.)]

If you disbelieve, then surely Allah does not need you, nor does He approve disbelief (or ingratitude) for His servants. And if you give thanks, He will appreciate that for you. No bearer shall bear the burden of another. Then to your Lord will be your return. Then He will inform you of what you used to do. Indeed, He is All-Knowing of what is (hidden) in the breasts.

When suffering afflicts human being, he supplicates his Lord, turning to Him penitently. But as soon as He bestows a blessing upon him from Himself, he forgets the One to Whom He had supplicated before. And he (again) sets up rivals to Allah to mislead (others) from His way. Say: ‘(O disbeliever!) Enjoy your disbelief for a short while. Indeed, you are among the inhabitants of the Fire.’

Is he who is devoutly obedient during hours of the night, prostrating and standing (in prayer), fearing the Hereafter and hoping for the mercy of his Lord (better or someone who disobeys Him)? Say: ‘Are those who know equal to those who do not know?’ So, only the people of intellect can accept advice.

(Esteemed Prophet!) Say (on My behalf): ‘O My servants who believe, be conscious of your Lord. For those who do good in this world, there will be a good (reward in the Hereafter). And Allah’s earth is vast. Indeed, the patient shall be paid their reward in full without reckoning.’
Say: ‘Indeed, I am commanded to worship Allah, being sincere to Him in devotion and obedience.
I am also commanded to be the first of those who submit (to Him).’
Say: ‘Indeed, should I disobey My Lord, I fear the punishment of a tremendous Day.’
Say: ‘(Only) Allah do I worship, devoting my faith exclusively to Him.

So worship whatever (gods) you wish besides Him.’ Say: ‘Indeed, the (true) losers are the ones who will lose themselves and their families on the Day of Resurrection. Beware! That is the most apparent loss.
There shall be canopies of fire above them and (similar) canopies beneath them. That is how Allah makes His servants fear.’ So, My servants! Be conscious of Me.
And those who stay away from worshipping false gods and turn to Allah (alone) will have good news. So give glad tidings to My servants,
who listen (attentively) to what is said and follow the best (sense) of it—they are the ones whom Allah has rightly guided, and they are ones who possess intellect.
Can he against whom the word of torment has become due (escape punishment)? And can you save someone who is (already) in the Fire?

But those conscious of their Lord will have elevated mansions (with other) lofty abodes built above them, with rivers flowing beneath them. (That is) the promise of Allah. Allah does not break His promise.

Do you not see that Allah sends down rain from the sky and then channels it as springs under the ground? Then, He produces crops of various colours through it. Then they wither, and you see them turning yellow. Then He makes them crushed and broken into pieces. There is indeed a reminder in that for the possessors of intellect.

What about the one whose breast Allah has opened to Islam, so he (walks) in a light from his Lord? Alas, for those whose hearts have been hardened to the remembrance of Allah. It is they who are in clear error.

Allah has revealed the most beautiful discourse (in the form) of a Book (composed) of similar patterns (in format and meaning) and repeated lessons (in resembling verses) that cause the skins of those who fear their Lord to tremble. Then, their skins and hearts soften at Allah’s remembrance. That is Allah’s guidance through which He guides whomever He wills. And whomever Allah leaves to stray will be left with no guide.
What about one who shields himself only with his face from the terrible punishment on the Day of Resurrection? And the wrongdoers will be told: ‘Taste what you used to earn.’
Those before them also denied (the messengers), and then the punishment overtook them whence they were unaware.
So Allah made them taste humiliation in the life of this world, and the punishment of the Hereafter will surely be greater. Had they only known!
Surely, We have set forth for people in this Qur’an all kinds of illustrations, so perhaps they may accept advice.
(It is) a Qur’an (revealed) in Arabic, without crookedness, so perhaps they may become God-conscious.

Allah draws an example: ‘There is an enslaved person jointly owned by several quarrelling masters. And (on the other hand) there is a person belonging entirely to one master. Are they both equal in condition?’ (Not at all!) All praise belongs to Allah! But most of them do not know.

(Esteemed Prophet!) You will die, and they (too) will indeed die.* [* The difference between the two deaths is worth seeing. As the two slaves mentioned in verse 29 cannot be equal, similarly, it is said that the passing of the Holy Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him) and the death of others can never be alike; in reality, both are completely different. A slave owned by one master is safe and secure; in contrast, a slave shared by many masters is ruined. My Beloved Prophet! You are an exclusively chosen and highly esteemed servant of the Lord; you do not share your devotion and loyalty to Him with anyone else. In contrast, the disbelievers share their servitude, devotion and loyalty with many self-made gods. Therefore, death cannot subdue you, but it will make them fall prey to eternal damnation and extinction.]

Then, on the Day of Resurrection, you will dispute with one another before your Lord.* [* One group will say to the other: ‘You stopped us from understanding the esteemed station of the Prophet and accepting his glorious message.’ Others will reply: ‘No, you were misguided yourselves.’]
So, who is more unjust than the one who attributes lies to Allah and denies the truth when it comes to him? Is not the final abode of the disbelievers in Hell?
The one who has brought the truth and the one who has confirmed it—they are the ones who are (truly) God-conscious.
They shall have whatever they desire in the presence of their Lord. That is the reward of the virtuously excellent.
(It is) so that Allah may absolve them of the worst of what they did and reward them with the best of what they used to do.

Is Allah not sufficient for His servant? Yet they (the disbelievers) seek to frighten you (Esteemed Prophet) with those (false gods) besides Him! But whoever Allah leaves to stray will be left with no guide.
And whoever Allah guides, none can lead him astray. Is Allah not All-Mighty, Capable of Vengeance?

If you ask them who created the heavens and the earth, they will surely say: ‘Allah’. Say: ‘Have you considered whatever (idols) you worship besides Allah? If Allah desires (some) harm for me, can they remove His harm? Or if He desires (some) mercy for me, can they withhold His mercy?’ Say: ‘Allah is Sufficient for me. In Him alone, the trusting people should put their trust.’
(Esteemed Prophet!) Say: ‘My people! Act according to your position and ability, (for) I (too) am acting (according to mine). You will soon come to know,
who is the one whom the punishment will overtake (in this life), which will disgrace him, and upon whom an everlasting torment (of the next life) will descend.’

Surely, We have revealed the Book to you with the truth for (the guidance of) people. So, whoever chooses to be guided is (guided) for his own benefit, and whoever goes astray strays only to his own detriment. And you are not a guardian over them.

Allah takes the souls (of people) at the moment of their death and (the souls of) those who are not dead during their sleep. Then He withholds those (souls) for whom He has ordained death and releases the others until (their) specified time. Indeed, there are signs in that for people who reflect.
Have they taken (idols as) intercessors besides Allah? Say: ‘Even though they (i.e. the idols) have no authority over anything, nor do they understand?’
Say: ‘All (permission of) intercession belongs to Allah. To Him belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. Then to Him, you (all) will be returned.’
Whenever Allah is mentioned alone, the hearts of those who do not believe in the Hereafter get shrunk with aversion. And whenever those (idols) besides Allah are mentioned, they get filled with joy!
Say: ‘O Allah! The Originator of the heavens and the earth, the Knower of the Unseen and the seen! You will judge between Your servants regarding what they used to dispute.’

Even if the unjust were to possess everything on the earth and twice of it besides, they will surely seek to ransom themselves with (all of) it from the terrible punishment on the Day of Resurrection. But there will appear to them clearly from Allah what they could never have imagined.
And the evil (consequence) of what they have earned will openly appear to them. And whatever they used to ridicule will besiege them.

When any suffering afflicts human being, he calls upon Us. Then, when We confer upon him a blessing from Us, he says: ‘I have been granted it because of (my) knowledge.’ Instead, it is just a trial. But most of them do not know.
Those before them did say the same. But what they used to earn did not benefit them.

So the evil (consequence) of what they had earned overtook them. And as for the wrongdoers among these (pagans), the evil (consequence) will (also) overtake them, and they will not be able to escape (from Allah’s might).
And do they not know that Allah expands provision for whomever He pleases and tightens (it for whomever He wills)? There are indeed signs in that for people who believe.
Say: ‘O My servants who have transgressed against their souls! Do not lose hope in the mercy of Allah. Indeed, Allah will forgive all sins. Certainly, He is the Most-Forgiving, the All-Merciful.’
Turn to your Lord in repentance and (fully) submit to Him before the punishment overtakes you. Then you will not be helped.
And follow the most beautiful (message) that has been revealed to you from your Lord before the punishment comes upon you suddenly, while you are unaware.
Lest any soul should say: ‘Woe to me for what I neglected of my duty to Allah! Indeed, I was among the ridiculers.’
Or one should say: ‘If only Allah had guided me, I would have surely been among the God-conscious.’
Or one should say when he sees the punishment: ‘If only there had been a second chance for me, I would be among the virtuous.’
(Allah will say:) ‘Nay! My revelations did come to you, but you denied them and acted arrogantly, and you were among the disbelievers.’
On the Day of Resurrection, you will see those who attributed lies to Allah having their faces blackened. Is not the final abode of the arrogant in Hell?
Allah will deliver those who were God-conscious, bestowing (them) with (ultimate) success. No evil will touch them, nor will they grieve.
Allah is the Creator of everything. And He is the Guardian over everything.
To Him belong the keys (to the treasures) of the heavens and the earth. And those who disbelieve in the revelations of Allah, it is they who are the losers.
Say: ‘Are you urging me to worship (someone) other than Allah, O you senseless people!’
Indeed, it has been revealed to you and those before you: ‘(O people!) If you ascribe partners (to Allah), your deeds will certainly be void, and you shall surely be among the losers.
Rather, worship Allah (alone) and be among the grateful.’

They do not appreciate Allah’s Glory with the appreciation that is due to Him. Yet the entire earth will be in His Grip on the Day of Resurrection, and the heavens will be rolled up in His right Hand. Glory be to Him (and) Exalted is He above whatever they associate (with Him).

The Trumpet will be blown. And whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth will fall senseless, except whomever Allah pleases. Then it will be blown a second time, and they will stand up at once, awaiting.

And the earth will shine with the Light of its Lord, and the Record (of people’s deeds) shall be laid down. And the prophets and the witnesses will be brought forward, and the judgement will be passed between them with justice. And they will not be wronged.
And every soul will be paid in full for what it has done, and He knows best what they do.

And those who disbelieve will be driven to Hell in groups. When they arrive there, its gates will be opened (then). Its keepers will say to them: ‘Did messengers not come to you from among yourselves, reciting upon you the verses of your Lord and warning you about the meeting of this Day of yours.’ They will say: ‘Yes, (they came).’ But the decree of the punishment had become due against the disbelievers.
It will be said: ‘Enter the gates of Hell, to remain there forever. So evil is the (final) abode of the arrogant!’

Those who were conscious of their Lord will be led to Paradise in (respectful) congregations. When they arrive there, its gates will already be opened. Its keepers will say to them: ‘Peace be upon you! You are welcome! Enter it to remain here forever.’

They will say: ‘All praise belongs to Allah, Who has fulfilled His promise to us and made us inherit the (everlasting) land, that we may settle in Paradise wherever we wish.’ So, how excellent is the reward for the (righteous) workers!

And you will see the angels encircling around the Throne, glorifying the praise of their Lord. Then, the judgement will be passed between them with justice. And it will be said: ‘All praise belongs to Allah, the Lord of all worlds.’
Ha-Mim.
The revelation of this Book is from Allah, the All-Mighty, the All-Knowing,
Forgiver of sins, Acceptor of repentance, Severe in retribution, Lord of bounty. There is no god except Him; (and) towards Him is the eventual destination.
No one disputes the Signs of Allah except the disbelievers. So let it not astonish you that they move back and forth in the towns.

The people of Nuh (Noah) denied (the truth) before them, and (so did) the (heathen) factions after them. And every community intended to seize and destroy its messenger. And they disputed through falsehood to refute the truth. Then, I seized and destroyed them. So how was My retribution?
That is how your Lord’s Word proved true against the disbelievers, that they would be the inhabitants of the Fire.

Those who carry the Throne and those around it glorify the praise of their Lord. They believe in Him and seek forgiveness for the believers (praying): ‘Our Lord! You encompass all things in mercy and knowledge. So forgive those who repent and follow Your path, and save them from the punishment of the Hellfire.
Our Lord! Make them enter the Gardens of Eternity that You have promised them, along with their righteous ancestors, their spouses and their descendants. Indeed, You are the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.
Protect them from evil deeds. And whoever You save from evils on that Day, You have shown them mercy. And that is great success indeed.’
Surely, it will be announced to the disbelievers: ‘Indeed Allah’s disgust (towards you) is greater than your disgust towards yourselves, for you were invited to faith, but you disbelieved.’
They will say: ‘Our Lord, You caused us to die twice, and twice did you give us life. We admit our sins. So is there any way out (from this situation)?’

(They will be told: ‘No!) That is because when Allah was worshipped alone, you would disbelieve, but if partners were associated with Him, you would believe. So (know that) the judgement belongs to Allah (alone), the All-Exalted, the All-Great.’
He is the One Who shows you His signs and sends down (the rain) for you as provision from the sky. Yet none becomes mindful of advice except him, who turns (to Him) penitently.
So call upon Allah, devoting (your) faith and obedience exclusively to Him, though the disbelievers should be averse.

(He is) the Raiser of the ranks, the Lord of the Throne. He sends down the Spirit (or the revelation) by His command upon whomever He pleases among His servants that He may warn (them) of the Day of Meeting.

The Day when they come out (from the graves) with nothing concerning them hidden from Allah, (He will say:) ‘To whom belongs the Kingdom this Day?’ (It will be announced:) ‘To Allah, the One, the Most Dominant.
Today, every soul will be recompensed for what it has earned. There will be no injustice today. Indeed, Allah is Swift in reckoning.’

So warn them of the Day of the Imminent Event when the hearts will rise to the throats, suppressing distress inwardly. The wrongdoers will have neither a loyal friend nor any intercessor who might be heard.
He knows the treachery of the eyes and whatever the breasts hide.
Allah judges with justice, while those (idols) they worship beside Him cannot judge anything. Indeed, it is Allah Who is the All-Hearing, the All-Seeing.

Have they not travelled throughout the land so they could observe what was the end of those before them? They were far mightier in power than these (pagans) and (richer) in the monumental marks (they left) in the land. Yet Allah seized them for their sins. And they had no protector from (the punishment of) Allah.
That was because their messengers used to bring them clear signs, yet they disbelieved. So Allah destroyed them. Indeed, He is All-Strong, Severe in retribution.
Surely, We sent Musa (Moses) with Our signs and manifest authority,
to Pharaoh, Haman and Qarun (Korah), but they said: ‘(He is) a magician! A total liar.’
So when he came to them with the truth from Us, they said: ‘Kill the sons of those who believe with him and spare their women alive.’ And the scheming of the disbelievers was nothing but astray.
Pharaoh said: ‘Leave me to kill Musa (Moses) and let him call upon his Lord. Indeed, I fear he will change your religion or cause corruption to appear in the land.’
And Musa (Moses) said: ‘I seek refuge in my Lord and your Lord from every arrogant (person) who does not believe in the Day of Reckoning.’

A believing man from the people of Pharaoh, who was concealing his faith, said: ‘Will you kill a man (only) for saying: ‘My Lord is Allah’, while he has come to you with manifest proofs from your Lord? If (supposedly) he is a liar, then (the burden of) his falsehood will be upon him; but if he is truthful, then some of what he promises you shall befall you. Indeed, Allah does not guide someone who is a transgressor, a total liar.

My people! Power belongs to you today while you prevail over the land. So, who will help us against Allah’s punishment if it overtakes us?’ Pharaoh said: ‘I am just telling you what I see (to be good for you), and I am guiding you only to the right path.’
And said the one who was a believer: ‘My people, indeed I fear for you (a day) like the day of the former (heathen) factions,
like the plight of the people of Nuh (Noah), ‘Ad and Thamud and those after them. Yet Allah does not intend injustice for (His) servants.
My people! Indeed, I fear for you the Day when all will cry out (to one another).
The Day when you will turn back (retreating) to run away, not having anyone to protect you from Allah. And whoever Allah leaves to stray has no guide.

(O people of Egypt!) Indeed, Yusuf (Joseph) came to you with clear signs earlier, but you continued to doubt what he had brought you. When he died, you said: ‘Allah will never send any messenger after him.’ Thus Allah leaves to stray whosoever is a transgressor (and) doubter.

Those who dispute Allah’s signs without any authority having come to them, (that is) intensely hateful in the sight of Allah and in the sight of the believers. That is how Allah seals the heart of every arrogant person (and) tyrant.’
Pharaoh said: ‘O Haman! Build me a tall tower so that I may reach the pathways,

the means of access to the heavens, so that I may look upon the God of Musa (Moses), although I consider him a liar.’ Thus was made to Pharaoh, the evil of his conduct so fair seeming that he was hindered from the path (of Allah). And the plotting of Pharaoh led (him) into nothing but ruin.
And the one who was a believer said: ‘O my people! Follow me; I will guide you to the right path.
O my people! This life of the world is only a brief enjoyment. And indeed, the Hereafter is the home of (eternal) settlement.

Whoever commits an evil deed shall not be paid back except with its like. But whoever acts righteously, male or female, and is a believer will enter Paradise. They will be given sustenance without measure.
My people! (Think!) What makes me invite you towards salvation while you invite me to the Fire?
You invite me to disbelieve in Allah and associate with Him things which I do not know while I call you to the All-Mighty, the Most Forgiving.

There is no doubt that whatever (idols) you call me to (worship) are not worthy to invoke in this world nor the Hereafter. And that our (final) return will be to Allah, and the transgressors are the ones to be the inhabitants of the Hellfire.
You shall soon remember what I tell you, and I entrust my affair to Allah. Indeed, Allah is All-Seeing of (His) servants.’
Then Allah saved him from the evil of their schemes, while a terrible punishment overtook the people of Pharaoh.
(It is) the Fire to which they are exposed (every) morning and evening. And on the Day the Hour comes (it will be said:) ‘Make the people of Pharaoh enter into the severest punishment.’

And when they argue with one another in the Fire, the weak will say to those who were arrogant (in the world): ‘Indeed we were your followers, so can you now relieve us from some portion of the Fire?’
Those who were arrogant will say: ‘Indeed, we all are in it (together). Indeed, Allah has passed (the final) judgement between (His) servants.’
And those in the Fire will say to the keepers of Hell: ‘Call upon your Lord to lighten the punishment for us (even) for one day.’

They will say: ‘Did your messengers not used to bring you clear proofs?’ They will say: ‘Yes (they did).’ Then they (i.e. the keepers) will say: ‘Now pray (yourselves).’ But the supplications of the disbelievers will (always) be in vain.
Indeed, We support Our messengers and the believers (both) in this worldly life and on the Day when the witnesses will arise (for testimony),
the Day when the excuses of the wrongdoers will not benefit them. The curse will lie on them, and for them will be the evil abode (of Hell).
And certainly We gave Musa (Moses) guidance, and made the Children of Israel inherit the Book,
as a guide and a reminder for the possessors of wisdom.

So be patient (O Prophet)! Allah’s promise is indeed true. And seek (Allah’s) forgiveness for the sins of your (community).* And glorify the praise of your Lord (every) morning and evening. [* In the word: ‘li-dhanbi-ka’, the co-related noun Umma is omitted. On this basis, the verse ‘wa-staghfir li-dhanbi-ka’ denotes the sins committed by the Umma. Many leading scholars have drawn the same meaning. For references, see the following:   1. ‘Wa-staghfir li-dhanbi-ka’ means ‘li-dhanbi Ummati-ka’, that is ‘the sins of your community.’ (al-Nasafi, Madarik al-Tanzil wa Haqa’iq al-Ta’wil, 4:359.)   2. ‘Wa’staghfir li-dhanbi-ka’ means ‘li-dhanbi Ummati-ka’. Hudhifa al-mudaf wa uqima al-mudaf ilayh maqama-hu. It is stated that it implies the sins of the Umma (the Community). The co-related noun has been omitted in the given phrase and is represented by the noun co-related to. (al-Qurtubi, al-Jami‘ li-Ahkam al-Qur’an, 15:324.)   3. ‘Wa qila li-dhanbi Ummati-ka fi haqqi-ka’. It is also said that li-dhanbi-ka means for the misdeeds committed by the Umma concerning to what is your due. (Ibn Hayyan al-Andalusi, Bahr al-Muhit, 7:471.)   4. ‘Wa-staghfir li-dhanbi-ka’ means ‘dhanbi Ummati-ka, fa-huwa ‘ala hadhfi al-mudaf’. It implies the sins of the Umma, and this interpretation is based on the omission of the co-related noun. (al-Shawkani, Fath al-Qadir, 4:497.)]

Surely, those who dispute the revelations of Allah without any authority that has come to them have nothing in their hearts except greed for greatness, which they will never attain. So, keep seeking refuge with Allah. Indeed, He is the All-Hearing, the All-Seeing.
Surely, the creation of the heavens and the earth is far greater than the creation of humankind, but most people do not know.
The blind and the seeing cannot be equal; neither are those who believe and perform righteous deeds and those who commit evil. Yet, seldom do you accept advice.
Indeed, the Hour is surely coming. There is no doubt about it. But most people do not believe.
Your Lord says: ‘Call upon Me, I shall respond to you. Indeed, those too arrogant to worship Me shall enter Hell in utter humiliation.’
Allah is the One Who made the night for you where you may rest and the day to see. Indeed, Allah is Ever-Gracious to humankind, but most people do not give thanks.
That is Allah, your Lord, Who is the Creator of everything. There is no god except Him. So, where are you being turned away?
Thus, those who used to deny the revelations of Allah were turned away (from the truth).

Allah is the One Who made the earth a settling place for you and the sky a canopy. And He shaped you (in your mother’s womb) and then perfected your forms. And He provided you with all the good things (required for your development). That is Allah, your Lord! So blessed is Allah, the Lord of all worlds.
He is the Ever-Living. There is no god except Him. So call upon Him, devoting faith and obedience entirely to Him. All praise belongs to Allah, the Lord of all worlds.
Say: ‘I have been forbidden to worship those (idols) you invoke besides Allah since clear proofs have come to me from my Lord. And I have been commanded to submit to the Lord of all worlds.’

He is the One Who initiated your (chemical) genesis from clay, then (your biological genesis) from a fertilised egg (i.e. a zygote*), then from a clinging mass (i.e. the embryo in the mother’s womb). Then He brings you out as infants, then (nourishes you) so that you may reach your maturity. Then He lets you grow old, though some of you (may) die sooner. And (the purpose of all this is) that you may reach an appointed term (in this world). So perhaps you may understand. [* In Arabic, the word ‘ al-nutfa ’ is mentioned, and it refers to a zygote, which is a mixture of male and female gametes. It is the new single cell formed after the union of a sperm and ovum.]
He is the One Who gives life and causes death. So when He decides upon a thing, He only says to it: ‘Be!’ And it is.
Have you not seen those who dispute the Signs of Allah? Where are they being turned away?
(They are) the ones who deny the Book and (the scriptures) that We have sent Our messengers with. But they will soon know (its consequences).
When, with iron shackles and chains around their necks, they will be dragged,
into the boiling liquid, then burned in the Fire as fuel.
Then they will be told: ‘Where are those (idols) you used to ascribe as partners,
apart from Allah?’ They will say: ‘They have left us. Rather, we did not use to worship anything before.’ That is how Allah lets the disbelievers go astray.
(They will be told:) ‘That is because you used to rejoice in the land unjustly and because you used to act arrogantly.
Enter the gates of Hell to remain in it forever.’ So evil is the (final) abode of the arrogant!

So be patient. Allah’s promise is indeed true. Whether We show you a part of (the punishment) that We have promised them (in this life) or We cause you to die (before that), they shall be returned to Us (in either case).

We have sent (many) messengers before you. Among them are some We have recounted to you, and some among them We have not recounted to you. And it was not for any messenger to bring any sign except by Allah’s permission. So when Allah’s command comes, judgement will be passed with justice. And those who make false claims will thence become losers.
Allah is the One Who made cattle for you; some of them you ride, and some of them you eat.
And there are (other) benefits for you in them—that, riding upon them, you may reach the destination (desired) in your hearts. And you are carried upon them and the ships (on your journeys).
And He shows you (many of) His signs. So, which of Allah’s signs will you deny?

Have they not travelled throughout the land so that they could observe what was the end of those before them? They were more numerous than them and were far mightier in power (than these pagans) and (richer) in the monumental marks (they left) in the land. But whatever they used to earn brought them no benefit.
So when their messengers came to them with clear proofs, they rejoiced in pride for whatever (worldly) knowledge they had. So (the torment) which they used to ridicule overtook them.
But when they saw Our punishment, they said: ‘We believe in Allah alone and reject what we had been associating with Him (as partners).’

But their belief could not profit them when they had seen Our punishment. Allah’s practice has always passed among His (defiant) servants (like this), and the disbelievers were, hence, at a total loss.
Ha-Mim.
(This is) a revelation from the Most-Compassionate, the All-Merciful.
(This is) a Book whose verses are well elaborated; a Qur’an, in Arabic, for people who know,
a bearer of good news and a warner. But most of them have turned away, and they do not listen (to it).
They say: ‘Our hearts are in veils (preventing us) from what you invite us to, and there is a deafness in our ears and a barrier between you and us. So do (as you please), and so shall we.’
Say: ‘I am only a human being like you. It has been revealed to me that your God is only One God, so be steadfast towards Him and seek forgiveness from Him.’ And woe to the polytheists,
those who do not give the Alms-due and are disbelievers in the Hereafter.
As for those who believe and perform righteous deeds, they will have an everlasting reward.
Say: ‘Do you indeed disbelieve in the One Who created the earth in two periods and ascribe equals to Him? That is the Lord of all worlds.’
He placed therein firm mountains rising above its surface. And He blessed it and apportioned its (various) means of sustenance in four periods, alike for all the seekers (of provision).

Then He turned towards the heavenly universe while it was (still like) smoke. Then He said to it and the earth: ‘Come (in compliance to My laws), willingly or unwillingly.’ Both said: ‘We submit (to Your laws of nature) with pleasure.’

Then He decreed them to be seven heavens in two periods. And He revealed in each heaven its ordinance (i.e. its particular laws). And We adorned the lowest heaven with lamps (i.e. stars and planets) and (provided them) with security (from collision). That (cosmic system) is the finely-measured design of the All-Dominant, the All-Knowing.
But if they turn away, say: ‘I have warned you of a thundering blast like the destructive blast of ‘Ad and Thamud.’

When the messengers came to them from their front and their rear (and said:) ‘Worship none but Allah,’ they said: ‘Had our Lord wished, He would certainly have sent down angels (to us). We indeed disbelieve in whatever you have been sent with.’

As for (the people of) ‘Ad, they acted arrogantly in the land with no right and said: ‘Who is superior to us in power?’ Did they not see that Allah (Himself), Who created them, is far superior to them in power? Still, they persisted in denying Our signs.

So We sent upon them a furiously violent wind during their days of ill fortune that We might make them taste the punishment of humiliation in the life of this world. Yet the punishment of the Hereafter is surely more disgraceful, and they will not be helped.
And as for (the people of) Thamud, We showed them the right path, but they preferred blindness to guidance. So, the blast of humiliating punishment overtook them for what they used to earn.
And We delivered those who believed and were God-conscious.
The Day when the enemies of Allah are gathered towards the Fire, they shall be driven in ranks.
Until they reach it, their ears will bear witness against them, and their eyes and skins (will also testify) against the works they used to do.

They will say to their skins: ‘Why did you bear witness against us?’ They will say: ‘Allah, Who makes everything speak, made us speak. And He created you the first time, and to Him shall you be returned.’

You did not seek to hide yourselves to prevent your ears from testifying against you, or (for that matter) your eyes and your skin. But you assumed that Allah did not know most of what you were doing.
And that false assumption you entertained about your Lord has ruined you. So you became losers.
Even if they are patient (now), the Fire is their abode, and should they seek (Allah’s) favour, they will never be redeemed.

And We assigned (evil) companions for them, and they made whatever was before them and whatever was behind them (i.e. their past and future) fair-seeming to them. And the decree (of punishment) became due against them as it did against the communities of jinn and humans before them. Indeed, they were losers.
And the disbelievers say: ‘Do not listen to this Qur’an, and hoot it down dismissively, so that you may prevail (over its recitation).’
So We shall indeed make the disbelievers taste a severe punishment. And We will most surely repay them for the worst of what they used to do.
That is the requital of the enemies of Allah: The Fire! There will be an eternal abode for them as a (befitting) reward for their having rejected Our signs.
The disbelievers will say: ‘Our Lord, show us those who led us astray, among the jinn and the humans. We will trample them under our feet so that they may become among the lowermost.’

Indeed, those who say: ‘Our Lord is Allah,’ and then remain steadfast, the angels descend upon them (saying:) ‘Do not fear, nor grieve, and rejoice with the good news of the Paradise you have been promised.
We are your supporters in the life of this world and the Hereafter. You shall have in it whatever your souls desire, and you shall have in it whatever you ask for,
as a hospitable welcome from the Most-Forgiving, the All-Merciful (Lord).’
And who is more beautiful in speech than the one who calls (others) to Allah, acts righteously and says: ‘Indeed, I am among those who submit (to Him)’?

Virtue and vice cannot be equal. And repel (evil) with what is best (in conduct). (If you do so,) behold the one with whom there is enmity between you and him—he will become as though he were a sympathetic friend.
But none is granted this (result) except those who are patient. And none is granted it except the one with a large share (of steadfastness by Allah’s Grace).
If an evil whisper of Satan disturbs you with intrusive thoughts, seek refuge in Allah. Indeed, He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
Among His signs are the night and the day, the sun and the moon. So do not prostrate to the sun or the moon, but prostrate to Allah Who created them (all) if you truly worship Him alone.
If they are (still) arrogant, (let them know that) those (angels) who are in the presence of your Lord glorify Him night and day, and they are never tired.

And among His signs is that you see the earth lying desolate, but as soon as We send down rainwater upon it, it becomes fresh and green and grows (vegetation). Indeed, He Who revives it is the Reviver of the dead. Indeed, He is All-Capable of everything.

Indeed, those who deviate (from the right path) regarding Our signs are not hidden from Us. Is he who is cast in the Fire better, or he who arrives safely (at his destination) on the Day of Resurrection? Do whatever you want; surely, He sees best whatever you do.
Indeed, those who disbelieve in the Reminder (i.e. the Qur’an) when it has come to them (will face a severe punishment). Indeed, it is a Mighty Book.
Falsehood cannot touch it from before it nor behind it. (It is) a revelation from the All-Wise, the All-Praiseworthy.

(Esteemed Prophet!) Nothing is being said to you (by the disbelievers) except what was already said to the messengers before you. Indeed, your Lord is the Lord of forgiveness and the Possessor of painful retribution.

Had We made it a non-Arabic Qur’an, they would surely have said: ‘Why have its verses not been expounded clearly? What! A non-Arabian (Book) and an Arabian (Prophet)?’ Say: ‘This (Qur’an) is a guidance and a healing for those who believe, but those who do not believe have deafness in their ears and are blind to it, (as if) they are being called from a distant place.’

Surely, We gave Musa (Moses) the Book, but differences arose regarding it. And were it not for a prior Word of your Lord, a judgement (settling their differences) would have been made between them (at once). Yet they are confounded by a deceitful doubt about it.
Whoever acts righteously, it is to (the benefit of) his (own) soul, and whoever commits evil, it is to his (own) detriment. And your Lord is never unjust to (His) servants.

To Him is referred the knowledge of the Hour. And no fruits come out from their covering, and no female conceives nor does she deliver except with His knowledge. And on the Day when He will call out to them (saying): ‘Where are My partners?’ They (the polytheists) will say: ‘We admit to You that none among us testifies (to those partners).’
Whatever they used to worship before has forsaken them. And they have realised that there is no escape for them.
Human being is never tired of praying for good, and if evil befalls him, he becomes desperate (and) hopeless.

And if We let him taste Our mercy after distress has befallen him, he will surely say: ‘This is my due; I do not think the Hour will ever come. And if I am returned to my Lord, I will surely have the best (reward) with Him.’ However, We will surely inform the disbelievers about what they have done. And We shall certainly cause them to taste a harsh punishment.
And when We bless human being, he turns away and withdraws himself arrogantly. But when evil befalls him, he becomes full of prolonged prayers.
Say: ‘Tell me, if this (Qur’an) is really (sent down) from Allah, and then you keep denying it, who will be more astray than he who is in extreme opposition (to the truth)?’

We shall show them Our signs in (all) horizons of the universe and within themselves until it becomes clear to them that this (Qur’an) is the truth. Is it not sufficient that your Lord is a Witness over all things?
Beware! They are indeed in doubt about the meeting with their Lord. Look! He indeed encompasses everything (with His Knowledge and Power).
Ha-Mim,
‘Ayn, Sin, Qaf.
Thus has He revealed to you and those before you, Allah, the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.
To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And He is the All-Exalted, the All-Supreme.

The heavens are almost breaking apart over them (in His reverence), while the angels glorify their Lord’s praise (all the time) and seek forgiveness for those on the earth. Behold! Allah is indeed the Most-Forgiving, the All-Merciful.
As for those who take protectors besides Him, Allah is Watchful over them. And (O Prophet,) you are not responsible for them.

Thus, We have revealed a Qur’an in Arabic to you so that you may warn the people of the Mother of the Towns (i.e. the city of Mecca) and those around it. And warn (them) of the Day of Gathering, about which there is no doubt, (when) a group shall be in Paradise, and a group shall be in the Blaze.
Had Allah wished, He could have (easily) made them one community. But He admits whom He pleases into His mercy. And the wrongdoers have no guardian and no helper.
Have they taken guardians besides Him? (Say:) ‘It is Allah Who is the Guardian. He gives life to the dead. And He is All-Capable of everything.
Whatever you may differ about, its judgement rests only with Allah. That is Allah, my Lord. In Him do I trust. And to Him, I turn penitently.

(He is) the Originator of the heavens and the earth. He has made mates for you among yourselves and mates for the cattle (as well), by which He multiplies you (all). Nothing is like Him, and He is the All-Hearing, the All-Seeing.
To Him belong the keys of the heavens and the earth. He expands the provision for whom He pleases and tightens (it for whom He wills). Indeed, He is All-Knowing of everything.’

He has prescribed for you the Religion which He had enjoined upon Nuh (Noah) and which We have (also) revealed to you, and which We had enjoined upon Ibrahim (Abraham), Musa (Moses) and ‘Isa (Jesus), that you should uphold the religion and make no divisions in it. Hard upon the polytheists is that to which you call them. Allah chooses for Himself whomever He pleases and guides to Himself whoever turns (to Him) penitently.

They did not split (into factions) until knowledge had come to them because of envy among themselves. Had it not been for a prior decree of your Lord (to give them respite) until an appointed time, the decision would have been made between them. Indeed, those made to inherit the Book after them are surely in grave doubt about this (Qur’an).

Therefore, go on inviting (them to this religion), and remain steadfast as you are commanded. And do not go by their desires, and say (to them): ‘I believe in whatever Book Allah has revealed. And I have been commanded to bring justice between you. Allah is our Lord and your Lord. Our deeds belong to us, and your deeds belong to you. There is no (need for an) argument between you and us. Allah will bring us together (for the final judgement), and towards Him is the journey’s end.’
As for those who argue about Allah after having responded to Him, their argument is void in the sight of their Lord. Upon them shall be (His) wrath, and there is a severe punishment for them.
Allah is the One Who has revealed the Book with the truth and the balance (of justice). How can you tell? The Last Hour may well be near.
Those who do not believe in it (mockingly) seek to hasten it, but the believers fear it and know it is the truth. Look! Those who dispute concerning the Hour have indeed gone far astray.
Allah is highly gentle with His servants. He provides sustenance to whomever He wills. And He is the All-Strong, the All-Mighty.

Whoever desires the harvest of the Hereafter, We will increase for him his harvest. And whoever desires the harvest of this world, We will give him some of it. But he will have no share in the Hereafter.

Do they have partners (besides Allah) who have ordained a religion for them that Allah has not authorised? Were it not for the final word, the decision would have been made between them. Indeed, the wrongdoers shall have a painful punishment.

You will see the wrongdoers fearing because of what they have earned, and that will fall upon them. But those who believe and perform righteous deeds will be in the Gardens of Paradise. They will have whatever they wish from their Lord. That is indeed the great bounty.

That (reward) is the glad tidings Allah gives to His servants, who believe and do righteous deeds. Say (O Prophet): ‘I do not ask you for any reward for this (message), except affection for (my) close relatives.’ Whoever earns good, We shall enhance him in goodness (in the Hereafter). Indeed, Allah is Most-Forgiving, Most-Appreciative.

Do they say: ‘He has fabricated a lie against Allah’? If Allah so wills, He may set a seal (of patience) on your heart (so their foolish talk may not hurt you). And Allah wipes out falsehood and establishes the truth with His Words. Indeed, He is All- Knowing of what is (hidden) in the breasts.
He is the One Who accepts repentance from His servants and pardons (their) sins and knows what you do.
He responds to those who believe and perform righteous deeds and gives them more (than they seek) out of His grace. But as for the disbelievers, there is a severe punishment for them.

Had Allah given abundant provisions to (all of) His servants, they would have certainly transgressed throughout the land. But He sends down according to (a precise) measure whatever He wills. Indeed, He is All-Aware, All-Seeing of His servants.
He is the One Who sends down the rain after they have given up hope, and He spreads His mercy. And He is the Guardian, the All-Praiseworthy.
And among His signs is the creation of the heavens, the earth and all (moving) creatures that He has scattered throughout them. And He is Most Capable of gathering them whenever He wills.
And whatever affliction befalls you is because of what your hands have earned, though He pardons most (of your faults).
You cannot escape (Him) on the earth (with your schemes), and you do not have, besides Allah, any guardian or helper.
And among His signs are the ships (that sail) through the sea, (appearing) like mountains.
If He wishes, He can calm the wind, so they (i.e. the ships) will lie motionless on the surface (of the water). Indeed, there are signs in that for every patient and grateful (person).
Or He causes them to perish because of what they have earned, though He pardons most (of their faults),
so those who dispute Our signs will know that they have no escape.
Whatever you have been given is only a provision of the life of this world, but what is with Allah is better and more lasting for those who believe and trust in their Lord,
those who avoid major sins and indecencies, and forgive whenever they are angry,
who respond to their Lord, establish the prayer, their affairs (are decided) by mutual consultation and spend (in charity) out of what We have provided for them,
and those who, when tyranny befalls them, help each other (against the wrongdoers).
And the requital of evil is punishment equivalent to that (evil). But whoever forgives and reconciles (to rectify the situation), his reward lies with Allah. Indeed, He does not like the wrongdoers.
And whoever defends himself after being wronged, there is no blame upon them.
The path (to blame) is only against those who unjustly persecute people and commit aggression in the land. For such, there is a painful punishment.
Whoever remains patient and forgives—indeed, this is a course worthy of resolve.
And whoever Allah leaves to stray has no guardian apart from Him. And you will see the wrongdoers when they face the punishment, saying: ‘Is there any way to return (to the world)?’

And you will see them being exposed to it, humbling themselves out of disgrace, looking with a faint glance secretly. And the believers will say: ‘The (real) losers are those who have ruined themselves and their families on the Day of Resurrection. Look! Indeed, the wrongdoers are in everlasting punishment.’
And they shall have no guardians to help them against Allah. And whoever Allah leaves to stray has no way out.
Respond to your Lord before there comes a day from Allah, for which there shall be no revoking. On that Day, you will have no refuge, nor shall you have (any chance of) denial.

And if they turn away (from your warnings), We have not sent you as a keeper over them. Your responsibility is only to communicate (the truth). Indeed, when We cause human being to taste mercy from Us, he rejoices in it. And if an evil befalls them because of what their hands have sent forth, then human beings are indeed very ungrateful.
To Allah belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. He creates whatever He wills. He bestows females upon whomever He wills, and bestows males to whomever He wills.
Or He couples them as males and females, making whomever He wills infertile. Indeed, He is All-Knowing, All-Powerful.

It is not (possible) for any human that Allah should speak to him except through revelation, or from behind a veil, or by sending a messenger who reveals by His permission whatever He pleases. Indeed, He is All-Exalted, All-Wise.

Thus have We revealed to you the Spirit of Our command (i.e. the Qur’an). You did not know (before the revelation) what the Book was nor (the details of) the faith.* But We made it a Light by which We may guide whomever We wish among Our servants. And indeed, you guide (people)to the straight path. [* This verse refers to the unique status of the Holy Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him) as being an Ummi , not tutored by anyone to read or write. So, after hearing the verses of the Qur’an and the details of the faith, the disbelievers may not have any plausible reason to accuse him of being the inventor of the Book through acquired knowledge and scholarship.]
(This straight path is) the path of Allah to Whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Beware! All matters will return to Allah alone.
Ha-Mim.
By the Manifest Book.
We have made it a Qur’an in Arabic so perhaps you may understand.
It is indeed (inscribed) with Us in the Original Source of the (Divine) Books. (It is) surely highly esteemed (and) rich in wisdom.
Shall We withhold the Reminder from you altogether because you have been a transgressing lot?
How many prophets did We send to the ancients?
Yet never a prophet came to them, but they used to mock him.
So, We destroyed those stronger than these (Meccans). And the example of (their) predecessors has passed before.
If you ask them: ‘Who created the heavens and the earth?’ They will surely say: ‘The All-Mighty, the All-Knowing (Lord) created them.’
(He is) the One Who made the earth a cradle for you and set pathways in it for you so that you may be guided (to your destinations),
and Who sent down water from the sky in perfect measure, and We revived with it a dead land. Thus shall you be brought forth (from the dead).
And Who created all the pairs, and made for you ships and animals that you ride,

so that you may mount their backs and remember the blessing of your Lord, when you are settled on them, and say: ‘Glory be to Him, Who has subjected this for us, and we (on our own) were no match for it.
And indeed we shall return to our Lord.’
Yet they (the polytheists) assign a portion from among His servants (as His offspring) to Him. Verily, humankind is manifestly ungrateful.
Did He adopt (for Himself) daughters from what He created while He preferred you with sons?
Whenever one of them is given the news of what he attributes to the Most-Compassionate (i.e. the birth of a daughter), his face blackens as he suppresses his anger.

What! (Do they attribute to Him that) which is reared amid ornaments and cannot articulate a clear argument.* [* The idolaters, out of their pagan mentality, regarded women as lacking intelligence and would not allow them to be heard in disputes. The Qur’an has thus rejected their claim by using their own argument against them.]

Still, they have classed the angels, who are the servants of the Most-Compassionate (Lord), as females. Did they witness their creation? Their testimony shall be written down, and they shall be interrogated (on the Last Day).
They (further) say: ‘Had the Most-Compassionate (Lord) wished, we would not have worshipped these (idols).’ They have no knowledge (in support) of this (claim). And they only tell lies out of fancy.
Did We give them a Book before this, so they are holding fast to it?
Rather, they say: ‘We found our fathers following a creed, and we are following in their footsteps.’
We did not send any warner to a town before you, but its elites said: ‘Surely, we found our fathers following a creed, and we are indeed following in their footsteps.’
He (the messenger) would say: ‘What! Even if I bring you better guidance than what you found your fathers following?’ They would say: ‘Indeed, we disbelieve in what you have been sent with.’
So We took vengeance upon them. And observe how the end of the deniers was!
When Ibrahim (Abraham) said to his (foster) father and his people: ‘I renounce whatever (gods) you worship,
except Him, Who originated me. Indeed, He will guide me.’
So he made it (i.e. the monotheistic creed) a lasting word among his posterity so they may always turn back (to Allah).
Rather, I provided for these (Arabs) and their fathers until the Truth (i.e. the Qur’an) and a Manifest Messenger (i.e. Muhammad (blessings and peace be upon him) came to them.
When the Truth came to them, they said: ‘This is magic, and we indeed disbelieve in it.’
And they said: ‘Why was this Qur’an not sent down to some great man (like a chief or an affluent leader) of the two towns (of Ta’if and Mecca)?’

Is it they who distribute the mercy of your Lord? We have dispensed among them (the resources of) their livelihood in the present life and raised some of them above others in rank. (Did We decide this) so that some of them (i.e. the rich) should make fun of others (i.e. the poor)? And your Lord’s mercy is better than anything they accumulate.

Were it not that people might (be tempted to) become one community (of disbelievers), We would have indeed made for those who disbelieve in the Most-Compassionate (Lord) silver roofs for their houses and (silver) stairways by which they ascend;
and (likewise, silver) gates for their houses and (silver) couches on which they recline;

and gold ornamentation (over this silver work). Yet all that would be nothing but temporary enjoyment of the life of this world. And the Hereafter, in the presence of your Lord, is for those who are God-conscious.
And whoever blindly turns away from the remembrance of the Most-Compassionate (Lord), We assign a Satan for him, who remains his close companion.
Indeed, they bar them from the (right) path while they suppose that they are (rightly) guided.
But when he comes to Us, he will say: ‘I wish there had been between me and you the distance of the two easts. What an evil companion (you were)!’
It will not benefit you on that Day. As you did wrong, so will you share in the punishment.
Can you make the deaf hear then, or guide the blind and the one who is in manifest error?
Either We shall take you away (from this world) and then take vengeance upon them,
or We shall show you what We have promised them (as punishment), for indeed We have power over them.
So hold fast to what has been revealed to you. Indeed, you are on the straight path.
Surely, this (Qur’an) is a Reminder for you and your people. (O people!) You will soon be questioned (about it).
Ask (the followers of) those of Our messengers whom We had sent before you. Did We ever appoint any gods to be worshipped besides the Most-Compassionate (Lord)?
We indeed sent Musa (Moses) with Our signs to Pharaoh and his notable chiefs. He said: ‘I am a messenger of the Lord of all worlds.’
But when he brought them Our signs, behold, they laughed at them.
We did not show them any sign, but it was greater (in significance) than the previous one, which was similar to it. And We seized them with punishment; perhaps they might return (to the right path).
And they would say: ‘O magician! Invoke your Lord for us by virtue of the covenant He made with you. (Then) surely we will be guided.’
But as soon as We removed the punishment from them (responding to Moses’ prayer), behold, they would break their pledge.
Pharaoh called out among his people, saying: ‘O my people! Does not the kingdom of Egypt belong to me? And these rivers that flow beneath my feet (are not mine also)? Can you not see?
Am I not better than this humble one who can hardly express himself?
Why have golden bracelets (of kingship) not been cast upon him, and why have the angels not come with him as (his) attendants?’
So he fooled his people, and they obeyed him. Indeed, they were a transgressing people.
So when they roused Our wrath, We took vengeance upon them and drowned them all together.
Thus, We made them a thing of the past and an example for those after them.
And when the son of Maryam (Mary) is cited as an example (in argument), behold, your people laugh at it (mockingly).
They say: ‘Are our gods better or he?’ They do not cite him to you but to dispute. No doubt, they are a contentious people.
He (Jesus) was just a servant whom We had blessed with (Our) favour. And We made him an exemplar for the Children of Israel.
Had We willed, We would have made angels from you to be (your) successors upon the earth.
Indeed, he (Jesus) will be (the sign for) the knowledge of the Hour (when his second coming takes place). So do not doubt it, and follow me. This is a straight path.
And do not let Satan bar you (from the path of Allah). Indeed, he is your manifest enemy.
And when ‘Isa (Jesus) came with clear proofs, he said: ‘I have indeed brought you wisdom, and (I have come) to make clear to you some of the things you differ about. So be God-conscious and obey me.
Allah is indeed my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him (alone). This is a straight path.’
But the factions differed among themselves. So woe to the wrongdoers for the punishment of a painful day!
Are they waiting for anything except that the Hour should come upon them suddenly while they are unaware?
Friends on that Day will be one another’s enemies except for the God-conscious.
(They will be told:) ‘O My servants! There is no fear for you today, nor shall you grieve.’
(They are) those who believed in Our revelations and fully submitted (to Us).
(The angels will say:) ‘Enter Paradise, you and your spouses (or close companions); you will be honoured and pleased.’
Golden trays and bowls will be passed around them. And there will be whatever the souls desire and the eyes find pleasing. And you will remain there forever.
That is Paradise, which you are made to inherit for what you used to do.
There will be abundant fruits for you from which you will eat.
Indeed, the guilty will remain forever in the punishment of Hell.
It will not be lightened for them, and they will be overwhelmed in utter despair.
We did not wrong them, but they were the wrongdoers themselves.
They will call out (to the guard of Hell): ‘O Malik! Let your Lord bring an end to us!’ He will say: ‘Indeed, you will stay on (forever).’
We certainly brought you the truth, but most of you were resentful of the truth.
Have they devised any (evil) plan? Indeed, We (too) are surely planning.
Do they think We do not hear their hidden thoughts and their secret talks? Yes, We do! And Our messenger angels are with them, writing (everything) down.
Say: ‘If (supposing the impossible that) the Most-Compassionate (Lord) had a son, I would be the first to worship (him).’
Glorified is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, the Lord of the Throne, far above what they claim (about Him)!
So leave them to gossip and play until they encounter their Day which they have been promised.
It is He Who is (the only) God in the heavens and (the only) God on the earth. And He is the All-Wise, the All-Knowing.
Blessed is He, to Whom the kingdom of the heavens and the earth belongs and whatsoever is between them. And with Him lies the knowledge of the Hour. And to Him, you will be returned.
Those (idols) they worship besides Him have no power of intercession, except those who testify to the truth and know (for whom to intercede).
(Esteemed Prophet!) If you ask them: ‘Who created them?’ They will certainly say: ‘Allah.’ How are they then deluded (from the truth)?
By the Messenger’s words: ‘O my Lord! Indeed, these are the people who do not believe!’
So bear with them and say: ‘Peace!’ They will soon come to know (their end).
Ha-Mim.
By the Manifest Book.
Indeed, We revealed it on a blessed night. And indeed, We have been warning (humankind against evil).
On this (night), every wise matter is ordained distinctively,
as a command from Us. Indeed, We have been sending (messengers),
as a mercy from your Lord. Indeed, He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing,
the Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, if only you had sure faith.
There is no god except Him. He (alone) gives life and causes death. (He is) your Lord and the Lord of your forefathers.
Nay, they are playing around in doubt.
So keep waiting for the Day when the sky brings an evident smoke.
That will engulf the people. (They will cry out:) ‘This is a painful punishment.’
‘Our Lord! Remove from us this punishment. Truly, we are believers.’
How could there be a reminder (beneficial for them on that Day) when a clear Messenger had already come to them?
But they turned away from him and said: ‘(He is) a tutored madman.’
We may remove the punishment a little, but you will surely revert (to your earlier ways).
The Day when We will strike (them) with the most terrible blow, We will indeed inflict retribution (upon them).
Indeed, We tested the people of Pharaoh before them when a noble messenger came to them,
(saying:) ‘Deliver the servants of Allah (i.e. the Children of Israel) to me. Indeed, I am a trustworthy messenger (sent) to you.
And do not rebel against Allah. Indeed, I have come to you with a manifest authority.
I seek refuge in my Lord and your Lord, lest you should stone me (to death).
If you do not believe me, keep away from me.’
Then he called upon his Lord, saying: ‘These are indeed guilty people.’
(Allah told him:) ‘Set out with My servants by night, for you will indeed be pursued.
Then leave the sea split open; they are certainly an army bound to drown.’
How many gardens and springs did they leave behind,
and green fields and magnificent buildings,
and luxuries, in which they rejoiced!
So it was, and We gave it (all) to other people as an inheritance.
Then neither the heaven wept over them nor the earth, nor were they granted respite.
And verily, We delivered the Children of Israel from the humiliating punishment,
from Pharaoh. Indeed, he was a rebel among the transgressors.
Indeed, We chose them knowingly above all the (contemporary) nations.
We gave them some signs wherein there was a clear test.
Indeed, these (Meccans) say:
‘There is nothing except our first death, and we shall not be resurrected.
Bring our fathers (back to life), if you are truthful.’

Are they better or the people of Tubba‘* and those who were before them? We destroyed them. Indeed, they were evildoers. [* Tubba‘, like Pharaoh, Caesar and Khaqan, is the dynastic title of the Himyari King of Yemen.]
We did not create the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them for play.
We only created them both with a genuine reason. But most of them do not know.
Indeed, the Day of Judgement is the appointed time for all of them.
The Day when a friend will not avail a friend in any way, nor will they be helped,
except for those to whom Allah has been merciful. (They will intercede for one another.) Indeed, He is the All-Mighty, the All-Merciful.
Indeed, the tree of Zaqqum,* [* It is the most bitter and vilest tree, bearing thorny fruit that grows in the depths of Hell.]
will be the food of the major sinners.
Like molten copper, it will boil in the bellies,
like the boiling of boiling liquid.
(It will be commanded:) ‘Seize him and drag him to the middle of Hell.
Then pour over his head the torment of boiling liquid.
Taste (this)! Indeed, you are the (self-claimed) mighty and noble!
Certainly, this is (the very Hell) you used to doubt.’
Indeed, the God-conscious will be in a secure place,
amid gardens and springs,
dressed in fine silk and rich brocade, sitting face to face.
So shall it be. And We shall wed them to pure and beautiful wide-eyed maidens (of Paradise known as al-Hur al-‘In).
Feeling comfortable and secure therein, they will call for every fruit.
There (in Paradise), they will not taste death beyond the first death (which would have passed already). And He will save them from the punishment of Hell,
as a grace from your Lord. That is the ultimate success.
Indeed, We have made this (Qur’an) easy in your (own) language (O Prophet); perhaps they may become mindful of advice.
So wait! They (too) are certainly waiting.
Ha-Mim.
The (piecemeal) revelation of (this) Book is from Allah, the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.
Indeed, in the heavens and the earth, there are signs for the believers.
And in your (own) creation, and whatever living beings He has scattered (throughout the world), there are signs for people of sure faith.

And (in) the alternation of night and day, and (in) the provision (of rain) that Allah sends down from the sky whereby He revives the earth after its death, and (in) the shifting of the winds, there are signs for people who understand.
These are the Signs of Allah that We recite to you in truth. So what discourse will they believe in after (denying) Allah and His Signs?
Woe to every false fabricator, dreadful evildoer,
who hears Allah’s revelations recited to him, then arrogantly persists (in his disbelief) as if he did not hear them. So give him the tidings of a painful punishment!
When he comes to know anything about Our Verses, he makes a mockery of them. For such (people), there will be a humiliating punishment.

Ahead of them (beyond the worldly life) is Hell. And whatever they have earned will not avail them in any way, nor those (idols) they have taken as guardians besides Allah. And there shall be a great punishment for them.
This (Qur’an) is guidance. And those who deny the revelations of their Lord, for them is the torment of painful punishment.
Allah is the One Who subjected the sea for you so that the ships may sail in it by His command, that you may seek His grace, and that you may give thanks.
And He has subjected (through His designed laws of nature) for you whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth—all is from Him. There are indeed signs in that for people who reflect.
Say to the believers to excuse those who do not hope for Allah’s Days so that He may recompense the people for what they used to earn.
Whoever acts righteously is to his (own) benefit, and whoever does evil is to his (own) detriment. Then you will be returned to your Lord.
Indeed, We gave the Children of Israel the Book, Governance and Prophethood, and We granted them good provisions. And We preferred them above all (contemporary) nations.

And We (also) gave them clear commandments regarding (their) faith. But they only differed (about it) after (explicit) knowledge had come to them, merely out of envy among themselves. Indeed, your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection regarding what they used to differ.
Then We have set you upon a (clear) course of the law; so follow it, and do not follow the desires of those who do not know (the truth).
Indeed, they will not avail you in any way against Allah. Indeed, the wrongdoers are patrons of one another, whereas Allah is the Patron of the God-conscious.
This (Qur’an) contains eye-opening insights for humanity, guidance and mercy for people with sure faith.
Do those who perpetrate evil deeds think that We shall treat them like those who believe and perform righteous deeds, making their life and their death equal? Evil is indeed what they judge.
Allah has created the heavens and the earth with a truthful order so that every soul is recompensed for what it has earned. And none of them will be wronged.

Have you seen the one who takes his desire as his god and whom Allah has held astray despite his knowledge, set a seal upon his hearing and his heart and put a covering upon his eyes? Who then can guide him after Allah? Will you not then be mindful of advice?

They say: ‘There is nothing but our life in this world. We die, we live, and nothing destroys us except (the passage of) time.’ But they have no knowledge (in support) of this (assumption). They only speculate.
When Our clear Verses are recited to them, their only argument is to say: ‘Bring our fathers back (to life) if you are truthful.’
Say: ‘Allah gives you life, then He causes you to die, then He will gather you (all) to the Day of Resurrection, in which there is no doubt. But most people do not know.’
To Allah belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. And the Day when the Last Hour sets in; the falsifiers will lose (everything) on that Day.
And you will see (on that Day) every community kneeling. Every community will be summoned to its book: ‘Today, you will be recompensed for what you used to do.
This is Our Book that speaks the truth against you. Indeed, We had been recording all that you used to do.’
But those who believed and performed righteous deeds, their Lord will admit them into His mercy. That is the manifest success.
But as for those who disbelieved (they will be asked:) ‘Were My Verses not recited to you? Yet you acted arrogantly, and you were a guilty people.’

And when it was said: ‘Indeed, Allah’s promise is true, and there is no doubt about the Last Hour.’ You said (mockingly): ‘We do not know what the Hour is! We think it is no more than speculation, and we are not certain (that it will ever come).’
The evil (consequences) of what they had done will appear before them. And the torment they used to ridicule will besiege them.
It will be said (to them): ‘Today, We neglect you as you neglected the meeting of this Day of yours. Your abode is the Fire, and you will have no helpers.

That is because you took Allah’s revelations in mockery, and the worldly life deluded you.’ So (from) this Day onwards, they will not be taken out of it (i.e. the Hellfire), nor will they be given a chance to make amends.
So all praise belongs to Allah, the Lord of the heavens, the Lord of the earth, the Lord of all worlds.
To Him belongs all supremacy in the heavens and the earth, and He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.
Ha-Mim.
The (piecemeal) revelation of this Book is from Allah, the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.
We did not create the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them but with truthful wisdom and for a specified term. Yet the disbelievers turn away from what they are warned about.

Say: ‘Have you considered whatever (idols) you worship besides Allah? Show me what (part) of the earth they have created. Do they have any share in (the creation of) the heavens? Bring me any (revealed) scripture before this (Qur’an) or some traces of (reliable) knowledge, if you are truthful.’
Who is more astray than the one who worships besides Allah those (idols) who do not respond to him till the Day of Resurrection and who are unaware of their prayers?
And when the people will be gathered (on the Day of Judgement), those (false gods) will be their enemies, and they will disown their worship.
When Our clear verses are recited to them, the disbelievers say about the truth when it reaches them: ‘This is plain magic.’

Do they say: ‘He (the Messenger) has fabricated this (Qur’an) himself?’ Say: ‘Should I have fabricated it, then you will have no power to save me from (the wrath of) Allah. He knows best what you (mockingly) gossip about among yourselves concerning it. He suffices as a Witness between me and you. And He is the Most-Forgiving, the All-Merciful.’
Say: ‘I am not the first Messenger ever sent, nor do I know (of my own accord) what will be done to me or you. I only follow whatever is revealed to me. I am just a manifest warner.’

Say: ‘Have you considered? If this (Qur’an) is from Allah and you disbelieve in it, and a witness from the Children of Israel has testified to its like and believed (in it) while you act arrogantly? Indeed, Allah does not guide wrongdoing people.’

And the disbelievers say about the believers: ‘Had this (religion) been something good, they would not have taken the lead over us in (accepting) it.’ And as they could not find a way of guidance through it, they will say: ‘This is an ancient fabrication.’

Yet before this (Qur’an), the Book of Moses (the Torah) was (revealed) as a guide and a mercy. This is a Book that confirms (it) in the Arabic language to warn those who do wrong. And (it) is a (bearer of) good news for the virtuous.
Indeed, those who say: ‘Our Lord is Allah’, and then remain steadfast, there shall be no fear on them, nor shall they grieve.
They shall be the residents of Paradise, remaining in it forever as a reward for what they used to do.

We have commanded human being to be virtuous to his parents. His mother carried him in hardship and delivered him in hardship. The (period of) bearing him (in the womb) and his weaning (i.e. pregnancy and feeding) is (around) thirty months. When he is fully grown and reaches forty years (of age), he says: ‘My Lord! Inspire me (always) to be thankful for Your blessings that You have bestowed upon me and my parents. (And inspire me) to do righteous deeds which may please You. And grant me righteousness in respect of my offspring. Indeed, I repent to You, and I am among those who (completely) surrender to You.’
Such are the ones from whom We accept the best of what they have done and overlook their misdeeds. (They will be) among the residents of Paradise. (This is) a true promise they had been given.

As for the one who says to his parents: ‘Fie upon you! Are you warning me that I shall be brought out (from the grave and given life once more) when generations have passed before me?’ Then they both call for Allah’s help (and say to their child:) ‘Woe to you! Have faith! Surely Allah’s promise is true.’ But he says (in reply): ‘These (words) are nothing but fables of the ancients.’
Such are the ones against whom the Word (of Allah) has proved true from the generations of jinn and humans who passed before them. They were indeed losers.
For each (person) there are ranks (in Paradise and Hell) according to what they have done so that He may pay them back fully for their deeds. And they will not be wronged.

The Day when the disbelievers are exposed to the Fire (they will be told:) ‘You have (already) exhausted your (share of) pleasures during your worldly life and (fully) enjoyed them. So today, you will be rewarded with the punishment of disgrace, for you used to act arrogantly on the earth without right and because you have been transgressing.’

And mention (Hud,) the kinship brother (of the people) of ‘Ad, when he warned his people (of the dire consequences of their evil deeds) at al-Ahqaf (the Sand-Hills—a valley in Yemen). And (other) warners had (also) passed away before and after him, (saying:) ‘Do not worship anyone but Allah. Indeed, I fear for you the punishment of a Terrible Day.’
They said: ‘Have you come to us to turn us away from our gods? So bring us whatever you threaten us with if you are truthful.’
He said: ‘The knowledge (of the Hour) is with Allah alone. I (only) convey to you what I have been sent with. But I can see you are an ignorant people.’
