So when they saw it (i.e. the torment) advancing towards their valleys as a (dense) cloud, they said: ‘This is a cloud bringing us rain.’ ‘(Nay!) Instead, this (cloud) is that (torment) you restlessly sought to hasten. (It is) a hurricane, carrying a painful punishment.
It will destroy everything by the command of its Lord.’ So they became such that nothing but their (ruined) houses could be seen. That is how We punish the evildoers.

(O Meccans!) Indeed, We had established them (in power) in such a manner that We did not establish you. And We gave them (advanced abilities of) hearing, sight and intellect (according to their time). But their hearing, their sight, and their minds did not avail them in any way since they persisted in denying the signs of Allah. So, the torment they used to ridicule besieged them.
(O Meccans!) Indeed, We destroyed many towns around you and repeated the signs that perhaps they might return (to the right path).

So why did those (idols) they had taken as gods besides Allah for attaining (His) nearness not help them? Instead, they failed them. And that was (the result of) their lies and what they had been forging.

And (remember) when We made a group of jinn incline towards you, who listened to the Qur’an. So when they were present there, they said (to one another): ‘Keep silent.’ And when it (i.e. the recitation) was finished, they returned to their people as warners.

They said: ‘O our people (from the community of the jinn)! Indeed, we have heard a Book revealed after (the Torah of) Musa (Moses) confirming (the Scriptures) that came before it. It guides (us) to the truth and to a straight path.
O our people! Respond to Allah’s caller and believe in him. He will forgive you your sins and protect you from a painful punishment.
Whoever does not respond to the caller of Allah cannot escape (His power) anywhere on the earth. He will not have any protectors against Him. They are in manifest error.’
Do they not see that Allah, Who created the heavens and the earth and did not tire by creating them, (also) has the power to revive the dead? Yes, indeed, He is All-Capable of everything.

And the Day when the disbelievers will be exposed to the Fire (they will be asked:) ‘Is this (i.e. the Hereafter) not the truth?’ They will say: ‘Yes, of course! By our Lord (it is true).’ He will say: ‘Then taste the punishment for having disbelieved.’

So be patient just as the Messengers of Firm Resolve were patient, and do not seek to hasten (the punishment) for these (deniers). The Day when they see what they were promised, it will be as if they had stayed (in this world) for only an hour of a day. (This is) a proclamation (from your Lord). So, shall anyone be destroyed except the transgressing people?
Those who disbelieve and bar (others) from the path of Allah, He will bring their deeds to nothing.

But those who believe, perform righteous deeds and believe in what is revealed to Muhammad (blessings and peace be upon him)—and it is the truth from their Lord—He will absolve them of their faults and improve their condition.
That is because the disbelievers follow falsehood, whereas the believers follow the truth from their Lord. That is how Allah sets forth for people their examples.

So when you encounter the (combatant) disbelievers (on the battlefield), strike their necks. When you overcome them, (make them prisoners and) bind them firmly (to stop them from creating disorder). After that, either oblige them (by releasing them) as a favour or for ransom until the war lays down its burdens (i.e. the war ends and a truce is announced). That (is Allah’s ordinance). And had Allah willed, He could have taken vengeance on them (without war). But (He did not do so) to test some of you through others. But for those who were martyred in the way of Allah, He will never render their deeds void.
He will guide them and set their affairs right.
And He will (ultimately) admit them into Paradise, which He has (already) made known to them.
O believers! If you stand up for Allah, He will help you and make your steps firm and steady.
And those who disbelieve, there is destruction for them, and He will bring their deeds to nothing.
That (will happen) because they hate what Allah has sent down, so He rendered their deeds void.
Have they not travelled throughout the land and seen how those before them met their end? Allah brought destruction upon them, and a similar (fate) awaits the disbelievers.
That is because Allah is the Guardian of the believers, while the disbelievers have no guardian.

Allah will surely admit those who believe and perform righteous deeds into Gardens with rivers flowing beneath them. As for the disbelievers, they enjoy themselves and eat just like the beasts eat, but the Fire will be their (final) abode.
And imagine how many towns were superior in might than your town, which (chose to) expel you. We destroyed them, and they had no helper (to protect them).
Can he (i.e. the believer) who stands on a manifest proof from his Lord be like someone whose evil deeds have been made alluring to him, and they follow their (own) desires?

(Here is) a description of Paradise promised to the God-conscious: rivers of water forever pure; rivers of fresh milk unchanging in taste (and flavour); rivers of wine, a delight for the drinkers; and rivers of purified honey. And there, they will find fruit of every kind and (pleasing) forgiveness from their Lord. How can they be compared with those permanently stuck in the Fire, given boiling water to drink, which tears their bowels (into pieces)?

Among them are some who seek to listen to you (O Prophet). But when they depart from your presence, they say (mockingly) to those given knowledge: ‘What did he say just now?’ These are the ones whose hearts Allah has sealed, and they (only) follow their desires.
As for those who are (rightly) guided, He increases their guidance and blesses them (more) with their God-consciousness.

Are they awaiting anything except that the Hour should come upon them suddenly? Indeed, (some of) its signs have already come. But once it has arrived upon them, will it not be too late to be mindful of advice?

Know that there is no god except Allah, and (O Prophet!) seek (Allah’s) protection from any action unworthy of your esteemed station. And (also) seek (His) pardon for the believing men and the believing women. And (O people!) Allah fully knows (about) your movements and your final abode (in the Hereafter).

The believers say: ‘If only a sura (i.e. a Qur’anic Chapter) was revealed (allowing fighting for self-defence).’ But when a decisive sura is revealed, in which (defensive) fighting is (clearly) mentioned, you see those with sickness in their hearts staring at you with the look of someone fainting at death. More fitting for them (would be)
obedience (to God) and honourable words. So when the matter is resolved, if they remain true to Allah, that will surely be better for them.
(O hypocrites!) Perhaps if you were to take command, you would indeed cause corruption in the land and cut off the ties of your blood relations.
They are the ones whom Allah has cursed, so He made them deaf and blinded their sight.
Do they not then reflect on the Qur’an, or are there locks upon their hearts?
Surely, those who turn their backs after guidance has become clear to them, Satan has tempted them, and he has deluded them with false hopes.
That is because they said (privately) to those who (also) dislike what Allah has revealed: ‘We shall obey you in some matters.’ And Allah knows their secret dealings.
Then how (horrible) will it be when the angels cause them to die, striking their faces and their backs?
That is because they followed what displeased Allah and were averse to His good pleasure. So He has rendered their deeds void.
Do those with sickness in their hearts think that Allah will not expose their malicious intentions?

And if We wish, We will show these (hypocrites) to you (in such a manner) that you may recognise them by their marks. And you will certainly recognise them by their tone of speech. And Allah (fully) knows your actions.
And We shall certainly test you until We make known those of you who (truly) strive hard (in God’s cause) and remain patient. And We shall test (the sincerity of) your assertions.

Indeed, those who disbelieve, bar (others) from Allah’s path and oppose the Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) after guidance has become clear to them will not be able to harm Allah in the least. And He shall render their deeds void.
O believers! Obey Allah, obey the Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) and do not let your deeds go to waste.
Indeed, those who disbelieve and bar (others) from the way of Allah and then die while they are disbelievers, Allah will never forgive them.

(O believers!) Do not lose heart (during your struggle), nor cry for peace (showing your weakness); you will have the upper hand, and Allah is with you. And He will never deprive you (of the reward) of your efforts.
The worldly life is nothing but play and amusement. But if you believe (in Allah) and remain God-conscious, He will give you your rewards and will not ask you for your wealth (in return).
If He were to ask it from you and press you (into handing your wealth over), you would be miserly, and He would expose your malicious intentions.

Ah! Here are you, being invited to spend for the cause of Allah, yet some among you are stingy. And whoever is miserly is miserly only to himself. And Allah is Self-Sufficient, whereas you (all) are needy (of Him). And if you turn away (from His path), He will replace you with other people, and then they will not be like you.
(O Prophet!) Indeed, We have opened for you (the path to) a clear victory,

so, for your sake, Allah may forgive the past faults of your community* and their future shortcomings. And He may complete His Blessing upon you, and guide you upon a straight path. [* The co-related noun is estimated and has been omitted here. The intended words are ‘ma taqaddama min dhanbi ummati-ka wa ma ta’akhkhar’. It is because the following subject relates to glad tidings of sending down tranquillity, admission to Paradise and forgiveness of sins. The meaning will become evident if verses 1 to 5 are studied together. The leading exegetes have elucidated it under verse 55 of Sura al-Mu’min. In li-dhanbi-ka, the co-related noun is ‘Umma’, which is omitted. On this basis, therefore, wastaghfir li-dhanbi-ka denotes the sins of the Umma. The leading scholars Imam al-Nasafi, Imam al-Qurtubi and al-Shawkani have drawn the same meanings. To summarise:   1. Wastaghfir li-dhanbi-ka means li-dhanbi Ummati-ka, that is ‘the sins of your Umma’. (al-Nasafi in Madarik al-Tanzil wa Haqa’iq al-Ta’wil, 4:359.)   2. Wastaghfir li-dhanbi-ka means li-dhanbi Ummati-ka, hudhifa al-mudaf wa uqima al-mudaf ilayh maqama-hu. It implies the sins of the Umma. The co-related noun is omitted in the given phrase. (al-Qurtubi in al-Jami‘ li-Ahkam al-Qur’an, 15:324.)   3. Wa qila li-dhanbi Ummati-ka fi haqqi-ka. It is said that li-dhanbi-ka, signifies the faults committed by the Umma, regarding your rights. (Ibn Hayyan al-Andalusi in al-Bahr al-Muhit, 7:471.)   4. Wastaghfir li-dhanbi-ka means dhanbi Ummati-ka, fa-huwa ‘ala hadhfi al-mudaf. It implies the sins of the Umma, and this interpretation is based on the principle of omission of the co-related noun. (al-Shawkani in Fath al-Qadir, 4:497.)]
And that Allah may help you with tremendously strong support.

He is the One Who sent down tranquillity into the hearts of the believers so that they might increase in faith along with their (existing) faith. To Allah belong the forces of the heavens and the earth. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

So He may cause the believing men and the believing women to enter Gardens with streams flowing forever. And that He may absolve them of their misdeeds. And that is a great success in the sight of Allah.

And He may punish the hypocritic men and the hypocritic women and the polytheistic men and the polytheistic women, who entertain evil assumptions about Allah. They will be encircled by evil. And Allah has afflicted them with His wrath, cursed them and prepared Hell for them. And it is an evil destination.
To Allah belong the forces of the heavens and the earth, and Allah is All-Mighty, All-Wise.
Indeed, We have sent you as a Witness, a Bearer of glad tidings and a Warner.
So that you may believe in Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him), support him and revere him exaltedly. And (with that) you may glorify Him morning and evening.

Indeed, those who pledge allegiance to you (O Prophet!) actually pledge allegiance to Allah. The Hand of Allah is over their hands. So, whoever breaks his oath breaks it only to his own detriment. And whoever fulfils the covenant he made with Allah, He will grant him a great reward.

The Bedouins who were left behind will tell you (apologetically): ‘Our possessions and our families kept us occupied. So plead (to Allah) for our forgiveness.’ They say with their tongues what is not in their hearts. Say: ‘Who can be of any avail to you against Allah if He intends to cause you harm or desires to benefit you? But Allah is well aware of what you do.

Rather, you thought that the Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) and the believers would never return to their families. And that (evil thought) was made appealing to your hearts. And you entertained vicious speculations, so you are a ruined people.’
And whosoever does not believe in Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him), We have prepared a blazing Fire for the disbelievers.
To Allah belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. He forgives whomever He pleases and punishes whomever He wills. And Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.

Those who stayed behind (on the occasion of al-Hudaybiya) will say when you set out to collect the spoils (of Khaybar): ‘Allow us (also) to follow you.’ They desire to change the Words of Allah. Say: ‘You will not follow us. Thus has Allah said before.’ Then they will say: ‘Nay! You are jealous of us.’ Instead, they cannot comprehend except a little.

Say to the Bedouins who stayed behind: ‘You will soon be invited (to fight) against a people possessing great might. You will fight them, or they will submit. So if you obey (the command), Allah will give you a good reward. But if you turn away as you did before, He will inflict upon you a painful punishment.

There is no blame on the blind, nor is there blame on the lame (or disabled), nor is there blame on the sick (for their incapacity to fight). And whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him), He will admit him into the Gardens with rivers flowing beneath them. But whoever refuses to comply, He will inflict upon him a painful punishment.

Allah was indeed well pleased with the believers when they pledged allegiance to you under the tree (at al-Hudaybiya). He knew what was in their hearts (of their loyalty to His Messenger). So He sent down tranquillity upon them and rewarded them with a forthcoming victory,* [* The forthcoming victory was either the Conquest of Khaybar or Mecca.]
and (with) abundant spoils of war that they will capture. And Allah is All-Mighty, All-Wise.

Allah has promised you (all) many (future) spoils of war that you will gain (from the coming victories), but He has expedited this (booty of Khaybar) for you. And He has restrained the hands of the people from (harming) you.* So that it may become a sign (of the forthcoming victories) for the believers. And He will keep guiding you along the straight path. [* These people include the inhabitants of Mecca, Khaybar, the Banu Asad, the Banu Ghatafan and all other enemy tribes.]

And (there are) other (great victories) yet beyond your reach. Allah has encompassed them (for you in the future).* And Allah is All-Capable of everything. [* These include the Conquests of Mecca, Hawazin, Hunayn, and then, the Conquests of Persia and Byzantine. Before the Treaty of al-Hudaybiya, the Muslims could not campaign to the north of Medina due to the threat from the south. But now that a truce was signed with the Quraysh and their allies, they could freely deal with the threat from the north. The Muslims were able to concentrate their energy against Khaybar as they had freed themselves from the attacks of their southern enemies, the Quraysh until the Quraysh themselves broke the Treaty. Breaking the Treaty was a significant political development, marking a turning point for the Muslims. That is why the Qur’an miraculously declared the Treaty of al-Hudaybiya a ‘clear victory’ as it opened the doors to great historical conquests. It led to the Conquests of Khaybar, Mecca and the entire Arabian Peninsula, and later, the expansion of the Islamic state outside of Arabia, overtaking both the Persian and Byzantine empires.]
Had the disbelievers fought you (at al-Hudaybiya), they would have fled, turning their backs. Then, they would not have found any protector or helper.
Such was Allah’s practice long established in the past. And you will never find any change in Allah’s practice.

He is the One Who held back their hands from you and your hands from them (restraining both sides from engaging in combat) in the valley of Mecca after He had given you the upper hand over (a group of) them. And Allah was All-Seeing of what you do.

They are the ones who disbelieved and barred you from the Sacred Mosque and prevented the sacrificial animals from reaching their destination. And were it not for (some) believing men and believing women (residing in Mecca), unknown to you, you would have trampled upon them in negligence, and thus, the blame of killing them would fall upon you. (You were held back from fighting) so that Allah may admit into His mercy whom He pleases. If those (unknown believers) had been clearly separated, We would have inflicted a painful punishment upon the disbelievers among them.

When the disbelievers had harboured pride in their hearts, the pride of pagan ignorance, Allah sent down His tranquillity upon His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) and upon the believers, and He made them abide by the Word of God-consciousness, for they were better entitled and more worthy of it. And Allah is All-Knowing of everything.

Allah has indeed fulfilled His Messenger’s vision in truth. You will most certainly enter the Sacred Mosque safely, God willing, (some with your) heads shaven and (others with their) hair shortened, not fearing—for He knew what you did not know. So, He granted you an immediate victory (of the Conquest of Khaybar) before that (i.e. the Conquest of Mecca).
He is the One Who sent His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) with guidance and the Religion of Truth that He may elevate it over all religions. And Allah suffices as a Witness.

Muhammad (blessings and peace be upon him) is the Messenger of Allah. And those with him are hard against the disbelievers and compassionate among themselves. You see them bowing (and) prostrating (in prayer), seeking Allah’s grace and (His) pleasure. The sign (of brightness) upon their faces is because of the (spiritual) effect of prostration. Such is their description in the Torah and their description in the Injil (the Gospel). (These Companions are) like a cultivated crop which (initially) brings out its shoot, then becomes strong, then grows thick, and then rises firmly on its stem, providing delight to the cultivators so that He may enrage the disbelievers on account of them. Allah has promised those among them who believe and perform righteous deeds forgiveness and a great reward.

O believers! Do not advance before Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) in any matter), and be God-conscious (lest slight discourtesy to His Beloved Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him) ruins your faith). Indeed, Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

O believers! Do not raise your voices above the voice of the Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him), and do not speak loud to him as you speak loud to one another, lest (all) your deeds become void while you are unaware (of it being ruined).

Indeed, those who lower their voices in the presence of Allah’s Messenger are the ones whose hearts Allah has chosen and refined for God-consciousness. For them, there will be forgiveness and an immense reward.
As for those who call you from outside the private chambers, most of them have no understanding (of the proprieties of your company).
Had they been patient until you came out to them, it would have been better for them. And Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.
O believers! If an evildoer brings you some news, verify it, lest you should harm some people unknowingly and later become regretful for what you have done.

Know that in your midst is the Messenger of Allah (blessings and peace be upon him). If he were to yield to you in many matters, you would surely suffer (the consequences). But Allah has caused you to love faith and has made it appealing in your hearts. And He has caused you to hate faithlessness, transgression and disobedience. They are the ones who are rightly guided.
(This is) a grace and blessing from Allah, and Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

If two groups of believers fight each other, then make peace between them. But if one group of them aggresses against the other, fight the aggressing one until it returns to Allah’s command. Then, if it returns (for reconciliation), make peace between them with justice and act equitably. Indeed, Allah loves the just.
The believers are but one brotherhood, so make peace between your brothers. And be God-conscious so you may be shown mercy.

O believers! Do not let some (men) ridicule others. They may be better than those (who ridicule). Nor should some women (make fun) of other women; they may be better than those (who make fun). And do not defame one another nor insult one another with offensive nicknames. How bad is the evil name after having faith? And whoever does not repent—they are the wrongdoers.

O believers! Avoid much suspicion. Indeed, some suspicions are sinful. And do not spy nor backbite one another. Would any of you like to eat the flesh of his dead brother? You would hate it. And be God-conscious (in such matters). Indeed, Allah is the Most-Relenting, the All-Merciful.

O people! Indeed, We created you from a male and a female and made you nations and tribes that you may come to know one another. Indeed, the most honourable of you in the sight of Allah is the most God-conscious among you. Indeed, Allah is All-Knowing, All-Aware.

(Some of) the Bedouins say: ‘We have believed.’ Say: ‘You have not believed. Rather, say: ‘We have embraced Islam.’ For faith has not yet entered your hearts. But if you obey Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him), He will not decrease anything (from the rewards) of your deeds. Surely, Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.’

The (true) believers are only those who believe in Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) and then have never doubted, and (who) strive with their wealth and their lives for the cause of Allah. They are the ones who are truthful.
Say: ‘Will you teach Allah about your religion while Allah knows whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth? And Allah is All-Knowing of everything.’

They count it as a favour to you that they have embraced Islam. Say: ‘Do not count your embracing of Islam as a favour to me. Rather, Allah has done you a favour by guiding you to the faith if you are truthful (in your claim).’
Indeed, Allah knows the Unseen of the heavens and the earth. And Allah is All-Seeing of whatever you do.
Qaf, by the Glorious Qur’an.
Rather, they are astonished that a Warner has come to them from among themselves. So the disbelievers say: ‘This is a strange thing!
What! When we are dead and have become dust (then we shall be raised again)? Such return is far-fetched (i.e. impossible to perceive).’
We surely know what the earth consumes of them (i.e. their bodies after death). And with Us is an all-preserving Book.
Rather, they reject the truth when it has come to them. So, they are in a state of confusion.
Do they not look at the heavenly universe above them, how We built it and decorated it (with galaxies, stars and planets), and there are no cracks (or defects) in it?
As for the earth, We spread it out and cast in it firm mountains and caused every pleasant kind of plant to grow in it.
(In this, there is) an insight and a reminder for every penitent servant.
And We send down blessed rainwater from the sky, whereby We grow gardens and grain for harvest.
And tall date palms loaded with clusters of fruits,
as a provision for the servants. And with it, We revive a dead land. Likewise shall be the emergence (from the graves).
Before them, the people of Nuh (Noah) denied (the truth), and so did the inhabitants of the blind well (al-Rass in Yamama) and Thamud (the people of Salih, in the town of al-Hijr near Tabuk).
So did ‘Ad (the people of Hud in the Ahqaf valley of Yemen), Pharaoh (the ruler of Egypt) and the people of Lut (Lot, living in Sodom and ‘Amura [Gomorrah] in Palestine).

So did the inhabitants of al-Ayka (the forest-dwellers of Madyan, i.e. the people of Shu‘ayb) and the people of Tubba‘ (As‘ad Abu Karib al-Himyari, the King of Yemen). All of them rejected the messengers. So, My promise of punishment (against them) proved true.
Did We get exhausted with the first creation? Instead, they are in doubt regarding (their) new creation.
We indeed created human being, and We know what (doubts) his soul whispers to him. And We are nearer to him than his jugular vein.
When the two receivers (i.e. the two recording angels) record (his deeds), seated on the right side and the left,
he does not utter a single word, but there is a vigilant observer beside him (to write it down).
Then, the agony of death comes with the truth. (It is said to him:) ‘This is what you were trying to escape.’
Then, the Trumpet will be blown: ‘This is the promised Day.’
Then every soul will come (before Us), accompanied by a driver (pushing it forward) and a witness (to its actions).
(It will be said to the denier:) ‘Indeed, you were in a state of negligence about this (Day). We have removed your veil (of negligence) from you, and today, your sight is intensely sharp.’
His companion (angel) will say: ‘This is what is ready with me (as a testimony against you).’
(Both angels will be told:) ‘Throw into Hell every stubborn disbeliever,
(who is) a hinderer of good, a transgressor (and) a doubter,
who has set up another god along with Allah. So the two of you throw him into the severe punishment.’
His companion (Satan) will say: ‘Our Lord! I did not make him rebel. Rather, he was in extreme error (of his own accord).’
He will say: ‘Do not dispute in My presence, for I had already sent you the warning.
My Word cannot be changed, nor am I unjust to the servants.’
That Day, We shall say to the Hellfire: ‘Are you full?’ It will say: ‘Are there any more?’
And Paradise will be brought near to the God-conscious; not distant (any longer).
(And they will be told:) ‘This is what you were promised. (It is) for every penitent and dutiful (servant),
who fears the Most-Compassionate (Lord) without seeing (Him) and comes (before His presence) with a penitent heart.
Enter it in peace! That is the Day of Eternity.’
There, they will have whatever they wish, and with Us, there is (even) more.

How many a generation We destroyed before them who were more powerful than these (Meccans)! (When the punishment descended upon them) they desperately searched the land (to find refuge). Is there any escape (from Allah’s command)?
There is indeed a reminder in that for whoever has a (mindful) heart or lends an attentive ear while he is a witness.
Indeed, We created the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them in six periods, but no fatigue touched Us (ever).
So be patient over what they say, and glorify your Lord’s praise before sunrise and sunset.
And glorify Him during part of the night and at the ends of prostrations (i.e. after every prayer).
Listen attentively on the Day when the caller will call from a nearby place.
The Day when all will hear the mighty blast in truth. That is the Day of emergence (from the graves).
It is indeed We Who give life and cause death. And towards Us is the final destination.
On that Day, the earth will be split open, letting them rush out hastening. That will be an easy gathering for Us.
We know best what they say. And you are not to compel them (to believe). So, with this Qur’an, remind those who fear My warning.
By the scattering (winds) that scatter (the clouds);
by the rain-bearing (clouds) loaded with the burden (of water);
by (the ships) which float gently (on the sea);
by (the angels) who distribute (blessings) by (His) command;
indeed, what you are promised is true.
And indeed, the judgement (of retributive justice) shall come to pass.
By the sky full of galaxies and pathways (of planets and stars),
indeed, you are (lost) in conflicting opinions.
Only he is turned away (from the Qur’an) who has been deluded.
Ruined are those who tell lies out of conjecture,
those steeped in ignorance and are neglectful (of the Hereafter).
They ask (mockingly): ‘When is the Day of Retribution?’
(Say:) ‘It is the Day (when) they will be tormented over the Fire.’
(And they will be told:) ‘Taste your torment. This is what you sought to hasten.’
Indeed, the pious will be (rejoicing) in gardens and springs.
Receiving (delightfully) what their Lord has granted them, for they had been virtuously excellent before that.
They used to sleep only a little during the night.
And they would seek forgiveness in the latter part of the night (i.e. before dawn).
And in their wealth, there was a rightful share for the beggar and the deprived.
Upon the earth, there are signs for those with sure faith,
and within yourselves (as well). Do you not then perceive?
And in the heaven is your sustenance and whatever you are promised.
By the Lord of the heaven and the earth, this (promise) is surely true, just as (the fact that) you can speak.
Did the story of the honoured guests of Ibrahim (Abraham) reach you?
When they (i.e. the angels) entered his presence, they greeted him with: ‘Peace!’ He (also) said (to them): ‘Peace (be upon you)!’ (Addressing himself, he said:) ‘They are unfamiliar people.’
Then he went quietly to his family and brought out a fat (roasted) calf.
He placed it before them and said: ‘Will you not eat?’
(When they did not eat,) he started becoming fearful of them. They (i.e. the angels) said: ‘Do not be afraid.’ And they gave him the glad tidings of a knowledgeable son (Ishaq [Isaac]).
Then his wife (Sara) came up with a loud cry (in joy), patting her forehead in wonder and said: ‘(What!) A barren old woman (giving birth to a child)?’
They (i.e. the angels) said: ‘Such has your Lord decreed. Indeed, He is the All-Wise, the All-Knowing.’
He (i.e. Abraham) said: ‘What is your intent now, O messengers?’
They said: ‘We have (actually) been sent to a guilty people (the people of Lot),
to rain upon them stones of (baked) clay,
marked by your Lord for the transgressors.’
Then (before the punishment descended upon them), We evacuated (from the town) whoever among them were believers.

But We did not find there other than one house of the Muslims.* [* One house refers to Prophet Lut and his two daughters; they were saved from the punishment, but his wife was not, for she was among the disbelievers.]
We have left a sign there (as a lesson) for those who fear the painful punishment.
And (there is another lesson) in (the story of) Musa (Moses), when We sent him to Pharaoh with a clear authority.
He turned away along with his cabinet and said: ‘(Here is) a magician or a crazy man!’
So We seized him and his forces, and We drowned them in the sea, for he was blameworthy.
And (there is another lesson) in (the story of) ‘Ad, when We sent upon them a destructive wind.
It left nothing that it came upon but reduced it to ashes.
And (there is another lesson) in (the story of) Thamud, when they were told: ‘Enjoy yourselves for a (short) while.’
Still, they revolted against the command of their Lord. So, a horrifying blast overtook them while they were looking on (helplessly).
So they were neither able to rise nor could they help one another.
And (We also destroyed) the people of Nuh (Noah) earlier. Indeed, they were a transgressing people.
And We built the whole universe with (great) energy. And indeed, We are continuously expanding it.
As for the earth, We have spread it out. So, how excellent spreaders We are!
We have created pairs of everything; perhaps you may be mindful of advice.
(Say:) ‘So flee to Allah. Indeed, I am a clear warner to you from Him.
Do not set up another god with Allah. Indeed, I am a clear warner to you from Him.’
Similarly, no messenger came to those before them, but they said: ‘(This is) a magician or a crazy man!’
Did they tell one another to do this? Instead, they were a rebellious people.
So turn away from them, for you will not be blamed (for their disbelief).
But (continue to) remind, for surely the Reminder benefits the believers.
I did not create the jinn and humans but to worship Me.
I desire no provision from them, nor do I want that they should feed Me.
Indeed, Allah is the One Who is the Supreme Sustainer, the Lord of Might, the All-Strong.
Indeed, those who commit wrong will have a share (of the torment) like the share of their (earlier) counterparts. So they need not ask Me to hasten on (their fate).
So, woe to the disbelievers for the Day they are promised.
By the Mount (Sinai [al-Tur], where Allah spoke to Musa [Moses]),
by the Book inscribed,
on an unrolled parchment;
by the (Sacred) House frequently visited (in the Heavens);
by the roof highly raised (in the Heavens);
by the ocean surging over,
indeed, the punishment of your Lord will come to pass.
None can avert it.
On the Day when the sky spins around violently,
and the mountains fly fast (and scatter like dust particles),
so terrible will be the suffering of the deniers on that Day,
those who amuse themselves with falsehood.
(It is) the Day they will be pushed forcibly towards the Fire of Hell.
(They will be told:) ‘This is the Fire which you used to deny.
Is this magic, or is it you who do not see?
Enter it (and burn): whether you are patient or impatient, it will be the same for you. You are only being rewarded for what you used to do.’
Indeed, the God-conscious will be in the Gardens and Bliss,
rejoicing in whatever their Lord has granted them. And their Lord has saved them from the punishment of Hell.
(They will be told:) ‘Enjoy your food and drink as a reward for what you used to do.’
They will be comfortably reclining on couches arranged in rows, and We will marry them to beautiful maidens with dazzling eyes.

The believers and their descendants who followed them in faith, We shall make their offspring join them (by elevating them to their rank). And We will not reduce anything from (the reward of) their deeds. Every person will be held in pledge for what he has earned.
We will provide them with fruit and meat, as they desire.
There, they will pass a cup (of pure wine) to each other, (and) there will be neither any foolish talk nor sinful speech.
And their (attendant) youths will go around them as if they were hidden pearls.
Some of them will advance towards others, questioning each other.
They will say: ‘Before this (blessing), we used to be apprehensive about our families.
But Allah showed us (His) favour and protected us from the punishment of the burning heat (of Hell).
Indeed, we used to supplicate Him before. Indeed, He is the Most-Gracious, the All-Merciful.’
So, (O Prophet!) continue to remind (them). By the grace of your Lord, you are neither a fortune-teller nor a madman.
Or do they say: ‘(He is) a poet, for whom we (eagerly) await a misfortune of time?”
Say: ‘Wait! For I am also waiting with you.’
Or do their minds prompt them to say this (foolish thing)? Or are they (just) rebellious people?
Or do they say: ‘He has forged it (i.e. the Qur’an)’? Instead, they do not believe (in the truth).
Let them bring a discourse like this (Qur’an) if they are truthful.
Or were they created from nothing? Or are they (their own) creators?
Did they create the heavens and the earth? Instead, they are not certain (about anything).
Or do they possess the treasures of your Lord? Or are they the controllers (of its distribution)?
Or do they have a ladder (leading up to heaven) whereby they can listen? Then, let their listener produce a manifest authority.
Or does He have daughters while you have sons?
Or do you ask them (O Prophet!) for a reward (for conveying the message) such that they are overburdened with debt?
Or do they have (knowledge of) the Unseen that they write down?
Or do they desire to scheme (against you, O Prophet)? Then, the disbelievers themselves shall be the victims of (their) schemes.
Or do they have any god besides Allah? Glory be to Allah from the partners they ascribe (to Him).
Even if they saw a fragment falling from the sky, they would say: ‘(This is just) a heap of layered clouds.’
So leave them until they encounter their Day, in which they will be thunderstruck,
the Day when their scheming will not avail them in any way, nor will they be helped.
Indeed, for the wrongdoers, there is a punishment besides that, but most of them do not know.
So submit patiently to the judgement of your Lord, for indeed you are before Our Eyes (all the time). And glorify the praise of your Lord when you rise (at dawn).
And glorify Him during the night and at the setting of the stars.

By the star when it sets,* [* According to some scholars, the star also refers to Prophet Muhammad (blessings and peace be upon him), when he ascended to the ends of the Heavens to meet Allah during the Night of Ascension and then descended back to the world. It is stated by Imam Ja‘far b. Muhammad al-Sadiq, al-Akhfash, al-Tustari, al-Sulami and others. See: al-Baghawi, al-Qurtubi, al-Khazin, Abu Hafs al-Hanbali, al-Tha‘labi, al-Shawkani, al-Alusi and others.]
Your guide (i.e. Prophet Muhammad (blessings and peace be upon him) has never erred nor gone astray.
He does not speak out of (his own) desire.
It (i.e. his speech) is only a revelation sent down (to him).
The Lord of Mighty Power taught him,
the Lord of absolute perfection. Then He intended to unveil (Himself).
And he (Muhammad (blessings and peace be upon him) was upon the highest horizon (above the Heavens).

Then He (Allah) drew nearer (to Prophet Muhammad (blessings and peace be upon him) approaching so close,* [* Imam al-Bukhari has reported a hadith in al-Jami‘ al-Sahih supporting these meanings from the venerable Companion Anas b. Malik. Moreover, ‘Abd Allah b. ‘Abbas, Imam al-Hasan al-Basri, Imam Ja‘far b. Muhammad al-Sadiq, Muhammad b. Ka‘b al-Qurazi, al-Dahhak and many other leading exegetes hold the same view. See: al-Tabari, Ibn Kathir, al-Baghawi, al-Razi, al-Khazin, Abu al-Sa‘ud, al-Qurtubi, al-Tha‘labi, Abu Hafs al-Hanbali, al-Suyuti, and others.]
until he was only two bows’ length away or nearer.
Then He revealed whatever He revealed to His (beloved) servant.
The (Prophet’s) heart did not doubt what he saw.
Do you then dispute with him about what he saw?

And indeed, he saw Him another time,* [* These meanings are based on the authority of Ibn ‘Abbas, Abu Dharr al-Ghifari, ‘Ikrima, al-Hasan al-Basri, Muhammad b. Ka‘b al-Qurazi, Abu al-‘Aliya al-Riyahi, ‘Ata’ b. Abi Rabah, Ka‘b al-Ahbar, Imam Ahmad b. Hanbal, Imam Abu al-Hasan al-Ash‘ari and many others.]
at the Lote Tree of the Ultimate Boundary (above the seventh heaven),
by which is the Garden of the Abode,
while it covered the Lote Tree, whatever (of the Glorious Radiance) covered it.
The (Prophet’s) eye did not turn aside, nor did it exceed the bounds.
Indeed, he saw the greatest of His Lord’s signs (on this night).
Have you considered (the idols) al-Lat and al-‘Uzza,
and Manat, the third one (as well)?
(O idolaters!) Are you to have males (i.e. sons) while He females (i.e. daughters)?
(If your estimation is correct,) then this is an unfair distribution!

These (idols) are only names you have made up—you and your fathers. Allah has not sent down any authority with them. They (the idolators) follow only conjectures and whatever their lower souls desire, although guidance from their Lord has already come to them.
Shall every human being have whatever he yearns for?
Yet to Allah belong the Hereafter and this world.

(Imagine) how many angels are in the heavens whose intercession will be of no benefit (to these idolaters). But (it will become beneficial only) after Allah grants permission to whomever He pleases and approves.
Indeed, those who do not believe in the Hereafter give female names to the angels.
And they do not have any knowledge (to substantiate this). They follow only (their) assumptions. And indeed, assumptions can in no way replace the truth.
So avoid those who turn away from Our remembrance and desire nothing but the life of this world.
That is the ultimate reach of their knowledge. Indeed, your Lord knows best those who stray from His way, and He knows best those who are (rightly) guided.
To Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth, so He may requite the evildoers for what they have done and reward the virtuous with the most beautiful goodness.

(They are) the ones who avoid major sins and shameful deeds, except minor mistakes (or the passing thoughts of sin). Surely, your Lord is liberal in forgiving. He knows you best since He originated your life from the earth and since you were embryos in the wombs of your mothers. So do not attribute purity to your souls. He knows best who is God-conscious.
Have you seen the one who turned away (from Islam),
and (initially) paid a little (for his salvation), and (then) withheld?
Does he possess the knowledge of the Unseen so that he sees (the Hereafter)?
Has he not been informed of what is in the scriptures of Musa (Moses),
and (in the scriptures of) Ibrahim (Abraham), who diligently fulfilled (Allah’s commandments)?
(They state) that no bearer shall bear the burden of another.

And that nothing belongs to human being (as his right) except what he strives for.* [* As for Allah’s Grace, there are no limits. He can grant whatever He wills to whomever He pleases, as stated in Sura al-Nur , verse 38: “So that Allah may reward them for the best of what they have performed and give them more out of His grace. And Allah provides for whomever He pleases without any measure.”]
And he will soon be shown his endeavours.
Then he will be rewarded for it with the fullest reward,
and that to your Lord is the ultimate return (of everything).
Moreover, He is the One Who makes (people) laugh (out of joy) and makes (them) weep (out of grief),
and that He is the One Who causes death and gives life,
and that He creates the two genders, male and female,

from a fertilised egg (i.e. a zygote*) when settled (into the mother’s womb). [* In Arabic, the word ‘ al-nutfa ’ is mentioned, and it refers to a zygote, which is a mixture of male and female gametes. It is the new single cell formed after the union of a sperm and ovum.]
And it is upon Him to bring out the second genesis.
And that He is the One Who enriches and grants possessions.
And that He is the One Who is the Lord of Sirius (i.e. a star worshipped by some ancient pagans).
And that He is the One Who destroyed the first (people of) ‘Ad,
then (the people of) Thamud, sparing none (of them),
and the people of Nuh (Noah) before (all of them). Indeed, they were most unjust and rebellious.
And He smashed the towns (of Sodom and Gomorrah), turning (them) upside down.
So whatever fell on them covered them completely.
Then which of your Lord’s blessings will you dispute?
This (Esteemed Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him) is a Warner (in the tradition) of the earlier warners.
The imminent (Hour) has drawn near.
None can disclose it besides Allah.
Do you then wonder at this (revealed) discourse,
and laugh (at it) and not weep (in fear),
and you remain heedless?
Instead, prostrate to Allah and worship (Him).
The Last Hour has drawn near, and the moon was split (in two).
If they (the disbelievers) see a (miraculous) sign, they turn away and say: ‘(This is) the same (old) magic.’
They reject (the truth) and follow their desires. So, every matter has an appropriate setting.
Indeed, the reports (of destroyed nations) that contain (sufficient) deterrents have already come to them.
(There is) far-reaching wisdom (in these reports), but warnings are of no benefit (to them).
So, turn away from them (and wait for) the Day when the caller (angel) calls them to something horrible.
With their eyes humbled, they will come out of their graves like scattered locusts,
rushing towards the caller. The disbelievers will say: ‘This is a difficult day.’
The people of Nuh (Noah) rejected (the truth) before them. So they denied Our servant and called (him): ‘crazy’, and he was intimidated.
Then he called upon his Lord, saying: ‘Indeed, I have been overcome (by the transgressors), so help (me).’
So We opened the gates of the sky with water pouring down.
And We caused the earth to burst out with springs, and the waters (from all sides) met for a determined matter.
And We carried him (i.e. Noah) upon a thing built with planks and nails (i.e. the Ark),
floating (over the flood waters) under Our eyes. (This was) retribution for the one who was rejected.
Indeed, We have left it as a sign. So is there anyone who will be mindful?
Then how (terrible) were My punishment and My warnings!
Certainly, We have made the Qur’an easy to remember (by simplifying its lessons). So, is there anyone who will be mindful?
(The people of) ‘Ad (also) rejected (their messenger). Then how (terrible) were My punishment and My warnings!
Indeed, We sent upon them a howling tornado on a day of (their) terrible misfortune,
knocking down people as if they were trunks of uprooted palm trees.
Then how (terrible) were My punishment and My warnings!
Certainly, We have made the Qur’an easy to remember (by simplifying its lessons). So, is there anyone who will be mindful?
(The people of) Thamud (also) rejected the warnings.
They said: ‘Are we to follow a single human being from among us? Then, we would certainly be in error and madness.
Has the revelation been sent to him (only) from among us? Instead, he is an insolent liar.’
By tomorrow, they will soon know who the insolent liar is.
We are sending the she-camel as a trial for them. So, watch them (O Salih) and remain patient.
Inform them that the water is to be shared between them. Every (share of the) drink will be attended (in equitable proportion).
But they roused a companion of theirs. So he took (a sword) in (his) hand and hamstrung (the camel).
Then how (terrible) were My punishment and My warnings!
Indeed, We sent upon them (only) one mighty blast, and they became like the dry sticks of the fence builder.
Certainly, We have made the Qur’an easy to remember (by simplifying its lessons). So, is there anyone who will be mindful?
(Then,) the people of Lut (Lot) rejected the warnings.
We sent a storm of stone upon them except the family of Lut (Lot). We saved them (from the torment) at dawn,* [* All members of Prophet Lot’s family were saved except his wife.]
as a blessing from Us. Thus, We reward those who give thanks.
He had already warned them of Our strike, but they disputed the warnings.
They even tried to turn him away from his (angel) guests (to snatch them from him). So We blinded their eyes, (saying:) ‘Taste My punishment and My warnings.’
Indeed, an abiding punishment visited them early in the morning.
(Then it was said to them:) ‘Taste My punishment and My warnings.’
Certainly, We have made the Qur’an easy to remember (by simplifying its lessons). So, is there anyone who will be mindful?
Indeed, the warnings (also) came to the people of Pharaoh,
who denied all Our signs. So, We seized them with the crushing grip of the All-Mighty, the Most-Powerful.
Are your disbelievers better than those (before), or is there an exemption for you in the scriptures?
Or do they say: ‘We (all) are a victorious confederate (to be dominant over the Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him)’?
Soon, their united front will be defeated (in the battle of Badr), and they will turn (their) backs, fleeing.
But the Hour is their appointed time. And the Hour will be most catastrophic and most bitter.
Indeed, the guilty are in error and madness.
The Day they are dragged into the Fire upon their faces (they will be told:) ‘Taste the touch of Hell.’
Indeed, We have created everything according to a (precisely) programmed measure.
And Our command is nothing but a single (word) like the twinkling of an eye.
Indeed, We have destroyed the likes of you. So, is there anyone who will be mindful?
Everything they have done is in the books (of deeds).
And every (thing), small or big, is noted (in their record).
Indeed, the God-conscious shall be (rejoicing) in gardens and rivers,
at the Seat of Honour (or in the Worthy Abode) in the presence of the Most-Powerful Sovereign.
The Most-Compassionate (Lord),
has taught the Qur󠄕’an.
He created the human being,
(and) taught him to communicate.
The sun and the moon are (rotating) in calculated courses.
The stars (or the herbs spread over the earth) and the trees bow down (in submission).
As for the heavens, He raised them high (by expanding them from all sides). And He set up the balance (to maintain their existence and rotation).
So that you may not infringe the balance.
Maintain the weights with justice, and do not shorten the balance.
As for the earth, He has laid it down for living creatures.
There are fruits in it and date palms bearing clusters,
and grain with husks and aromatic herbs.
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
He created humans from dried clay sounding like pottery,
and He created the jinn from a smokeless flame of fire.
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
(He is) the Lord of the two easts and the Lord of the two wests.
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
He merged the two seas, meeting each other.
Between them is a barrier they do not transgress.
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
Out of both (waters) come out pearls and coral stones.
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
(Under) His (control) are the ships floating on the sea, (appearing) like mountains.
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
Every being on earth is bound to perish.
Your Lord (alone), full of Majesty and Honour, will remain forever.
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
Those in the heavens and the earth ask Him (only). Every day, He manifests (a new state of) Glory.
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
We will soon attend to you (for judgement), O two multitudes (of humans and jinn)!
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?

O assembly of the jinn and the humans! If you can penetrate beyond the regions of the heavens and the earth, then penetrate through (them). But you cannot penetrate through (them) without (Our) authority.
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
Flames of fire and (a cloud of) smoke will be released upon you; then, you will not be able to defend yourselves.
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
(How terrible it will be) when the sky splits apart and turns rose-red like burnt oil (or tanned leather).
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
On that Day, neither humans nor the jinn will be questioned about their sins.
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
The guilty shall be recognised by their mark and seized by (their) forelocks and feet.
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
(They will be told:) ‘This is the Hell which the guilty deny.’
They shall go around between its flames and scalding water.
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
The one who fears standing before his Lord, there will be two Gardens for him.
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
Both will be full of lush green branches.
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
In both of them, there will be two flowing springs.
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
In both of them, there will be two kinds of every fruit.
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
(The residents of Paradise will be) reclining upon couches lined with silky green brocade, and the fruits of two Gardens will hang low (over them).
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
In them, there will be those (chaste maidens) with modest gazes whom no human has ever touched before, nor jinn.
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
Those (maidens) will be (beautiful and elegant) like rubies and corals.
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
Can the reward of goodness be anything but goodness?
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
Besides these two, there will be two (other) gardens.
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
Both (will be) dark green.
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
In both of them, there will be two gushing springs.
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
In both of them, there will be fruits, date palms and pomegranates.
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
In them (all), there will be good-natured and beautiful (chaste maidens).
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
Wide-eyed maidens, secluded in pavilions.
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
Whom no human has ever touched before, nor jinn.
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
Reclining upon green cushions and magnificent carpets.
Then which of your Lord’s blessings do you both deny?
Blessed is the Name of your Lord—the Possessor of Majesty and Honour.
When the Inevitable Event takes place,
none shall deny its occurrence.
(It will be) debasing (for the disbelievers), elevating (for the believers).
When the earth is shaken violently,
and the mountains are crushed to pieces,
and they become scattered (particles of) dust,
you will be (divided) into three groups:
The People of the Right Hand, how (happy) are the People of the Right Hand!
And the People of the Left Hand, how (miserable) are the People of the Left Hand!
And the Foremost (in faith) will be the Foremost (in Paradise).
Those are the ones brought near (to Allah),
(who will reside) in the Gardens of Bliss.
Many (of them are) from the earlier generations,
and a few from the later generations.
(They will be) upon couches brocaded with golden embroidery,
reclining on them facing one another.
Youths never altering in age will keep going around them,
with goblets, pitchers and cups (full of pure wine) from flowing springs,
which will neither cause them headaches nor will they get intoxicated.
(They will be served) any fruit they choose,
and the meat of any bird they desire.
And (they will have) maidens with beautiful wide eyes,
like pearls hidden in covers,
as a reward for what they used to do.
There, they will not hear any idle or sinful talk,
except the (virtuous) word: ‘Peace!’ ‘Peace!’
And the People of the Right Hand, how (blessed) are the People of the Right Hand!
(They will be) among thornless lote trees,
and banana trees clustered with fruits,
and widely extended shades,
and ever-flowing waters,
and abundant fruits,
never out of season nor forbidden.
And (they will be lodged) on raised floors (with noble spouses).
Indeed, We have created these (maidens) as a (unique) creation,
and We made them virgins,
loving, of matching age,
for the People of the Right Hand.
(There will be) a large group of them from the earlier generations,
and a large group from the later generations.
And the People of the Left Hand, how (miserable) are the People of the Left Hand!
(They will be) in the hot, scorching wind and the boiling water (of Hell),
and the shadow of dense black smoke.
(It is) neither cool nor refreshing.
Indeed, before that, they used to live in luxury,
and they would persist in great sin.
They used to say (mockingly): ‘When we are dead and become dust and bones, shall we be raised (alive again)?
And our forefathers of old (will also be given life)?’
Say: ‘Indeed, the former and later generations,
will (all) surely be gathered for the appointment of a known Day.
Then, indeed, you, O straying deniers,
shall eat from the (bitter thorny) tree of Zaqqum.
And you will fill (your) bellies with it,
and drink boiling water over it,
drinking (it) like thirsty camels.’
This shall be the welcome feast they receive on the Day of Resurrection.
It is We Who created you. Then why do you not acknowledge it?
Have you considered (the sperm) you ejaculate (into the womb)?
Are you creating (a child from) it, or are We the Creators?
We have decreed death among you, and We cannot be stopped,
from replacing you with your likes and recreating you in (a way) that you do not know.
You indeed know the first genesis. Then why are you not mindful (of guidance)?
Have you considered what you sow?
Are you causing it to grow, or are We the Growers?
Had We willed, We would have reduced this (harvest) to trash, and you would have been left astoundingly regretful,
(saying to yourself:) ‘Indeed, We have suffered a (great) loss.
Rather, we are deprived (of our livelihood).’
Have you considered the water you drink?
Do you bring it down from the clouds, or are We the Ones Who bring (it) down?
Had We willed, We would have made it bitter. Why do you not give thanks?
Have you considered the fire that you kindle?
Are you who brought its tree into being, or are We the Creator?
We have made it (i.e. the fire) a reminder (of the Hell) and (the trees) a (source of) provision for the desert dwellers.
So glorify the Name of your Lord, the All-Magnificent.

So I swear by the positions of the stars.* [* It also means: ‘I swear by the places where different portions of the Qur’an are descending.’ This translation is based on the exposition given by ‘Abd Allah b. ‘Abbas, ‘Ikrima, Mujahid, ‘Abd Allah b. Jubayr, al-Suddi, al-Farra’, al-Zujaj and others. The context also denotes the same meaning. In the preceding sura, Sura al-Najm , the Holy Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him) was given the title: ‘ al-Najm ’ (the Star), and in this verse, different Qur’anic chapters ( suras ) are mentioned as ‘ al-nujum ’ (stars). See: Tafsir al-Tabari, Ibn Abi Hatim, al-Baghawi, Ibn Kathir, al-Baydawi, al-Lubab, Abu Sa‘ud, al-Khazin, al-Durr al-Manthur, Ruh al-Ma‘ani, al-Bahr al-Muhit, al-Jumal, Zad al-Masir, Fath al-Qadir, al-Mazhari and Majma‘ al-Bayan.]
Indeed, it is a magnificent oath if you could know.
It is indeed a Noble Qur’an.
(Before this, it was written) in a Hidden, Guarded Book (al-Lawh al-Mahfuz).
None shall touch it except the purified ones.
(It is) a piecemeal revelation from the Lord of all worlds.
Do you then take this discourse lightly?
Do you make your means of subsistence through your (act of) rejecting (it)?
Why, then, when it (i.e. the soul of a dying person) reaches the throat (to pass out of his body),
you are looking on (miserably) at that moment?
And We are nearer to him (i.e. the dying person) than you are, but you cannot see (Us).
If you are not subject to (Our) command,
why do you not bring that (soul) back if you are truthful?
If that (dying person) is one among those brought near (to Us),
then (such a person will have) comfort, fragrance and a Garden of Bliss.
And if he is one among the people of the Right Hand,
then (he will be told:) ‘Peace be on you from the people of the Right Hand!’
But if he is one among the deniers, the misguided ones,
then (he will have) a hospitality of boiling water,
and burning in the Hellfire.
Indeed, this is the truth of absolute Certainty.
So glorify the Name of your Lord, the All-Magnificent.
Whatever is in the heavens and the earth glorifies Allah, and He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.
To Him belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. He gives life and causes death. And He is All-Capable of everything.
He is the First and the Last, the Manifest and the Hidden. And He is All-Knowing of everything.

He is the One Who created the heavens and the earth in six periods, then established His authority on the Throne (i.e. He organised the universe under His Glorious Command). He knows whatever goes deep down into the earth and whatever emerges out of it, and whatever descends from the heaven and whatever ascends to it. He is with you wherever you are. And Allah is All-Seeing of whatever you do.
To Him belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and to Allah, (all) matters are returned.
He merges the night into day and merges the day into night. And He is All-Knowing of whatever is (hidden) in the breasts.

Believe in Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him), and spend out of what He has made you the trustees. Those among you who believe and spend (in Allah’s way) shall have a great reward.
Why do you not believe in Allah while (His) Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) calls you to believe in your Lord? He has already taken a pledge from you if you are believers.
He is the One Who sends down clear revelations upon His servant that He may bring you out of utter darkness into light. Indeed, Allah is All-Kind, All-Merciful to you.

Why should you not spend in the way of Allah, while to Allah belongs the heritage of the heavens and the earth? None of you is equal to those who spent (their wealth in the way of Allah) and fought before the victory (of Mecca). They are more exalted in rank than those who spent (their wealth) and fought afterwards. Yet Allah has promised a good reward to all. And Allah is All-Aware of what you do.
Who is the one that will lend Allah a good loan so He may multiply it for him? And there will be a generous reward for him.

On that Day, you will see the believing men and the believing women with their light spreading swiftly ahead of them and on their right. (They will be told:) ‘Glad tidings to you this Day: Gardens with flowing rivers beneath them, to stay there forever. That is the magnificent success.’

On that Day, the male and female hypocrites will say to the believers: ‘Wait for us; let us have some of your light.’ They will be told: ‘Go back (to the world) and look for a light (there).’ Then, a (separating) wall with a gate will be raised between them. The inner side of it will contain mercy, and towards its outer side, there will be punishment.

They will call out to those (believers): ‘Did we not use to be with you (in the world)?’ They will say: ‘Yes indeed! But you caused yourselves to fall into temptation; you (eagerly) awaited (our destruction) and remained doubtful, and false hopes deceived you until the command of Allah arrived. Thus, the Deceiver tricked you about Allah.
So this Day, no ransom will be accepted from you (O hypocrites), nor from the disbelievers. The Fire is your abode. It is your ultimate refuge. What an evil destination.’

Has the time not yet come for the believers that their hearts become humbled to Allah’s remembrance and the truth that has come down and not be like those given the Book before, such that a long time passed upon them that their hearts became hardened? Many of them were transgressors.
Know that Allah revives the earth after its death. We have clarified the signs for you, so perhaps you may understand.
Indeed, those men who give in charity, those women who give in charity and those who lend Allah a good loan—it will be multiplied for them, and there is a generous reward for them.

And those who believe in Allah and His messengers, it is they who are truthful and are witnesses before their Lord. They shall have their reward and their light. But those who disbelieve and reject Our Signs—they shall be the inmates of the Hellfire.

Know that the life of this world is only play, diversion, glamour, mutual boasting and competition for increasing wealth and children. (It is) like the rain (that causes plants to grow), whose vegetation impresses the farmers. Then it withers, and you see it turning yellow, and then it becomes bits of trash. And in the Hereafter, there will be severe punishment or forgiveness from Allah and (His) great pleasure. And the life of this world is nothing but the enjoyment of delusion.

So race towards forgiveness from your Lord and Paradise, as vast as the heavens and the earth. (It is) prepared for those who believe in Allah and His messengers. That is Allah’s grace, which He grants to whomsoever He pleases. And Allah is the Possessor of the Tremendous Grace.
No calamity befalls the earth or yourselves, but it is (recorded) in a Book before We make it happen. Indeed, that is easy for Allah.
(It is) so that you may not grieve for what has escaped you nor boast for what has come to you. And Allah does not like whoever is arrogant (and) boastful.

(Such as) those who are miserly (themselves) and tell (other) people to be miserly. And whosoever turns away (from Allah’s injunctions should know that) Allah is indeed the All-Sufficient, the All-Praised.

Indeed, We sent Our messengers with clear proofs, and We sent down the Book and the Balance with them so that people could uphold justice. And We sent down iron, in which there is great might and benefits for humanity. So that Allah may make known those who help Him and His messengers without seeing (Him). Indeed, Allah is All-Strong, All-Mighty.
Indeed, We sent Nuh (Noah) and Ibrahim (Abraham) and established prophethood and the Book among their descendants. Some of them are rightly guided, yet many of them are transgressors.

Then We followed them up with Our messengers, and afterwards We sent ‘Isa, son of Maryam (Jesus, son of Mary), and We gave him the Injil (the Gospel). And We instilled kindness and mercy into the hearts of those who followed him. As for monasticism, they innovated it (themselves). We did not ordain it for them, but (they invented it) only to seek Allah’s pleasure. Yet they did not observe it with proper observance. So We gave to the believers among them their (due) reward. But many of them are transgressors.

O believers! Be God-conscious and have faith in His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him). He will grant you a double share of His mercy, provide light to help you walk (in this world and the Hereafter) and forgive you. Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.

So that the People of the Book may know that they have no power over anything of Allah’s grace and that (all) grace is in Allah’s Hand. He grants it to whomever He pleases. And Allah is the Possessor of Tremendous Grace.
Indeed, Allah has heard the speech of the woman who disputes with you about her husband and complains to Allah. Allah hears the conversation of you both. Indeed, Allah is All-Hearing, All-Seeing.

Those among you who repudiate their wives (by zihar, saying to them: ‘You are to me like my mother’s back,’ they should know that) their wives are in no way their mothers. Their mothers are only the ones who gave birth to them. Indeed, they utter dishonourable words and falsehood. Surely, Allah is All-Pardoning, Most-Forgiving.

Those who divorce their wives by likening their backs to their mothers’ backs (i.e. zihar), then wish to retract what they have said, must free a captive before they touch each other. That is what you are advised to do. And Allah is All-Aware of what you do.

And whosoever does not find the means shall fast for two consecutive months before they touch each other. And whosoever is unable (to do so) shall feed sixty needy persons. It is so that you may believe in Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him). These are the bounds set by Allah. And there is a painful punishment for the disbelievers.

Those who oppose Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) shall be disgraced as those before them were disgraced. And indeed, We have sent down clear revelations, and there is a humiliating punishment for the disbelievers.
The Day when Allah resurrects them all together, He will inform them about what they have done. Allah has kept account of it while they have forgotten it. And Allah is a Witness over all things.

Do you not see that Allah knows whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth? There is no secret conversation among the three, but He is their fourth; nor among the five, but He is their sixth; nor less than that, nor more, but He is with them wherever they may be. Then, He will inform them about what they did on the Day of Resurrection. Indeed, Allah is All-Knowing of everything.

Have you not seen those (Jews and hypocrites) who were forbidden from the secret conversation, but they return to what they were forbidden from? (They are constantly engaged in) conspiring for sin, revolt and disobedience to the Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him)? Whenever they come to you (O Prophet), they greet you in a manner that Allah has not greeted you and say to themselves: ‘(If this Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) is truthful,) then why does Allah not punish us for what we say?’ Hell is enough for them; they will burn in it. And it is an evil destination!

O believers! When you converse privately, do not converse for sin, enmity or disobedience to the Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him). You may talk secretly (in a spirit of) virtuousness and God-consciousness. And be God-conscious towards Whom you shall be gathered.

Indeed, the (malicious) secret conversation is inspired by Satan so that he may upset the believers. But he cannot harm them in the least, except by Allah’s will. And the believers always put their trust in Allah.

O believers! When you are told: ‘Make room for one another in (collective) gatherings,’ do make room; Allah will make room for you (by His Grace). And when you are told: ‘Move up!’ Then move up. Allah will elevate those of you who are believers and (raise) those gifted with knowledge in ranks. And Allah is All-Aware of what you do.

O believers! When you consult the Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) privately, offer some charity (to the needy) before your private consultation. That is better for you and purer. But if you do not find (anything to give in charity), then Allah is surely Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.

Are you worried about charitable offerings before you come to speak privately (with the Messenger [blessings and peace be upon him])? Since you could not do it, Allah has mercifully turned to you. So establish the prayer, pay the alms due and obey Allah and His Messenger ([blessings and peace be upon him] persistently). And Allah is All-Aware of what you do.

Have you not seen those who become intimate friends with a people with whom Allah is wrathful? They are neither from you nor (are you) from them. And they knowingly swear false oaths (claiming to be with you).
Allah has prepared a severe punishment for them. Evil indeed is what they used to do.
They take their (false) oaths as a shield and bar (others) from the way of Allah. So, there shall be a humiliating punishment for them.
Neither their properties nor their children will avail them against Allah in the least. They shall be the inmates of the Fire, and they shall remain in it forever.
The Day Allah resurrects them all together, they will swear to Him, just like they swear to you (now), supposing they have some standing. Beware! They are indeed liars!
Satan has gained control over them and has caused them to forget the remembrance of Allah. They are the party of Satan. Know! Indeed, it is the party of Satan who are the losers.
Indeed, those who oppose Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) will be among the most abased.
Allah has decreed: ‘I and My messengers will surely prevail.’ Indeed, Allah is All-Strong, All-Mighty.

You shall never find a people believing in Allah and the Last Day loving those who oppose Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him), even if they were their parents, their children, their brothers or their families. They are the ones whom He has inscribed faith into their hearts and strengthened them with a spirit from Him. And He will admit them into Gardens with rivers flowing beneath them, to remain there forever. Allah is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him. They are the party of Allah. Know! The party of Allah is indeed prosperous.
Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth glorifies Allah, and He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.

He is the One Who expelled the disbelievers of the People of the Book (i.e. the tribe of Banu al-Nadir) from their homes at the outset of the first banishment (because of their treason against the state). You never thought they would go; they thought their strongholds would defend them against Allah. But (the decree of) Allah came upon them from where they did not expect, and He cast terror into their hearts. They demolished their houses with their own hands and the hands of the believers. So take a lesson (from it)! O people of vision!
Had Allah not decreed exile for them, He would have surely punished them (more severely) in this world. And in the Hereafter, there is the punishment of the Fire for them.
That is because they acted in (treacherous) opposition to Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him). And whoever works in opposition to Allah, indeed Allah is severe in retribution.
Whatever palm trees you cut down or left standing on their roots (during the siege of Banu Nadir), it was (all) by Allah’s will so that He might disgrace the wrongdoers.

(O believers!) The spoils of war that Allah has returned to His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) from them, you did not spur any horse or ride any camel for its sake. But Allah gives authority to His messengers over whomever He wills. And Allah is All-Capable of everything.

The spoils of war that Allah has returned to His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) from the people of the towns* belong to Allah and the Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) and the (Messenger’s) near relatives, the orphans, the needy and the wayfarers (at large), so that (material wealth) does not circulate (only) among your wealthy. Whatsoever the Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) gives you, take it, and abstain from whatsoever he forbids you. Be God- conscious. Indeed, Allah is Severe in retribution. [* These include the towns of Qurayza, Nadir, Fadak, Khaybar, ‘Urayna and other towns captured without fighting.]

(These spoils of war are also) for the poor emigrants who were expelled from their homes and properties, seeking Allah’s grace and pleasure and helping Allah and His Messenger. It is they who are truthful.

(These spoils are also) for those (helpers, i.e. the Ansar) who were (already) firmly settled in the land (of Medina) and (firmly rooted) in faith before (the arrival of the emigrants). They love whoever migrated to them, never finding any need in their hearts for whatever (of the gains) is given to the emigrants. They give preference (to others) over themselves, even though they may be in dire need. And those saved from the greed of their souls, it is they who are prosperous.

And those who came after them say: ‘Our Lord! Forgive us and (forgive) our brothers who preceded us in faith, and do not put any malice in our hearts for the believers. Our Lord! Indeed, You are Most-Gracious, All-Merciful.’

Have you not seen those who have become hypocrites? They say to their brothers—the disbelievers among the People of the Book: ‘If you are expelled (from here), we will surely go out with you. And we will never obey anyone against you. And if you are fought, we will surely help you.’ Allah bears witness that they are certainly liars.

If they were expelled, these (hypocrites) would never leave with them. And if they were fought, they would never help them. Were they to help them, they would surely turn their backs (to flee), and then they would not be supported (from anywhere).
(O Believers!) Indeed, there is more fear in their hearts for you than for Allah. That is because they are a people who do not comprehend.

They (i.e. the treacherous Jews of Medina and the hypocrites) will not fight against you (even allied) altogether, except in fortified townships or from behind walls. There is severe hostility between them. You think they are united, but their hearts are divided. That is because they are a people who do not understand,

like those who, shortly before them, tasted the evil consequences of their conduct.* There is a painful punishment for them (in the Hereafter). [* This verse refers to the idolaters of Mecca at Badr and the Banu Nadir, Banu Qaynuqa‘ and Banu Qurayza from the Jews who committed an act of treason against the state by breaching the Treaty of Medina.]
(The example of the hypocrites is) like Satan when he says to human being: ‘Disbelieve’, but when he disbelieves, he says: ‘I am absolved of you. Surely, I fear Allah, the Lord of all worlds.’
The fate of both is that they will be in the Hellfire, remaining there forever. Such is the requital of the wrongdoers.
O believers! Be God-conscious. And let every soul consider what it has sent forth for tomorrow. And (always) remain God-conscious. Indeed, Allah is All-Aware of what you do.
Do not be like those who forget Allah, so He makes them forget themselves. It is they who are the transgressors.
The inmates of the Hellfire cannot be equal to the residents of Paradise. It is the residents of Paradise who will be victorious.
Had We sent this Qur’an down upon a mountain, you would have seen it humbled (to pieces and) torn asunder by the fear of Allah. We offer such illustrations for people, so perhaps they may reflect.
He is Allah, besides Whom there is no god, the Knower of the Unseen and the seen. He is the Most-Compassionate, the All-Merciful.

He is Allah, besides Whom there is no god: the All-Sovereign, the Most Holy, the All-Perfect, the Securer, the Guardian (watching all), the All-Mighty, the Most Supreme (and) the All-Magnanimous. Glorified is Allah from whatever they associate with Him (in worship).
He is Allah, the Creator, the Originator (and) the Shaper. To Him belong the Most Beautiful Names. Whatever is in the heavens and the earth glorifies Him. And He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.

60.e: She that is to be examined

v.t: O believers! Do not take My enemy and your enemy as trusted allies, conveying to them (secret news of the Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him) and the believers) because of your intimate affection (with them). (In contrast,) they have rejected the truth that has come to you, expelling the Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) and you (from your homeland) simply because you believe in Allah, your Lord. If you have emigrated (from Mecca), striving in My path and seeking My good pleasure (then how can you take them as trusted allies)? You secretly disclose (the believers’ news) to them because of your intimate affection, while I know best whatever you conceal and whatever you reveal. And whoever among you does this has certainly strayed from the right way.* [* This verse was reportedly revealed concerning Hatib b.
