Abi Balta‘a, who sent a letter to the Quraysh of Mecca that was later intercepted by the efforts of some Companions. This letter contained information about the Prophet’s plan of marching upon Mecca. During the interrogation, he told the Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him) that he did it because he had allied with the Quraysh to obtain assistance from them. Hence, this commandment prohibited choosing an alliance with the disbelievers over the alliance of the Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him) and the believers.]

If they get (a chance) to prevail over you, they will be your (open) enemies and stretch out their hands and their tongues against you with evil intentions. And they eagerly desire that you renounce your faith.
Your relatives and your children will not benefit you. On the Day of Resurrection, He will distinguish between you (all), and Allah is All-Seeing of what you do.

Indeed, there is an excellent example for you in Ibrahim (Abraham) and those with him when they said to their people: ‘We disown you and whatever (idols) you worship besides Allah. We have rejected you all. And enmity and hatred have appeared between you and us forever until you believe in Allah alone.’ The only exception was Ibrahim’s (Abraham’s) saying to his (foster) father: ‘I will surely seek forgiveness for you, though I cannot protect you from (the punishment of) Allah.’ (The believers said:) ‘Our Lord! In You (alone), we trust. To You (alone), we turn penitently. And towards You (alone) is the final destination.
Our Lord! Do not make us a trial for (the persecution of) the disbelievers, and forgive us. Our Lord! Indeed, You are the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.’

Indeed, there is an excellent example in them for you (to adopt) for whoever looks forward to Allah and the Last Day. But whoever turns away (should know that) Allah is the All-Sufficient, the All-Praiseworthy.
Allah may still bring about affection between you and those among them whom you (presently) hold as enemies. And Allah is All-Capable, and Allah is All-Forgiving, All-Merciful.

Allah does not forbid you, regarding those who have not fought you on account of (your) religion, nor expelled you from your homes, from treating them kindly and being just towards them. Indeed, Allah loves those who are just.

Allah only forbids you to take those as allies who fought you because of (your) religion and expelled you from your homes or supported (others) in your expulsion. Whoever takes them as trusted friends (or allies), they are the wrongdoers.

O believers! When believing women come to you as emigrants, test their intentions. Allah knows best (the state of) their faith. Then, if you ascertain them to be faithful women, do not send them back to the disbelievers. These women are not lawful for those (idolators), nor are they lawful for them. And repay the disbelievers whatever (dowries) they have paid (to these believing women). There is no sin upon you in marrying them when you have given them their bridal gifts. Do not hold to the (marital) ties with disbelieving women. And demand (the disbelievers for repayment of) whatever you have spent (upon them as dowries), and let the disbelievers demand you for whatever they have spent. That is Allah’s judgement; He judges between you. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

If any of your wives deserts you, going over to the disbelievers, and later you acquire the spoils of war (from them), then pay those (idolators) whose wives have deserted them the equivalent of whatever (dowry) they had paid. And be God-conscious in Whom you are believers.

O Prophet! When believing women come to you, pledging to you that they will not associate anything with Allah (in worship), nor steal, nor commit adultery, nor kill their children, nor falsely attribute (any illegitimate) child to their husbands, nor disobey you in whatever is honourable, then accept their allegiance and plead to Allah for their forgiveness. Indeed, Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.
O believers! Do not take a people as trusted friends (or allies) with whom Allah is wrathful. They have lost hope of the Hereafter, just like the disbelievers inhabiting the graves had lost hope.
Whatever is in the heavens glorifies Allah, and whatever is on the earth, and He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.
O believers! Why do you say what you do not do?
It is most hateful to Allah that you say what you do not do.
Allah loves those who fight in His way (for the cause of truth) in ranks as if they were a well-compact structure.

And (remember) when Musa (Moses) said to his people: ‘O my people! Why do you hurt me while you already know that I am Allah’s Messenger to you?’ So when they continued deviating, Allah caused their hearts to deviate (permanently from the truth). And Allah does not guide the transgressing people.

And when ‘Isa, the son of Maryam (Jesus, the son of Mary), said: ‘O Children of Israel! I am Allah’s Messenger to you, confirming the Torah that came before me, and bearing the glad tidings of a Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) who will come after me, whose name (in the heavens) is Ahmad.’ Yet when he (i.e. the promised Messenger) came to them with clear proofs, they said: ‘This is plain magic.’
Who is a greater wrongdoer than the one who fabricates lies against Allah while being invited to Islam? And Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.

They (i.e. the deniers of the truth) desire to extinguish the Light of Allah with their mouths, but Allah shall bring His Light to (complete) perfection, though the disbelievers may intensely dislike it.
He is the One Who sent His Messenger with Guidance and the Religion of Truth so that He may elevate it over all religions, though the polytheists may dislike it.
O believers! Shall I guide you to a trade that will deliver you from a painful punishment?

(It is that) you believe in Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) and struggle hard in Allah’s way with your possessions and your persons. That would be better for you if only you knew.
He shall forgive you your sins, admit you into Gardens with rivers flowing beneath them and (make you reside) in pleasant dwellings in the Gardens of Eternity. That is the ultimate success.

And (He will also grant you) other (blessings) you long for: (that is,) help from Allah and an imminent victory. So give glad tidings to the believers.* [* It refers to the Conquest of Mecca and the victories over the Persians and Byzantines.]

O believers! Be helpers (in the cause) of Allah, as ‘Isa, the son of Maryam (Jesus, the son of Mary), said to the disciples: ‘Who are my helpers in the cause of Allah?’ The disciples replied: ‘We are (your) helpers (in the cause) of Allah.’ Then, a faction of the Children of Israel believed, and a faction disbelieved. So, We strengthened the believers against their enemy, and they came to prevail.
Whatever is in the heavens glorifies Allah, and whatever is on the earth, the Sovereign, the Most Holy, the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.

He is the One Who sent among the unlettered people a Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) from among themselves, who recites His revelations to them, purifies them (spiritually) and teaches them the Book and wisdom. Indeed, they had previously been in clear error,
along with others among them who have not yet joined them. And He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.
That is Allah’s grace. He grants it to whomever He pleases. And Allah is the Lord of Mighty Grace.

The example of those entrusted with the Torah but failed to carry this (trust) is like that of a donkey carrying books. How evil is the example of the people who reject Allah’s revelations? And Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.
Say: ‘O Jews! If you claim that you are Allah’s chosen friends, leaving all other people aside, then long for death, if you are truthful.’
Yet they will never long for it because of what (deeds) their hands have sent forth. And Allah is All-Knowing of the wrongdoers.
Say: ‘Indeed, the death you are running away from will surely encounter you. Then you will be returned to the Knower of the Unseen and the seen, and He will inform you of what you used to do.’

O believers! When the call to prayer is made on the Day of Congregation (i.e. Friday), proceed hastily to the remembrance of Allah and leave (all) business. That would be better for you if only you knew.
After the prayer has ended, disperse in the land and seek Allah’s grace. And remember Allah frequently so you may be successful.

When they observed a (caravan with) trade or entertainment, they rushed towards it (out of dire need) and left you standing (there, during the sermon). Say: ‘Whatever is with Allah is far better than amusement and trade. And Allah is the Best of providers.’* [* This verse refers to an incident when a trade caravan arrived in Medina from Syria. People suffering from financial difficulties left the mosque during the Friday sermon to fulfil their needs. After this incident, the Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him) adopted the practice of delivering the sermon before the congregational prayer, which has continued to this day.]

When the hypocrites come to you (O Prophet!), they say: ‘We bear witness that you certainly are the Messenger of Allah.’ And Allah knows that you are indeed His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him), but Allah bears witness that the hypocrites are surely liars.
They take their (false) oaths as a shield, and then they hinder (others) from the way of Allah. Evil indeed is what they used to do.
That is because they believed and then abandoned (their) faith. So their hearts were sealed. Hence, they do not understand.

When you see them, their appearance impresses you, and if they speak, you listen to their speech, but they are (just) like dried timbers leaning (against a wall). They think every cry (they hear) is against them. They are the enemy, so beware of them. May Allah destroy them. How perverted they are!

And when it is said to them: ‘Come! So Allah’s Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) may seek forgiveness for you,’ they turn their heads aside (rudely), and you see them turning away in arrogance.

It makes no difference for them whether you seek forgiveness for them or do not seek forgiveness for them: Allah will never forgive them (for they mock you and behave arrogantly towards you). Indeed, Allah does not guide the rebellious people.

They are the ones who say: ‘Do not spend (anything) on those who remain (as devotees) in the presence of the Messenger of Allah until they break away (from him).’ And to Allah belong the treasures of the heavens and the earth, but the hypocrites do not understand.

They say: ‘If we return to Medina, then (we) the honourable will surely expel the dishonourable (i.e. the Muslims) from it.’ But all honour belongs to Allah, His Messenger and the believers. However, the hypocrites do not understand.
O believers! Do not let your possessions or your children divert you from the remembrance of Allah. For whoever does that, it is they who are the losers.

Spend (in charity) from whatever We have provided you before death comes upon one of you, and he says: ‘O my Lord! If only You would grant me respite for a while so I could give charity and be among the righteous?’
Allah shall never grant respite to a soul when its appointed time (of death) has arrived. And Allah is All-Aware of what you do.
Whatever is in the heavens glorifies Allah, and whatever is on the earth. To Him belongs (all) sovereignty, and to Him belongs (all) praise. And He is All-Capable of everything.
He is the One Who created you, yet some of you are disbelievers, while others among you are believers. And Allah is All-Seeing of what you do.
He is the One Who created the heavens and the earth with a real purpose. And He shaped you (in the wombs), then perfected your forms. And towards Him is the final return.
He knows whatever is in the heavens and the earth. And He knows whatever you conceal and whatever you reveal. And Allah is All-Knowing of whatever is (hidden) in the breasts.
Has there not come to you the story of those who disbelieved before? They tasted the evil consequences of their conduct, and there shall be a painful punishment for them.

That was because their messengers used to bring them clear proofs, but they said: ‘Shall a human being guide us?’ So they disbelieved and turned away. And Allah is not in need (of their faith). And Allah is Self-Sufficient, All-Praised.
The disbelievers claim that they will not be resurrected. Say: ‘Yes indeed, by my Lord! You will surely be resurrected. Then, you will surely be informed of what you did. And that is easy for Allah.’
So believe in Allah, His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) and the Light We have sent down. And Allah is All-Aware of what you do.

The Day when He will gather you (all), for the Day of (the Great) Gathering, that will be the Day of Mutual Loss and Gain. And whoever believes in Allah and acts righteously, He will absolve him of his misdeeds and admit him into Gardens with rivers flowing beneath them, to abide there forever. That is the ultimate success.
Those who disbelieve and reject Our revelations shall be the inmates of the Fire, to abide there forever. What a miserable destination!
No calamity befalls (anyone) except by Allah’s Will. And whoever believes in Allah, He guides his heart (through adversity). And Allah is All-Knowing of everything.

Obey Allah and obey the Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him). But if you turn away, (then know that) Our Messenger’s (blessings and peace be upon him) responsibility is only the clear proclamation (of Our message).
Allah (is the One God). There is no god except Him. So, in Allah (alone) should all the believers trust.

O believers! You (can) have some enemies, even among your spouses and children. So beware of them! Yet if you pardon, overlook and forgive (their faults), then Allah is indeed Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.
Your possessions and your children are only a trial. But with Allah (alone), there is a great reward (for you).

So be God-conscious to the best of your ability. And listen (to Him) and obey (Him) and spend (in charity). (That is) better for your souls. Those saved from the selfishness of their souls, it is they who will be prosperous.
If you lend Allah a generous loan, He will multiply it for you and forgive you. And Allah is Most-Appreciative, All-Forbearing;
(He is) the Knower of the Unseen and the seen, the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.

O Prophet! (Say to the believers:) ‘When you (intend to) divorce women, divorce them considering their waiting period, and count accurately the waiting period. Be God-conscious, your Lord. Do not force them out of their homes, nor shall they leave (themselves), unless they commit a flagrant indecency. These are the limits set by Allah. And whoever transgresses the limits set by Allah certainly wrongs himself. You never know; perhaps Allah may bring some new situation afterwards (that leads to a reunion).

So when they have (almost) reached the end of their waiting period, either retain them honourably or separate from them honourably. And take two of your just persons as witnesses and uphold the testimony for the sake of Allah. (Compliance of) this (command) is advised to whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day. And whosoever is God-conscious, He will make a way out for him,

and provide for him from where he cannot imagine. And whoever puts his trust in Allah, He is Sufficient for him. Indeed, Allah carries through His Command. Allah has already set a measure for everything.

As for those of your women who have no hope for menstruation, in case you doubt (regarding their term), their waiting period shall be three months, and those (as well) who have not yet menstruated. As for the pregnant women, their term shall be until they deliver. And whoever is God-conscious, He shall grant him ease in his affairs.
That is Allah’s command, which He has sent down to you. And whoever is God-conscious, He will absolve him of his misdeeds and honour him with a great reward.

(During their waiting period,) house them where you live, according to your means. And do not harass them to make their stay unbearable. And if they are pregnant, then spend upon them until they deliver. Then, if they are suckling (the child) for you (after the divorce is finalised), give them their wages and consult together in an honourable way. But if you struggle to deal with each other, another woman will suckle (the child) for him.

Let the wealthy man spend according to his means. As for the one with limited resources, let him provide according to whatever Allah has given him. Allah does not task any soul but (according to the capacity) that He has given it. Allah will bring ease after hardship.

How many a town rebelled against the command of its Lord and His messengers? Then, We called it to a severe account and punished it with a horrible torment (the like of which had not been seen before).
So it tasted the evil consequences of its conduct, and the end of its affair was a total loss.

Allah has (also) prepared for them a severe punishment (in the Hereafter). So be God-conscious, O possessors of intellect, who have believed! Indeed, Allah has sent down to you a Reminder (i.e. the Qur’an),

a Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) reciting to you the elucidative verses of Allah, that he may bring those who believe and perform righteous deeds out of darkness into light. And whoever believes in Allah and acts righteously, He will admit him into Gardens with rivers flowing beneath them, to abide in them forever. Indeed, Allah has granted him an excellent provision.

Allah is the One Who created seven heavens and the earth (with a number) similar to them. The (Divine) Command descends gradually between them, so you may realise that Allah is All-Capable of everything. And Allah has surely encompassed all things in (His) Knowledge.
O Prophet! Why do you prohibit (yourself from) what Allah has made lawful to you, (kindly) desiring the good pleasure of your wives? And Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.
(O believers!) Indeed, Allah has already ordained for you the (method of) absolution of your oaths. And Allah is your Guardian, and He is the All-Knowing, the All-Wise.

And (remember) when the Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him) had once said something secretly to one of his wives. But when she disclosed it (to another wife), and Allah made it known to him (blessings and peace be upon him), then he told (her) some part of it and held back the rest. So when he (blessings and peace be upon him) informed her of it, she said: ‘Who informed you about this?’ He (blessings and peace be upon him) said: ‘The All-Knowing, the All-Aware has informed me.’

(It will be better) if you both repent to Allah because your hearts have inclined (together towards the same thing). But if you collaborate against him, Allah Himself is surely his Guardian, and are Jibril (Gabriel) and the righteous among the believers. Moreover, the angels are (also) his supporters.

It may be that if he divorces you, his Lord will give him wives better than yourselves. (They will also be) submissive, faithful, devoutly obedient, penitent, dedicated to worship and fasting, previously married and virgins.

O believers! Protect yourselves and your families from a Fire whose fuel is people and stones. It is overseen by severe and mighty angels who never disobey whatever Allah commands them and carry out what they are commanded (to do).
(It will be said:) ‘O disbelievers! Do not make excuses today. You are only being repaid for what you used to do.’

O believers! Turn to Allah in sincere repentance. Perhaps your Lord shall absolve you of your misdeeds and admit you into the Gardens with rivers flowing beneath them. That Day, Allah will not disgrace the Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him) and the believers with him. Their light will shine, spreading before them and on their right. They will say: ‘Our Lord, complete our light for us and forgive us! Surely, You are All-Capable of everything.’
O Prophet! Struggle against the disbelievers and the hypocrites and be firm with them. Their refuge is Hell, and it is an evil destination.

Allah has given an example for the disbelievers: The wife of Nuh (Noah) and the wife of Lut (Lot). They both were under two of Our righteous servants, but they betrayed them. So, they did not benefit them against Allah in any way. It was said (to them): ‘Enter the Fire (the both of you) along with (other) incomers.’* [* Noah’s wife (Wahila) did not believe in him; she reportedly used to say that Noah was a madman. Lot’s wife (Wa‘ila) did not believe in him either; she reportedly told the disbelievers of Sodom the whereabouts of Lot’s guests. So, they both were punished along with other disbelievers.]

Allah has given an example for the believers: The wife of Pharaoh, when she said: ‘My Lord! Build for me a house in Paradise near Your presence and deliver me from Pharaoh and his (evil) conduct. Save me from the wrongdoing people.’* [* The wife of Pharaoh was Asiya, the daughter of Mazahim. She was righteous despite being married to the greatest of tyrants. Therefore, she benefitted from her righteousness and became one of the four most blessed women of Paradise.]

And (there is also the example of) Maryam (Mary), the daughter of ‘Imran, who guarded her chastity well. So We breathed in her (womb) through Our Spirit (Gabriel). She confirmed the Words of her Lord and His (revealed) Scriptures, and she was one of the devoutly obedient.
Blessed is the One in Whose Hand lies all sovereignty, and He is All-Capable of everything.
(He is) the One Who created death and life that He may test you (as to) which of you is best in conduct, and He is the All-Mighty, the Most-Forgiving.
The One Who created seven heavens in layers, one above the other. You will never see any disproportion (or defect) in the creation of the Most-Compassionate (Lord). Look again! Do you see any cracks?
Then look again and again (inquisitively). (Your) sight will turn back to you exhausted and defeated.

We have adorned the lowest heaven with lamps (i.e. stars, planets, comets, asteroids, etc.). And We have made (some of) them missiles against the devils (to eliminate the harmful effects of destructive forces). And We have prepared for them a punishment of the Blaze.
For those who disbelieve in their Lord will be the punishment of Hell, and it is an evil destination.
When they are thrown into it, they will hear it blaring as it boils over,
almost exploding with rage. Every time a group is thrown in it, its keepers will ask them: ‘Did no warner come to you?’
They will say: ‘Yes, a warner did come to us, but we denied (him) and said: ‘Allah has not revealed anything. You are only in great error’.’
And they will say: ‘If only we had listened (to the warner) or used reason (to understand the truth), we would not be among the inmates of the Blaze.’
Thus, they will confess their sins. So away with the inmates of the Blaze!
Those who fear their Lord without seeing (Him), there will be forgiveness for them and a great reward.
(Whether) you speak secretly or loudly, indeed, He is All-Knowing of what is (hidden) in the breasts.
Does He Who created (everything) not know? He is the All-Attentive, the All-Aware.
He is the One Who made the earth subservient to you. So, travel in its (different) routes and eat from His provision. And towards Him is the Resurrection.
Do you feel secure that the One in Heaven will not cause the earth to swallow you up when it quakes violently?
Or do you feel secure that the One in Heaven will not unleash a storm of stones upon you? You shall soon (come to) know how (severe) My Warning is!
And surely those before them had (also) denied; then how (terrible) was My Rebuttal!

Have they not seen the birds above them expanding and contracting their wings? Nothing can hold them (from falling) except (the command of) the Most-Compassionate (Lord). Indeed, He is All-Seeing of everything.
Or who is it that could be a force for you to help you besides the Most-Compassionate (Lord)? The disbelievers are only (lost) in delusion.
Or who is it that could provide for you if He withholds His sustenance? Instead, they persist in defiance and aversion.
Is the one who crawls upon his face better guided or the one who walks upright on a straight path?
Say: ‘He is the One Who created you and endowed you with ears, eyes and hearts. (Yet) you hardly give any thanks.’
Say: ‘He is the One Who scattered you throughout the earth, and to Him you shall be gathered.’
They say: ‘When will this promise come to pass if you are truthful?’
Say: ‘(Its) knowledge is only with Allah. And I am just a manifest Warner.’
But when they shall see it quite near, the faces of the disbelievers will become darkened (and deformed), and it will be said (to them): ‘This is what you were calling for.’
Say: ‘Tell me, whether Allah destroys me and those with me or shows mercy to us, but who will shelter the disbelievers from a painful punishment?’
Say: ‘He is the Most-Compassionate (Lord); we believe in Him, and upon Him do we trust. You will soon know who is in obvious error.’
Say: ‘Tell me, if your water were to sink deep (into the earth), then who would bring you flowing water?’
Nun. By the pen, and whatever they (the angels) write down!
(O Prophet!) By the Grace of your Lord, you are not insane.
And most surely, you shall have an unceasing reward.
And indeed, you are upon an exalted moral character.
You will see, and they will see,
which one of you is afflicted (with madness).
Indeed, your Lord knows best who strays from His way, and He knows best who are rightly guided.
So do not yield to the deniers.
They wish you to compromise (with them) so they can compromise (with you).
So do not listen to any disgraceful swearer,
(or to any) defamer (and slanderer), scandalmonger, mischief spreader (and backbiter),
(or) forbidder of the good, sinful transgressor,
(or) ignoble, and on top of that, baseborn (i.e. an illegitimate child),
simply because he has wealth and children.
When Our Verses are recited to him, he says: ‘(These are) the fictitious stories of the ancients.’
Soon, We shall brand him on (his) trunk-like nose.
Indeed, We have tested them (i.e. the inhabitants of Mecca) as We tested the owners of a (particular) garden (in Yemen) when they swore they would harvest (all) its fruit in the early morning.
They did not make any exception (for Allah’s will or by setting aside a portion for the poor).
Then, your Lord’s visitation (of a disaster) struck that (garden) while they were asleep.
So, that (garden) became like a harvested field.
At dawn, they called out to one another:
‘Leave early to your field if you want to pick the fruits.’
So they set off whispering to one another:
‘Do not let any poor person enter your garden today.’
So they proceeded early in the morning, (considering themselves) competent to execute their plan.
When they saw it (ruined), they said: ‘We must have lost our way.
No! We have indeed been deprived (of our livelihood).’
The most sensible of them said: ‘Did I not tell you: ‘Why do you not glorify (Allah)?’’
(Then) they said: ‘Glory be to our Lord! We have surely been wrongdoers!’
Then they turned to one another, blaming each other.
They said: ‘Woe to us! Indeed, we have been rebellious.
Perhaps our Lord will give us a better one in its place. We are indeed turning to our Lord with hope.’
Such is the punishment (of this world). But the punishment of the Hereafter is far greater if only they knew.
Indeed, for the God-conscious, the Gardens of Bliss will be with their Lord.
Shall We then treat the submitters like the guilty?
What is the matter with you? How do you judge?
Do you have a scripture in which you read:
that you shall have in it (i.e. the next world) whatever you choose?
Or do you have any pledges binding on Us until the Day of Resurrection that you shall have whatever you decide (for yourselves)?
Ask them (O Prophet), which of them shall guarantee that (baseless assertion)!
Or do they have any partners (supporting this assertion)? Then, let them produce their partners if they are truthful.
The Day when the Catastrophic Occurrence (of the Last Hour) is disclosed, they (i.e. the guilty) will be summoned to prostrate, but they will not be able to do so.

Their eyes will be cast down (with shame and terror), and disgrace will cover them (completely). Indeed, they were (always) summoned to prostrate (in the world) while they were healthy (but they chose not to).
So leave Me with those who reject this (Divine) discourse. We shall gradually lead them (to destruction) whence they cannot comprehend.
And I will grant them respite (for some time). Indeed, My scheme is firm.
Do you ask them for a reward (for conveying the message) so they feel burdened with debt?
Do they possess (the knowledge of) the Unseen, so they write it down (for their judgements)?
So submit patiently to the judgement of your Lord, and do not be like the Man of the Whale (Jonah), who called out (to his Lord) while he was in distress.
Had not the blessing reached him from his Lord, he would surely have been thrown upon the open shore and be (left) blameworthy.
Then his Lord chose him and made him among the righteous.
Indeed, the disbelievers almost strike you down with their looks when they hear the Reminder (you recite), and they say: ‘He must be insane.’
But it is just a Reminder for all nations (of the world).
The Undeniable Reality!
What is the Undeniable Reality?
And what will make you realise what the Undeniable Reality is?
Thamud and ‘Ad denied the Catastrophe (i.e. the Day when all cosmic bodies will be destroyed through a gravitational collision).
As for Thamud, they were destroyed by a highly violent crashing blast.
And as for ‘Ad, they were destroyed by a furiously bitter icy wind.
He imposed it upon them consecutively for seven nights and eight days. You would have seen the people lying dead as if they were hollow trunks of uprooted palm trees.
So, do you see any trace of them alive?
Then Pharaoh and those before him and the overthrown towns (of the community of Lut) committed grave sins.
They (also) disobeyed the messenger of their Lord. So He seized them with a terrible crushing grip.
Indeed, when the flood water rose high, We carried you in the floating ship,
so that We make it a reminder for you, and attentive ears may grasp it.
When the Trumpet is blown with a single blast,
and the earth and the mountains are lifted and crushed (into sand particles) with a single blow,
on that Day, the Inevitable event will occur.

The heavenly universe shall split asunder. So, (by losing its force) it will become torn into holes.* [* The word ‘ wahiya ’ means wear and tear of some leather, cloth or something similar with holes. It refers to the phenomenon that modern science has described as ‘black holes’. ( al-Mufradat, Lisan al-‘Arab, Qamus al-Muhit, al-Munjid , etc.)]
And the angels shall be (standing) at its edges. On that Day, eight (mighty angels) will carry the Throne of your Lord above them.
That Day, you will be exposed. None of your secrets will remain hidden.
As for the one given his book in his right hand, he will say (happily): ‘Come here and read my book!
I knew that I would surely face my reckoning.’
So he will have a pleasant life,
in an elevated garden,
whose clusters of fruit will be low-hanging (within easy reach).
(They will be told:) ‘Enjoy your food and drink for what you had sent in advance in the past days.’
But as for the one given his book in his left hand, he will say: ‘I wish I had not been given my book,
nor had I known what my account was!
I wish death had been the decisive end (of all affairs).
My wealth has not benefited me.
My authority has departed from me.’
(The angels will be told:) ‘Seize him and shackle him.
Then put him into Hellfire.
Then tie him up in a chain, seventy arms long.
Indeed, he did not believe in Allah, the Most Magnificent,
nor did he encourage the feeding of the needy.
So today, he has no loyal friend here,
nor any food except filthy pus,
which none shall eat except the evildoers.’
So I swear by whatever you see,
and whatever you do not see.
Indeed, this (Qur’an) is the word spoken by a Noble Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him).
And it is not the word of a poet (as you claim). How little do you believe!
Nor is it the word of a fortune-teller. How little do you reflect!
(It is) a revelation gradually sent down from the Lord of all worlds.
Had he fabricated any sayings about Us,
We would have surely seized him by the right hand,
and then cut off his aorta.
Then none of you could have shielded him (from Us).
Indeed, this (Qur’an) is a reminder for the God-conscious.
And We certainly know that some of you are (its) deniers.
Indeed, it will be a (matter of) regret for the disbelievers.
But most surely, it is the Certain Truth.
So glorify the Name of your Lord, the Most Magnificent.
A questioner asked about the punishment bound to occur,
upon the disbelievers, which none can repel,
from Allah, Lord of the Ascending Ways.
The angels and the Spirit ascend to Him in a Day whose span is fifty thousand (earthly) years.
So endure with graceful patience.
Indeed, they think this (Day) to be distant,
but We see it as near.
On that Day, the sky will be like molten copper,
and the mountains like (flakes of) coloured wool.
And no close friend will ask about his friend,
(although) they will be made to see each other. The guilty will wish to ransom himself from the punishment of that Day at the expense of his children,
and his wife and his brother,
and his close family who had sheltered him,
and all those upon the earth, if it could save him (from the punishment).
But never! Indeed, it is a blazing Fire,
that strips away the whole skin.
It invites whoever turns back and flees (from the truth),
and accumulates wealth and holds it (from distribution).
Indeed, humankind has been created impatient and greedy.
He gets distressed when misfortune afflicts him,
and he becomes miserly when good fortune comes his way,
except for the performers of prayer,
those who consistently observe their prayers,
and those in whose wealth there is a known share,
for the beggar and the deprived,
and those who firmly believe in the Day of Judgement,
and those who are fearful of the punishment of their Lord.
Indeed, the punishment of their Lord is not something to feel safe from.
And those who guard (the chastity of) their private parts,
except with their wives or their slave women, for they are not blameworthy (for them).
But those who seek to go beyond this (limit) are the transgressors.
And those who are faithful in keeping their trusts and covenants,
and those who are firm upholders of their testimonies,
and those who are (consistently) watchful over their prayers,
they shall be in Gardens, held in great honour.
What is the matter with the disbelievers that they are hastening towards you,
from the right and the left, in crowds (to mock you)?
Does every one of them hope to enter the Garden of Bliss (without possessing faith and performing good deeds)?
Never! Indeed, We have created them from (a substance) they already know.
So I swear by the Lord of all horizons of sunrise and all horizons of sunset that We are Capable,
of replacing them with others better than them. And We cannot be prevented (from doing so).
So leave them occupied with idle talk and to amuse (themselves) until they encounter their Day, which they have been promised (as Retribution),
the Day they come out of the graves, hastening, as if racing towards a goal.
Their (shameful) eyes will be downcast (and) disgrace will overtake them. That is the Day they have been promised.
Indeed, We sent Nuh (Noah) to his people, (saying:) ‘Warn your people before a painful punishment overtakes them.’
He said: ‘O my people! I am a clear warner to you.
Worship Allah, fear Him and obey me.
He will forgive your sins and grant you respite until an appointed time. Indeed, when Allah’s (appointed) time comes, it cannot be deferred if only you knew.’
He said: ‘My Lord! Indeed, I have called my people night and day,
but my calling has only increased them in fleeing further away.

Every time I call them (towards faith) so that You may forgive them, they put their fingers in their ears, cover their heads with their garments, persist (in their disbelief) and act more proudly in their arrogance.
Then I called them openly,
and (again) appealed to them publicly and confided with them secretly.
I said (to them): ‘Seek forgiveness of your Lord. Indeed, He is Most-Forgiving.
He will send clouds upon you with abundant rains,
and He will help you with wealth and sons, provide you with gardens and bless you with rivers.
What is the matter with you that you do not hope for Allah with veneration,
while He has created you through (various) stages?
Have you not seen how Allah has created the seven heavens in layers one above the other?
And (how) He has placed the moon within them as a (reflected source of) light and made the sun a (light emitting radiant) lamp?
Allah caused you to grow from the earth like (the growth of) a plant.* [* It refers to the similarities between the animal and plant kingdoms.]
Then He makes you return to this (earth), and He will bring you out (of it) again.
And Allah has made the earth a vast expanse for you,
so that you may travel through its broad ways.’
Nuh (Noah) said: ‘My Lord! They have disobeyed me, following those (rebellious elites) whose wealth and children have increased them in nothing but loss.
They have devised a tremendous plan (to keep the people astray).
They say: ‘Do not leave your gods at any cost. Do not abandon Wadd or Suwa‘ or Yaghuth or Ya‘uq or Nasr (all of these idols).’
They have already led many (people) astray. So do not increase the wrongdoers except in error.’
Because of their evildoings, they were drowned and admitted into the Fire. So they did not find any helpers for themselves against Allah.
And Nuh (Noah) said: ‘My Lord! Do not leave upon the earth any inhabitant from among the disbelievers.
If You leave them, they will certainly lead Your servants astray, and they will give birth only to vicious sinners (and) staunch disbelievers.

My Lord! Forgive me and my parents, and (forgive) whoever enters my house as a believer, and (all) the believing men and the believing women, and do not increase the wrongdoers except in destruction.’
Say: ‘It has been revealed to me that a group of the jinn listened (to my recitation) and said (to their fellow jinn): ‘Indeed, we have heard a wonderful Qur’an.
It guides towards righteousness. So, we have believed in it, and we will never ascribe any partner to our Lord.
And exalted be the Majesty of our Lord. He has not taken (for Himself) a spouse or a child.
Indeed, the foolish among us used to utter falsehoods about Allah.
And indeed, we had thought that no human or jinn would (dare to) tell a lie about Allah.
Some persons among the humans used to seek refuge with some persons of the jinn, so they further increased in rebellion.
And they (i.e. the humans) thought as you (jinn) thought, that Allah would never raise anyone (from the dead).
We tried to reach heaven (for news) but found it filled with mighty guards and shooting flames.
We used to sit in some of its sitting places to listen, but whoever dares to listen now finds a flame waiting for him.
We do not know whether any evil is intended for those upon the earth or whether their Lord intends any good for them.
Among us, some are righteous, and among us, some are otherwise; we are divided into different paths.
We know that we can never escape Allah upon the earth, nor can we escape Him by flying (off the planet).
When we heard the (message of) guidance, we believed in it. So, whoever believes in his Lord shall neither fear detraction nor oppression.
Some among us have obediently submitted (to Allah), and some among us are unjust deviants. So those who submit (to Allah), it is they who pursue righteousness.
As for the unjust deviants, they will be the fuel of Hell.’
And if they remain steadfast on the path (of Allah), We shall provide them with abundant water to drink,
so We may test them concerning this (blessing). And whoever turns away from the remembrance of his Lord, He will lead him to enter a rigorous punishment.
The places of worship belong to Allah (alone), so do not worship anyone along with Allah.
When the servant of Allah (i.e. Muhammad (blessings and peace be upon him) stood up to worship Him, they (i.e. the idolaters) almost crowded around him.
Say: ‘I worship only my Lord, and I do not associate any partner with Him.’
Say: ‘I do not own the authority to bring you (any) harm or good.’
Say: ‘None can shelter me from Allah, nor can I find any refuge besides Him.

(I have no duty) except to convey (the truth) from Allah and (to communicate) His messages. Whoever disobeys Allah and His Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him), indeed, for him, there will be the Fire of Hell, remaining in it forever.’
When they see what they have been promised, they will know who is weaker regarding helpers and fewer regarding numbers.
Say: ‘I do not know whether what you have been warned about is near or whether my Lord has set a distant time for it.’
(He is) the Knower of the Unseen. He does not disclose His Unseen to anyone,
except to the one whom He chooses as (His) messenger. Then He appoints guards in front of him and behind him,
so that He may ensure that they have indeed communicated the messages of their Lord. And He (already) encompasses all that is with them and keeps account of everything.
O you enfolded (in your cloak)!
Rise (to pray) in the night, but (for) a short while,
half of it or reduce it a little,
or add to it, and recite the Qur’an distinctly in a measured tone.
Indeed, We shall soon send you a substantial Word.
Indeed, the vigil of the night is more impactful in crushing (the lower self) and more suitable for (creating harmony between the heart and) speech.
Indeed, during the day, you are extensively engaged (in your affairs).
Remember the Name of your Lord and dedicate yourself to Him with complete dedication (detaching your heart from everything else).
(He is) the Lord of the East and the West. There is no god but Him, so take Him as (your) Guardian.
Remain patient over what they say, and keep away from them in a graceful manner.
Leave Me with the deniers—(the ones) living in luxury—and give them a little respite (so that their evil deeds may cross the limit).
Surely, We have heavy shackles (for them) and the Blazing Fire,
and choking food and a painful punishment,
on the Day when the earth and the mountains will shake violently, and the mountains will become heaps of shifting sand.
Indeed, We have sent a Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) to you as a witness over you, just as We sent a messenger to Pharaoh.
Then Pharaoh disobeyed the messenger, and We seized him with a terrible grip.
If you disbelieve, how will you guard yourselves against (the torment of) the Day that will turn children grey-headed,
(and) the heaven will split apart? His promise is bound to be fulfilled.
Surely, this (Qur’an) is a Reminder. So whoever wishes, let him take the path towards his Lord.

(O Prophet!) Indeed, your Lord knows that you stand nearly two-thirds of the night (in prayer), and (sometimes) half of it, and (sometimes) a third of it, and a group of those who are with you (also join you in the night vigil). Allah measures the night and the day. He knows that you (believers) cannot endure it. So He has turned to you (in mercy). Therefore, recite (in the prayer) whatever you can from the Qur’an easily. He knows that some of you will be sick while others will travel in the land, seeking Allah’s grace, yet others will fight in the way of Allah. So recite whatever of it is feasible (in the prayer). Establish the prayer, pay the alms due, and lend Allah a generous loan. Whatever good you send forth for yourselves, you will find it with Allah (in a form) that is better and greater in reward. And seek Allah’s forgiveness. Indeed, Allah is Most-Forgiving, All-Merciful.
O you enwrapped (in your cloak)!
Arise and warn (the people).
Magnify your Lord.
Purify your garments,
and (continue as usual to) avoid impurities.
And do not do a favour, expecting a greater return.
And remain patient (as usual) for the sake of your Lord.
When the Trumpet will be sounded,
that Day shall be a day of hardship.
(It is) not at all easy for the disbelievers.
And leave Me (to deal) with the one whom I created Myself,
and then granted him abundant wealth,
and sons ever-present (by his side),
and facilitated all matters for him, making (his life) easy.
Still, he is eager that I should give him more.
No, indeed! He has been stubbornly hostile to Our revelations.
Soon, I will overtake him with unbearable hardship.
Indeed, he reflected and plotted.
So may he perish! How he plotted!
Again, may he perish! How he plotted!
Then he looked (into his scheme again).
Then he frowned and twisted his face.
Then he turned his back and showed arrogance,
and said: ‘This (Qur’an) is nothing but magic transmitted (from the ancients).
This is nothing but the word of a man.’

I shall soon burn him in Saqar (the Fire of Hell).* [* Verses 11–26 refer to one of the staunchest opponents of the Prophet (blessings and peace be upon him), al-Walid b. al-Mughira, who was among the wealthiest men in Mecca and who was also among the most learned and eloquent of the Quraysh. He was a prominent leader of the Meccan opposition against Islam.]
What will make you realise what Saqar is?
It (is the Hellfire which) does not let anyone live nor leave to die.
It burns and blackens the skin.
There are nineteen (guards) over it.

And We have appointed none but angels as the wardens of the Fire. And We have not made their number but as a test for the disbelievers, so that the People of the Book be reassured, and the believers may increase in (their) faith. And those given the Book may not doubt, and the believers as well. And those in whose hearts is a disease (of hypocrisy) may say, and the disbelievers as well: ‘What does Allah intend by this example?’ In this way, Allah leaves whomever He wills to stray and guides whomever He pleases. And none knows (the number of) the forces of your Lord except Him. And this (description of Hell) is only a reminder for all humans.
No, indeed! By the moon!
By the night, when it retreats!
By the morning, when it brightens!
Indeed! It (i.e. Hell) is one of the greatest (calamities),
a warner to all humans,
for those of you who wish to take the lead or those who linger behind (trapped in evil).
Every soul is held in pledge for what it has earned,
except the People of the Right Hand.
(They will be) in Gardens, asking one another,
about the evildoers:
‘What has led you into Saqar (i.e. the Hellfire)?’
They will answer: ‘We were not among those who prayed,
nor did we feed the poor.
We used to gossip with the gossipers (in mocking the believers),
and we used to deny the Day of Judgement,
until the Certain End came upon us.’
So (now), the intercession of the intercessors will not benefit them.
What is the matter with these (disbelievers) that they (still) turn away from the Reminder,
as if they were frightened donkeys,
fleeing from a lion?
Rather, each one of them desires to be given unrolled scriptures (for them to read).
No, indeed! Instead, they do not fear the Hereafter.
No, indeed! This (Qur’an) is a Reminder.
So let anyone who wishes remember it.
And they will not remember (it) unless Allah wills. He is worthy of (your) being conscious (of Him) and worthy of (granting) forgiveness.
I swear by the Day of Resurrection!
And I swear by the self-blaming soul!
Does human being think We shall not reassemble his bones?
Yes, indeed! We are capable of proportioning (even) his fingertips.
Still, human being wants to deny what lies before him.
He asks: ‘When is the Day of Resurrection?’
So when the eyes are dazzled,
and the moon will be eclipsed,
and the sun and the moon are brought together,
on that Day, humans will say: ‘Where is the escape?’
No, indeed! There shall be no refuge.
On that Day, the (final) abode will be towards your Lord.
On that Day, human being will be informed about (the deeds) that he has sent forth and left behind.
Rather, human being shall be a clear witness against himself,
though he should offer his excuses.
Do not rush your tongue with (the recitation of) this (Qur’an) to hasten (in memorising) it.
Indeed, it is upon Us to collect it (in your breast) and to recite it (by your tongue).
So once We have recited it (entirely through Gabriel), follow its recitation.
Then its clear explanation is (also) upon Us.
(O disbelievers!) No, indeed! Instead, you love this transitory life,
and forsake the Hereafter.
Some faces shall be fresh and radiant on that Day,
looking at their Lord (unveiled).
And some faces will be scowling (in desperation) on that Day,
anticipating that they will be treated with a spine-crushing calamity.
No, indeed! When the soul reaches the throat (before it leaves),
and it is said: ‘Who is the healer (to save his life)?’
And he (i.e. the dying person) knows this is the (time of) departure.
And each leg clings to the other leg.
On that day is the driving towards your Lord.
He neither confirmed (the truth) nor prayed,
but persisted in denial and turned away,
then, he went to his family boastfully.
So, woe to you (at the time of death)! Then, woe to you (in the grave)!
Again, woe to you (on the Last Day)! Then, woe to you (in Hell)!
Does human being think he will be left aimless (without reckoning)?

Was he not a fertilised egg (i.e. a zygote*) from a drop of seminal fluid? [* In Arabic, the word ‘ al-nutfa ’ is mentioned, and it refers to a zygote, which is a mixture of male and female gametes. It is the new single cell formed after the union of a sperm and ovum.]
Then he became a clinging mass (in the mother’s womb). Then He created (him in a bodily form with limbs) and proportioned (him) perfectly,
producing two genders from this (zygote): the male and the female.
Is such (a Creator) not capable of reviving the dead?
Indeed, there has been a span of time upon human being when he was not something worth mentioning.

Indeed, We created human being from a drop of mingled fluid (i.e. a zygote*) that We may examine him (from stage to stage). Then, We endowed him with hearing (and) seeing. [* In Arabic, the word ‘ al-nutfa ’ is mentioned, and it refers to a zygote, which is a mixture of male and female gametes. It is the new single cell formed after the union of a sperm and ovum.]
Indeed, We have shown him the way, whether (he chooses to be) grateful or ungrateful.
Indeed, We have prepared for the disbelievers chains, iron collars and a blazing Fire.
The virtuous will have a (pure) drink mixed with camphor.
(Camphor is) a spring (in Paradise) from which the servants of Allah will drink; they will make it to gush out abundantly (and flow like streams wherever they desire).
They fulfil their vows and fear a Day whose horror will be widespread,
and give food for His love (despite their need for it) to the needy, the orphan and the prisoner,
(and say:) ‘We feed you only for the sake of Allah. We do not desire any reward from you, nor thanks.
Indeed, We fear from our Lord the Day that will make (faces) look very dark (and) ugly.’
So Allah has shielded them from the horror of that Day and granted them freshness and radiance (upon their faces) and happiness (in their hearts).
He rewarded them for being patient with a Garden and (garments of) silk.
Reclining there upon couches, they will find in it neither (the severe heat of) the sun nor intense cold.
Its shades will be close over them, and its clusters (of fruit) will be made to hang low.
And silver vessels and shining crystal goblets will be brought around them.
(These goblets will be made of) gleaming crystalline silver, which they will apportion in a precise measure.
They will be served there with a glass of (pure) drink mixed with ginger,
(from) a spring therein called Salsabil.
Immortal youths will serve around them. When you see them, you will think they were scattered pearls.
As you look (at Paradise), you will see bliss (in abundance) and a great kingdom (all around).
Upon them will be garments of fine green silk and rich brocade. And they will be adorned with bracelets of silver. Their Lord will give them to drink a most pure drink.
(They will be told:) ‘This is indeed your reward, and your endeavour is well appreciated.’
Indeed, We have sent down the Qur’an upon you as a piecemeal revelation.
So (continue to) be patient with your Lord’s decree and do not listen to any sinner or disbeliever from among them.
And (always) celebrate the Name of your Lord, morning and evening.
And prostrate before Him, in some part of the night, and glorify Him by night at length.
Indeed, these (disbelievers) love this transitory life and (neglectfully) put a tough day behind them.
We created them and strengthened their physical structure. And when We wish, We can easily replace them with others like them.
This (Qur’an) is indeed a Reminder. So whoever wishes, let him take the (right) path towards his Lord.
But you cannot wish (to do so) unless Allah wills (it). Indeed, Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.
He admits whomever He pleases into His mercy. But as for the wrongdoers, He has prepared a painful punishment for them.
By those (pleasant winds) sent forth successively,
by those (storming winds) blowing violently,
by those (spreading winds) scattering (rain clouds) widely,
by those (discerning winds) splitting (the clouds) apart forcefully,
by the inspirers of the Reminder,
to end excuses or give warnings,
indeed, what you are promised will certainly occur.
So when the stars are made to lose their light,
and when the heavens are split apart,
and when the mountains are blown away (like dust),
and when the time is set for the messengers (to witness),
for what Day has all this been set (to occur)?
For the Day of Judgement!
What will make you realise what the Day of Judgement is?
Woe to the deniers on that Day!
Did We not destroy the earlier generations (of deniers),
and then We will cause the later ones to follow them (in destruction)?
This is how We deal with the guilty.
Woe to the deniers on that Day!
Did We not create you from a (drop of) detestable fluid,
and then placed it in a secure place (i.e. the mother’s womb),
until an appointed period (of time)?
Then We designed (its development in stages). So how Excellent Designers We are!
Woe to the deniers on that Day!
Did We not make the earth an assembling expanse,
(which contains) the living and the dead,
and placed therein lofty (and) firm mountains, and gave you sweet water to drink (from refreshing springs)?
Woe to the deniers on that Day!
(They will be told:) ‘Proceed towards (the Fire) that you used to deny.
Proceed to the shade (of the smoke of Hellfire) that rises in three columns,
which provides neither coolness nor protection from the flames (of the Fire).’
Indeed, it throws up sparks (enormous) like lofty palaces,
as if they were yellow camels (coming out successively in large numbers).
Woe to the deniers on that Day!
This is the Day they will not (be able to) speak,
nor will they be permitted to offer excuses.
Woe to the deniers on that Day!
This is the Day of Judgement. We have gathered you and the earlier generations.
If you have any scheme (left), then scheme against Me.
Woe to the deniers on that Day!
Indeed, the God-conscious will enjoy cool shades and springs,
and fruits, such as they desire.
(They will be told:) ‘Eat and drink joyfully (as a reward) for what you used to do.’
Indeed, this is how We reward the virtuously excellent.
Woe to the deniers on that Day!
(O deniers!) Eat and enjoy yourselves for a short while. You are indeed evildoers.
Woe to the deniers on that Day!
When they are told: ‘Bow down (before Allah),’ they do not bow!
Woe to the deniers on that Day!
So, which discourse will they believe in after this (Qur’an)?
What are they asking one another about?
(Is it) about the Great News,
regarding which they disagree?
No, indeed! They will soon come to know.
Again, no, indeed! They will soon come to know.
Did We not make the earth a resting place,
and (fixed therein) the mountains like metal stakes,
and create you in pairs,
and make your sleep a repose,
and make the night a covering,
and make the day for livelihood,
and build the seven mighty heavens above you,
and place (the sun as) a radiant lamp (i.e. a source of heat and light),
and send down pouring water from the rain clouds,
so that We may produce by it grains and vegetation,
and dense (and) luxuriant gardens?
Indeed, the Day of Judgement is an appointed moment.
(That is) the Day when the Trumpet will be blown, and you all will come out in crowds,
and the heavens will be opened and become (like) gates,
and the mountains (after being reduced to dust) will be blown away and become a mirage.
Indeed, Hell is (lying) in ambush,
a place of return for the rebellious,
to reside there for endless ages.
They will not taste therein any coolness nor drink,
except boiling water and pus (oozing from the wounds of the inmates of Hell),
as a fitting recompense (for their rebellion).
Indeed, they were not anticipating any Reckoning.
They denied Our signs with utter rejection.
And We have everything recorded precisely in a Book.
So taste! We shall increase you in nothing but punishment.
For the God-conscious, there will indeed be success,
(in the form of) gardens and vineyards,
and full-bosomed maidens of matching age,
and overflowing cups (of pure wine).
They will not hear therein any vain talk nor lying,
as a reward from your Lord, a generously befitting bestowal (in proportion to your works),
(from) the Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, the Most-Compassionate (Lord), whom they will not be able to address,
on the Day when the Holy Spirit and the angels stand in rows. They will not speak except whom the Most-Compassionate (Lord) permits and who says what is right.
That is the True Day. So whosoever wishes, let him take the path leading back to his Lord.
We have indeed warned you of an imminent punishment on the Day when (every) person will see what his hands have sent forth, and the disbeliever will say: ‘Oh, I wish if only I had been dust!’

By (the angels) who snatch (the evil souls) violently,* [* A scientific indication can also be inferred from this verse: ‘By the waves of energy that pierce into matter and break up the chemical bonds fiercely.’]

and by (the angels) who draw out (the blessed souls) gently,* [* A scientific indication can also be inferred from this verse: ‘By the waves of energy that disintegrate the chemical bonds within matter gently and peacefully.’]

and by (the angels) who float (in space) swiftly,* [* A scientific indication can also be inferred from this verse: ‘By the waves of energy that move around freely in space and within the atmosphere.’]

and by (the angels) who take the lead, racing vigorously,* [* A scientific indication can also be inferred from this verse: ‘By the waves of energy that surpass other waves in speed, force and assimilation.’]

and then by (the angels) who manage the affairs (of creatures obediently).* [* A scientific indication can also be inferred from this verse: ‘By the waves of energy that, through mutual dynamism and coordination, maintain equilibrium and keep the balance for the survival of the system of the universe.’]
The Day when the quaking Blast quakes violently,
the succeeding Blast will follow it,
the hearts on that Day will be trembling (in horror),
their eyes will be downcast (bearing a terrified look).
They (the disbelievers) say: ‘Will we surely be returned to our earlier state,
even after we have become decayed bones?’
They say: ‘Then, this will be a (great) losing return.’
Yet it will be only a single Mighty Blast.
And behold! Suddenly, they will be upon the vast expanse (of the Day of Resurrection).
Has the story of Musa (Moses) reached you?
When his Lord called out to him in the sacred valley of Tuwa,
(commanding:) ‘Go to Pharaoh, for indeed he has rebelled,
and say (to him): ‘Would you like to purify yourself?
I will guide you to your Lord so that you may fear (Him).’’
Then he showed him the most significant sign.
But he denied and disobeyed.
Then he turned back, swiftly endeavouring (in his scheme against Musa [Moses]).
So he gathered (his people) and called out,
saying: ‘I am your lord, the most exalted!’
So Allah overtook him with an exemplary punishment in the Hereafter and this world.
Surely, in this (incident), there is an admonition for one who fears (Allah).
Are you more difficult to create or (the entire) universe that He has built?
He raised all its heavenly spheres (by expanding them from all sides), then fashioned them perfectly.
Then darkened its night and brought out its daylight.
As for the earth, He separated (it from the solar nebula) and expanded it after that (to make it inhabitable).
He brought out its water from it (separately) and its green pastures (from the rest of the dry regions).
As for the mountains, He raised them firmly (upon it).
(He did all this) as a (means of) sustenance for you and your livestock.
When the great overwhelming catastrophe arrives,
that Day, every human being will remember whatever he has endeavoured.
The Hellfire will be made visible to all who can see.
As for him, who was rebellious,
and preferred the life of this world,
the Hellfire will certainly be (his) refuge.
But as for those who feared standing in the presence of his Lord and restrained (his) soul from evil desires,
indeed, Paradise will be his abode.
They (the disbelievers) ask you about the Hour: ‘When shall it set in?’
What do you have to do with mentioning it?
(Tell them that) towards your Lord is its conclusion.
You are only a Warner for one who fears it.
On the Day they see it, it shall be as if they had stayed (in the world) no more than an evening or forenoon.
He looked displeased and turned (his attention) away,
because the blind man came to him (interrupting his discourse).
What would make you realise? Perhaps he would purify himself (by your attention),
or he would become mindful so that the reminder would benefit him.
As for the one who considers himself beyond need (of faith),
you attend to him earnestly (to make him embrace Islam),
but you are not responsible if he does not purify himself.
As for the one who came to you striving,
while he feared (his Lord),
you were inattentive to him.
No, indeed! These (verses of the Qur’an) are a Reminder.
So let anyone who wishes remember it.
(It is written) in honoured scriptures,
highly esteemed (and) purified,
by the hands of angel scribes,
honourable (and) virtuous.
Perish (the disbelieving) human! How ungrateful he is!
From what substance has He created him?

He has created him from a fertilised egg (i.e. a zygote*), then proportioned him (during his development until the prenatal stage). [* In Arabic, the word ‘ al-nutfa ’ is mentioned, and it refers to a zygote, which is a mixture of male and female gametes. It is the new single cell formed after the union of a sperm and ovum.]
Then He made the way easy for him (to come out of the mother’s womb).
Then (at the end of his life span), He caused him to die and buried him.
Then, whenever He wills, He will resurrect him.
But no! He (the disobedient man) has not yet accomplished what He commanded him.
Then let human being consider his food:
How We poured down water in abundance,
then, We split the earth, tearing (it) asunder,
and cause grains to grow in it,
and grapes and fresh vegetables,
and olives and date palms,
and gardens, densely planted,
and fruits and (animals’) fodder,
as a sustenance for you and your livestock.
So, when the Deafening Blast comes,
the Day when a man flees from his brother,
and his mother and his father,
and his spouse and his children,
on that Day, every person will have such a (stressful) matter that it will make him unconcerned (with everyone else).
(Some) faces, on that Day, will be shining,
laughing (and) rejoicing.
As for (some) faces, there shall be staining dust upon them that Day,
(and) darkness will overcast them.
These are the disbelievers, the wicked evildoers.
When the sun is rolled up (and darkened),
when the stars scatter (and fall from their galaxies),
when the mountains are blown away (after turning to dust),
when the pregnant camels are left unattended,
when the wild beasts are assembled together,
when the seas are set on fire,
when the souls are paired (again with their bodies),
when the female infant, buried alive, is asked,
for what sin was she slain?
When the records (of deeds) are unfolded,
when the heavens are stripped away,
when the Hellfire is set ablaze,
and when Paradise is brought closer,
then each soul will know what (deeds) it has brought along (with it).
So I swear by those (stars and planets) which recede (after appearing),
those which move freely (in their orbit) and (then) hide,
and (I swear) by the night, as it withdraws (gradually),
and by the dawn, as it breathes,
this (Qur’an) is indeed the word of (God, delivered by Gabriel,) a noble messenger,
full of strength, (and) held in honour by the Lord of the Throne.
(He is) well obeyed (in the heavens), and trustworthy as well.
And, (O people,) your guide (Muhammad (blessings and peace be upon him) is not insane.
He surely saw him upon the clear horizon.
And he (i.e. the Esteemed Messenger (blessings and peace be upon him) is not ungenerous in disclosing (the knowledge of) the Unseen.
And this (Qur’an) is not the word of a cursed Satan.
So where are you going?
Indeed, this (Qur’an) is just a Reminder for all nations,
(especially) for the one among you who wishes to be steadfast (on the right path).
But you cannot wish (to do so) unless Allah, the Lord of all worlds, pleases.
When the heavens split apart,
when the stars (and planets) are scattered,
when the oceans (and rivers) are made to overflow,
and when the graves are overturned,
then (every) soul shall know what it has sent forth and left behind.
O humankind! What has deceived you concerning your Generous Lord,
Who created you, fashioned you and perfected you in proportional balance,
and in whatever form He pleased, composed you?
No, indeed! Instead, you deny the (final) Retribution.
Indeed, there are vigilant watchers over you,
(the) noble recorders, writing everything (down).
They know (all) that you do.
Indeed, the virtuous shall be in Bliss,
and the wicked shall be in Hell.
They will enter it to burn on the Day of Retribution,
and they will never be absent from it.
What will make you realise what the Day of Retribution is?
Again! What will make you realise what the Day of Retribution is?
(It is) the Day when a soul shall not possess (any benefit) for another soul. And all authority on that Day will belong to Allah.
Woe to those who give short measures!
Those, when they take the measure (of any commodity) from people, they take (it) in full.
But, when they measure or weigh for others, they give less.
Do such people not think that they will be resurrected,
for a tremendous Day,
a Day when (all) people will stand before the Lord of all worlds?
No, indeed! The record of the wicked is in Sijjin.
And what will make you realise what Sijjin is?
(It is) an inscribed book (kept in the record room of Hell).
Woe to the deniers on that Day,
those who deny the Day of Retribution!
And none denies it except every sinful transgressor.
Whenever Our revelations are recited to him, he says: ‘(These are) tales of the ancients.’
No, indeed! Instead, whatever (evil) they have been earning has stained their hearts.
Undoubtedly, they will be veiled from their Lord that Day.
Then they will enter Hell to burn.
Then they will be told: ‘This is what you used to deny.’
Undoubtedly, the record of the pious is in ‘Illiyyun.
And what will make you realise what ‘Illiyyun is?
(It is) an inscribed book (kept in the record room of Paradise).
The (angels) nearest (to Allah) remain present here (as witnesses).
Indeed, the virtuous shall be in Bliss,
(reclining) upon couches, they will gaze around (the beautiful sights of Paradise).
You will perceive upon their faces the delightful freshness of Bliss.
They will be served with a sealed pure wine.
Its last sip will be (as fragrant as) musk. So, for it, let the aspirants strive to aspire.
And the flavour (of this drink) is (seasoned) from Tasnim.
(It is) a spring from which those nearest (to Allah) will drink.
Indeed, the criminals used to laugh at the believers.
Whenever they (the believers) passed by them, they would wink at one another.
When they returned to their people, they would return jokingly,
and when they saw them (i.e. the believers), they would say: ‘They are the ones astray.’
Yet, they were not sent to be watchers over them.
So today, the believers will laugh at the disbelievers,
(reclining) upon couches, they will observe:
Have the disbelievers been paid back for what they used to do?
When the sky bursts open,
and obeys (the command of) its Lord, as it rightly should,
and when the earth is stretched out,
and throws out all that is in it and becomes empty,
and obeys (the command of) its Lord, as it rightly should.
O humankind! You are labouring towards your Lord restlessly and shall (eventually) meet Him.
Whoever is given his record in his right hand,
he shall be reckoned with an easy reckoning,
and he will return to his family joyfully.
And whoever is given his record (in his left hand) from behind his back,
he will pray for (instant) annihilation,
and he will enter into a burning Fire.
Indeed, he used to be joyful among his people.
Indeed, he thought he would never return (to Allah for the Reckoning).
Yes! Indeed, his Lord was All-Seeing of him.
So I swear by the twilight,
by the night and what it covers,
and by the moon, when it grows full:
You will indeed ride from stage to stage.
So what is (the matter) with them that they do not believe?
When the Qur’an is recited to them, they do not prostrate (in submission).
Instead, the disbelievers persist in denial.
But Allah knows best what they hide.
So warn them of a painful punishment.
But as for those who believe and perform righteous deeds, they will have a never-ending reward.
By the sky full of constellations,
by the Promised Day,
and by the witness and the witnessed:
Perished are the people of the trenches!
The fire (pit), full of fuel,
when they sat around it,
as they were witnessing what they were doing to the believers.
They were vindictive towards them only because they had believed in Allah, the All-Mighty, the All-Praised,
to Whom belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and Allah is Witness over everything.
Indeed, those who persecute the believing men and the believing women and then do not repent will have the punishment of Hell, and for them, there is a (severe) torment of burning.
Indeed, those who believe and perform righteous deeds—for them will be Gardens with rivers flowing beneath them. That is the supreme success.
Indeed, your Lord’s striking is very severe.
It is He Who originates and brings back again,
and He is the Most-Forgiving, the All-Affectionate,
the Lord of the Throne, the All-Glorious,
Doer of whatever He intends.
Has the story of the (destroyed) forces reached you,
(the troops) of Pharaoh and Thamud?
Yet the disbelievers are (persistently) in denial.
But Allah besieges (them) from all sides.
Rather, it is a Glorious Qur’an,
in a Guarded Tablet.
By the sky and by the Night-Comer!
And what will make you realise what the Night-Comer is?
(It is) the star of intense brightness.
There is no soul without a (vigilant) guard over it.
So let human being consider from what (substance) he has been created.
He has been created from a gushing fluid,
that stems from between the loins and the pelvic bones.
Indeed, He is Capable of returning him (to life again),
on the Day when the secrets are disclosed,
and one will have neither (any) power nor helper.
By the heavens returning to their primitive state,
and by the earth splitting asunder (into dust particles):
It is indeed a decisive Word,
and it is not a joke!
Indeed, they are devising an evil scheme.
And I (too) am designing a strategy.
So give the disbelievers respite; let them relax a little (more).
Glorify the Name of your Lord, the Most Exalted,
Who created and fashioned (all in perfect proportion),
Who designed (everything) with precisely programmed measure and guided (accordingly),
and brought out the green pasture (from the earth),
then turned it into blackish withered husks.
We shall make you recite (the Qur’an) so that you do not forget (any of it),
except what Allah wills. Indeed, He knows what is open and what is hidden.
We will facilitate you to (the way of) ease.
So keep reminding (with this Qur’an), even if the reminder is beneficial (only to some).
The fearful will (accept it and) become mindful,
while the most unfortunate will avoid it.
He (is the one) who will enter the Greatest Fire,
where he will neither die nor live.
He is successful indeed, who purifies himself,
and (who) remembers the Name of his Lord and prays (regularly).
But you (deniers) prefer the life of this world,
while the Hereafter is far better and more lasting.
This is indeed (mentioned) in the earlier scriptures,
the scriptures of Ibrahim (Abraham) and Musa (Moses).
Has the news of the overwhelming calamity reached you?
Some faces on that Day will be humiliated,
overstressed (and) exhausted.
They will burn in a Blazing Fire,
(and) made to drink from a boiling spring.
They will have no food except a bitter, thorny shrub,
neither nourishing (them) nor availing (them) against hunger.
Some faces on that Day will be glowing blissfully,
well pleased with their (righteous) endeavour.
(They will be) in a magnificent Paradise,
where they will not hear any vain talk.
In it, there is a flowing spring,
(and) in it, there are raised couches,
and goblets (decently) set in place,
and silken cushions laid in rows,
and splendid carpets spread out.
Do they not reflect on the camels, how (remarkably) they have been created?
And the sky, how it has been raised high?
And the mountains, how they have been firmly set?
And the earth, how it has been levelled out?
So (continue to) remind (them), for your duty is only to remind.
You are not (appointed as) a watcher over them (to compel them).
But whoever turns away and disbelieves,
Allah will inflict upon him the greatest punishment.
Indeed, to Us will be their return,
then, indeed, upon Us will be their Reckoning.

By the dawn,* [* It implies the daily dawn that dispels the darkness of the night, or the Morning Prayer, or particularly the first daybreak of the month of Dhu al-Hijja or the first dawn of Muharram or the ‘Id al-Adha morning. It also connotes the Holy Prophet Muhammad (blessings and peace be upon him), whose Prophethood brought the dawn of truth and faith and ended the darkness of ignorance and misguidance.]

and by the ten nights,* [* This verse can also refer to the last ten nights of the month of Ramadan, the period during which the Qur’an was first revealed. It may also refer to the first ten nights of Muharram or the first ten nights of Dhu al-Hijja, full of Allah’s bounties and blessings.]

and by the even and the odd,* [* The ‘even’ refers to all creation begotten in pairs, while the ‘odd’ implies the One, the Creator, Who has no equal. Some say that shaf ‘ connotes the ‘Day of Sacrifice’, and watr refers to the ‘the Day of ‘Arafat’ (during Hajj). Shaf ‘ may also refer to the days and nights of the worldly life, while watr may denote the Day of Resurrection. Shaf‘ also stands for all even nights of the year, while watr are all the blissful odd nights of the year, such as the night of al-Mi‘raj , the night of al-Bara’a , the night of al-Qadr , which come successively in the months of Rajab, Sha‘ban and Ramadan. Shaf‘ also points to the first couple, Adam and Hawwa’ (Eve [peace be upon both of them]), when together, whereas watr connotes Adam (peace be upon him) when alone because he was the origin of the human race. This verse may also refer to the prayers, most of which have an even number of cycles, whereas one of them, the sunset prayer, has an odd number. Some say the ‘even’ refers to Safa and Marwa, while the ‘odd’ refers to the Ka‘ba .]

and by the night when it departs!* [* It refers to every night in general, which indicates the Wisdom of Allah’s measuring between night and day. But, according to some scholars, it could be a specific reference to the night of the Hajj, when the pilgrims gather at Muzdalifa before moving to Mina. Others say it could refer to the Night of Power ( Layla al-Qadr ).]
Surely, there is an oath in it for the one possessing wisdom.
Have you not seen how your Lord dealt with (the people of) ‘Ad,
and (the people of) Iram, (the city) of the towering castles,
the likes of which had not been created in the countries (at that time),
and (the people of) Thamud, who carved out (their homes in) the rocks in the (Qura) valley,
and Pharaoh, the possessor of large armies?
(They were) those who rebelled (against Allah) in the lands,
and spread much corruption therein.
So your Lord unleashed the scourge of punishment upon them.
Indeed, your Lord is (always) on watch.
As for the human being, whenever his Lord tests him, grants him honour and blesses him (with bounties), he says: ‘My Lord has honoured me.’
But when He tests him and tightens his provision, he says: ‘My Lord has humiliated me.’
No, indeed! Instead, you do not honour the orphan,
and you do not urge one another to feed the poor,
and you eat away the inheritance (neglecting others) greedily,
and you love wealth with an exceeding passion.
No, indeed! When the earth is crumbled into pieces and pounded to dust particles,
and your Lord comes (to judge), with the angels (appearing) in rows upon rows,
(and) Hell is brought (closer) that Day. On that Day, humans will become mindful of advice, but what will be the benefit of remembering then?
He will say: ‘I wish I had sent ahead (some good) for my (real) life!’
On that Day, none shall be able to punish (anyone) like His punishment,
and none shall be able to bind (anyone) like His binding.
(Allah will say to the righteous:) ‘O contented soul!
Return to your Lord, well-pleased (with Him) and well-pleasing (to Him)!
Then enter among My (chosen) servants!
And enter My Paradise (the ultimate abode of My pleasure and nearness)!’
I swear by this city (of Mecca),

as you reside in this city* (O Esteemed Prophet!). [* In the first verse, the particle la has come for emphasis, not negation. According to some scholars, the particle la may also refer to the true negation. In this case, the translation will be: ‘I will not swear by this city (O Prophet!) when you depart from this city.’ According to another linguistic probability, if the particle la is for the negating rhetorical question ( al-istifham al-inkari ), in this case, the translation will be: ‘Why should I not swear by this city (O Prophet!), while you reside there?’]

By the father and that whom he begot,* [* This refers to Adam and his progeny or every begetter and every begotten, indicating all of humanity from the first to the last. This phrase can also refer to Prophets Ibrahim and Isma‘il.
